He said: "This was the day of Badr." Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud said: "We looked at the polytheists and saw them as more numerous than us, doubled over us. Then we looked at them again and did not see them exceeding us by even one man." And that is His saying: {And when He showed them to you, when you met, as few in your eyes, and He made you appear as few in their eyes}.

Abu Ishaq said, from Abu Ubaydah, from Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: "They were indeed made to appear few in our eyes, so much so that I said to a man beside me, 'Do you see them as seventy?' He said, 'I see them as a hundred.'" He said: "Then we captured a man from among them and asked him, 'How many were you?' He said, 'A thousand.'" So when each of the two groups observed the other, the Muslims saw the polytheists as twice their number, that is, more than them by double, so that they would put their trust in Allah, turn to Him, and seek His aid, mighty and majestic is He. And the polytheists saw the believers likewise, so that terror, fear, distress, and dread would befall them. Then, when the ranks were arrayed and the two groups met, Allah made each side appear few in the eyes of the other, so that each would advance against the other.

{So that Allah might accomplish a matter already destined}, that is, so as to distinguish truth from falsehood, so that the word of faith would prevail over disbelief, and the believers would be given might and the disbelievers would be brought low, as the Almighty said: {And Allah had already given you victory at Badr while you were weak} [Al Imran: 123]. And He said here: {And Allah supports with His victory whom He wills. Indeed in that is a lesson for those who have eyes}, that is, in that is surely a lesson for whoever has insight and understanding, by which he is guided to the judgment of Allah, His actions, and His decree that runs its course in giving victory to His believing servants in this worldly life and on the Day when the witnesses will stand.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: قُلْ يَا مُحَمَّدُ لِلْكَافِرِينَ: ﴿سَتُغْلَبُونَ﴾ أَيْ: فِي الدُّنْيَا، ﴿وَتُحْشَرُونَ﴾ أَيْ: يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ﴿إِلَى جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهَادُ﴾ .

وَقَدْ ذَكَرَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ قَتَادَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم لما أَصَابَ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَدْرٍ مَا أَصَابَ وَرَجَعَ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ، جَمَعَ الْيَهُودَ فِي سُوقِ بَنِي قَيْنُقَاع وَقَالَ: " يَا مَعْشَرَ يَهُودَ، أَسْلِمُوا قَبْلَ أَنْ يُصِيبَكُمُ اللَّهُ مَا أَصَابَ قُرَيْشًا". فَقَالُوا: يَا مُحَمَّدُ، لَا يَغُرَّنَّكَ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ أَنْ قَتَلْتَ نَفَرًا مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ كَانُوا أَغْمَارًا لَا يَعْرِفُونَ الْقِتَالَ، إِنَّكَ وَاللَّهِ لَوْ قَاتَلْتَنَا لَعَرَفْتَ أَنَّا نَحْنُ النَّاسُ، وَأَنَّكَ لَمْ تَلْقَ مِثْلَنَا؟ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فِي ذَلِكَ مِنْ قَوْلِهِمْ: ﴿قُلْ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا سَتُغْلَبُونَ وَتُحْشَرُونَ إِلَى جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهَادُ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿لَعِبْرَةً لأولِي الأبْصَارِ﴾ .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ إِسْحَاقَ أَيْضًا، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ أَوْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فَذَكَرَهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿قَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ آيَةٌ﴾ أَيْ: قَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ -أَيُّهَا الْيَهُودُ الْقَائِلُونَ مَا قُلْتُمْ - ﴿آيَةٌ﴾ أَيْ: دَلَالَةٌ عَلَى أَنَّ اللَّهَ مُعِزٌّ دِينَهُ، وَنَاصِرٌ رَسُولَهُ، وَمُظْهِرٌ كَلِمَتَهُ، وَمُعْلٍ أَمْرَهُ ﴿فِي فِئَتَيْنِ﴾ أَيْ: طَائِفَتَيْنِ ﴿الْتَقَتَا﴾ أَيْ: لِلْقِتَالِ ﴿فِئَةٌ تُقَاتِلُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ﴾ وَهْمُ الْمُسْلِمُونَ، ﴿وَأُخْرَى كَافِرَةٌ﴾ وَهْمْ مُشْرِكُو قُرَيْشٍ يَوْمَ بَدْرٍ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿يَرَوْنَهُمْ مِثْلَيْهِمْ رَأْيَ الْعَيْنِ﴾ قَالَ بَعْضُ الْعُلَمَاءِ -فِيمَا حَكَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: يَرَى الْمُشْرِكُونَ يَوْمَ بَدْرٍ الْمُسْلِمِينَ مِثْلَيْهِمْ فِي الْعَدَدِ رَأْيَ أَعْيُنِهِمْ، أَيْ: جَعَلَ اللَّهُ ذَلِكَ فِيمَا رَأَوْهُ سَبَبًا لِنُصْرَةِ الْإِسْلَامِ عَلَيْهِمْ. وَهَذَا لَا إِشْكَالَ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا مِنْ جِهَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ، وَهِيَ أَنَّ الْمُشْرِكِينَ بَعَثُوا عُمَرَ بْنَ سَعْدٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ قَبْلَ الْقِتَالِ يَحْزِرُ لَهُمُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَأَخْبَرَهُمْ بِأَنَّهُمْ ثَلَاثُمِائَةٍ، يَزِيدُونَ قَلِيلًا أَوْ يَنْقُصُونَ قَلِيلًا. وَهَكَذَا كَانَ الْأَمْرُ، كَانُوا ثَلَاثَمِائَةٍ وَبِضْعَةَ عَشَرَ رَجُلًا ثُمَّ لَمَّا وَقْعَ الْقِتَالُ أَمَدَّهُمُ اللَّهُ بِأَلْفٍ مِنْ خَوَاصِّ الْمَلَائِكَةِ وَسَادَاتِهِمْ.

وَالْقَوْلُ الثَّانِي: " أَنَّ الْمَعْنَى فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿يَرَوْنَهُمْ مِثْلَيْهِمْ رَأْيَ الْعَيْنِ﴾ أَيْ: تَرَى الْفِئَةُ الْمُسْلِمَةُ الْفِئَةَ الْكَافِرَةَ مِثْلَيْهِمْ، أَيْ: ضِعْفَيْهِمْ فِي الْعَدَدِ، وَمَعَ هَذَا نَصَرَهُمُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ. وَهَذَا لَا إِشْكَالَ فِيهِ عَلَى مَا رَوَاهُ الْعَوْفِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كَانُوا يَوْمَ بَدْرٍ ثَلَاثَمِائَةٍ وَثَلَاثَةَ عَشَرَ رَجُلًا وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ كَانُوا سِتَّمِائَةٍ وَسِتَّةً وَعِشْرِينَ رَجُلًا. وَكَأَنَّ هَذَا الْقَوْلَ مَأْخُوذٌ مِنْ ظَاهِرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ، وَلَكِنَّهُ خِلَافُ الْمَشْهُورِ عِنْدَ أَهْلِ التَّوَارِيخِ وَالسِّيَرِ وَأَيَّامِ النَّاسِ، وَخِلَافُ الْمَعْرُوفِ عِنْدَ الْجُمْهُورِ مِنْ أَنَّ الْمُشْرِكِينَ كَانُوا مَا بَيْنَ التِّسْعِمِائَةِ إِلَى الْأَلْفِ كَمَا رَوَاهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ رُومَانَ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ لَمَّا سَأَلَ ذلك العبد الْأَسْوَدَ لِبَنِي الْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ عِدَّةِ قُرَيْشٍ، فَقَالَ: كَثِيرٌ، قَالَ: "كَمْ يَنْحَرُونَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ؟ " قَالَ: يَوْمًا تِسْعًا وَيَوْمًا عَشْرًا، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "الْقَوْمُ مَا بَيْنَ التِّسْعِمِائَةِ إِلَى الْأَلْفِ" .

وَرَوَى أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ السَّبِيعي، عَنْ حَارِثَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: كَانُوا أَلْفًا، وَكَذَا قَالَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ. وَالْمَشْهُورُ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا مَا بَيْنَ التِّسْعِمِائَةِ إِلَى الْأَلْفِ، وَعَلَى كُلِّ تَقْدِيرٍ فَقَدْ كَانُوا ثَلَاثَةَ أَمْثَالِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، وَعَلَى هَذَا فَيُشْكَلُ هَذَا الْقَوْلُ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. لَكِنْ وَجَّهَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ هَذَا، وَجَعْلَهُ صَحِيحًا كَمَا تَقُولُ: عِنْدِي أَلْفٌ وَأَنَا مُحْتَاجٌ إِلَى مِثْلَيْهَا، وَتَكُونُ مُحْتَاجًا إِلَى ثَلَاثَةِ آلَافٍ، كَذَا قَالَ. وَعَلَى هَذَا فَلَا إِشْكَالَ.

لَكِنْ بَقِيَ سُؤَالٌ آخَرُ وَهُوَ وَارِدٌ عَلَى الْقَوْلَيْنِ، وَهُوَ أَنْ يُقَالَ: مَا الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ وَبَيْنَ قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى فِي قِصَّةِ بَدْرٍ: ﴿وَإِذْ يُرِيكُمُوهُمْ إِذِ الْتَقَيْتُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِكُمْ قَلِيلا وَيُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِهِمْ لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولا﴾ ؟ [الْأَنْفَالِ: ٤٤] وَالْجَوَابُ: أَنَّ هَذَا كَانَ فِي حَالٍ، وَالْآخَرُ كَانَ فِي حَالٍ أُخْرَى، كَمَا قَالَ السُّدِّي، عَنِ [مرة] الطَّيِّبِ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿قَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ آيَةٌ فِي فِئَتَيْنِ الْتَقَتَا [فِئَةٌ تُقَاتِلُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَأُخْرَى كَافِرَةٌ يَرَوْنَهُمْ مِثْلَيْهِمْ رَأْيَ الْعَيْنِ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، قَالَ: هَذَا يَوْمُ بَدْرٍ. قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مَسْعُودٍ: وَقَدْ نَظَرْنَا إِلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ فَرَأَيْنَاهُمْ يُضْعَفون عَلَيْنَا، ثُمَّ نَظَرْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ فَمَا رَأَيْنَاهُمْ يَزِيدُونَ عَلَيْنَا رَجُلًا وَاحِدًا، وَذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِذْ يُرِيكُمُوهُمْ إِذِ الْتَقَيْتُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِكُمْ قَلِيلا وَيُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِهِمْ﴾ .

وَقَالَ أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: لَقَدْ قُلِّلُوا فِي أَعْيُنِنَا حَتَّى قُلْتُ لِرَجُلٍ إِلَى جَانِبِي تَرَاهُمْ سَبْعِينَ؟ قَالَ: أَرَاهُمْ مِائَةً. قَالَ: فَأَسَرْنَا رَجُلًا مِنْهُمْ فَقُلْنَا: كَمْ كُنْتُمْ؟ قَالَ: أَلْفَا. فَعِنْدَمَا عَايَنَ كُلُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ الْآخَرَ رَأَى الْمُسْلِمُونَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ مِثْلَيْهِمْ، أَيْ: أَكْثَرُ مِنْهُمْ بِالضِّعْفِ، لِيَتَوَكَّلُوا وَيَتَوَجَّهُوا وَيَطْلُبُوا الْإِعَانَةَ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ. وَرَأَى الْمُشْرِكُونَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كَذَلِكَ لِيَحْصُلَ لَهُمُ الرُّعْبُ وَالْخَوْفُ وَالْجَزَعُ وَالْهَلَعُ، ثُمَّ لَمَّا حَصَلَ التَّصَافُّ وَالْتَقَى الْفَرِيقَانِ قَلَّلَ اللَّهُ هَؤُلَاءِ فِي أَعْيُنِ هَؤُلَاءِ، وَهَؤُلَاءِ فِي أَعْيُنِ هَؤُلَاءِ، لِيُقْدِمَ كُلٌّ مِنْهُمَا عَلَى الْآخَرِ.

﴿لِيَقْضِيَ اللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولا﴾ أَيْ: لِيُفَرِّقَ بَيْنَ الْحَقِّ وَالْبَاطِلِ، فَيُظْهِرَ كَلِمَةَ الْإِيمَانِ عَلَى الْكُفْرِ، وَيُعِزَّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَيُذِلَّ الْكَافِرِينَ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِبَدْرٍ وَأَنْتُمْ أَذِلَّةٌ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ: ١٢٣] وَقَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿وَاللَّهُ يُؤَيِّدُ بِنَصْرِهِ مَنْ يَشَاءُ إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لأولِي الأبْصَارِ﴾ أَيْ: إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَمُعْتَبَرًا لِمَنْ لَهُ بَصِيرَةٌ وَفَهْمٌ يَهْتَدِي بِهِ إِلَى حُكْمِ اللَّهِ وَأَفْعَالِهِ، وَقَدَرِهِ الْجَارِي بِنَصْرِ عِبَادِهِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي هَذِهِ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَيَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْأَشْهَادُ.

---

## 3:14-15 — verses 14-15 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty informs about what has been made appealing to people in this worldly life, of the various pleasures found in women and sons. He began with women because temptation through them is more severe, as is established in the sound hadith that he, peace be upon him, said: "I have not left after me any temptation more harmful to men than women." As for when the purpose of them is chastity (i'faf) and abundance of children, this is sought, desired, and recommended, as the hadiths have come recommending marriage and multiplying it: "Indeed the best of this nation were those with the most wives," and his saying, peace be upon him: "The world is provision, and the best provision of it is the righteous woman. If he looks at her she pleases him, if he commands her she obeys him, and if he is absent from her she guards herself and his wealth." And his saying in another hadith: "Women and perfume have been made beloved to me, and the coolness of my eye has been placed in prayer." And Aisha, may God be pleased with her, said: "There was nothing more beloved to the Messenger of God ﷺ than women, except horses," and in another narration: "than horses, except women."

Love of sons is sometimes for the sake of boasting and adornment, and this falls within the same category. And sometimes it is for the sake of increasing offspring and increasing the nation of Muhammad ﷺ among those who worship God alone with no partner, and this is praiseworthy and commended, as is established in the hadith: "Marry the loving, fertile woman, for I will boast of your numbers before the nations on the Day of Resurrection."

Likewise, love of wealth is sometimes for the sake of boasting, arrogance, and haughtiness toward the weak, and tyranny over the poor, and this is blameworthy. And sometimes it is for spending on acts of nearness to God, maintaining kinship ties and relations, and various forms of righteousness and obedience, and this is praised and commended according to the Sacred Law.

The commentators have differed regarding the amount of a qintar (a great measure of wealth), with various sayings, the sum of which is that it refers to abundant wealth, as al-Dahhak and others said. It has also been said: a thousand dinars. And it has been said: a thousand and two hundred dinars. And it has been said: twelve thousand. And it has been said: forty thousand. And it has been said: sixty thousand. And it has been said: seventy thousand. And it has been said: eighty thousand. And other than that has been said.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Hurayrah, may God be pleased with him, said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "The qintar is twelve thousand uqiyyah (ounces), each uqiyyah being better than what is between heaven and earth."

Ibn Majah narrated it [chain] from Abd al-Samad ibn Abd al-Warith, from Hammad ibn Salamah, with it. Ibn Jarir narrated it [chain] from Hammad ibn Zayd, from Asim, that is Ibn Bahdalah, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah, as a mawquf (halted) report, and this is more sound. Ibn Jarir likewise narrated it from Mu'adh ibn Jabal and Ibn Umar. Ibn Abi Hatim related it from Abu Hurayrah and Abu al-Darda, that they said: the qintar is a thousand and two hundred uqiyyah.

Then Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ubayy ibn Ka'b said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "The qintar is one thousand and two hundred uqiyyah."

This is also a munkar (rejected) hadith, and it is more likely to be a mawquf report attributed to Ubayy ibn Ka'b, as with other Companions. Ibn Mardawayh narrated, through the route of Musa ibn Ubaydah al-Rabadhi, from Muhammad ibn Ibrahim, from Yahannas Abu Musa, from Umm al-Darda, from Abu al-Darda, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever recites one hundred verses will not be recorded among the heedless, and whoever recites from one hundred verses up to a thousand will have, by morning, a qintar of reward with God, a qintar of which is like a great mountain." Waki' narrated it from Musa ibn Ubaydah with the same meaning. Al-Hakim said in his Mustadrak: [chain] Anas ibn Malik said: The Messenger of God ﷺ was asked about the saying of God, mighty and majestic, {and the accumulated qanatir (hoarded treasures)}, and he said: "The qintar is two thousand uqiyyah." Sound according to the criterion of the two Shaykhs (Bukhari and Muslim), though they did not include it. Thus al-Hakim narrated it.

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it with another wording, saying: [chain] Humayd al-Tawil and another man he named, namely Yazid al-Raqashi, from Anas, from the Messenger of God ﷺ regarding His saying: qintar, meaning: "a thousand dinars." Ibn Mardawayh likewise [narrated it], and al-Tabarani narrated it from Abd Allah ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Maryam, from Amr ibn Abi Salamah, mentioning it with his chain exactly the same.

Ibn Jarir narrated from al-Hasan al-Basri, as a mursal (report with a missing link) from him and as a mawquf report attributed to him: the qintar is a thousand and two hundred dinars. Al-Awfi likewise narrated it from Ibn Abbas.

Al-Dahhak said: among the Arabs there are those who say the qintar is a thousand dinars, and among them are those who say it is twelve thousand.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, may God be pleased with him, said: [the qintar] is the fill of a bull's hide in gold.

Abu Muhammad (Ibn Abi Hatim) said: Muhammad ibn Musa al-Harashi narrated it from Hammad ibn Zayd as a marfu (elevated) report, but the mawquf version is more sound.

Love of horses is of three kinds. Sometimes their owners keep them prepared for the path of God, Almighty, so that whenever they need them they go out to fight upon them, and such people are rewarded. And sometimes they are kept for boasting and hostility toward the people of Islam, and this is a burden upon its owner. And sometimes they are kept for the sake of chastity and breeding their offspring, and their owner does not neglect God's due regarding them, and this is a shield (satr) for its owner, as the hadith concerning this will come, if God Almighty wills, at His saying: {And prepare against them whatever you are able of power and of steeds of war, by which you may terrify the enemy of God and your enemy} [al-Anfal: 60].

As for {al-musawwamah}, from Ibn Abbas, may God be pleased with both of them: al-musawwamah means the grazing horses, and al-mutahhamah means the fine, handsome ones. The same has been narrated from Mujahid, Ikrimah, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Abd al-Rahman ibn Abd Allah ibn Abza, al-Suddi, al-Rabi' ibn Anas, Abu Sinan, and others.

Makhul said: al-musawwamah refers to the blaze (on the forehead) and white markings (on the legs) of horses. And other than that has been said.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Dharr, may God be pleased with him, said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "There is no Arabian horse but that it is permitted, with every dawn, to make two supplications, saying: 'O God, You have entrusted me to whoever You have entrusted me among the sons of Adam, so make me among the most beloved of his wealth and family to him,' or 'the most beloved of his family and wealth to him.'"

His saying: {and the cattle} means: camels, cattle, and sheep. {and the tilled land} means: land taken up for planting and cultivation.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] the Prophet ﷺ said: "The best wealth of a man is a fertile mare or well-pollinated rows of palm trees." Al-ma'murah means abundant in offspring, and al-sikkah means rows of aligned palm trees, and al-ma'burah means pollinated.

Then the Almighty says: {That is the enjoyment of the worldly life}, meaning: this is only the flower of worldly life and its adornment, perishable and passing away. {But with God is the good return}, meaning: the good place of return and reward.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Umar ibn al-Khattab, may God be pleased with him, said: When {Made appealing to people is the love of desires} was revealed, I said, "Now, O Lord, when You have made it appealing to us?" Then there was revealed: {Say: Shall I inform you of something better than that? For those who fear God [with their Lord are gardens beneath which rivers flow]}.

For this reason the Almighty says: {Say: Shall I inform you of something better than that}, meaning: Say, O Muhammad, to the people: shall I inform you of something better than what has been made appealing to people in this worldly life, of its flower and bliss, which is bound to pass away without fail. Then He informs about that, saying: {For those who fear God, with their Lord are gardens beneath which rivers flow}, meaning: rivers cut through their sides and expanses, of various kinds of drinks: honey, milk, wine, water, and other than that, of what no eye has seen, no ear has heard, and what has never occurred to the heart of any human being.

{Abiding therein eternally}, meaning: remaining therein forever and ever, not desiring to be removed from it.

{And purified spouses}, meaning: purified from filth, impurity, harm, menstruation, postnatal bleeding, and other than that which afflicts the women of this world.

{And approval from God}, meaning: His approval descends upon them, so that He is never angry with them after it ever; for this reason the Almighty says in the other verse, in Surat Bara'ah (al-Tawbah): {And approval from God is greater} [al-Tawbah: 72], meaning: greater than what He has given them of everlasting bliss. Then He says: {And God is watchful over His servants}, meaning: He gives each one according to what he deserves of giving.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى عَمَّا زُيِّن لِلنَّاسِ فِي هَذِهِ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا مِنْ أَنْوَاعِ الْمَلَاذِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ وَالْبَنِينِ، فَبَدَأَ بِالنِّسَاءِ لِأَنَّ الْفِتْنَةَ بِهِنَّ أَشَدُّ، كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحِ أَنَّهُ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، قَالَ مَا تَرَكْتُ بَعْدِي فِتْنَةً أَضَرُّ عَلَى الرِّجَالِ مِنَ النِّساء". فَأَمَّا إِذَا كَانَ الْقَصْدُ بِهِنَّ الْإِعْفَافَ وَكَثْرَةَ الْأَوْلَادِ، فَهَذَا مَطْلُوبٌ مَرْغُوبٌ فِيهِ مَنْدُوبٌ إِلَيْهِ، كَمَا وَرَدَتِ الْأَحَادِيثُ بِالتَّرْغِيبِ فِي التَّزْوِيجِ وَالِاسْتِكْثَارِ مِنْهُ، "وإنَّ خَيْرَ هَذه الأمَّةِ كَانَ أكْثرهَا نسَاءً" وَقَوْلُهُ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ الدُّنْيَا مَتَاع، وخَيْرُ مَتَاعِهَا المرْأةُ الصَّالحةُ، إنْ نَظَرَ إلَيْها سَرَّتْهُ، وإنْ أَمَرَهَا أَطَاعَتْه، وإنْ غَابَ عَنْها حَفِظْتُه فِي نَفْسهَا وَمَالِهِ" وَقَوْلُهُ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الْآخَرِ: "حُبِّبَ إلَيَّ النِّسَاءُ والطِّيبُ وجُعلَتْ قُرة عَيْني فِي الصَّلاةِ" وَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا: لَمْ يَكُنْ شَيْءٌ أَحَبَّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم من النِّسَاءِ إِلَّا الْخَيْلُ، وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ: مِنَ الْخَيْلِ إِلَّا النِّسَاءُ .

وَحُبُّ الْبَنِينَ تَارَةً يَكُونُ لِلتَّفَاخُرِ وَالزِّينَةِ فَهُوَ دَاخِلٌ فِي هَذَا، وَتَارَةً يَكُونُ لِتَكْثِيرِ النَّسْلِ، وَتَكْثِيرِ أُمَّةِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ مِمَّنْ يَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، فَهَذَا مَحْمُودٌ مَمْدُوحٌ، كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: "تَزَوَّجُوا الوَدُودَ الوَلُودَ، فَإنِّي مُكَاثِرٌ بِكُمُ الأمَمَ يَوْمَ القِيَامَةِ" 

وَحُبُّ الْمَالِ -كَذَلِكَ-تَارَةً يَكُونُ لِلْفَخْرِ وَالْخُيَلَاءِ وَالتَّكَبُّرِ عَلَى الضُّعَفَاءِ، وَالتَّجَبُّرِ عَلَى الْفُقَرَاءِ، فَهَذَا مَذْمُومٌ، وَتَارَةً يَكُونُ لِلنَّفَقَةِ فِي الْقُرُبَاتِ وَصِلَةِ الْأَرْحَامِ وَالْقَرَابَاتِ وَوُجُوهِ الْبِرِّ وَالطَّاعَاتِ، فَهَذَا مَمْدُوحٌ مَحْمُودٌ عَلَيْهِ شَرْعًا.

وَقَدِ اخْتَلَفَ الْمُفَسِّرُونَ فِي مِقْدَارِ الْقِنْطَارِ عَلَى أَقْوَالٍ، وَحَاصِلُهَا: أَنَّهُ الْمَالُ الْجَزِيلُ، كَمَا قَالَهُ الضَّحَّاكُ وَغَيْرُهُ، وَقِيلَ: أَلْفُ دِينَارٍ. وَقِيلَ: أَلْفٌ وَمِائَتَا دِينَارٍ. وَقِيلَ: اثْنَا عَشَرَ أَلْفًا. وَقِيلَ: أَرْبَعُونَ أَلْفًا. وَقِيلَ: سِتُّونَ أَلْفًا وَقِيلَ: سَبْعُونَ أَلْفًا. وَقِيلَ: ثَمَانُونَ أَلْفًا. وَقِيلَ غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادٌ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "القِنْطَارُ اثْنَا عَشَرَ ألْف أوقيَّةٍ، كُلُّ أوقِيَّةٍ خَيْر مِمَّا بَيْنَ السَّمَاءِ والأرْضِ".

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الصَّمَدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْوَارِثِ، عن حماد ابن سَلَمَةَ، بِهِ. وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ بُنْدار، عَنِ ابْنِ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ -هُوَ ابْنُ بَهْدَلة-عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ مَوْقُوفًا، وَهَذَا أَصَحُّ. وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ مُعَاذِ بْنِ جَبَلٍ وَابْنِ عُمَرَ. وَحَكَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ وَأَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ، أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا: الْقِنْطَارُ أَلْفٌ وَمِائَتَا أُوقِيَّةٍ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي زَكَرِيَّا بْنُ يَحْيَى الضَّرِيرُ، حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ، حَدَّثَنَا مَخْلَد بْنُ عَبْدِ الْوَاحِدِ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي مَيْمُونَةَ، عَنْ زِرّ بْنِ حُبَيْش عَنْ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "القِنْطَارُ ألْفُ أوقِيَّةٍ ومائَتَا أوقِيَّةٍ" .

وَهَذَا حَدِيثٌ مُنْكَرٌ أَيْضًا، والأقربُ أَنْ يَكُونَ مَوْقُوفًا عَلَى أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ، كَغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ. وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه، مِنْ طَرِيقِ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدة الرَبَذِي عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ يحنَّش أَبِي مُوسَى، عَنْ أُمِّ الدَّرْدَاءِ، عَنْ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ قَرَأ مِائَةَ آيةٍ لَمْ يُكْتَبْ مِنَ الْغَافِلِينَ، ومَنْ قَرَأ مِائَةَ آيةٍ إِلَى ألْف أصْبَح لَهُ قِنْطار مِنْ أجْرٍ عندَ اللَّهِ، القِنْطارُ مِنْهُ مِثلُ الجبَلِ العَظِيمِ". وَرَوَاهُ وَكِيع، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيدة، بِمَعْنَاهُ وَقَالَ الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْعَبَّاسِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عِيسَى بْنِ زَيْدٍ اللَّخْمِيُّ بتنِّيس حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرو بْنُ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا زُهَيْرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيد الطَّوِيلُ، وَرَجُلٌ آخَرُ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ: سُئِلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿وَالْقَنَاطِيرِ الْمُقَنْطَرَةِ﴾ قَالَ: "القِنْطَارُ أَلْفَا أُوقِيَّةٍ". صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ الشيخين ولم يخرجاه، هكذا رواه الحاكم .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ بِلَفْظٍ آخَرَ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّقِّي، حدثنا عمرو ابن أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا زُهَيْرٌ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ-حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدٌ الطَّوِيلُ وَرَجُلٌ آخَرُ قَدْ سَمَّاهُ-يَعْنِي يَزِيدَ الرَّقَاشي-عَنْ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي قَوْلِهِ: قِنْطَارٌ، يَعْنِي: "أَلْفَ دِينَارٍ". وَهَكَذَا [رَوَاهُ] ابْنُ مَرْدُويه، وَرَوَاهُ الطَّبَرَانِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، عَنْ عَمْرو بْنِ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، فَذَكَرَ بِإِسْنَادِهِ مِثْلَهُ سَوَاءً .

وَرَوَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ مُرْسَلًا عَنْهُ وَمَوْقُوفًا عَلَيْهِ: الْقِنْطَارُ أَلْفٌ وَمِائَتَا دِينَارٍ. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ العَوْفي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ.

وَقَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ: مِنَ الْعَرَبِ مَنْ يَقُولُ: الْقِنْطَارُ أَلْفُ دِينَارٍ. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَقُولُ: اثْنَا عَشَرَ أَلْفًا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عارِم، عَنْ حَمّاد، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ الجُرَيرِي عَنْ أَبِي نضْرة، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: [الْقِنْطَارُ] مِلْءُ مَسْك الثَّوْرِ ذَهَبًا.

قَالَ أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ: وَرَوَاهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَى الْحَرَشِيُّ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، مَرْفُوعًا. وَالْمَوْقُوفُ أَصَحُّ .

وَحُبُّ الْخَيْلِ عَلَى ثَلَاثَةِ أَقْسَامٍ، تَارَةً يَكُونُ ربطَها أصحابُها معدَّة لِسَبِيلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى، مَتَى احْتَاجُوا إِلَيْهَا غزَوا عَلَيْهَا، فَهَؤُلَاءِ يُثَابُونَ. وَتَارَةً تُرْبَطُ فَخْرًا وَنِوَاءً لِأَهْلِ الْإِسْلَامِ، فَهَذِهِ عَلَى صَاحِبِهَا وزْر. وَتَارَةً لِلتَّعَفُّفِ وَاقْتِنَاءِ نَسْلِهَا. وَلَمْ يَنْسَ حَقَّ اللَّهِ فِي رِقَابِهَا، فَهَذِهِ لِصَاحِبِهَا ستْر، كَمَا سَيَأْتِي الْحَدِيثُ بِذَلِكَ [إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى] عِنْدَ قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَأَعِدُّوا لَهُمْ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ قُوَّةٍ وَمِنْ رِبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ [تُرْهِبُونَ بِهِ عَدُوَّ اللَّهِ وَعَدُوَّكُمْ] ﴾ [الْأَنْفَالِ: ٦٠] .

وَأَمَّا ﴿الْمُسَوَّمَةِ﴾ فَعَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: الْمُسَوَّمَةُ الرَّاعِيَةُ، والمُطَهَّمة الحسَان، وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أبْزَى، والسُّدِّي، وَالرَّبِيعِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، وَأَبِي سِنَان وَغَيْرِهِمْ.

وَقَالَ مَكْحُولٌ: الْمُسَوَّمَةُ: الغُرَّة وَالتَّحْجِيلُ. وَقِيلَ غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ سُوَيْد بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ حُدَيج، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "ليسَ مِنْ فَرَسٍ عَرَبِي إِلَّا يُؤذَنُ لَهُ مَعَ كُلِّ فَجْر يَدْعُو بِدَعْوَتَيْنِ، يَقُولُ: اللَّهُمَّ إنَّكَ خَوَّلْتَنِي مِنْ خَوَّلْتَني من] بَنِي آدَم، فاجْعَلنِي مِنْ أحَبِّ مَالِهِ وأهْلِهِ إِلَيْهِ، أوْ أحَب أهْلِه ومالِهِ إليهِ" .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَالأنْعَامِ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْإِبِلَ وَالْبَقَرَ وَالْغَنَمَ ﴿وَالْحَرْث﴾ يَعْنِي: الْأَرْضَ الْمُتَّخَذَةَ للغِرَاس وَالزِّرَاعَةِ .

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْح بْنُ عُبَادَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو نَعَامَةَ الْعَدَوِيُّ، عَنْ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ بُدَيل عَنْ إياسِ بْنِ زُهَيْرٍ، عَنْ سُويد بْنِ هُبَيرة، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "خَيْرُ مَالِ امْرِئٍ لَهُ مُهْرة مَأمُورة، أَوْ سِكَّة مَأبُورة" الْمَأْمُورَةُ الْكَثِيرَةُ النَّسْلِ، والسّكَّة: النَّخْلُ الْمُصْطَفُّ، وَالْمَأْبُورَةُ: الْمُلَقَّحَةُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿ذَلِكَ مَتَاعُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا﴾ أَيْ: إِنَّمَا هَذَا زَهْرَةُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتُهَا الْفَانِيَةُ الزَّائِلَةُ ﴿وَاللَّهُ عِنْدَهُ حُسْنُ الْمَآبِ﴾ أَيْ: حُسْنُ الْمَرْجِعِ وَالثَّوَابِ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ حُمَيْدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ حَفْصِ بْنِ عُمَر بْنِ سَعْدٍ قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ: لَمَّا أُنْزِلَتْ: ﴿زُيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ حُبُّ الشَّهَوَاتِ﴾ قُلْتُ: الْآنَ يَا رَبِّ حِينَ زَيَّنْتَهَا لَنَا فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿قُلْ أَؤُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِخَيْرٍ مِنْ ذَلِكُمْ لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا [عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الأنْهَارُ] ﴾ .

وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ أَؤُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِخَيْرٍ مِنْ ذَلِكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: قُلْ يَا مُحَمَّدُ لِلنَّاسِ: أَأُخْبِرُكُمْ بِخَيْرٍ مِمَّا زُيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ فِي هَذِهِ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا مِنْ زَهْرَتِهَا وَنَعِيمِهَا، الَّذِي هُوَ زَائِلٌ لَا مَحَالَةَ. ثُمَّ أَخْبَرَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ، فَقَالَ: ﴿لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الأنْهَارُ﴾ أَيْ: تَنْخَرِقُ بَيْنَ جَوَانِبِهَا وَأَرْجَائِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ، مِنْ أَنْوَاعِ الْأَشْرِبَةِ؛ مِنَ الْعَسَلِ وَاللَّبَنِ وَالْخَمْرِ وَالْمَاءِ وَغَيْرِ ذَلِكَ، مِمَّا لَا عَيْنٌ رَأَتْ، وَلَا أُذُنٌ سَمِعَتْ، وَلَا خَطَرَ عَلَى قَلْبِ بَشَرٍ.

﴿خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا﴾ أَيْ: مَاكِثِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدَ الْآبَادِ لَا يَبْغُونَ عَنْهَا حِوَلا.

﴿وَأَزْوَاجٌ مُطَهَّرَةٌ﴾ أَيْ: مِنَ الدَّنَس، والخَبَث، وَالْأَذَى، وَالْحَيْضِ، وَالنِّفَاسِ، وَغَيْرِ ذَلِكَ مِمَّا يَعْتَرِي نِسَاءَ الدُّنْيَا.

﴿وَرِضْوَانٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: يَحِلُّ عَلَيْهِمْ رِضْوَانُهُ، فَلَا يَسْخَط عَلَيْهِمْ بَعْدَهُ أَبَدًا؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى فِي الْآيَةِ الْأُخْرَى الَّتِي فِي بَرَاءَةٌ: ﴿وَرِضْوَانٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ٧٢] أَيْ: أَعْظَمُ مِمَّا أَعْطَاهُمْ من النعيم المقيم، ثُمَّ قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ﴾ أَيْ: يُعْطِي كُلًّا بِحَسْبِ مَا يَسْتَحِقُّهُ مِنَ الْعَطَاءِ.

---

## 3:16-17 — verses 16-17 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Allah, exalted is He, describes His God-fearing servants whom He has promised abundant reward. He says, exalted is He: {Those who say, "Our Lord, indeed we have believed"} that is, in You, in Your Book, and in Your Messenger, {so forgive us our sins} that is, because of our faith in You and in what You have prescribed for us, forgive us our sins and our shortcomings in our affairs, by Your bounty and Your mercy, {and protect us from the punishment of the Fire}.

Then He says: {the patient ones} that is, in their steadfastness in performing acts of obedience and abandoning what is forbidden, {and the truthful ones} in what they report of their faith, matched by their commitment to arduous deeds, {and the devoutly obedient} and qunut (devout obedience) means obedience and submission, {and those who spend} that is, from their wealth in all that they have been commanded regarding acts of obedience, maintaining ties of kinship and relations, meeting needs, and comforting those in want, {and those who seek forgiveness in the last hours before dawn}. This indicates the virtue of seeking forgiveness at the time before dawn.

It has been said that when Jacob (peace be upon him) said to his sons, {I will seek forgiveness for you from my Lord} [Yusuf: 98], he delayed doing so until the time before dawn. It is established in the two Sahihs and other collections of musnads and sunan, through more than one route, from a group of the Companions, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah, blessed and exalted, descends every night to the lowest heaven when the last third of the night remains, and says: Is there anyone who asks, that I may give him? Is there anyone who supplicates, that I may answer him? Is there anyone who seeks forgiveness, that I may forgive him?" the hadith continues. The hafiz Abu al-Hasan al-Daraqutni devoted a separate volume to this, narrating it through numerous chains.

In the two Sahihs, from Aisha, may Allah be pleased with her, she said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed the witr prayer at every part of the night, at its beginning, its middle, and its end, until his witr came to rest at the time before dawn.

Abdullah ibn Umar used to pray during the night, then say: "O Nafi', has the time before dawn come?" And when he said, "Yes," he would turn to supplication and seeking forgiveness until morning came. Narrated by Ibn Abi Hatim.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ibrahim ibn Hatib, from his father, who said: I heard a man at the time before dawn in a corner of the mosque saying: "My Lord, You commanded me and I obeyed You, and this is the time before dawn, so forgive me." I looked, and there was Ibn Mas'ud, may Allah be pleased with him.

Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Anas ibn Malik, who said: We were commanded, when we prayed during the night, to seek forgiveness seventy times in the last part of the time before dawn.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَصِفُ تَعَالَى عِبَادَهُ الْمُتَّقِينَ الَّذِينَ وَعَدَهُمُ الثَّوَابَ الْجَزِيلَ، فَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا آمَنَّا﴾ أَيْ: بِكَ وَبِكِتَابِكَ وَبِرَسُولِكَ ﴿فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا﴾ أَيْ بِإِيمَانِنَا بِكَ وَبِمَا شَرَعْتَهُ لَنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَتَقْصِيرَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا بِفَضْلِكَ وَرَحْمَتِكَ ﴿وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿الصَّابِرِين﴾ أَيْ: فِي قِيَامِهِمْ بِالطَّاعَاتِ وَتَرْكِهِمُ الْمُحَرَّمَاتِ ﴿وَالصَّادِقِينَ﴾ فِيمَا أَخْبَرُوا بِهِ مِنْ إِيمَانِهِمْ بِمَا يَلْتَزِمُونَهُ مِنَ الْأَعْمَالِ الشَّاقَّةِ ﴿وَالقَانِتِينَ﴾ وَالْقُنُوتُ: الطَّاعَةُ وَالْخُضُوعُ ﴿والْمُنفِقِينَ﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِمْ فِي جَمِيعِ مَا أُمِرُوا بِهِ مِنَ الطَّاعَاتِ، وصلة الأرحام والقرابات، وَسَدِّ الخَلات، وَمُوَاسَاةِ ذَوِي الْحَاجَاتِ ﴿وَالْمُسْتَغْفِرِينَ بِالأسْحَارِ﴾ دَلَّ عَلَى فَضِيلَةِ الِاسْتِغْفَارِ وَقْتَ الْأَسْحَارِ.

وَقَدْ قِيلَ: إِنَّ يَعْقُوبَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، لَمَّا قَالَ لِبَنِيهِ: ﴿سَوْفَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكُمْ رَبِّي﴾ [يُوسُفَ:٩٨] أَنَّهُ أَخَّرَهُمْ إِلَى وَقْتِ السَّحَرِ. وَثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ وَغَيْرِهِمَا مِنَ الْمَسَانِدِ وَالسُّنَنِ، مِنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ، عَنْ جَمَاعَةٍ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "ينزلُ اللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى فِي كُلِّ لَيْلَةٍ إلَى سمَِاءِ الدُّنيا حِينَ يَبْقَى ثُلُثُ اللَّيْلِ الآخِر فيقولُ: هَلْ مِنْ سَائل فأعْطِيَه؟ هَلْ مِنْ دَاع فَأسْتجيبَ لَهُ؟ هَلْ مِنْ مُسْتَغْفِر فأغْفِرَ لَهُ؟ " الْحَدِيثَ وَقَدْ أَفْرَدَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ الدَّارَقُطْنِيُّ فِي ذَلِكَ جُزْءًا عَلَى حِدَةٍ فَرَوَاهُ مِنْ طُرُقٍ مُتَعَدِّدَةٍ.

وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا، قَالَتْ: مِنْ كُلِّ اللَّيلِ قَدْ أوْترَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، مِنْ أولِهِ وأوْسَطِهِ وآخِرِهِ، فَانْتَهَى وِتره إلَى السّحَرِ .

وَكَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ يُصَلِّي مِنَ اللَّيْلِ، ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: يَا نَافِعُ، هَلْ جَاءَ السَّحَر؟ فَإِذَا قَالَ: نَعَمْ، أَقْبَلَ عَلَى الدُّعَاءِ وَالِاسْتِغْفَارِ حَتَّى يُصْبِحَ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، عَنْ حُرَيْث بْنِ أَبِي مَطَرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ حَاطِبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَجُلًا فِي السَّحَرِ فِي نَاحِيَةِ الْمَسْجِدِ وهو يقول: ربّ أمرتني فأطعتك، وَهَذَا سَحَرٌ، فَاغْفِرْ لِي. فَنَظَرْتُ فَإِذَا ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ .

وَرَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويه عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ: كُنَّا نُؤْمَرُ إِذَا صَلَّيْنَا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ أنْ نَسْتَغْفِرَ فِي آخِرِ السَّحَرِ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً.

---

## 3:18-20 — verses 18-20 (shared commentary)

### Translation

God, exalted is He, testifies, and He suffices as a witness, for He is the most truthful of witnesses and the most just of them, and the most truthful of speakers, {that there is no god but He}, meaning: He alone possesses divinity over all created beings, and that all of them are His servants and His creation, and are in need of Him, while He is free of need of anything besides Himself, as He, exalted is He, said: {But Allah bears witness to what He has sent down to you; He sent it down with His knowledge, and the angels bear witness as well, and Allah suffices as a witness} [al-Nisa: 166].

Then He joined the testimony of His angels and those possessed of knowledge to His own testimony, saying: {Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and the angels, and those possessed of knowledge}. This is a great distinction granted to the scholars in this station.

{Maintaining justice} is in the accusative case as a circumstantial qualifier (hal), and He is thus in every circumstance.

{There is no god but He} is a confirmation of what preceded. {The Mighty, the Wise}: al-Aziz is He whose court cannot be approached with any design against it, owing to His greatness and majesty; al-Hakim is He who is wise in His sayings, His deeds, His law, and His decree.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Yazid ibn Abd Rabbih, from Baqiyyah ibn al-Walid, from Jubayr ibn Amr al-Qurashi, from Abu Sa'id al-Ansari, from Abu Yahya the freedman of the family of al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, from al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, while he was at Arafah, reciting this verse: {Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and the angels, and those possessed of knowledge, maintaining justice; there is no god but He, the Mighty, the Wise}, and he said, "And I am among those who bear witness to that, O my Lord."

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it by another route, saying: [chain] Ali ibn Husayn, from Muhammad ibn al-Mutawakkil al-Asqalani, from Umar ibn Hafs ibn Thabit Abu Sa'id al-Ansari, from Abd al-Malik ibn Yahya ibn Abbad ibn Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr, from his father, from his grandfather, from al-Zubayr, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he recited this verse: {Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and the angels}, say: "And I bear witness, O my Lord."

Al-Hafiz Abu al-Qasim al-Tabarani said in al-Mu'jam al-Kabir: [chain] Abdan ibn Ahmad and Ali ibn Sa'id al-Razi, both from Ammar ibn Umar ibn al-Mukhtar, from his father, from Ghalib al-Qattan, who said: I came to Kufah on business and lodged near al-A'mash. One night when I intended to travel on, he rose and performed the night prayer (tahajjud), and in it he passed by this verse: {Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and the angels, and those possessed of knowledge, maintaining justice; there is no god but He, the Mighty, the Wise. Indeed, the religion in the sight of Allah is Islam}. Then al-A'mash said: "And I bear witness to what Allah has borne witness to, and I entrust to Allah this testimony, and it is with Allah for me a trust: {Indeed, the religion in the sight of Allah is Islam}," repeating it several times. I said to myself: he has heard something concerning it. So I went to him in the morning to bid him farewell, then said: "O Abu Muhammad, I heard you repeating this verse." He said: "Have you not heard what is in it?" I said: "I have been with you for a month and you have not told me." He said: "By Allah, I will not tell you of it until a year has passed." So I stayed a year, remaining at his door. When the year had passed, I said: "O Abu Muhammad, the year has passed." He said: Abu Wa'il told me, from Abdullah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The one who holds to it will be brought on the Day of Resurrection, and Allah, mighty and exalted is He, will say: 'My servant made a covenant with Me, and I am most deserving of fulfilling the covenant; admit My servant into Paradise.'"

His saying, {Indeed, the religion in the sight of Allah is Islam}, is a report from Allah, exalted is He, that there is no religion with Him which He accepts from anyone save Islam, which is following the messengers in whatever Allah sent them with, at every time, until they were sealed with Muhammad ﷺ, who closed all paths to Him except through the way of Muhammad ﷺ. So whoever meets Allah after the mission of Muhammad ﷺ with a religion contrary to his law, it will not be accepted, as He, exalted is He, said: {And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it will never be accepted from him, and he will be, in the Hereafter, among the losers} [Al Imran: 85]. And He said in this verse, informing that the religion accepted with Him is confined to Islam: {Indeed, the religion in the sight of Allah is Islam}.

Ibn Jarir mentioned that Ibn Abbas read: {Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and the angels, and those possessed of knowledge, maintaining justice; there is no god but He, the Mighty, the Wise. Indeed, the religion in the sight of Allah is Islam}, with a kasrah on "innahu" and a fathah on "inna al-din", that is: reading it so that it means, He, His angels, and those possessed of knowledge among mankind bore witness that the religion in the sight of Allah is Islam. The majority read it with kasrah, as a plain statement. Both meanings are sound, but this, according to the majority's reading, is clearer. And Allah knows best.

Then He, exalted is He, informed that those who were given the first Scripture differed only after the proof had been established against them, through the sending of messengers to them and the revelation of books to them, saying: {And those who were given the Scripture did not differ except after knowledge had come to them, out of rivalry among themselves}, meaning: some of them transgressed against others, and so they differed concerning the truth because of their mutual envy, hatred, and estrangement, so that the hatred of some for others led them to oppose them in all their sayings and deeds, even if these were true. Then He said: {And whoever disbelieves in the signs of Allah, then indeed Allah is swift in reckoning}, meaning: whoever denies what Allah has sent down in His Book, Allah will requite him for that, hold him to account for his denial, and punish him for his opposition to His Book.

Then He, exalted is He, said: {So if they argue with you}, meaning: if they dispute with you concerning the oneness of Allah, {then say, "I have submitted my face to Allah, and so have those who follow me"}, meaning: say, I have devoted my worship purely to Allah alone, with no partner, no equal, no son, and no consort ascribed to Him, {and those who follow me}, upon my religion, saying as I say, as He, exalted is He, said: {Say, "This is my way; I invite to Allah upon sure knowledge, I and those who follow me, and glory be to Allah, and I am not among the polytheists"} [Yusuf: 108].

Then He, exalted is He, commanded His servant and messenger Muhammad ﷺ to call to his way and his religion, and to entry into his law, and to that with which Allah sent him to the People of the Scripture from the two communities, and to the illiterate ones among the polytheists, saying: {And say to those who were given the Scripture and to the illiterate ones, "Have you submitted?" If they submit, they are rightly guided, but if they turn away, then upon you is only the conveyance}, meaning: their reckoning is upon Allah, and to Him is their return and their final destination. He is the One who guides whom He wills and leads astray whom He wills, and He has wisdom in that and a decisive proof. Therefore He said: {And Allah is All-Seeing of His servants}, meaning: He knows who deserves guidance from among those who deserve error, and He is the One {who is not questioned about what He does, while they will be questioned} [al-Anbiya: 23], and that is only due to His wisdom and His mercy.

This verse and others like it are among the clearest indications of the universality of his mission, prayers and peace of Allah be upon him, to all of creation, as is known necessarily from his religion, and as the Book and the Sunnah indicate in more than one verse and hadith. Among these is His saying, exalted is He: {Say, "O mankind, indeed I am the Messenger of Allah to you all"} [al-A'raf: 158], and His saying, exalted is He: {Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His servant, that he might be a warner to all the worlds} [al-Furqan: 1]. And it is established in the two Sahihs and elsewhere, through what has been proven by numerous corroborating incidents, that he ﷺ sent his letters calling to Allah the kings of the horizons and the various peoples of the sons of Adam, their Arabs and their non-Arabs, their People of the Scripture and their illiterate ones, in compliance with Allah's command to him to do so.

Abd al-Razzaq narrated, from Ma'mar, from Hammam, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "By Him in whose hand is my soul, no one of this nation, be he Jew or Christian, hears of me and dies without having believed in what I was sent with, except that he will be among the people of the Fire." Narrated by Muslim.

And he ﷺ said: "I was sent to the red and the black." And he said: "A prophet used to be sent to his people specifically, but I have been sent to mankind generally." Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Mu'ammal, from Hammad, from Thabit, from Anas, may Allah be pleased with him: that a Jewish boy used to prepare the water for the ablution of the Prophet ﷺ and hand him his sandals. He fell ill, and the Prophet ﷺ came to visit him, entering upon him while his father sat at his head. The Prophet ﷺ said to him: "O so-and-so, say: there is no god but Allah." The boy looked at his father, and his father remained silent. The Prophet ﷺ repeated it to him, and he looked at his father again, and his father said: "Obey Abu al-Qasim." So the boy said: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah and that you are the Messenger of Allah." The Prophet ﷺ went out saying: "Praise be to Allah who saved him from the Fire through me." Recorded by al-Bukhari in the Sahih, along with other verses and hadiths besides these.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

شَهِدَ تَعَالَى -وَكَفَى بِهِ شَهِيدًا، وَهُوَ أَصْدَقُ الشَّاهِدِينَ وَأَعْدَلُهُمْ، وَأَصْدَقُ الْقَائِلِينَ- ﴿أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ﴾ أَيْ: المتفَرد بِالْإِلَهِيَّةِ لِجَمِيعِ الْخَلَائِقِ، وَأَنَّ الْجَمِيعَ عَبِيدُهُ وَخَلْقُهُ، وَالْفُقَرَاءُ إِلَيْهِ، وَهُوَ الْغَنِيُّ عَمَّا سِوَاهُ كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَكِنِ اللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ بِمَا أَنزلَ إِلَيْكَ أَنزلَهُ بِعِلْمِهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ يَشْهَدُونَ وَكَفَى بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا﴾ الْآيَةَ [النِّسَاءِ: ١٦٦] .

ثُمَّ قَرَنَ شَهَادَةَ مَلَائِكَتِهِ وَأُولِي الْعِلْمِ بِشَهَادَتِهِ فَقَالَ: ﴿شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ﴾ وَهَذِهِ خُصُوصِيَّةٌ عَظِيمَةٌ لِلْعُلَمَاءِ فِي هَذَا الْمَقَامِ.

﴿قَائِمًا بِالْقِسْطِ﴾ مَنْصُوبٌ عَلَى الْحَالِ، وَهُوَ فِي جَمِيعِ الْأَحْوَالِ كَذَلِكَ.

﴿لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ﴾ تَأْكِيدٌ لِمَا سَبَقَ ﴿الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ﴾ الْعَزِيزُ: الَّذِي لَا يُرَامُ جَنَابُهُ عَظَمَةً وَكِبْرِيَاءً، الْحَكِيمُ فِي أَقْوَالِهِ وَأَفْعَالِهِ وَشَرْعِهِ وَقَدَرِهِ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ، حَدَّثَنَا بَقِيَّة بْنُ الْوَلِيدِ، حَدَّثَنِي جُبَيْرُ بْنُ عَمْرو الْقُرَشِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيد الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي يَحْيَى مَوْلَى آلِ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ الْعَوَّامِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَهُوَ بعرفةَ يَقْرَأُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ قَائِمًا بِالْقِسْطِ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ﴾ "وأَنَا عَلَى ذلِكَ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ يَا رَبِّ" .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ مِنْ وَجْهٍ آخَرَ، فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُسَيْنٍ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُتَوَكِّلِ الْعَسْقَلَانِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عُمَر بْنُ حَفْصِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ عَبَّادِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ حِينَ قَرَأَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ﴾ قال: "وأَنَا أشْهَدُ أيْ رَبِّ" .

وَقَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ الطَّبَرَانِيُّ فِي الْمُعْجَمِ الْكَبِيرِ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَانُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ وَعَلِيُّ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الرَّازِيُّ قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا عَمَّار بْنُ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْمُخْتَارِ، حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، حَدَّثَنِي غَالِبٌ الْقَطَّانُ قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ الْكُوفَةَ فِي تِجَارَةٍ، فَنَزَلْتُ قَرِيبًا مِنَ الْأَعْمَشِ، فَلَمَّا كَانَتْ لَيْلَةٌ أردتُ أَنْ أنْحَدِرَ قَامَ فَتَهَجَّدَ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ، فَمَرَّ بِهَذِهِ الآية: ﴿شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ قَائِمًا بِالْقِسْطِ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ. إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الإسْلامُ﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ الْأَعْمَشُ: وَأَنَا أَشْهَدُ بِمَا شَهِدَ اللَّهُ بِهِ، وَأَسْتَوْدِعُ اللَّهَ هَذِهِ الشَّهَادَةَ، وَهِيَ لِي عِنْدَ اللَّهِ وَدِيعَةٌ: ﴿إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الإسْلامُ﴾ قَالَهَا مِرَارًا. قُلْتُ: لَقَدْ سَمِعَ فِيهَا شَيْئًا، فَغَدَوْتُ إِلَيْهِ فَوَدَّعْتُهُ، ثُمَّ قُلْتُ: يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ، إِنِّي سمعتك تردد هذه الآية. قال: أو ما بَلَغَكَ مَا فِيهَا؟ قُلْتُ: أَنَا عِنْدَكَ مُنْذُ شَهْرٍ لَمْ تُحَدِّثْنِي. قَالَ: وَاللَّهِ لَا أُحَدِّثُكَ بِهَا إِلَى سَنَةٍ. فَأَقَمْتُ سَنَةً فَكُنْتُ عَلَى بَابِهِ، فَلَمَّا مَضَتِ السَّنَةُ قُلْتُ: يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ، قَدْ مَضَتِ السَّنَةُ. قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو وَائِلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يُجَاءُ بِصَاحِبِهَا يَوْمَ القِيامَةِ، فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: عَبْدِي عَهِدَ إلَيَّ، وأنَا أحَقُّ مَن وَفَّى بالْعَهْدِ، أدْخِلُوا عَبْدِي الْجَنَّةَ" .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الإسْلامُ﴾ إِخْبَارٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى بِأَنَّهُ لَا دِينَ عِنْدَهُ يَقْبَلُهُ مِنْ أَحَدٍ سِوَى الْإِسْلَامِ، وَهُوَ اتِّبَاعُ الرُّسُلِ فِيمَا بَعَثَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِهِ فِي كُلِّ حِينٍ، حَتَّى خُتِمُوا بِمُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، الَّذِي سَدَّ جَمِيعَ الطُّرُقِ إِلَيْهِ إِلَّا مِنْ جِهَةِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، فَمَنْ لَقِيَ اللَّهَ بَعْدَ بِعْثَتِهِ مُحَمَّدًا ﷺ بدِين عَلَى غَيْرِ شَرِيعَتِهِ، فَلَيْسَ بِمُتَقَبَّلٍ. كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الإسْلامِ دِينًا فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ [وَهُوَ فِي الآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ] ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ:٨٥] وَقَالَ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ مُخْبِرًا بِانْحِصَارِ الدِّينِ الْمُتَقَبَّلِ عِنْدَهُ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ: ﴿إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الإسْلامُ﴾

وَذَكَرَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَنَّ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ قَرَأَ: ﴿شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ قَائِمًا بِالْقِسْطِ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ. إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الإسْلامُ﴾ بِكَسْرِ إِنَّهُ وَفَتْحِ ﴿إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الإسْلامُ﴾ أَيْ: شَهِدَ هُوَ وَمَلَائِكَتُهُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ مِنَ الْبَشَرِ بِأَنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الْإِسْلَامُ. وَالْجُمْهُورُ قرأوها بِالْكَسْرِ عَلَى الْخَبَرِ، وَكِلَا الْمَعْنَيَيْنِ صَحِيحٌ. وَلَكِنَّ هَذَا عَلَى قَوْلِ الْجُمْهُورِ أَظْهَرُ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

ثُمَّ أَخْبَرَ تَعَالَى بِأَنَّ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ الْأَوَّلَ إِنَّمَا اخْتَلَفُوا بَعْدَ مَا قَامَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْحُجَّةُ، بِإِرْسَالِ الرُّسُلِ إِلَيْهِمْ، وَإِنْزَالِ الْكُتُبِ عَلَيْهِمْ، فَقَالَ: ﴿وَمَا اخْتَلَفَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ إِلا مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ بَغْيًا بَيْنَهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: بَغَى بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ، فَاخْتَلَفُوا فِي الْحَقِّ لِتَحَاسُدِهِمْ وَتَبَاغُضُهِمْ وَتَدَابُرِهِمْ، فَحَمَلَ بَعْضَهُمْ بُغْض البَعْض الْآخَرِ عَلَى مُخَالَفَتِهِ فِي جَمِيعِ أَقْوَالِهِ وَأَفْعَالِهِ، وَإِنْ كَانَتْ حَقًّا، ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ﴾ أَيْ: مَنْ جَحَدَ بِمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فِي كتابه فإن الله سَيُجَازِيهِ عَلَى ذَلِكَ، وَيُحَاسِبُهُ عَلَى تَكْذِيبِهِ، وَيُعَاقِبُهُ عَلَى مُخَالَفَتِهِ كِتَابَهُ 

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَإِنْ حَاجُّوكَ﴾ أَيْ: جَادَلُوكَ فِي التَّوْحِيدِ ﴿فَقُلْ أَسْلَمْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّهِ وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِ﴾ أَيْ: فَقُلْ أَخْلَصْتُ عِبَادَتِي لِلَّهِ وَحْدَهُ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَلَا نِدَّ [لَهُ] وَلَا وَلَدَ وَلَا صَاحِبَةَ لَهُ ﴿وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِ﴾ عَلَى دِينِي، يَقُولُ كَمَقَالَتِي، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ هَذِهِ سَبِيلِي أَدْعُو إِلَى اللَّهِ عَلَى بَصِيرَةٍ أَنَا وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِي [وَسُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَمَا أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ] ﴾ [يُوسُفَ: ١٠٨] .

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى آمِرًا لِعَبْدِهِ وَرَسُولِهِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ إِلَى طَرِيقَتِهِ وَدِينِهِ، وَالدُّخُولِ فِي شَرْعِهِ وَمَا بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ بِهِ الْكِتَابِيِّينَ مِنَ الْمِلَّتَيْنِ وَالْأُمِّيِّينَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ فَقَالَ: ﴿وَقُلْ لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ وَالأمِّيِّينَ أَأَسْلَمْتُمْ فَإِنْ أَسْلَمُوا فَقَدِ اهْتَدَوْا وَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ الْبَلاغُ﴾ أَيْ: وَاللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ حِسَابُهُمْ وَإِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُهُمْ وَمَآبُهُمْ، وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشَاءُ، وَيُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشَاءُ، وَلَهُ الْحِكْمَةُ فِي ذَلِكَ، وَالْحُجَّةُ الْبَالِغَةُ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ عَلِيمٌ بِمَنْ يَسْتَحِقُّ الْهِدَايَةَ مِمَّنْ يَسْتَحِقُّ الضَّلَالَةَ، وَهُوَ الَّذِي ﴿لَا يُسْأَلُ عَمَّا يَفْعَلُ وَهُمْ يُسْأَلُونَ﴾ [الْأَنْبِيَاءِ: ٣٣] وَمَا ذَاكَ إِلَّا لِحِكْمَتِهِ وَرَحْمَتِهِ.

وَهَذِهِ الْآيَةُ وَأَمْثَالُهَا مَنْ أَصْرَحِ الدَّلَالَاتِ عَلَى عُمُومِ بِعْثَتِهِ، صَلوات اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ، إِلَى جَمِيعِ الْخَلْقِ، كَمَا هُوَ مَعْلُومٌ مِنْ دِينِهِ ضَرُورَةً، وَكَمَا دَلَّ عَلَيْهِ الْكِتَابُ وَالسُّنَّةُ فِي غَيْرِ مَا آيَةٍ وَحَدِيثٍ، فَمِنْ ذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِلَيْكُمْ جَمِيعًا﴾ [الْأَعْرَافِ: ١٥٨] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿تَبَارَكَ الَّذِي نزلَ الْفُرْقَانَ عَلَى عَبْدِهِ لِيَكُونَ لِلْعَالَمِينَ نَذِيرًا﴾ [الْفُرْقَانِ: ١] وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ وَغَيْرِهِمَا، مِمَّا ثَبَتَ تَوَاتُرُهُ بِالْوَقَائِعِ الْمُتَعَدِّدَةِ، أَنَّهُ بَعَثَ كُتُبَهُ ﷺ يَدْعُو إِلَى اللَّهِ مُلُوكَ الْآفَاقِ، وَطَوَائِفَ بَنِي آدَمَ مِنْ عَرَبِهِمْ وَعَجَمِهِمْ، كتابِيِّهم وأمِّيِّهم، امْتِثَالًا لِأَمْرِ اللَّهِ لَهُ بِذَلِكَ. وَقَدْ رَوَى عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَر، عَنْ هَمَّام، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "والَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ، لَا يَسْمَعُ بِي أحَدٌ مِنْ هَذِهِ الأمَّةِ يَهُوديّ وَلا نَصْرَانِي، ومَاتَ وَلمَ يُؤْمِنْ بِالَّذِي أرْسلتُ بِهِ، إِلَّا كَانَ مِنْ أَهْلِ النَّارِ" رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ .

وَقَالَ ﷺ: "بُعِثْتُ إلَى الأحْمَرِ والأسْودِ" وَقَالَ: "كَانَ النَّبيُّ يُبْعَثُ إلَى قَوْمِه خَاصَّةً وَبُعِثْتُ إلَى النَّاسِ عَامَّةً". وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُؤَمِّل، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّاد، حَدَّثَنَا ثَابِتٌ عَنْ أَنَسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ: أَنَّ غُلَامًا يَهُودِيًّا كَانَ يَضع لِلنَّبِيِّ ﷺ وَضُوءه وَيُنَاوِلُهُ نَعْلَيْهِ، فَمَرِضَ، فَأَتَاهُ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ وَأَبُوهُ قَاعِدٌ عِنْدَ رَأْسِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "يَا فُلانُ، قُلْ: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ" فَنَظَرَ إلَى أَبِيهِ، فَسَكَتَ أَبُوهُ، فأعَادَ عَلَيْهِ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ، فَنَظَرَ إلَى أَبيهِ، فَقَالَ أبُوهُ: أطِعْ أَبَا الْقَاسِم، فَقَالَ الْغُلامُ:: أشْهَدُ أن لَا إلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وأَنَّكَ رَسُولُ اللهِ، فَخَرَجَ النَّبَيُّ ﷺ وَهُوَ يَقُولُ: "الْحَمْدُ للهِ الَّذِي أخْرَجَهُ بِي مِنِ النَّارِ" أَخْرَجَهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي الصَّحِيحِ إِلَى غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْآيَاتِ وَالْأَحَادِيثِ.

---

## 3:21-22 — verses 21-22 (shared commentary)

### Translation

This is a rebuke from Allah, may He be exalted, directed at the People of the Book for the sins and forbidden acts they committed in denying the signs of Allah, both in ancient times and in more recent times, the signs that the messengers had conveyed to them, out of arrogance toward them and stubborn opposition to them, out of pride against the truth and disdain for following it. And along with this, they killed those they killed among the prophets when the prophets conveyed to them Allah's law, without any cause or crime committed by the prophets against them, except that they called them to the truth. {And they kill those among the people who command justice}. This is the utmost extreme of arrogance, as the Prophet ﷺ said: "Arrogance is disdaining the truth and belittling people."

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu al-Zubayr al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Muslim al-Naysaburi, resident of Makkah, told us, Abu Hafs Umar ibn Hafs, that is, the son of Thabit ibn Zurarah al-Ansari, told me, Muhammad ibn Hamzah told us, Abu al-Hasan, a freedman of Banu Asad, told me, from Makhul, from Qabisah ibn Dhu'ayb al-Khuza'i, from Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: I said, "O Messenger of Allah, which people will have the severest punishment on the Day of Resurrection?" He said: "A man who killed a prophet, or one who was killed for enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong." Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited: {Indeed, those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah and kill the prophets without right, and kill those among the people who command justice, give them tidings of a painful punishment} up to His saying: {and they will have no helpers}, the verse. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Abu Ubaydah, the Children of Israel killed forty-three prophets at the beginning of a single day, within one hour. Then one hundred and seventy men from the Children of Israel rose up and enjoined upon those who had killed them what was right and forbade them from what was wrong, so all of them were killed by the end of that same day. They are the ones whom Allah, Mighty and Majestic, mentioned."

Ibn Jarir likewise related this from Abu Ubayd al-Wassabi Muhammad ibn Hafs, from Ibn Humayr, from Abu al-Hasan, the freedman of Banu Asad, from Makhul, with the same content.

And from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Children of Israel killed three hundred prophets at the beginning of a day, and by its end they had set up their vegetable market. Ibn Abi Hatim related this.

For this reason, when they grew too proud to accept the truth and too arrogant toward creation, Allah answered them for that with humiliation and lowliness in this world, and with a degrading punishment in the Hereafter. So He said: {give them tidings of a painful punishment}, meaning agonizing and humiliating. {Those are the ones whose deeds have come to nothing in this world and the Hereafter, and they will have no helpers.}

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

هَذَا ذَمٌّ مِنَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى لِأَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ فِيمَا ارْتَكَبُوهُ مِنَ الْمَآثِمِ وَالْمَحَارِمِ فِي تَكْذِيبِهِمْ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ قَدِيمًا وَحَدِيثًا، الَّتِي بَلَّغَتْهُمْ إِيَّاهَا الرُّسُلُ، اسْتِكْبَارًا عَلَيْهِمْ وَعِنَادًا لَهُمْ، وَتَعَاظُمًا عَلَى الْحَقِّ وَاسْتِنْكَافًا عَنِ اتِّبَاعِهِ، وَمَعَ هَذَا قَتَلُوا مَنْ قَتَلُوا مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ حِينَ بَلَّغُوهُمْ عَنِ اللَّهِ شَرْعَهُ، بِغَيْرِ سَبَبٍ وَلَا جَرِيمَةٍ مِنْهُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ، إِلَّا لِكَوْنِهِمْ دَعَوْهُمْ إِلَى الْحَقِّ ﴿وَيَقْتُلُونَ الَّذِينَ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْقِسْطِ مِنَ النَّاسِ﴾ وَهَذَا هُوَ غَايَةُ الْكِبْرِ، كَمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "الْكِبْرُ بَطَرُ الحَقِّ وَغَمْط النَّاسِ".

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الزُّبَيْر الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ النَّيْسَابُورِيُّ، نَزِيلُ مَكَّةَ، حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو حَفْصٍ عُمَرُ بْنُ حَفْصٍ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ ثَابِتِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ-حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَمْزَةَ، حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو الْحَسَنِ مَوْلًى لِبَنِي أَسَدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، عَنْ قَبِيصَةَ بْنِ ذُؤَيْبٍ الْخُزَاعِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ بْنِ الْجَرَّاحِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ الله، أي النَّاسِ أَشَدُّ عَذَابًا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ؟ قَالَ: "رَجلٌ قَتَلَ نَبِيا أوْ مَنْ أَمَرَ بِالمْعْرُوفِ ونَهَى عَنِ المُنْكَر". ثُمَّ قَرَأَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ النَّبِيِّينَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَيَقْتُلُونَ الَّذِينَ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْقِسْطِ مِنَ النَّاسِ فَبَشِّرْهُمْ بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ﴾ [إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنْ نَاصِرِينَ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ. ثُمَّ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يَا أبَا عُبَيَدَةَ، قَتَلَتْ بَنُو إسْرَائِيلَ ثَلاثَةً وأَرْبَعين نَبيا، مِنْ أوَّلِ النّهَارِ فِي ساعةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ، فَقَامَ مِائَة وسَبْعُونَ رَجُلا مِنْ بَني إسْرائيلَ، فأمَرُوا مَنْ قَتَلَهُم بالْمَعْرُوفِ ونَهَوْهُمْ عَنِ المنكرِ، فَقُتِلُوا جَمِيعًا مِنْ آخِرِ النَّهارِ مِنْ ذَلكَ اليَوْمِ، فَهُم الذِينَ ذَكَرَ اللهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ الْوَصَّابِيِّ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَفْصٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ حُمَيْر، عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ مَوْلَى بَنِي أسد، عن مكحول، به .

وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَتَلَتْ بَنُو إِسْرَائِيلَ ثَلَاثَمِائَةِ نَبِيٍّ مِنْ أَوَّلِ النَّهَارِ، وَأَقَامُوا سُوقَ بَقْلِهِمْ مِنْ آخِرِهِ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ. وَلِهَذَا لَمَّا أَنْ تَكَبَّرُوا عَنِ الْحَقِّ وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا عَلَى الْخَلْقِ، قَابَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ بِالذِّلَّةِ وَالصَّغَارِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْعَذَابِ الْمُهِينِ فِي الْآخِرَةِ، فَقَالَ: ﴿فَبَشِّرْهُمْ بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ﴾ أَيْ: مُوجِعٍ مُهِينٍ. ﴿أُولَئِكَ الَّذِينَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالآخِرَةِ وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنْ نَاصِرِينَ﴾

---

## 3:23-25 — verses 23-25 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Most High says, denouncing the Jews and Christians who cling, as they claim, to the two books in their possession, namely the Torah and the Gospel, that when they are called to submit to judgment by what is in them, namely obedience to God in what He commanded them therein, which includes following Muhammad ﷺ, they turn away, averting themselves from the two of them. This is the utmost degree of censure of them, and a highlighting of their mention through opposition and stubborn defiance.

Then He said: {That is because they said, "The Fire will not touch us except for a few numbered days."} That is: what drove them and emboldened them to oppose the truth was their fabrication against God in what they claimed for themselves, namely that they would only be punished in the Fire for seven days, one day for every thousand years of worldly life. The explanation of this has already been given in Surat al-Baqarah. Then He said: {And what they used to fabricate deceived them in their religion} that is, it deceived them in their religion, meaning: what they deceived themselves with, of their claim that the Fire would not touch them for their sins except for a few numbered days, kept them firm upon their false religion. They are the ones who fabricated this from themselves and invented it, and God sent down no authority for it. God, the Most High, says, threatening them and warning them: {How will it be when We gather them for a day about which there is no doubt} that is: what will their state be, when they had fabricated lies against God, denied His messengers, killed His prophets and the scholars among their people who commanded good and forbade evil? God, the Most High, will question them about all of that, hold them to account for it, and repay them for it. That is why He said: {How will it be when We gather them for a day about which there is no doubt}, there being no doubt in its occurrence and its coming to pass, {and every soul will be paid in full for what it earned, and they will not be wronged}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُنْكِرًا عَلَى الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى، الْمُتَمَسِّكِينَ فِيمَا يَزْعُمُونَ بِكِتَابَيْهِمُ اللَّذين بِأَيْدِيهِمْ، وَهُمَا التَّوْرَاةُ وَالْإِنْجِيلُ، وَإِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى التَّحَاكُمِ إِلَى مَا فِيهِمَا مِنْ طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ فِيمَا أَمَرَهُمْ بِهِ فِيهِمَا، مِنَ اتِّبَاعِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، تولَّوا وَهُمْ مُعْرِضُونَ عَنْهُمَا، وَهَذَا فِي غَايَةِ مَا يَكُونُ مِنْ ذَمِّهِمْ، وَالتَّنْوِيهِ بِذِكْرِهِمْ بِالْمُخَالَفَةِ وَالْعِنَادِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا لَنْ تَمَسَّنَا النَّارُ إِلا أَيَّامًا مَعْدُودَاتٍ﴾ أَيْ: إِنَّمَا حَمَلَهُمْ وجَرّأهم عَلَى مُخَالَفَةِ الْحَقِّ افْتِرَاؤُهُمْ عَلَى اللَّهِ فِيمَا ادَّعَوْهُ لِأَنْفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ إِنَّمَا يُعَذَّبُونَ فِي النَّارِ سَبْعَةَ أَيَّامٍ، عَنْ كُلِّ أَلْفِ سَنَةٍ فِي الدُّنْيَا يَوْمًا. وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ تَفْسِيرُ ذَلِكَ فِي سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَغَرَّهُمْ فِي دِينِهِمْ مَا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ﴾ [أَيْ غَرَّهُمْ فِي دِينِهِمْ] أَيْ: ثَبَّتهم عَلَى دِينِهِمُ الْبَاطِلِ مَا خَدَعُوا بِهِ أَنْفُسَهُمْ مِنْ زَعْمِهِمْ أَنَّ النَّارَ لَا تَمَسُّهُمْ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ إِلَّا أَيَّامًا مَعْدُودَاتٍ، وَهُمُ الَّذِينَ افْتَرَوْا هَذَا مِنْ تِلْقَاءِ أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَافْتَعَلُوهُ، وَلَمْ يُنَزِّلِ اللَّهُ بِهِ سُلْطَانًا قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى مُتَهَدِّدًا لَهُمْ وَمُتَوَعِّدًا: ﴿فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جَمَعْنَاهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ﴾ أَيْ: كَيْفَ يَكُونُ حَالُهُمْ وَقَدِ افْتَرَوْا عَلَى اللَّهِ وَكَذَّبُوا رُسُلَهُ وَقَتَلُوا أَنْبِيَاءَهُ وَالْعُلَمَاءَ مِنْ قَوْمِهِمْ، الْآمِرِينَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّاهِينَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، وَاللَّهُ تَعَالَى سَائِلُهُمْ عَنْ ذَلِكَ كُلِّهِ، وَمُحَاسِبُهُمْ عَلَيْهِ، وَمُجَازِيهِمْ بِهِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جَمَعْنَاهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ﴾ لَا شَكَّ فِي وُقُوعِهِ وَكَوْنِهِ ﴿وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ﴾

---

## 3:26-27 — verses 26-27 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted says: {Say}, O Muhammad, glorifying your Lord, relying upon Him, thanking Him, and entrusting your affair to Him: {O Allah, Master of Dominion} that is, all dominion belongs to You, {You give dominion to whom You will and You take dominion away from whom You will, and You honor whom You will and You humiliate whom You will} that is, You are the Giver and You are the Withholder, and You are the One for whom whatever You will comes to be, and whatever You do not will does not come to be.

In this verse there is a reminder and a guidance toward gratitude for the favor of Allah the Exalted upon His Messenger ﷺ and upon this nation, because Allah transferred prophethood from the Children of Israel to the Arab prophet, the Qurashi, the Meccan, the unlettered one, the seal of all prophets absolutely, and the Messenger of Allah to both weighty kinds, mankind and jinn together. Allah gathered in him the excellent qualities of those who came before him, and singled him out with distinctions He gave to no prophet among the prophets nor to any messenger among the messengers, in knowledge of Allah and His law, in his being informed of unseen matters both past and future, in his unveiling of the realities of the Hereafter, in the spread of his nation across the horizons, in the easts and wests of the earth, and in the manifestation of his religion and his law over all other religions and laws. So may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him continuously until the Day of Judgment, as long as night and day succeed one another. For this reason the Exalted said: {Say: O Allah, Master of Dominion, [You give dominion to whom You will and You take dominion away from whom You will, and You honor whom You will and You humiliate whom You will; in Your hand is all good; indeed You have power over all things]}, that is, You are the One who disposes freely over Your creation, doing whatever You wish, just as the Blessed and Exalted responded to those who sought to impose their own judgment upon Him regarding His affair, where He said: {And they said, "Why was this Qur'an not sent down upon a great man from the two towns?"} [al-Zukhruf: 31].

Allah the Exalted said in response to them: {Do they distribute the mercy of your Lord? [It is We who have distributed among them their livelihood in the life of this world, and We have raised some of them above others in degrees]} the verse [al-Zukhruf: 32], meaning: We dispose freely over Our creation as We wish, with no one to prevent Us or oppose Us, and We possess the wisdom and the proof in that. Thus We grant prophethood to whom We wish, as the Exalted said: {Allah knows best where to place His message} [al-An'am: 124], and He the Exalted said: {See how We have favored some of them over others, and the Hereafter is greater in degrees and greater in favor} [al-Isra: 21].

Al-Hafiz Ibn 'Asakir related, in the biographical entry on "Ishaq ibn Ahmad" in his history, from the caliph al-Ma'mun, that he saw in a palace in the lands of Rome something written in Himyaritic script. It was translated into Arabic for him, and it read: In the name of Allah, night and day have never alternated, nor have the stars of the sky ever revolved in their orbit, except by the transfer of blessing from a king whose authority has ceased to another king. But the dominion of the Possessor of the Throne is everlasting forever, neither perishing nor shared.

Concerning His saying: {You cause the night to enter into the day and You cause the day to enter into the night}, that is, You take from the length of this one and add it to the shortness of the other so that they become equal, then You take from this one into that one so that they become unequal, then they become equal again. And so it is with the seasons of the year: spring, summer, autumn, and winter.

Concerning His saying: {And You bring forth the living from the dead and You bring forth the dead from the living}, that is, You bring forth the grain from the crop and the crop from the grain, the palm tree from the date stone and the date stone from the palm tree, the believer from the disbeliever and the disbeliever from the believer, the hen from the egg and the egg from the hen, and whatever follows this pattern among all things. {And You provide for whom You will without account}, that is, You give to whomever You wish of wealth an amount that cannot be counted or reckoned, while You restrict provision for others, according to the wisdom, will, decree, and justice that belong to You.

Al-Tabarani said: [chain] from Ibn 'Abbas, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "The Greatest Name of Allah, which when invoked He answers, is in this verse from Al 'Imran: {Say: O Allah, Master of Dominion, [You give dominion to whom You will and You take dominion away from whom You will, and You honor whom You will and You humiliate whom You will; in Your hand is all good; indeed You have power over all things]}."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ﴾ يَا مُحَمَّدُ، مُعَظِّمًا لِرَبِّكَ وَمُتَوَكِّلًا عَلَيْهِ، وَشَاكِرًا لَهُ وَمُفَوِّضًا إِلَيْهِ: ﴿اللَّهُمَّ مَالِكَ الْمُلْكِ﴾ أَيْ: لَكَ الْمُلْكُ كُلُّهُ ﴿تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتَنزعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ﴾ أَيْ: أَنْتَ الْمُعْطِي، وَأَنْتَ الْمَانِعُ، وَأَنْتَ الَّذِي مَا شِئْتَ كَانَ وَمَا لَمْ تَشَأْ لَمْ يَكُنْ.

وَفِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ تَنْبِيهٌ وَإِرْشَادٌ إِلَى شُكْرِ نِعْمَةِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى عَلَى رَسُولِهِ ﷺ وَهَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ؛ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ حَوَّلَ النُّبُوَّةَ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ الْعَرَبِيِّ الْقُرَشِيِّ الْمَكِّيِّ الْأُمِّيِّ خَاتَمِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ عَلَى الْإِطْلَاقِ، وَرَسُولِ اللَّهِ إِلَى جَمِيعِ الثَّقَلَيْنِ الْإِنْسِ وَالْجِنِّ، الَّذِي جَمَعَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ مَحَاسِنَ مَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَهُ، وَخَصَّهُ بِخَصَائِصَ لَمْ يُعْطهَا نَبِيًّا مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَلَا رَسُولًا مِنَ الرُّسُلِ، فِي الْعِلْمِ بِاللَّهِ وَشَرِيعَتِهِ وَإِطْلَاعِهِ عَلَى الْغُيُوبِ الْمَاضِيَةِ وَالْآتِيَةِ، وَكَشْفِهِ عَنْ حَقَائِقِ الْآخِرَةِ وَنَشْرِ أُمَّتِهِ فِي الْآفَاقِ، فِي مَشَارِقِ الْأَرْضِ وَمَغَارِبِهَا، وَإِظْهَارِ دِينِهِ وَشَرْعِهِ عَلَى سَائِرِ الْأَدْيَانِ، وَالشَّرَائِعِ، فَصَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ دَائِمًا إِلَى يَوْمِ الدِّينِ، مَا تَعَاقَبَ اللَّيْلُ وَالنَّهَارُ. وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ مَالِكَ الْمُلْكِ [تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتَنزعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ بِيَدِكَ الْخَيْرُ إِنَّكَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ] ﴾ أَيْ: أَنْتَ الْمُتَصَرِّفُ فِي خَلْقِكَ، الْفَعَّالُ لِمَا تُرِيدُ، كَمَا رَدَّ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى عَلَى مَنْ يَتَحَكَّمُ عَلَيْهِ فِي أَمْرِهِ، حَيْثُ قَالَ: ﴿وَقَالُوا لَوْلا نزلَ هَذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلَى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ﴾ [الزُّخْرُفِ: ٣١] .

قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى رَدًّا عليهم: ﴿أَهُمْ يَقْسِمُونَ رَحْمَتَ رَبِّكَ [نَحْنُ قَسَمْنَا بَيْنَهُمْ مَعِيشَتَهُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَرَفَعْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجَاتٍ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ [الزُّخْرُفِ: ٣٢] أَيْ: نَحْنُ نَتَصَرَّفُ فِي خَلْقِنَا كَمَا نُرِيدُ، بِلَا مُمَانِعٍ وَلَا مُدَافِعٍ، وَلَنَا الْحِكْمَةُ وَالْحُجَّةُ فِي ذَلِكَ، وَهَكَذَا نُعْطِي النُّبُوَّةَ لِمَنْ نُرِيدُ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ حَيْثُ يَجْعَلُ رِسَالَتَهُ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١٢٤] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿انْظُرْ كَيْفَ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ [وَلَلآخِرَةُ أَكْبَرُ دَرَجَاتٍ وَأَكْبَرُ تَفْضِيلا] ﴾ [الْإِسْرَاءِ: ٢١] وَقَدْ رَوَى الْحَافِظُ ابْنُ عَسَاكِرَ فِي تَرْجَمَةِ "إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ" مِنْ تَارِيخِهِ عَنِ الْمَأْمُونِ الْخَلِيفَةِ: أَنَّهُ رَأَى فِي قَصْرٍ بِبِلَادِ الرُّومِ مَكْتُوبًا بِالْحِمْيَرِيَّةِ، فَعُرِّبَ لَهُ، فَإِذَا هُوَ: بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ مَا اخْتَلَفَ اللَّيْلُ وَالنَّهَارُ، وَلَا دَارَتْ نُجُومُ السَّمَاءِ فِي الْفَلَكِ إِلَّا بِنَقْلِ النَّعِيمِ عَنْ مَلِك قَدْ زَالَ سُلْطَانُهُ إِلَى مَلِكٍ. ومُلْكُ ذِي الْعَرْشِ دَائِمٌ أَبَدًا لَيْسَ بِفَانٍ وَلَا بِمُشْتَرِكٍ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿تُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهَارِ وَتُولِجُ النَّهَارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ﴾ أَيْ: تَأْخُذُ مِنْ طُولِ هَذَا فَتَزِيدُهُ فِي قِصَرِ هَذَا فَيَعْتَدِلَانِ، ثُمَّ تَأْخُذُ مِنْ هَذَا فِي هَذَا فَيَتَفَاوَتَانِ، ثُمَّ يَعْتَدِلَانِ. وَهَكَذَا فِي فُصُولِ السَّنَةِ: رَبِيعًا وَصَيْفًا وَخَرِيفًا وَشِتَاءً.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَتُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَتُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِّ﴾ أَيْ: تُخْرِجُ الحبَّة مِنَ الزَّرْعِ وَالزَّرْعَ مِنَ الْحَبَّةِ، وَالنَّخْلَةَ مِنَ النَّوَاةِ وَالنَّوَاةَ مِنَ النَّخْلَةِ، وَالْمُؤْمِنَ مِنَ الْكَافِرِ وَالْكَافِرَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِ، وَالدَّجَاجَةَ مِنَ الْبَيْضَةِ وَالْبَيْضَةَ مِنَ الدَّجَاجَةِ، وَمَا جَرَى هَذَا الْمَجْرَى مِنْ جَمِيعِ الْأَشْيَاءِ ﴿وَتَرْزُقُ مَنْ تَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ﴾ أَيْ: تُعْطِي مَنْ شِئْتَ مِنَ الْمَالِ مَا لَا يَعده وَلَا يَقْدِرُ عَلَى إِحْصَائِهِ، وَتَقْتُرُ عَلَى آخَرِينَ، لِمَا لك فِي ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْحِكْمَةِ وَالْإِرَادَةِ وَالْمَشِيئَةِ وَالْعَدْلِ. قَالَ الطَّبَرَانِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ زَكَرِيَّا الْغَلَّابِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ جسْر بْنِ فَرْقَد، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، عَنْ عَمْرو بْنِ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْجَوْزَاءِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "اسْم اللهِ الأعْظَمَ الَّذي إذَا دُعِيَ بِهِ أَجَابَ، فِي هَذِهِ الآيةِ مِنْ آلِ عِمْرانَ: ﴿قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ مَالِكَ الْمُلْكِ [تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتَنزعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ بِيَدِكَ الْخَيْرُ إِنَّكَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ] ﴾ .

---

## 3:28 — verse 28

### Translation

Allah, blessed and exalted, forbade His believing servants from allying with the disbelievers, and from taking them as protecting friends, confiding affection to them apart from the believers. Then He threatened concerning that, saying: {And whoever does that has nothing to do with Allah} meaning, whoever commits what Allah has forbidden in this matter has disowned himself from Allah, as He said: {O you who believe, do not take the disbelievers as protecting friends instead of the believers. Do you wish to give Allah a clear proof against yourselves?} [al-Nisa: 144]. And He said, exalted is He: {O you who believe, do not take the Jews and Christians as protecting friends. They are protecting friends of one another. And whoever among you takes them as friends is indeed one of them. Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people} [al-Ma'idah: 51].

And He, exalted is He, said: {O you who believe, do not take My enemy and your enemy as protecting friends, offering them affection} up to His saying: {And whoever among you does that has certainly strayed from the straight path} [al-Mumtahanah: 1]. And He, exalted is He, said, after mentioning the mutual alliance of the believers among the Muhajirin, the Ansar, and the Bedouins: {And those who disbelieve are protecting friends of one another. If you do not do so, there will be turmoil in the land and great corruption} [al-Anfal: 73].

As for His saying, {except that you fear from them a fear}, meaning: except one who fears their harm in some lands or at some times, for such a person it is permissible to guard against them outwardly, not inwardly nor in intention, as al-Bukhari related from Abu al-Darda that he said: "We indeed show smiling faces to certain people while our hearts curse them."

Al-Thawri said: Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with him and his father, said: "taqiyyah (precautionary dissimulation) is not by deed, rather taqiyyah is only by the tongue." Al-Awfi likewise related from Ibn Abbas: "taqiyyah is only by the tongue." Abu al-Aliyah, Abu al-Sha'tha, al-Dahhak, and al-Rabi' ibn Anas said the same. What they said is supported by the word of Allah, exalted is He: {Whoever disbelieves in Allah after his belief, except one who is compelled while his heart remains at rest with faith, but whoever opens his breast to disbelief, upon them is wrath from Allah, and for them is a great punishment} [al-Nahl: 106].

Al-Bukhari said: al-Hasan said: "Taqiyyah remains valid until the Day of Resurrection."

Then He, exalted is He, said: {And Allah warns you of Himself}, meaning He warns you of His vengeance, that is, of His retribution and the severity of His punishment against whoever allies with His enemies and shows enmity toward His friends.

Then He, exalted is He, said: {And to Allah is the final return}, meaning to Him is the return and the ultimate destination, and He will recompense every doer according to his deed.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Amr ibn Maymun ibn Mihran said: Mu'adh ibn Jabal stood among us and said: "O Banu Awd, I am the messenger of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to you. You know that the return is to Allah, either to Paradise or to the Fire."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

نَهَى اللَّهُ، تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى، عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْ يُوَالُوا الْكَافِرِينَ، وَأَنْ يَتَّخِذُوهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ يُسِرُّون إِلَيْهِمْ بِالْمَوَدَّةِ مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، ثُمَّ تَوَعَّدَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذَلِكَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَ اللَّهِ فِي شَيْءٍ﴾ أَيْ: مَنْ يَرْتَكِبُ نَهْيَ اللَّهِ فِي هَذَا فَقَدْ بَرِئَ مِنَ اللَّهِ كَمَا قَالَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا الْكَافِرِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَتُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَجْعَلُوا لِلَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطَانًا مُبِينًا﴾ [النِّسَاءِ:١٤٤] وَقَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا الْيَهُودَ وَالنَّصَارَى أَوْلِيَاءَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ [إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ] ﴾ [الْمَائِدَةِ: ٥١] .

[وَقَالَ تَعَالَى] ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا عَدُوِّي وَعَدُوَّكُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ تُلْقُونَ إِلَيْهِمْ بِالْمَوَدَّةِ﴾ إِلَى أَنْ قَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْهُ مِنْكُمْ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَوَاءَ السَّبِيلِ﴾ [الْمُمْتَحِنَةِ: ١] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى -بَعْدَ ذِكْرِ مُوَالَاةِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنَ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنْصَارِ وَالْأَعْرَابِ-: ﴿وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ إِلا تَفْعَلُوهُ تَكُنْ فِتْنَةٌ فِي الأرْضِ وَفَسَادٌ كَبِيرٌ﴾ [الْأَنْفَالِ: ٧٣] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِلا أَنْ تَتَّقُوا مِنْهُمْ تُقَاةً﴾ أَيْ: إِلَّا من خاف في بعض البلدان أوالأوقات مِنْ شَرِّهِمْ، فَلَهُ أَنْ يَتَّقِيَهُمْ بِظَاهِرِهِ لَا بِبَاطِنِهِ وَنِيَّتِهِ، كَمَا حَكَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "إنَّا لَنَكْشرُ فِي وُجُوهِ أقْوَامٍ وَقُلُوبُنَا تَلْعَنُهُمْ".

وَقَالَ الثَّوْرِيُّ: قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: لَيْسَ التَّقِيَّةُ بِالْعَمَلِ إِنَّمَا التَّقِيَّةُ بِاللِّسَانِ، وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ الْعَوْفِيُّ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: إِنَّمَا التَّقِيَّةُ بِاللِّسَانِ، وَكَذَا قَالَ أَبُو الْعَالِيَةِ، وَأَبُو الشَّعْثَاءِ وَالضَّحَّاكُ، وَالرَّبِيعُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ. وَيُؤَيِّدُ مَا قَالُوهُ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿مَنْ كَفَرَ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ إِيمَانِهِ إِلا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالإيمَانِ [وَلَكِنْ مَنْ شَرَحَ بِالْكُفْرِ صَدْرًا فَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ] ﴾ [النحل: ١٠٦] .

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: قَالَ الْحَسَنُ: التَّقِيَّةُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللَّهُ نَفْسَهُ﴾ أَيْ: يُحَذِّرُكُمْ نِقْمَتَهُ، أَيْ مُخَالَفَتَهُ وَسَطْوَتَهُ فِي عَذَابِهِ لِمَنْ وَالَى أَعْدَاءَهُ وَعَادَى أَوْلِيَاءَهُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِلَى اللَّهِ الْمَصِيرُ﴾ أَيْ: إِلَيْهِ الْمَرْجِعُ وَالْمُنْقَلَبُ، فَيُجَازِي كُلَّ عَامِلٍ بِعَمَلِهِ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا سُوَيْدُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا مُسْلِمُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي حُسَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ سَابِطٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَيْمُونِ [بْنِ مِهْران] قال: قام فينا معاذ ابن جَبَلٍ فَقَالَ: يَا بَنِي أَوْدٍ، إِنِّي رَسُولُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ إِلَيْكُمْ، تَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ الْمَعَادَ [إِلَى اللَّهِ] إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ أَوْ إِلَى النَّارِ .

---

## 3:29-30 — verses 29-30 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Blessed and Exalted informs His servants that He knows secrets, innermost thoughts, and outward matters, and that no hidden thing about them escapes Him. Rather, His knowledge encompasses them in all states, moments, instants, and all times, and encompasses all that is in the heavens and the earth. Not the weight of a speck, nor anything smaller than that, is absent from Him in all the regions of the earth, the seas, and the mountains. And He is {over all things capable}, meaning His power is effective throughout all of that.

This is a warning from Him to His servants regarding fear and reverence of Him, so that they do not commit what He has forbidden and what He hates from them, for He is knowing of all their affairs, and He is capable of hastening punishment upon them. Even if He grants respite to whomever He grants it, He delays and then seizes with the seizing of One mighty and all-powerful. Therefore He says after this: {The Day every soul will find what it has done of good brought forth, [and what it has done of evil, it will wish that between it and that there were a great distance]}, the verse, meaning: on the Day of Resurrection, all of a servant's deeds, of good and evil, will be presented to him, just as He, Exalted, said: {Man will be informed that Day of what he sent forth and what he left behind} [al-Qiyamah: 13].
