Then God, exalted is He, said: {Ibrahim was not a Jew, nor a Christian, but he was a hanif (upright monotheist), a muslim (one submitted to God)}, that is, one who turned away from associating partners with God, inclining purposefully toward faith. {And he was not of those who associate partners with God} [al-Baqarah: 135].

This verse is like the one that preceded it in Surat al-Baqarah: {And they say, "Be Jews or Christians, and you will be guided." Say: "Rather, the religion of Ibrahim, a hanif, and he was not of those who associate partners with God"} [al-Baqarah: 135].

Then God, exalted is He, said: {Indeed, the people most worthy of Ibrahim are those who followed him, and this Prophet, and those who believe. And God is the Guardian (wali) of the believers.} God, exalted is He, says: those most deserving of following Ibrahim the Intimate Friend are those who followed him in his religion, and this Prophet, meaning Muhammad ﷺ, and those who believed among his Companions, the Emigrants (Muhajirun) and the Helpers (Ansar), and those who came after them.

Sa'id ibn Mansur said: Abu al-Ahwas informed us, from Sa'id ibn Masruq, from Abu al-Duha, from Masruq, from Ibn Mas'ud, may God be pleased with him, that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Every prophet has guardians (wulat) among the prophets, and my guardian among them is my father and the Intimate Friend of my Lord, Mighty and Majestic is He." Then he recited: {Indeed, the people most worthy of Ibrahim are those who followed him, and this Prophet, and those who believe. And God is the Guardian of the believers.}

Al-Tirmidhi and al-Bazzar related it from the hadith of Abu Ahmad al-Zubayri, from Sufyan al-Thawri, from his father, with the same chain. Then al-Bazzar said: Others besides Abu Ahmad related it from Sufyan, from his father, from Abu al-Duha, from Abdullah, without mentioning Masruq. Al-Tirmidhi likewise related it by the route of Waki', from Sufyan, then said: this is more authentic (asahh). But Waki' related it in his Tafsir, saying: Sufyan told us, from his father, from Abu Ishaq, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said... and he mentioned it.

As for His saying: {And God is the Guardian of the believers}, that is, He is the Guardian of all the believers, through His messengers.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُنْكِرُ تَعَالَى عَلَى الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى فِي مُحَاجَّتِهِمْ فِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْخَلِيلِ، وَدَعْوَى كُلِّ طَائِفَةٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنَّهُ كَانَ مِنْهُمْ، كَمَا قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ:

حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ مَوْلَى زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، حَدَّثَنِي سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ أَوْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عباس قال: اجْتَمَعَتْ نَصَارَى نَجْرَانَ وَأَحْبَارُ يَهُودَ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَتَنَازَعُوا عِنْدَهُ، فَقَالَتِ الْأَحْبَارُ: مَا كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ إِلَّا يَهُودِيًّا. وَقَالَتِ النَّصَارَى مَا كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ إِلَّا نَصْرَانِيًّا. فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تُحَاجُّونَ فِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ [وَمَا أُنزلَتِ التَّوْرَاةُ وَالإنْجِيلُ إِلا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ] ﴾ أَيْ: كَيْفَ تَدّعُون، أَيُّهَا الْيَهُودُ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَهُودِيًّا، وَقَدْ كَانَ زَمَنُهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ اللَّهُ التَّوْرَاةَ عَلَى مُوسَى، وَكَيْفَ تَدّعُون، أَيُّهَا النَّصَارَى، أَنَّهُ كَانَ نَصْرَانِيًّا، وَإِنَّمَا حَدَثَتِ النَّصْرَانِيَّةُ بَعْدَ زَمَنِهِ بِدَهْرٍ. وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ﴾ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿هَا أَنْتُمْ هَؤُلاءِ حَاجَجْتُمْ فِيمَا لَكُمْ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلِمَ تُحَاجُّونَ فِيمَا لَيْسَ لَكُمْ بِهِ عِلْمٌ [وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُون] َ ﴾

هَذَا إِنْكَارٌ عَلَى مَنْ يُحَاجُّ فِيمَا لَا عِلْمَ لَهُ بِهِ، فإنَّ الْيَهُودَ وَالنَّصَارَى تَحَاجوا فِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ بِلَا عِلْمٍ، وَلَوْ تَحَاجُّوا فِيمَا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ مِنْهُ علْم مِمَّا يَتَعَلَّقُ بِأَدْيَانِهِمُ الَّتِي شُرِعَتْ لَهُمْ إِلَى حِينِ بِعْثَةِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ لَكَانَ أَوْلَى بِهِمْ، وَإِنَّمَا تَكَلَّمُوا فِيمَا لَمْ يَعْلَمُوا بِهِ، فَأَنْكَرَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ذَلِكَ، وَأَمَرَهُمْ بِرَدِّ مَا لَا عِلْمَ لَهُمْ بِهِ إِلَى عَالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ، الَّذِي يَعْلَمُ الْأُمُورَ عَلَى حَقَائِقِهَا وَجَلِيَّاتِهَا، وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنْتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿مَا كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ يَهُودِيًّا وَلا نَصْرَانِيًّا وَلَكِنْ كَانَ حَنِيفًا مُسْلِمًا﴾ أَيْ: مُتَحَنفًا عَنِ الشِّرْكِ قَصْدًا إِلَى الْإِيمَانِ ﴿وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٣٥]

وَهَذِهِ الْآيَةُ كَالَّتِي تَقَدَّمَتْ فِي سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ: ﴿وَقَالُوا كُونُوا هُودًا أَوْ نَصَارَى تَهْتَدُوا [قُلْ بَلْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ] ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٣٥] .

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ وَهَذَا النَّبِيُّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: أَحَقُّ النَّاسِ بِمُتَابَعَةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْخَلِيلِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ عَلَى دِينِهِ، وَهَذَا النَّبِيُّ -يَعْنِي مُحَمَّدًا ﷺ-وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنْصَارِ ومَنْ بَعْدَهُمْ.

قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ: أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ مَسْرُوقٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم قال: "إنَّ لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ وُلاةً مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ، وإنَّ وَليِّي مِنْهُمْ أَبِي وخَلِيلُ رَبِّي عَزَّ وَجَلَّ". ثُمَّ قَرَأَ: ﴿إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ [وَهَذَا النَّبِيُّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ] ﴾ .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ وَالْبَزَّارُ مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَبِي أَحْمَدَ الزُّبيري، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، بِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ الْبَزَّارُ: وَرَوَاهُ غَيْرُ أَبِي أَحْمَدَ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ مَسْرُوقًا. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ مِنْ طَرِيقِ وَكِيع، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَهَذَا أَصَحُّ لَكِنْ رَوَاهُ وَكِيعٌ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الله ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ ... فَذَكَرَهُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ أي: ولي جميع المؤمنين برسله.

---

## 3:69-74 — verses 69-74 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty informs of the envy of the Jews toward the believers and their aggression against them in seeking to lead them astray, and He informs that the evil consequence of that returns only upon themselves, while they are unaware that they are the ones being schemed against.

Then the Almighty says, rebuking them: {O People of the Book, why do you disbelieve in the signs of Allah while you bear witness?} that is, while you know their truth and are certain of their validity. {O People of the Book, why do you mix truth with falsehood and conceal the truth while you know?} that is, you conceal what is in your scriptures regarding the description of Muhammad ﷺ while you recognize it and are certain of it.

{And a party of the People of the Book said, "Believe in what was revealed to those who believe at the beginning of the day and disbelieve at its end, [that perhaps they will return]."} This is a scheme they intended in order to confuse the weak among the people regarding the matter of their religion. That is, they consulted among themselves to outwardly show belief at the beginning of the day and pray the dawn prayer with the Muslims, then when the end of the day came, they would revert to their own religion, so that the ignorant among the people would say: what turned them back to their own religion was their discovery of some deficiency or flaw in the religion of the Muslims. This is why they said: {that perhaps they will return}.

Ibn Abi Najih said, on the authority of Mujahid, regarding the Almighty's statement informing about the Jews in this verse: it means the Jews prayed the dawn prayer with the Prophet ﷺ and then disbelieved at the end of the day, as a scheme of theirs, so as to show the people that misguidance had become apparent to them concerning him, after they had followed him.

Al-Awfi said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas: a party of the People of the Book said, "When you meet the companions of Muhammad at the beginning of the day, then believe, and when it is the end of the day, then pray your own prayer, so that perhaps they will say: these are People of the Book and they know better than us." [This has likewise been reported from Qatadah, al-Suddi, al-Rabi, and Abu Malik.]

His statement: {And do not believe except in one who follows your religion} means: do not feel at ease and do not reveal your secret and what you possess except to one who follows your religion, and do not disclose what is in your hands to the Muslims, lest they believe in it and use it as an argument against you. The Almighty said: {Say, indeed guidance is the guidance of Allah}

That is, He is the One who guides the hearts of the believers to the most complete faith, through what He reveals to His servant and Messenger Muhammad ﷺ of clear signs, decisive proofs, and manifest arguments, even if you, O Jews, conceal what is in your hands concerning the description of Muhammad in your scriptures which you transmitted from the earlier prophets.

His statement: {that anyone should be given the like of what you were given, or that they should argue with you before your Lord} means: they are saying, do not reveal what knowledge you possess to the Muslims, lest they learn it from you and become equal to you in it, and even surpass you through it due to the strength of their belief in it, or lest they use it as an argument against you before Allah, that is, take it as a proof against you from what is in your hands, so that the evidence stands against you and the argument is composed against you in this world and the next. The Almighty said: {Say, indeed bounty is in the hand of Allah; He gives it to whom He wills} that is, all matters are under His disposal, He is the One who gives and withholds, He bestows upon whom He wills faith, knowledge, and complete comprehension, and He leads astray whom He wills, blinding his sight and his insight, sealing his hearing and his heart, and placing a covering over his sight. To Him belongs the argument and the wisdom.

{And Allah is All-Encompassing, All-Knowing. He singles out for His mercy whom He wills, and Allah is the Possessor of great bounty} that is, He singled you out, O believers, with bounty that cannot be limited or described, through what He honored your Prophet Muhammad ﷺ with above all the other prophets, and guided you thereby to the most praiseworthy of the sacred laws.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى عَنْ حَسَدَ الْيَهُودِ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وبَغْيهم إِيَّاهُمُ الْإِضْلَالَ، وَأَخْبَرَ أنَّ وَبَالَ ذَلِكَ إِنَّمَا يَعُودُ عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ، وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ أَنَّهُمْ مَمْكُورٌ بِهِمْ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى مُنْكِرًا عَلَيْهِمْ: ﴿يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَأَنْتُمْ تَشْهَدُونَ﴾ أَيْ: تَعْلَمُونَ صِدْقَهَا وَتَتَحَقَّقُونَ حَقَّهَا ﴿يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تَلْبِسُونَ الْحَقَّ بِالْبَاطِلِ وَتَكْتُمُونَ الْحَقَّ وَأَنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ﴾ أَيْ: تَكْتُمُونَ مَا فِي كُتُبِكُمْ مِنْ صِفَةِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ وَأَنْتُمْ تَعْرِفُونَ ذَلِكَ وَتَتَحَقَّقُونَهُ.

﴿وَقَالَتْ طَائِفَةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ آمِنُوا بِالَّذِي أُنزلَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَجْهَ النَّهَارِ وَاكْفُرُوا آخِرَهُ [لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ] ﴾ هَذِهِ مَكِيدَةٌ أَرَادُوهَا ليلْبسُوا عَلَى الضُّعَفَاءِ مِنَ النَّاسِ أمْر دِينِهِمْ، وَهُوَ أَنَّهُمُ اشْتَوروا بَيْنَهُمْ أَنْ يُظْهِرُوا الْإِيمَانَ أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ ويُصَلّوا مَعَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ صَلَاةَ الصُّبْحِ، فَإِذَا جَاءَ آخِرُ النَّهَارِ ارْتَدُّوا إِلَى دِينِهِمْ لِيَقُولَ الْجَهَلَةُ مِنَ النَّاسِ: إِنَّمَا رَدّهم إِلَى دِينِهِمُ اطّلاعهُم عَلَى نَقِيصَةٍ وَعَيْبٍ فِي دِينِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، وَلِهَذَا قَالُوا: ﴿لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ﴾ .

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي نَجِيح، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى إِخْبَارًا عَنِ الْيَهُودِ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: يَعْنِي يَهُودَ، صَلَّت مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ صَلَاةَ الْفَجْرِ وَكَفَرُوا آخِرَ النَّهَارِ، مَكْرًا مِنْهُمْ، ليُرُوا النَّاسَ أَنَّ قَدْ بَدَتْ لَهُمْ مِنْهُ الضَّلَالَةُ، بَعْدَ أَنْ كَانُوا اتَّبِعُوهُ.

وَقَالَ العَوْفِي، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: قَالَتْ طَائِفَةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ: إِذَا لَقِيتُمْ أَصْحَابَ مُحَمَّدٍ أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ فَآمِنُوا، وَإِذَا كَانَ آخِرُهُ فَصَلّوا صَلَاتَكُمْ، لَعَلَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ: هَؤُلَاءِ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ وَهُمْ أَعْلَمُ مِنَّا. [وَهَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ قَتَادَةَ وَالسُّدِّيِّ وَالرَّبِيعِ وَأَبِي مَالِكٍ] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا تُؤْمِنُوا إِلا لِمَنْ تَبِعَ دِينَكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: لَا تَطْمَئِنُّوا وتظهروا سركم وما عندكم إلا لمن اتبع دِينَكُمْ وَلَا تُظْهِرُوا مَا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَيُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ وَيَحْتَجُّوا بِهِ عَلَيْكُمْ؛ قَالَ اللَّهُ تعالى: ﴿قُلْ إِنَّ الْهُدَى هُدَى اللَّهِ﴾

أَيْ هُوَ الَّذِي يَهْدِي قُلُوبَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِلَى أَتَمِّ الْإِيمَانِ، بِمَا يُنَزِّلُهُ عَلَى عَبْدِهِ وَرَسُولِهِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ من الآيات الْبَيِّنَاتِ، وَالدَّلَائِلِ الْقَاطِعَاتِ، وَالْحُجَجِ الْوَاضِحَاتِ، وَإنْ كَتَمْتُمْ -أَيُّهَا الْيَهُودُ-مَا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ مِنْ صِفَةِ مُحَمَّدٍ فِي كُتُبِكُمُ الَّتِي نَقَلْتُمُوهَا عَنِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ الْأَقْدَمِينَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ ﴿أَنْ يُؤْتَى أَحَدٌ مِثْلَ مَا أُوتِيتُمْ أَوْ يُحَاجُّوكُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّكُمْ﴾ يَقُولُونَ: لَا تُظْهِرُوا مَا عِنْدَكُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَيَتَعَلَّمُوهُ مِنْكُمْ، وَيُسَاوُوكُمْ فِيهِ، وَيَمْتَازُوا بِهِ عَلَيْكُمْ لِشِدَّةِ الْإِيمَانِ بِهِ، أَوْ يُحَاجُّوكُمْ بِهِ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ، أَيْ: يَتَّخِذُوهُ حُجَّةً عَلَيْكُمْ مِمَّا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ، فَتَقُومُ بِهِ عَلَيْكُمُ الدَّلَالَةُ وتَتَركَّب الحجةُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ. قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ إِنَّ الْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشَاءُ﴾ أَيِ: الأمورُ كُلَّهَا تَحْتَ تَصْرِيفِهِ، وَهُوَ الْمُعْطِي الْمَانِعُ، يَمُنّ عَلَى مَنْ يَشَاءُ بِالْإِيمَانِ وَالْعِلْمِ وَالتَّصَوُّرِ التَّامِّ، وَيَضِلُّ مَنْ يَشَاءُ ويُعمي بَصَرَهُ وَبَصِيرَتَهُ، وَيَخْتِمُ عَلَى سَمْعِهِ وَقَلْبِهِ، وَيَجْعَلُ عَلَى بَصَرِهِ غِشَاوَةً، وَلَهُ الْحُجَّةُ وَالْحِكْمَةُ .

﴿وَاللَّهُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ. يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ﴾ أَيِ: اخْتَصَّكُمْ -أَيُّهَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ-مِنَ الْفَضْلِ بِمَا لَا يُحَد وَلَا يُوصَف، بِمَا شَرَّفَ بِهِ نَبِيَّكُمْ مُحَمَّدًا ﷺ عَلَى سَائِرِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَهَدَاكُمْ بِهِ لِأَحْمَدِ الشَّرَائِعِ.

---

## 3:75-76 — verses 75-76 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty informs us about the Jews, that among them are those who betray trust, and He warns the believers against being deceived by them. For among them is {one who, if you entrust him with a qintar} that is, of wealth, {he will render it back to you}, meaning: and what is less than that, all the more so, he would render it back to you. {And among them is one who, if you entrust him with a dinar, he will not render it back to you unless you keep standing over him}, that is, through demanding, persisting, and pressing insistently to extract your right. And if this is his conduct regarding a dinar, then regarding what is more than that, it is all the more likely that he will not render it back.

The discussion of the qintar has already been given at the beginning of the surah, and as for the dinar, it is well known.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Malik ibn Dinar said: the dinar was named as such because it is "din" (religious obligation) and "nar" (fire). He said: its meaning is that whoever takes it by its rightful due, it is his religious due, and whoever takes it without its rightful due, the fire is for him.

It is fitting to mention here the hadith that al-Bukhari cites in suspended form (mu'allaq) in more than one place in his Sahih, the best formulation of which occurs in the Book of Suretyship, where he said: al-Layth said: [chain] from Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that he mentioned a man from the Children of Israel who asked another of the Children of Israel to lend him a thousand dinars. The man said: bring me witnesses so that I may have them testify. He said: Allah suffices as a witness. He said: bring me a guarantor. He said: Allah suffices as a guarantor. He said: you have spoken truly. So he gave him the money for a specified term. The borrower went out to sea, accomplished his purpose, then sought a vessel to carry him back for the appointed term but found none. So he took a piece of wood, hollowed it out, placed inside it a thousand dinars along with a letter to his creditor, then sealed the opening, and brought it to the sea, saying: O Allah, You know that I borrowed from so-and-so a thousand dinars, and he asked me for a guarantor, and I said Allah suffices as a guarantor, and he accepted You. And he asked me for a witness, and I said Allah suffices as a witness, and he accepted You. And I have striven to find a vessel to send to him what is his due, but I have not been able, so I entrust it to You. Then he cast it into the sea until it went into it, and he departed, while still seeking a vessel to take him to his own land.

Meanwhile the man who had lent him the money went out looking, hoping perhaps that a vessel would bring him his wealth, and there he found the piece of wood containing the money. He took it home for his family as firewood, and when he broke it open, he found the money and the letter. Then the one who had borrowed from him arrived, and brought him the thousand dinars, saying: by Allah, I did not cease striving to find a vessel so that I could bring you your money, but I found no vessel before the one I came in. He said: did you send me anything before? He said: did I not tell you that I found no vessel before this one? He said: indeed, Allah has already delivered to you on your behalf what you sent in the piece of wood, so go your way with the thousand dinars, rightly guided.

Thus al-Bukhari related it in its place in suspended form with a decisive formulation, and he related it with a full chain in some other places of the Sahih, from Abdullah ibn Salih, the scribe of al-Layth, from him. Imam Ahmad related it in his Musnad in this lengthy form, from Yunus ibn Muhammad al-Mu'addib, from al-Layth, with the same content. Al-Bazzar related it in his Musnad, [chain] from the Prophet ﷺ, similarly, then said: it is not related from the Prophet ﷺ except by this route with this chain. So he said, but this is an error, as has already been shown.

As for His saying: {That is because they said: we bear no responsibility regarding the unlettered ones}, that is: what drove them to deny the truth is that they say: there is no sin upon us in our religion in consuming the wealth of the unlettered ones, meaning the Arabs, for Allah has made it lawful for us. Allah the Almighty said: {And they speak falsehood against Allah while they know it}, that is: they invented this statement and fabricated this falsehood, for Allah has forbidden them from consuming wealth except by its rightful due, and they are but a people of slander.

Abd al-Razzaq said: [chain] a man asked Ibn Abbas, saying: we take, during military expeditions, from the wealth of the People of the Covenant (dhimmah), a chicken or a sheep. Ibn Abbas said: what do you say about that? He said: we say there is no harm upon us in that. He said: this is just like what the People of the Book said: {we bear no responsibility regarding the unlettered ones}. Indeed, if they pay the jizyah, their wealth is not lawful for you except with their willing consent.

Likewise al-Thawri related it from Abu Ishaq, with similar wording.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: when the People of the Book said, {we bear no responsibility regarding the unlettered ones}, the Prophet of Allah [ﷺ] said: the enemies of Allah have lied. There is nothing from the pre-Islamic era (Jahiliyyah) except that it is beneath these two feet of mine, except trustworthiness, for it is to be rendered to the righteous and the wicked alike.

Then the Almighty said: {Yes, whoever fulfills his covenant and is God-fearing}, that is: but whoever among you, O People of the Book, fulfills the covenant that Allah made a covenant with you concerning, namely believing in Muhammad ﷺ when he is sent, just as He took the covenant and pledge upon the prophets and their nations regarding this, and is God-fearing of the things Allah has forbidden and follows His obedience and His law, which He sent with the seal of His messengers and the master of mankind, then {indeed Allah loves the God-fearing}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى عَنِ الْيَهُودِ بِأَنَّ فِيهِمُ الْخَوَنَةَ، وَيُحَذِّرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنَ الِاغْتِرَارِ بِهِمْ، فَإِنَّ مِنْهُمْ ﴿مَنْ إِنْ تَأْمَنْهُ بِقِنْطَارٍ﴾ أَيْ: مِنَ الْمَالِ ﴿يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ﴾ أَيْ: وَمَا دُونَهُ بِطَرِيقِ الْأُولَى أَنْ يُؤَدِّيَهُ إِلَيْكَ ﴿وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ إِنْ تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينَارٍ لَا يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ إِلا مَا دُمْتَ عَلَيْهِ قَائِمًا﴾ أَيْ: بِالْمُطَالَبَةِ وَالْمُلَازَمَةِ وَالْإِلْحَاحِ فِي اسْتِخْلَاصِ حَقِّكَ، وَإِذَا كَانَ هَذَا صَنِيعُهُ فِي الدِّينَارِ فَمَا فَوْقَهُ أُولَى أَلَّا يُؤَدِّيَهُ.

وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ الْكَلَامُ عَلَى الْقِنْطَارِ فِي أَوَّلِ السُّورَةِ، وَأَمَّا الدِّينَارُ فَمَعْرُوفٌ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو السَّكُوني، حَدَّثَنَا بَقِيَّة، عَنْ زِيَادِ بْنِ الْهَيْثَمِ، حَدَّثَنِي مَالِكُ بْنُ دِينَارٍ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا سُمِّيَ الدِّينَارُ لِأَنَّهُ دِينٌ وَنَارٌ. وَقَالَ: مَعْنَاهُ: أَنَّهُ مَنْ أَخَذَهُ بِحَقِّهِ فَهُوَ دِينُهُ، وَمَنْ أَخَذَهُ بِغَيْرِ حَقِّهِ فَلَهُ النَّارُ.

وَمُنَاسِبٌ أَنْ يَكُونَ هَاهُنَا الْحَدِيثَ الَّذِي عَلَّقَهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي غَيْرِ مَوْضِعٍ مِنْ صَحِيحِهِ، وَمِنْ أَحْسَنِهَا سِيَاقُهُ فِي كِتَابِ الْكَفَالَةِ حَيْثُ قَالَ: وَقَالَ اللَّيْثُ: حَدَّثَنِي جَعْفَرُ بْنِ رَبِيعَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ هُرْمُز الْأَعْرَجِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ: أَنَّهُ ذَكَرَ رَجُلا مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ سَأَلَ [بَعْضَ] بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ أَنْ يُسْلِفَهُ أَلْفَ دِينَارٍ، فَقَالَ: ائْتِنِي بِالشُّهَدَاءِ أُشْهِدُهُمْ. فَقَالَ: كَفَى بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا. قَالَ: ائْتِنِي بِالْكَفِيلِ. قَالَ: كَفَى بِاللَّهِ كَفِيلا. قَال َ: صَدَقْتَ. فَدَفَعَهَا إِلَيْهِ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى، فَخَرَجَ فِي الْبَحْرِ فَقَضَى حَاجَتَهُ، ثُمَّ الْتَمَسَ مَرْكَبًا يَرْكَبُهَا يَقْدَمُ عَلَيْهِ لِلأجَلِ الَّذِي أَجَّلَهُ، فَلَمْ يَجِدْ مَرْكِبًا، فَأَخَذَ خَشَبَةً فَنَقَرَهَا فَأَدْخَلَ فِيهَا أَلْفَ دِينَارٍ، وَصَحِيفَةً مِنْهُ إِلَى صَاحِبِهِ، ثُمَّ زَجَّجَ مَوْضِعَهَا، ثُمَّ أَتَى بِهَا إِلَى الْبَحْرِ، فَقَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ أَنِّي استَسْلَفْت ُ فُلانًا أَلْفَ دِينَارٍ فَسَأَلَنِي كَفِيلا فَقُلْتُ: كَفَى بِاللَّهِ كَفِيلا فَرَضِيَ بِكَ . وَسَأَلَنِي شَهِيدًا، فَقُلْتُ: كَفَى بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا فَرَضِيَ بِكَ، وَإِنِّي جَهَدْتُ أَنْ أَجِدَ مَرْكَبًا أَبْعَثُ إِلَيْهِ الَّذِي لَهُ فَلَمْ أَقْدِرْ، وَإِنِّي اسْتَوْدَعْتُكَهَا . فَرَمَى بِهَا فِي الْبَحْرِ حَتَّى وَلَجَتْ فِيهِ، ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ وَهُوَ فِي ذَلِكَ يَلْتَمِسُ مَرْكَبًا يَخْرُجُ إِلَى بَلَدِهِ، فَخَرَجَ الرَّجُلُ الَّذِي كَانَ أَسْلَفَهُ يَنْظُرُ لَعَلَّ مَرْكَبًا يَجِيئُهَُ بِمَالِهِ، فَإِذَا بِالْخَشَبَةِ الَّتِي فِيهَا الْمَالُ، فَأَخَذَهَا لأهْلِهِ حَطَبًا، فَلَمَّا كَسَرَهَا وَجَدَ الْمَالَ وَالصَّحِيفَةَ، ثُمَّ قَدِمَ الَّذِي كَانَ تَسَلَّف مِنْهُ، فَأَتَاه بِأَلْفِ دِينَارٍ، وَقَالَ: وَاللَّهِ مَا زِلْتُ جَاهِدًا فِي طَلَبِ مَرْكَبٍ لآتِيَكَ بِمَالِكَ، فَمَا وَجَدْتُ مَرْكَبًا قَبْلَ الَّذِي أَتَيْتُ فِيهِ. قَالَ: هَلْ كُنْتَ بَعَثْتَ إِلَيَّ بِشَيْءٍ؟ قَالَ: أَلَمْ أُخْبِرْكَ أَنِّي لَمْ أَجِدْ مَرْكَبًا قَبْلَ هَذَا؟ قَالَ: فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَدَّى عَنْكَ الَّذِي بَعَثْتَ فِي الْخَشَبَةِ، فَانْصَرِفْ بِأَلْفِ دِينَارٍ رَاشِدًا.

هَكَذَا رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي مَوْضِعِهِ مُعَلَّقًا بِصِيغَةِ الْجَزْمِ، وَأَسْنَدَهُ فِي بَعْضِ الْمَوَاضِعِ مِنَ الصَّحِيحِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ كَاتِبِ اللَّيْثِ عَنْهُ. وَرَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ فِي مُسْنَدِهِ هَكَذَا مُطَوَّلًا عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْمُؤَدِّبِ، عَنِ اللَّيْثِ بِهِ وَرَوَاهُ الْبَزَّارُ فِي مُسْنَدِهِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُدْرِك، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عَوَانة، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ بِنَحْوِهِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَا يُرْوَى عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ إِلَّا مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ. كَذَا قَالَ، وَهُوَ خَطَأٌ، لِمَا تَقَدَّمَ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا لَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا فِي الأمِّيِّينَ سَبِيلٌ﴾ أَيْ: إنَّمَا حَمَلهم عَلَى جُحود الْحَقِّ أَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا فِي دِينِنَا حَرَج فِي أَكْلِ أَمْوَالِ الْأُمِّيِّينَ، وَهُمُ الْعَرَبُ؛ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَحَلَّهَا لَنَا. قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ﴾ أَيْ: وَقَدِ اخْتَلَقُوا هَذِهِ الْمَقَالَةَ، وَائْتَفَكُوا بِهَذِهِ الضَّلَالَةِ، فَإن اللَّهَ حَرم عَلَيْهِمْ أَكْلَ الْأَمْوَالِ إِلَّا بِحَقِّهَا، وَإِنَّمَا هُمْ قَوْمٌ بُهْت.

قَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَنْبَأَنَا مَعْمَر، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ الْهَمْدَانِيِّ، عَنْ [أَبِي] صَعْصَعَة بْنِ يَزِيدَ ؛ أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَأَلَ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: إِنَّا نُصِيب فِي الْغَزْوِ مِنْ أَمْوَالِ أَهْلِ الذِّمَّةِ الدجاجةَ والشاةَ؟ قال ابن عَبَّاسٍ: فَتَقولون مَاذَا؟ قَالَ: نَقُولُ لَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا بِذَلِكَ بَأْسٌ. قَالَ: هَذَا كَمَا قَالَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا فِي الأمِّيِّينَ سَبِيلٌ﴾ إِنَّهُمْ إِذَا أَدَّوُا الْجِزْيَةَ لَمْ تَحل لَكُمْ أموالهُم إِلَّا بِطِيب أَنْفُسِهِمْ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ بِنَحْوِهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو الرَّبِيعِ الزَّهْرَانِيُّ حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا قَالَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا فِي الأمِّيِّينَ سَبِيلٌ﴾ قَالَ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ [ﷺ] كَذَبَ أَعْدَاءُ اللهِ، مَا مِنْ شِيٍء كَانَ فِي الجَاهِلِيَّةِ إِلا وَهُوَ تَحْتَ قَدَمَيَّ هَاتَيْنِ إِلا الأمَانَةَ، فَإِنَّهَا مُؤَدَّاةٌ إِلَى الْبَرِّ وَالفَاجِرِ" 

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿بَلَى مَنْ أَوْفَى بِعَهْدِهِ وَاتَّقَى﴾ أَيْ: لَكِنْ مَنْ أَوْفَى بِعَهْدِهِ مِنْكُمْ يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ الَّذِي عَاهَدَكُمُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ، مِنَ الْإِيمَانِ بِمُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ إِذَا بُعِثَ، كَمَا أَخَذَ الْعَهْدَ وَالْمِيثَاقَ عَلَى الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَأُمَمَهُمْ بِذَلِكَ، وَاتَّقَى مَحَارِمَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى وَاتَّبَعَ طَاعَتَهُ وَشَرِيعَتَهُ الَّتِي بَعَثَ بِهَا خَاتَمَ رُسُلِهِ وَسَيِّدَ الْبَشَرِ " فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ "

---

## 3:77 — verse 77

### Translation

The Almighty says: those who take in exchange for what God bound them to, namely following Muhammad ﷺ, mentioning his description to the people, and clarifying his matter, and in exchange for their lying, sinful, wicked oaths, a small, paltry price, which is the goods of this perishing, vanishing world, {those, there is no share for them in the Hereafter}, meaning no portion is theirs in it and no lot of it belongs to them. {And God will not speak to them, nor look at them on the Day of Resurrection}, meaning with mercy from Him toward them, that is, He will not address them with speech of kindness, nor look at them with the eye of mercy. {Nor will He purify them}, meaning from sins and impurities, but rather He will command them to the Fire. {And theirs is a painful punishment}. Hadiths have come down that relate to this noble verse, so let us mention what is readily available of them.

The first hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Affan told us, Shu'bah told us, saying: Ali ibn Mudrik informed me, saying: I heard Abu Zur'ah, from Kharashah ibn al-Hurr, from Abu Dharr, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "Three whom God will not speak to, nor look at, nor purify on the Day of Resurrection, and theirs is a painful punishment." I said: O Messenger of God, who are they? They have failed and lost! He said, and the Messenger of God [ﷺ] repeated it three times: "The one who trails his garment out of pride, the one who sells his merchandise by false oath, and the one who gives and then boasts of it (al-mannan)." Muslim and the compilers of the Sunan collections related it from the hadith of Shu'bah, with this chain.

Another route: Ahmad said: Isma'il told us, from al-Hurayri, from Abu al-Ala ibn al-Shikhkhir, from Abu al-Ahmas, who said: I met Abu Dharr and said to him: it has reached me about you that you narrate a certain hadith from the Messenger of God ﷺ. He said: you should know that you would not suspect me of lying about the Messenger of God ﷺ after I heard it from him, so what has reached you from me? I said: it has reached me that you say: three whom God loves, and three whom God, Mighty and Majestic, detests. He said: I said it and I heard it. I said: who are those whom God loves? He said: a man who meets the enemy with a small band and offers his own throat to them until he is killed or victory is granted to his companions; and a group traveling who continue their night journey so long that they long to reach evening on solid ground, so they halt, and one of them withdraws and prays until it is time to wake them for their departure; and a man who has a neighbor who harms him, and he bears the harm patiently until death or a move separates them. I said: and who are those whom God detests? He said: the merchant who swears much, or the seller who swears much, and the poor man who is arrogant, and the miser who gives and then boasts of it. This is gharib (a singular report) from this angle.

The second hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yahya ibn Sa'id told us, from Jarir ibn Hazim, who said: Adi ibn Adi told us, Raja ibn Haywah and al-Urs ibn Amirah informed me, from his father Adi, that is, Ibn Amirah al-Kindi, who said: a man from Kindah called Imru' al-Qays ibn Abis disputed with a man from Hadramawt before the Messenger of God ﷺ over some land. He ruled that the Hadrami must produce proof, but he had no proof, so he ruled that Imru' al-Qays must take an oath. The Hadrami said: if I let him take the oath, O Messenger of God, by the Lord of the Kaaba my land will be gone! The Prophet ﷺ said: "Whoever swears a false oath to seize thereby someone's property will meet God, Mighty and Majestic, while He is angry with him." Raja said: and the Messenger of God ﷺ recited {Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of God and their oaths for a small price}. Imru' al-Qays said: what is there for the one who abandons it, O Messenger of God? He said: "Paradise." He said: then bear witness that I have abandoned all of it for him. Al-Nasa'i related it from the hadith of Adi ibn Adi with this chain.

The third hadith: Ahmad said: Abu Mu'awiyah told us, al-A'mash told us, from Shaqiq, from Abdullah, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever swears an oath in which he is sinful, to seize thereby the property of a Muslim man, will meet God, Mighty and Majestic, while He is angry with him."

Al-Ash'ath said: By God, that was about me. There was land between me and a man from the Jews, and he denied my claim, so I brought him before the Messenger of God ﷺ. The Messenger of God ﷺ said to me: "Do you have proof?" I said: no. So he said to the Jew: "Swear." I said: O Messenger of God, then he will swear and my property will be gone! So God, Mighty and Majestic, revealed {Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of God and their oaths for a small price} to the end of the verse. Both [al-Bukhari and Muslim] related it from the hadith of al-A'mash.

Another route: Ahmad said: Yahya ibn Adam told us, Abu Bakr ibn Ayyash told us, from Asim ibn Abi al-Najud, from Shaqiq ibn Salamah, Abdullah ibn Mas'ud told us, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever seizes the property of a Muslim man without right will meet God while He is angry with him." He said: then al-Ash'ath ibn Qays came and said: what is Abu Abd al-Rahman narrating to you? So we told him, and he said: this hadith was about me. I disputed with a cousin of mine before the Messenger of God ﷺ over a well of mine that was in his possession, and he denied my claim. The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Your proof that it is your well, or else his oath." I said: O Messenger of God, I have no proof, and if you give it to his oath my well will be gone, for my opponent is a wicked man. The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever seizes the property of a Muslim man without right will meet God while He is angry with him." He said: and the Messenger of God ﷺ recited this verse: {Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of God and their oaths for a small price [those, there is no share for them in the Hereafter, and God will not speak to them, nor look at them on the Day of Resurrection, nor purify them, and theirs is a painful punishment]}.

The fourth hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yahya ibn Ghaylan told us, Rishdin told us, from Zaban, from Sahl ibn Mu'adh ibn Anas, from his father, from the Prophet ﷺ: "God, the Almighty, has servants whom He will not speak to on the Day of Resurrection, nor purify, nor look at." It was said: and who are those, O Messenger of God? He said: "One who disowns his parents and turns away from them in aversion, and one who disowns his child, and a man whom a people favored with kindness, so he denied their kindness and disowned them."

The fifth hadith: Ibn Abi Hatim said: al-Hasan ibn Arafah told us, Hushaym told us, al-Awwam, that is, Ibn Hawshab, informed us, from Ibrahim ibn Abd al-Rahman, that is al-Saksaki, from Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa: that a man set up merchandise of his in the market and swore by God that he had been offered for it what he had not in fact been offered, in order to entrap a Muslim man into buying it, so this verse came down: {Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of God and their oaths for a small price}. Al-Bukhari related it, by more than one route, from al-Awwam.

The sixth hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Waki' told us, al-A'mash told us, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurairah, may God be pleased with him, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Three whom God will not speak to on the Day of Resurrection, nor look at, nor purify, and theirs is a painful punishment: a man who withholds surplus water from a traveler, and a man who swears falsely over merchandise after the afternoon prayer, that is, falsely, and a man who pledges allegiance to a ruler, and if the ruler gives him something he keeps his loyalty, but if the ruler gives him nothing he does not keep his loyalty." Abu Dawud and al-Tirmidhi related it from the hadith of Waki', and al-Tirmidhi said: hasan sahih (sound and authentic).

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَعْتَاضُونَ عَمَّا عَهِدَهُمُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ، مِنَ اتِّبَاعِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، وَذِكْرِ صِفَتِهِ النَّاسَ وَبَيَانِ أَمْرِهِ، وَعَنْ أَيْمَانِهِمُ الْكَاذِبَةِ الْفَاجِرَةِ الْآثِمَةِ بِالْأَثْمَانِ الْقَلِيلَةِ الزَّهِيدَةِ، وَهِيَ عُرُوضُ هَذِهِ الدُّنْيَا الْفَانِيَةِ الزَّائِلَةِ " أُولَئِكَ لَا خَلاقَ لَهُمْ فِي الآخِرَةِ " أَيْ: لَا نَصِيبَ لَهُمْ فِيهَا، وَلَا حَظَّ لَهُمْ مِنْهَا " وَلا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللَّهُ وَلا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ " أَيْ: بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنْهُ لَهُمْ، بِمَعْنَى: لَا يُكَلِّمُهُمْ كَلَامَ لُطْفٍ بِهِمْ، وَلَا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ بِعَيْنِ الرَّحْمَةِ " وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ " أَيْ: مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ وَالْأَدْنَاسِ، بَلْ يَأْمُرُ بِهِمْ إِلَى النَّارِ " وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ " وَقَدْ وَرَدَتْ أَحَادِيثُ تَتَعَلَّقُ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ فَلْنَذْكُرْ مَا تَيَسَّرَ مِنْهَا:

الْحَدِيثُ الْأَوَّلُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ قَالَ: عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُدْرِك أخْبرَني قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا زُرْعَة، عَنْ خَرَشة بْنِ الحُر، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "ثَلاثَة لَا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللهُ وَلا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ" قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَنْ هُمْ؟ خَابُوا وَخَسِرُوا. قَالَ: وَأَعَادَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ [ﷺ] ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ قَالَ: "المُسْبِل، والمُنْفِقُ سِلْعَتَهُ بِالْحَلِفِ الْكاذِبِ، والمنانُ". وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ، وَأَهْلُ السُّنَنِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ شُعْبَةَ، بِهِ.

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنِ الحُرَيري، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ الشِّخِّير، عَنْ أَبِي الأحْمَس قَالَ: لقيتُ أَبَا ذَرٍّ، فقلتُ لَهُ: بَلَغَنِي عَنْكَ أَنَّكَ تُحدِّث حَدِيثًا عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ. فَقَالَ: أَمَا إِنَّهُ لَا تَخَالُني أكذبُ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ بَعْدَ مَا سَمِعْتُهُ مِنْهُ، فَمَا الَّذِي بَلَغَكَ عَنِّي؟ قلتُ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّكَ تَقُولُ: ثَلَاثَةٌ يُحِبُّهُمُ اللَّهُ، وَثَلَاثَةٌ يَشْنَؤهم اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ. قَالَ: قُلْتُهُ وَسَمِعْتُهُ. قُلْتُ: فَمَنْ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ يُحِبُّهُمُ اللَّهُ؟ قَالَ: الرَّجُلُ يَلْقَى الْعَدُوَّ فِي فِئَةٍ فَيَنْصِبُ لَهُمْ نَحْرَه حَتَّى يُقْتَلَ أَوْ يُفْتَحَ لِأَصْحَابِهِ. والقومُ يُسَافِرُونَ فَيَطُولُ سِرَاهُمْ حَتَّى يَحنُّوا أَنْ يُمْسُوا الْأَرْضَ فَيَنْزِلُونَ، فَيَتَنَحَّى أَحَدُهُمْ فَيُصَلِّي حَتَّى يُوقِظَهُمْ لِرَحِيلِهِمْ. والرجلُ يَكُونُ لَهُ الْجَارُ يُؤْذِيهِ فَيَصْبِرُ عَلَى أذاهُ حَتَّى يُفَرِّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا مَوْتٌ أَوْ ظَعْن. قُلْتُ: وَمَنْ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ يَشْنَأُ اللَّهُ؟ قَالَ: التَّاجِرُ الْحَلَّافُ -أَوِ الْبَائِعُ الْحَلَّافُ -وَالْفَقِيرُ الْمُخْتَالُ، وَالْبَخِيلُ الْمَنَّانُ غَرِيبٌ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ .

الْحَدِيثُ الثَّانِي: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ جَرِيرِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَدِيّ بْنُ عَدِيٍّ، أَخْبَرَنِي رَجَاءُ بْنُ حَيْوة والعُرْس بْنُ عَمِيرة عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَدِي -هَو ابْنُ عَمِيرَةَ الْكِنْدِيُّ-قَالَ: خَاصَمَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ كِنْدةَ يُقَالُ لَهُ: امْرُؤُ الْقَيْسِ بْنُ عَابِسٍ رَجلا مِنْ حَضْرمَوْت إلى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي أَرْضٍ، فَقَضَى عَلَى الْحَضْرَمِيِّ بِالْبَيِّنَةِ، فَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ بَيِّنَةٌ، فَقَضَى عَلَى امْرِئِ الْقَيْسِ بِالْيَمِينِ. فَقَالَ الْحَضْرَمِيُّ: إِنْ أَمْكَنْتُهُ مِنَ الْيَمِينِ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ذهبتْ وَرَبِّ الْكَعْبَةِ أَرْضِي. فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "مَنْ حَلَفَ عَلَى يَمِينٍ كَاذِبَةٍ لِيقتطِعَ بِهَا مَال أحَد لَقِيَ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِ غَضْبَانُ" قَالَ رَجَاءٌ: وَتَلَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَيْمَانِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلا﴾ فَقَالَ امْرُؤُ الْقَيْسِ: مَاذَا لِمَنْ تَرَكَهَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ فَقَالَ الْجَنَّةُ" قَالَ: فاشهَدْ أَنِّي قَدْ تَرَكْتُهَا لَهُ كُلَّهَا. وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَدِيِّ بْنِ عَدِيٍّ، بِهِ .

الْحَدِيثُ الثَّالِثُ: قَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ شَقيق، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ حَلَفَ عَلَى يَمِينٍ هُوَ فِيهَا فَاجِر، لِيقْتَطِعَ بِهَا مَال امْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ، لَقِيَ الله عَزَّ وجَلَّ وَهُوَ عَليْهِ غَضْبَانُ".

فَقَالَ الْأَشْعَثُ: فِيَّ وَاللَّهِ كَانَ ذَلِكَ، كَانَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ أَرْضٌ فجَحَدني، فقدَّمته إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أَلَكَ بَيَّنة؟ " قلتُ: لَا فَقَالَ لِلْيَهُودِيِّ: "احْلِفْ" فقلتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِذًا يَحْلِفُ فَيَذْهَبُ مَالِي. فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَيْمَانِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلا﴾ [إِلَى آخِرِ] الْآيَةِ: أَخْرَجَاهُ مِنْ حَدِيثِ الْأَعْمَشِ .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاش، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ أَبِي النَّجُود، عَنْ شَقِيق بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنِ اقْتَطَعَ مَالَ امْرِئٍ مسلمٍ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ لَقِيَ اللَّهَ وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِ غَضْبَان" قَالَ: فَجَاءَ الأشْعث بْنُ قَيْس فَقَالَ: مَا يُحدِّثكم أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ؟ فَحَدَّثْنَاهُ، فَقَالَ: فِيَّ كَانَ هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ، خاصمتُ ابْنَ عمٍّ لِي إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي بِئْرٍ لِي كَانَتْ فِي يَدِهِ، فجَحَدني، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "بَيِّنَتُكَ أنَّها بِئْرُكَ وَإلا فَيَمِينُهُ" قَالَ: قلتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا لِي بَيِّنَةٌ، وَإِنْ تَجْعَلْهَا بِيَمِينِهِ تَذْهَبُ بِئْرِي ؛ إنَّ خَصْمي امْرُؤٌ فَاجِرٌ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنِ اقْتَطَعَ مَالَ امْرِئٍ مسلمٍ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ لَقِيَ اللَّهَ وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِ غَضْبَان" قَالَ: وَقَرَأَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَيْمَانِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلا [أُولَئِكَ لَا خَلاقَ لَهُمْ فِي الآخِرَةِ وَلا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللَّهُ وَلا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ] ﴾ .

الْحَدِيثُ الرَّابِعُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ غَيْلان، حَدَّثَنَا رشْدين عن زَبّان، عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ مُعَاذِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: "إنَّ لِلَّهِ تَعَالى عِبَادًا لَا يُكَلِّمُهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلا يَنْظُرُ إلَيهِمْ" قِيلَ: وَمَنْ أُولَئِكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: "مُتَبَرِّئٌ مَنْ وَالِدَيهِ رَاغِبٌ عَنْهُمَا، ومُتَبَرِّئٌ مِنْ وَلَدِهِ، وَرَجُلٌ أنْعَمَ عَلِيْهِ قَوْمٌ فكَفَر نعْمَتَهُمْ وتَبَرَّأ مِنْهُمْ" .

الْحَدِيثُ الْخَامِسُ: قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَرَفَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم، أَنْبَأَنَا الْعَوَّامُ -يَعني ابْنَ حَوْشَبَ-عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ -يَعْنِي السَّكْسَكي-عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي أوْفَى: أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَقَامَ سِلْعَةً لَهُ فِي السُّوقِ، فَحَلَفَ بِاللَّهِ لَقَدْ أعْطَى بِهَا مَا لَمْ يُعْطه، ليُوقع فِيهَا رَجُلًا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الآية: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَيْمَانِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلا﴾ وَرَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ، مِنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ، عَنِ الْعَوَّامِ .

الْحَدِيثُ السَّادِسُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "ثَلاثَةٌ لَا يُكَلِّمُهُمْ اللَّهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَة وَلا يَنْظُرُ إلَيْهِمْ، وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ ولَهم عذابٌ أَلِيمٌ: رَجُلٌ مَنَعَ ابْنَ السَّبِيلِ فَضْلَ مَاءٍ عِنْدَهُ، وَرَجُلٌ حَلَفَ عَلَى سِلْعَةٍ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ -يَعْنِي كَاذِبًا-وَرَجُلٌ بَايَعَ إِمَامًا، فَإِنْ أَعْطَاهُ وَفَى لَهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يُعْطِهِ لَمْ يَفِ لَهُ". وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ وَكِيعٍ، وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حسن صحيح .

---

## 3:78 — verse 78

### Translation

The Almighty informs us about the Jews, upon whom be the curses of God, that a party among them distort the words from their places and alter the speech of God, removing it from its intended meaning, in order to make the ignorant believe that it is thus in the Book of God, and they attribute it to God, which is a lie against God. Yet they know within themselves that they have lied and fabricated in all of that. That is why He said: {And they say a lie against God while they know}.

Mujahid, al-Sha'bi, al-Hasan, Qatadah, and al-Rabi' ibn Anas said regarding {they twist their tongues with the Book}: they distort it.

Likewise, al-Bukhari narrated from Ibn Abbas that they distort and add, and that none among God's creation removes the wording of any of God's books, but rather they distort it by interpreting it other than its true interpretation.

Wahb ibn Munabbih said: the Torah and the Gospel, as God revealed them, have not had a single letter changed in them, but they lead people astray through distortion and interpretation, and through writings which they used to write from themselves, {and they say, it is from God, while it is not from God}. As for the books of God, they are preserved and cannot be altered.

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated this. If Wahb meant what is in their hands of that, then there is no doubt that alteration, distortion, addition, and omission have entered into it. As for the Arabic rendering of what is present among them, it contains great error, many additions and omissions, and gross mistakes, for it is a matter of the interpretation of a translated, Arabized text, and the understanding of many of them, indeed most of them, indeed all of them, is corrupt. But if he meant the books of God which are truly His revealed books, then those, as he said, are preserved, and nothing has entered into them.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى عَنِ الْيَهُودِ، عَليهم لَعَائِنُ اللَّهِ، أَنَّ مِنْهُمْ فَرِيقًا يُحَرِّفون الْكَلِمَ عَنْ مَوَاضِعِهِ ويُبَدِّلون كَلَامَ اللَّهِ، وَيُزِيلُونَهُ عَنِ الْمُرَادِ بِهِ، ليُوهِموا الْجَهَلَةَ أَنَّهُ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ كَذَلِكَ، وَيَنْسُبُونَهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ، وَهُوَ كَذِبٌ عَلَى اللَّهِ، وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ كَذَبُوا وَافْتَرَوْا فِي ذَلِكَ كُلِّهِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ﴾

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ، وَالشَّعْبِيُّ، وَالْحَسَنُ، وَقَتَادَةُ، وَالرَّبِيعُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ: ﴿يَلْوُونَ أَلْسِنَتَهُمْ بِالْكِتَابِ﴾ يُحَرِّفُونَهُ.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَى الْبُخَارِيُّ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: أَنَّهُمْ يُحَرِّفُونَ وَيَزِيدُونَ وَلَيْسَ أَحَدٌ مِنْ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ يُزِيلُ لَفْظَ كِتَابٍ مِنْ كُتُبِ اللَّهِ، لَكِنَّهُمْ يُحَرِّفُونَهُ: يَتَأَوَّلُونَهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ تَأْوِيلِهِ.

وَقَالَ وَهَبْ بْنُ مُنَبِّه: إِنَّ التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ كَمَا أَنْزَلَهُمَا اللَّهُ لَمْ يُغَيَّرْ مِنْهُمَا حَرْفٌ، وَلَكِنَّهُمْ يُضِلّونَ بِالتَّحْرِيفِ وَالتَّأْوِيلِ، وَكُتُبٍ كَانُوا يَكْتُبُونَهَا مِنْ عِنْدِ أَنْفُسِهِمْ، ﴿وَيَقُولُونَ هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ وَمَا هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ﴾ فَأَمَّا كُتُبُ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّهَا مَحْفُوظَةٌ وَلَا تُحَوَّلُ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، فَإِنْ عَنَى وَهْب مَا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، فَلَا شَكَّ أَنَّهُ قَدْ دَخَلَهَا التَّبْدِيلُ وَالتَّحْرِيفُ وَالزِّيَادَةُ وَالنَّقْصُ، وَأَمَّا تَعْرِيبُ ذَلِكَ الْمُشَاهَدِ بِالْعَرَبِيَّةِ فَفِيهِ خَطَأٌ كَبِيرٌ، وَزِيَادَاتٌ كَثِيرَةٌ وَنُقْصَانٌ، ووَهْم فَاحِشٌ. وَهُوَ مِنْ بَابِ تَفْسِيرِ الْمُعَبَّرِ الْمُعْرَبِ، وفَهْم كَثِيرٍ مِنْهُمْ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ، بَلْ جَمِيعُهُمْ فَاسِدٌ. وَأَمَّا إِنْ عَنَى كُتُبَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي هِيَ كُتُبُهُ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ، فَتِلْكَ كَمَا قَالَ مَحْفُوظَةٌ لَمْ يَدْخُلْهَا شَيْءٌ.

---

## 3:79-80 — verses 79-80 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said: Muhammad ibn Abi Muhammad narrated to us, from Ikrimah or Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Abu Rafi' al-Qurazi said, when the rabbis of the Jews and the Christians of the people of Najran gathered before the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he called them to Islam: "Do you want us, O Muhammad, to worship you as the Christians worship Jesus son of Mary?" A man from the people of Najran, a Christian called al-Ra'is, said: "Is that what you want from us, O Muhammad, and is that what you are calling us to?" or words to that effect. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "God forbid that we should worship other than Allah, or command the worship of other than Him. That is not what He sent me for, nor what He commanded me to do," or words to that effect ﷺ. So Allah, mighty and exalted, revealed concerning their statement: {It is not for a human being that Allah should give him the Book, and judgment, and prophethood} [the verse] up to His saying: {after you had been submitters (Muslims)}.

His saying, {It is not for a human being that Allah should give him the Book, and judgment, and prophethood, and then he should say to the people, "Be servants to me instead of Allah"}, means: it is not fitting for a human being to whom Allah has given the Book, judgment, and prophethood to say to the people, "Worship me instead of Allah," that is, alongside Allah. If this is not fitting for a prophet or a messenger, then it is all the more fitting that it not be proper for anyone else among the people. This is why al-Hasan al-Basri said: "It is not proper for a believer to command people to worship him." He said: this is because the people used to worship one another, meaning the People of the Book, they used to devote themselves in worship to their rabbis and monks, as Allah, exalted is He, said: {They took their rabbis and their monks as lords besides Allah, and the Messiah son of Mary, though they were not commanded except to worship one God. There is no god but He. Glory be to Him above what they associate with Him} [al-Tawbah: 31]. In the Musnad and in al-Tirmidhi, as will come, it is reported that Adi ibn Hatim said: "O Messenger of Allah, they did not worship them." He said: "Yes indeed, they made lawful for them what was forbidden and forbade them what was lawful, and they followed them; that was their worship of them."

So the ignorant among the rabbis, monks, and shaykhs of misguidance fall under this censure and rebuke, unlike the messengers and their followers among the scholars who act upon their knowledge. For these only command what Allah has commanded and what His noble messengers conveyed to them; they only forbid what Allah has forbidden and what His noble messengers conveyed to them. The messengers, Allah's prayers and peace be upon them all, are the intermediaries between Allah and His creation in delivering the message they were entrusted with and conveying the trust. They fulfilled that in the most complete manner, gave sincere counsel to the creation, and conveyed the truth to them.

And His saying: {But be, rather, people of the Lord (Rabbaniyyin) because of what you have been teaching of the Book and because of what you have been studying}, means: rather, the messenger says to the people, "Be Rabbaniyyin." Ibn Abbas, Abu Razin, and others said this means: wise, learned, forbearing men. Al-Hasan and others said: jurists (fuqaha). This is likewise reported from Ibn Abbas, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Qatadah, Ata' al-Khurasani, Atiyyah al-Awfi, and al-Rabi' ibn Anas. It is also reported from al-Hasan: meaning people of worship and people of piety.

Al-Dahhak said concerning His saying, {because of what you have been teaching of the Book and because of what you have been studying}: it is incumbent upon one who learns the Qur'an to be a jurist. "Tu'allimuna" (you teach) means: you make its meaning understood. It was also read as "tu'allimuna" with doubling, from teaching (ta'lim). {and because of what you have been studying} means: you memorize its wording.

Then He said: {and he does not command you to take the angels and the prophets as lords}, meaning: nor does he command you to worship anyone besides Allah, neither a sent prophet nor an angel brought near. {Would he order you to disbelief after you had been submitters?} meaning: he would not do that, for whoever calls to the worship of other than Allah has called to disbelief, and the prophets only command faith, which is the worship of Allah alone, without partner, as Allah, exalted is He, said: {And We sent not before you any messenger except that We revealed to him that there is no god but I, so worship Me} [al-Anbiya: 25]. And Allah, exalted is He, said: {And We certainly sent into every nation a messenger, saying, "Worship Allah and avoid false gods"} [al-Nahl: 36]. And Allah, exalted is He, said: {And ask those We sent before you of Our messengers: did We make besides the Most Merciful gods to be worshipped?} [al-Zukhruf: 45]. And He, exalted is He, said, reporting concerning the angels: {And whoever of them should say, "Indeed, I am a god besides Him," that one We would recompense with Hell. Thus do We recompense the wrongdoers} [al-Anbiya: 29].

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بن أبي محمد، عن عِكْرِمة أو سعيد بن جُبَير، عن ابن عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو رَافِعٍ القُرَظِي، حِينَ اجْتَمَعَتِ الْأَحْبَارُ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى مِنْ أَهْلِ نَجْرَانَ، عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَدَعَاهُمْ إِلَى الْإِسْلَامِ: أَتُرِيدُ يَا مُحَمَّدُ أَنْ نعبدكَ كَمَا تَعْبُدُ النَّصَارَى عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ؟ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ نَجْرَانَ نَصْرَانِيٌّ يقال له الرئيس: أوَ ذاك تُرِيدُ مِنَّا يَا مُحَمَّدُ، وَإِلَيْهِ تَدْعُونَنَا؟ أَوْ كَمَا قَالَ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَعَاذَ اللهِ أنْ نَعْبُدَ غَيْرَ اللهِ، أَوْ أنْ نَأْمُرَ بِعِبَادَةِ غَيْرِه، مَا بِذَلِكَ بَعَثَنِي، وَلَا بِذَلِكَ أَمَرَنِي". أَوْ كَمَا قَالَ ﷺ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فِي ذَلِكَ مِنْ قَوْلِهِمَا: ﴿مَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ اللَّهُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحُكْمَ وَالنُّبُوَّةَ﴾ [الْآيَةَ] إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿بَعْدَ إِذْ أَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ﴾ .

فَقَوْلُهُ ﴿مَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ اللَّهُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحُكْمَ وَالنُّبُوَّةَ ثُمَّ يَقُولَ لِلنَّاسِ كُونُوا عِبَادًا لِي مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: مَا يَنْبَغِي لِبَشَرٍ آتَاهُ اللَّهُ الْكِتَابَ والحُكْم وَالنُّبُوَّةَ أَنْ يَقُولَ لِلنَّاسِ: اعْبُدُونِي مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ. أَيْ: مَعَ اللَّهِ، فَإِذَا كَانَ هَذَا لَا يَصْلُحُ لِنَبِيٍّ وَلَا لِمُرْسَلٍ، فَلِأَنْ لَا يَصْلُحَ لِأَحَدٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ غَيْرَهُمْ بِطَرِيقِ الْأَوْلَى وَالْأَحْرَى؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ: لَا يَنْبَغِي هَذَا لِمُؤْمِنٍ أَنْ يَأْمُرَ النَّاسَ بِعِبَادَتِهِ. قَالَ: وَذَلِكَ أَنَّ الْقَوْمَ كَانَ يَعْبُدُ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا -يَعْنِي أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ-كَانُوا يَتعبَّدون لِأَحْبَارِهِمْ وَرُهْبَانِهِمْ، كَمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿اتَّخَذُوا أَحْبَارَهُمْ وَرُهْبَانَهُمْ أَرْبَابًا مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ [وَالْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلا لِيَعْبُدُوا إِلَهًا وَاحِدًا لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ سُبْحَانَهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ] ﴾ [التَّوْبَةَ:٣١] وَفِي الْمُسْنَدِ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيِّ -كَمَا سَيَأْتِي-أَنَّ عَديّ بْنَ حَاتِمٍ قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا عَبَدُوهُمْ. قَالَ: "بَلَى، إنَّهُمْ أَحَلُّوا لَهُمُ الْحَرَامَ وحَرَّمُوا عَلَيْهِمُ الْحَلالَ، فَاتَّبَعُوهُمْ، فَذَلِكَ عِبَادَتُهُمْ إِيَّاهُمْ".

فَالْجَهَلَةُ مِنَ الْأَحْبَارِ وَالرُّهْبَانِ وَمَشَايِخِ الضَّلَالِ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي هَذَا الذَّمِّ وَالتَّوْبِيخِ، بِخِلَافِ الرُّسُلِ وَأَتْبَاعِهِمْ مِنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ الْعَامِلِينَ، فَإِنَّمَا يَأْمُرُونَ بِمَا أمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ وَبَلَّغَتْهُمْ إِيَّاهُ رُسُلُهُ الْكِرَامُ. إِنَّمَا يَنْهَوْنهم عَمَّا نَهَاهُمُ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ وَبَلَّغَتْهُمْ إِيَّاهُ رُسُلُهُ الْكِرَامُ. فَالرُّسُلُ، صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِمْ أجمعينَ، هُمُ السُّفَرَاءُ بَيْنَ اللَّهِ وَبَيْنَ خَلْقِهِ فِي أَدَاءِ مَا حَمَلُوهُ مِنَ الرِّسَالَةِ وَإِبْلَاغِ الْأَمَانَةِ، فَقَامُوا بِذَلِكَ أَتَمَّ قِيَامٍ، وَنَصَحُوا الْخَلْقَ، وَبَلَّغُوهُمُ الْحَقَّ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلَكِنْ كُونُوا رَبَّانِيِّينَ بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تُعَلِّمُونَ الْكِتَابَ وَبِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَدْرُسُونَ﴾ أَيْ: وَلَكِنْ يَقُولُ الرَّسُولُ للناسِ: كُونُوا رَبَّانيين. قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَأَبُو رَزِين وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، أَيْ: حُكَمَاءَ عُلَمَاءَ حُلَمَاءَ. وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ: فُقَهَاءَ، وَكَذَا رُوِي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبير، وَقَتَادَةَ، وَعَطَاءٍ الْخُرَاسَانِيِّ، وَعَطِيَّةَ الْعَوْفِيِّ، وَالرَّبِيعِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ. وَعَنِ الْحَسَنِ أَيْضًا: يَعْنِي أَهْلَ عِبَادَةٍ وَأَهْلَ تَقْوَى.

وَقَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تُعَلِّمُونَ الْكِتَابَ وَبِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَدْرُسُونَ﴾ حَقٌ عَلَى مَنْ تَعَلَّمَ الْقُرْآنَ أَنْ يَكُونَ فَقيهًا: "تَعْلَمُون" أَيْ: تُفَهِّمُونَ مَعْنَاهُ. وَقُرِئَ ﴿تُعَلِّمُون﴾ بِالتَّشْدِيدِ مِنَ التَّعْلِيمِ ﴿وَبِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَدْرُسُونَ﴾ تحفظون ألفاظه.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَلا يَأْمُرَكُمْ أَنْ تَتَّخِذُوا الْمَلائِكَةَ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ أَرْبَابًا﴾ أَيْ: وَلَا يَأْمُرُكُمْ بِعِبَادَةِ أَحَدٍ غَيْرِ اللَّهِ، لَا نَبِيٍّ مُرْسَلٍ وَلَا مَلَكٍ مُقَرَّب ﴿أَيَأْمُرُكُمْ بِالْكُفْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ﴾ أَيْ: لَا يَفْعَل ذَلِكَ؛ لأنَّ مَنْ دَعَا إِلَى عِبَادَةِ غَيْرِ اللَّهِ فَقَدْ دَعَا إِلَى الْكَفْرِ، وَالْأَنْبِيَاءُ إِنَّمَا يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْإِيمَانِ، وَهُوَ عِبَادَةُ اللَّهِ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلا نُوحِي إِلَيْهِ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا أَنَا فَاعْبُدُونِ﴾ [الْأَنْبِيَاءِ:٢٥] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلَقَدْ بَعَثْنَا فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولا أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَاجْتَنِبُوا الطَّاغُوتَ﴾ الْآيَةَ، [النَّحْلِ:٣٦] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى ﴿وَاسْأَلْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رُسُلِنَا أَجَعَلْنَا مِنْ دُونِ الرَّحْمَنِ آلِهَةً يُعْبَدُونَ﴾ [الزُّخْرُفِ:٤٥] وَقَالَ [تَعَالَى] إِخْبَارًا عَنِ الْمَلَائِكَةِ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَقُلْ مِنْهُمْ إِنِّي إِلَهٌ مِنْ دُونِهِ فَذَلِكَ نَجْزِيهِ جَهَنَّمَ كَذَلِكَ نَجْزِي الظَّالِمِينَ﴾ [الْأَنْبِيَاءِ:٢٩] .

---

## 3:81-82 — verses 81-82 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty informs that He took the covenant of every prophet He sent, from Adam (peace be upon him) to Jesus (peace be upon him), that whatever Book and wisdom God gave any one of them, and to whatever degree he attained, then if a messenger came to him afterward, he must believe in him and support him. What he possessed of knowledge and prophethood must not prevent him from following the one sent after him and supporting him. Therefore the Blessed and Exalted said: {And when God took the covenant of the prophets, saying: Whatever I have given you of the Book and wisdom}, meaning: whatever I give you of the Book and wisdom, {and then there comes to you a messenger confirming what is with you, you must believe in him and you must support him. He said: Do you affirm this and take on that basis My covenant (isri)?}

Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, al-Rabi', Qatadah, and al-Suddi said: it means My covenant.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said: {isri} means the weight of what you have been made to bear of My covenant, that is, My binding and confirmed pact.

{They said: We affirm it. He said: Then bear witness, and I am with you among the witnesses. Then whoever turns away after that}, meaning, from this pact and covenant, {those are the transgressors (fasiqun)}.

Ali ibn Abi Talib and his cousin Abdullah ibn Abbas, may God be pleased with both of them, said: God never sent a prophet from among the prophets except that the covenant was taken from him: that if Muhammad were sent while he was alive, he must believe in him and support him. And he was commanded to take the covenant from his community: that if Muhammad [ﷺ] were sent while they were alive, they must believe in him and support him.

Tawus, al-Hasan al-Basri, and Qatadah said: God took the covenant of the prophets that they would confirm one another as true.

This does not contradict or negate what Ali and Ibn Abbas said, rather it necessarily implies and requires it. This is why Abd al-Razzaq narrated it, from Ma'mar, from Ibn Tawus, from his father, in the same wording as the statement of Ali and Ibn Abbas.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from Abdullah ibn Thabit, who said: Umar came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: O Messenger of God, I passed by a brother of mine from the Banu Qurayzah, and he wrote for me comprehensive passages from the Torah. Shall I present them to you? He said: the face of the Messenger of God ﷺ changed. Abdullah ibn Thabit said: I said to him, do you not see what has come over the face of the Messenger of God ﷺ? So Umar said: We are content with God as Lord, with Islam as religion, and with Muhammad as messenger. He said: then the distress was lifted from the Messenger of God ﷺ, and he said: "By Him in whose hand is the soul of Muhammad, if Moses (peace be upon him) were to appear among you this morning, and you were to follow him and abandon me, you would go astray. You are my portion among the nations, and I am your portion among the prophets."

Another hadith: al-Hafiz Abu Bakr said: [chain] from Jabir, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Do not ask the People of the Book about anything, for they will never guide you, and they have themselves gone astray. You would either affirm falsehood or deny truth. And by God, if Moses were alive among you, it would not be lawful for him to do anything but follow me."

In some of the hadiths of his it reads: "If Moses and Jesus were alive, it would not be permissible for either of them except to follow me."

So the Messenger Muhammad is the seal of the prophets ﷺ always until the Day of Judgment, and he is the supreme leader (al-imam al-a'zam) who, were he to be found in any age, would be the one whose obedience is obligatory, given precedence over all the prophets. This is why he was their leader on the Night of the Night Journey (al-Isra) when they gathered at Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis). Likewise he is the intercessor on the Day of Gathering when the Lord comes to render the final judgment, and he holds the Praised Station (al-Maqam al-Mahmud), which befits none but him, from which the resolute prophets and messengers (ulu al-'azm) turn away, until the turn comes round to him, and he alone is singled out for it.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى أَنَّهُ أَخَذَ مِيثَاقَ كُلِّ نَبِيٍّ بَعَثَهُ مِنْ لَدُنْ آدَمَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، إِلَى عِيسَى، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، لَمَهْمَا آتَى اللَّهُ أحدَهم مِنْ كِتَابٍ وَحِكْمَةٍ، وَبَلَغَ أَيَّ مبلَغ، ثُمَّ جَاءَهُ رَسُولٌ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ، ليؤمنَنَّ بِهِ ولينصرَنَّه، وَلَا يَمْنَعْهُ مَا هُوَ فِيهِ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ وَالنُّبُوَّةِ مِنَ اتِّبَاعِ مَنْ بُعِثَ بَعْدَهُ وَنُصْرَتِهِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى وَتَقَدَّسَ: ﴿وَإِذْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ النَّبِيِّينَ لَمَا آتَيْتُكُمْ مِنْ كِتَابٍ وَحِكْمَةٍ﴾ أَيْ: لَمَهْمَا أَعْطَيْتُكُمْ مِنْ كِتَابٍ وَحِكْمَةٍ ﴿ثُمَّ جَاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مُصَدِّقٌ لِمَا مَعَكُمْ لَتُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهِ وَلَتَنْصُرُنَّهُ قَالَ أَأَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَخَذْتُمْ عَلَى ذَلِكُمْ إِصْرِي﴾

وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٌ، وَالرَّبِيعُ، وَقَتَادَةُ، وَالسُّدِّيُّ: يَعْنِي عَهْدِي.

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ: ﴿إِصْرِي﴾ أَيْ: ثَقُلَ مَا حمّلْتم مِنْ عَهْدِي، أَيْ مِيثَاقِي الشَّدِيدِ الْمُؤَكَّدِ.

﴿قَالُوا أَقْرَرْنَا قَالَ فَاشْهَدُوا وَأَنَا مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ. فَمَنْ تَوَلَّى بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ﴾ أَيْ: عَنْ هَذَا الْعَهْدِ وَالْمِيثَاقِ، ﴿فَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ﴾

قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ وَابْنُ عَمِّهِ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: مَا بَعَثَ اللَّهُ نَبِيًّا مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ إِلَّا أُخِذَ عَلَيْهِ الْمِيثَاقُ، لئن بَعَث محمدًا وَهُوَ حَيّ لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ بِهِ وَلَيَنْصُرَنَّهُ، وأمَرَه أَنْ يَأْخُذَ الْمِيثَاقَ عَلَى أُمَّتِهِ: لَئِنْ بُعِثَ مُحَمَّدٌ [ﷺ] وَهُمْ أَحْيَاءٌ ليؤمِنُنَّ به ولينصرُنَّه.

وقال طاووس، وَالْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ، وَقَتَادَةُ: أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ النَّبِيِّينَ أَنْ يَصْدُقَ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا.

وَهَذَا لَا يُضَادُّ مَا قَالَهُ عَلِيٌّ وَابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَلَا يَنْفِيهِ، بَلْ يَسْتَلْزِمُهُ وَيَقْتَضِيهِ. وَلِهَذَا رَوَاهُ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عن مَعْمَر، عن ابن طاووس، عَنْ أَبِيهِ مِثْلَ قَوْلِ عَلِيٍّ وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، أَنْبَأَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ قَالَ: جَاءَ عُمَرُ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنِّي مررتُ بأخٍ لِي مِنْ قُرَيْظَة، فَكَتَبَ لِي جَوَامعَ مِنَ التَّوْرَاةِ، أَلَا أَعْرِضُهَا عَلَيْكَ؟ قَالَ: فتغيَّرَ وَجْهُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ -قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: أَلَا تَرَى مَا بِوَجْهِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ؟ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: رَضِينَا بِاللَّهِ رِبَّا، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ رَسُولًا -قَالَ: فسُرِّيَ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَقَالَ: "وَالَّذِي نَفْسُ مُحَمَّدٍ بِيَدِهِ لَوْ أَصْبَحَ فِيكُمْ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، ثُمَّ اتَّبَعْتُمُوهُ وَتَرَكْتُمُونِي لَضَلَلْتُمْ، إِنَّكُمْ حَظِّي مِنْ الأمَمِ، وَأَنَا حَظُّكُمْ مِنْ النَّبِيِّينَ" .

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادٌ، عَنْ مُجالد، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَا تَسْأَلُوا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ عَنْ شَيْءٍ، فَإِنَّهُمْ لَنْ يَهْدُوكُمْ وَقَدْ ضَلُّوا، وَإِنَّكُمْ إِمَّا أَنْ تُصَدِّقُوا بِبَاطِلٍ وَإِمَّا أنْ تُكَذِّبُوا بِحَقٍّ، وَإِنَّه -واللهِ-لَوْ كَانَ مُوسَى حَيًّا بَيْنَ أَظْهُرِكُمْ مَا حَلَّ لَهُ إِلا أَنْ يَتَّبِعَنِي" .

وَفِي بَعْضِ الْأَحَادِيثِ [لَهُ] :"لَوْ كَانَ مُوسَى وَعِيسَى حَيَّينِ لَمَا وَسِعَهُما إِلَّا اتِّباعِي" .

فَالرَّسُولُ مُحَمَّدٌ خَاتَمُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ، دَائِمًا إِلَى يَوْمِ الدِّينِ، وَهُوَ الْإِمَامُ الْأَعْظَمُ الَّذِي لَوْ وُجِدَ فِي أَيِّ عَصْرٍ وَجِدَ لَكَانَ هُوَ الْوَاجِبُ الطَّاعَةِ المقدَّم عَلَى الْأَنْبِيَاءِ كُلِّهِمْ؛ وَلِهَذَا كَانَ إِمَامَهُمْ لَيْلَةَ الْإِسْرَاءِ لَمَّا اجْتَمَعُوا بِبَيْتِ الْمَقْدِسِ، وَكَذَلِكَ هُوَ الشَّفِيعُ فِي يَوْمِ الْحَشْرِ فِي إِتْيَانِ الرَّبِّ لِفَصْل الْقَضَاءِ، وَهُوَ الْمَقَامُ الْمَحْمُودُ الَّذِي لَا يَلِيقُ إِلَّا لَهُ، وَالَّذِي يَحِيدُ عَنْهُ أُولُو الْعَزْمِ مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَالْمُرْسَلِينَ، حَتَّى تَنْتَهِيَ النَّوْبَةُ إليه، فيكونَ هو المخصوص به.

---

## 3:83-85 — verses 83-85 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty says, disavowing whoever seeks a religion other than the religion of God, the religion with which He sent down His books and sent His messengers, which is worshipping Him alone without partner, He of whom {and to Him submitted whoever is in the heavens and the earth}, meaning: whoever is in them submitted to Him, willingly or unwillingly, as the Almighty said: {And to God prostrates whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly, and their shadows in the mornings and the evenings} [al-Ra'd: 15]. And the Almighty said: {Have they not seen how everything God has created casts its shadow to the right and to the left, prostrating to God in humility? And to God prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth of moving creatures, and the angels, and they are not arrogant. They fear their Lord above them and they do what they are commanded} [al-Nahl: 48-50].

So the believer submits to God with his heart and his outward form, while the disbeliever submits to God unwillingly, for he is under compulsion, subjugation, and the tremendous dominion which cannot be opposed or resisted. A hadith has been related in the exegesis of this verse with another meaning in which there is gharabah (a singular, unusual quality), for the Hafiz Abu al-Qasim al-Tabarani said:

[chain] from Ata' ibn Abi Rabah, from the Prophet ﷺ regarding {and to Him submitted whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly}: "As for whoever is in the heavens, they are the angels. As for whoever is in the earth, it is whoever was born into Islam. As for 'unwillingly,' it is whoever is brought from among the captives of the nations in chains and shackles, being led to Paradise while they dislike it."

And it has been related in the Sahih: "Your Lord marvels at a people who are led to Paradise in chains." A corroborating report will come by another route, but the first meaning of the verse is stronger.

Waki' said in his tafsir: [chain] from Mujahid, regarding {and to Him submitted whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly}, he said: it is like His saying: {And if you asked them who created the heavens and the earth, they would surely say: God} [Luqman: 25].

He also said: [chain] from Mujahid, from Ibn Abbas, regarding {and to Him submitted whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly}, he said: at the time the covenant was taken.

{And to Him they will be returned}, meaning: on the Day of Return, when He will requite each according to his deed.

Then the Almighty said: {Say: We believe in God and what has been sent down to us}, meaning the Qur'an, {and what was sent down to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, and Jacob}, meaning, of scrolls and revelation, {and the tribes}, who are the branches of the Children of Israel descended from the twelve sons of Israel, that is, Jacob (peace be upon him). {And what was given to Moses and Jesus}, meaning by that the Torah and the Gospel, {and the prophets from their Lord}, and this includes all the prophets collectively. {We make no distinction between any of them}, meaning: rather we believe in all of them. {And we are submitted to Him}. So the believers of this ummah believe in every prophet who was sent, and in every book that was sent down; they do not disbelieve in any of that, but rather they affirm what was sent down from God and every prophet God raised up.

Then the Almighty said: {And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it will never be accepted from him}, meaning: whoever follows a path other than what God has legislated, it will never be accepted from him, {and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers}, as the Prophet ﷺ said in the sound hadith: "Whoever does a deed that is not in accordance with our affair, it is rejected."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Sa'id, the freedman of Banu Hashim, told us, Abbad ibn Rashid told us, al-Hasan told us, Abu Hurayrah told us, at that time while we were in Medina, he said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "The deeds will come on the Day of Resurrection. Prayer will come and say: O Lord, I am prayer. He will say: You are indeed upon good. Then charity will come and say: O Lord, I am charity. He will say: You are indeed upon good. Then fasting will come and say: O Lord, I am fasting. He will say: You are indeed upon good. Then the deeds will come, and each time God the Almighty says: You are indeed upon good. Then Islam will come and say: O Lord, You are al-Salam (the Source of Peace) and I am Islam. God [the Almighty] will say: You are indeed upon good, by you today I take and by you I give. God said in His Book: {And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it will never be accepted from him, and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers}."

Ahmad alone related it. Abu Abd al-Rahman Abdullah, son of Imam Ahmad, said: Abbad ibn Rashid is trustworthy, but al-Hasan did not hear from Abu Hurayrah.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُنْكِرًا عَلَى مَنْ أَرَادَ دِينًا سِوَى دِينِ اللَّهِ، الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ بِهِ كتبَه وَأَرْسَلَ بِهِ رسلَه، وَهُوَ عِبَادَتُهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شريك له، الذي ﴿وَلَهُ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ﴾ أَيِ: اسْتَسْلَمَ لَهُ مِنْ فِيهِمَا طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا وَظِلالُهُمْ بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالآصَالِ﴾ [الرَّعْدِ:١٥] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَى مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ يَتَفَيَّأُ ظِلالُهُ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَالشَّمَائِلِ سُجَّدًا لِلَّهِ وَهُمْ دَاخِرُونَ. وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الأرْضِ مِنْ دَابَّةٍ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ. يَخَافُونَ رَبَّهُمْ مِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ مَا يُؤْمَرُونَ﴾ [النَّحْلِ: ٤٨ -٥٠] .

فَالْمُؤْمِنُ مُسْتَسْلِمٌ بِقَلْبِهِ وَقَالَبِهِ لِلَّهِ، وَالْكَافِرُ مُسْتَسْلِمٌ لِلَّهِ كَرْهَا، فَإِنَّهُ تَحْتَ التَّسْخِيرِ وَالْقَهْرِ وَالسُّلْطَانِ الْعَظِيمِ، الَّذِي لَا يُخَالِفُ وَلَا يُمَانِعُ. وَقَدْ وَرَدَ حَدِيثٌ فِي تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ، عَلَى مَعْنًى آخَرَ فِيهِ غَرَابَةٌ، فَقَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ الطَّبَرَانِيُّ:

حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ النَّضْرِ الْعَسْكَرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ حَفْصٍ النُّفَيْلي، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مِحْصَن الْعُكَّاشِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَوْزَاعِيُّ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي رَبَاحٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: ﴿وَلَهُ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا﴾ أمَّا مَنْ فِي السَّمَاواتِ فَالْمَلائِكَةُ، وأمَّا مَنْ فِي الأرضِ فَمَنْ وُلِدَ عَلَى الإسْلامِ، وأمَّا كَرْهًا فَمَنْ أُتِي بِهِ مِنْ سَبَايا الأمَمِ فِي السَّلاسِلِ والأغْلالِ، يُقَادُونَ إلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَهُمْ كَارِهُونَ" .

وَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي الصَّحِيحِ: "عَجِبَ رَبُّكَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ يُقَادُونَ إلَى الْجَنَّةِ فِي السَّلاسِل" وَسَيَأْتِي لَهُ شَاهِدٌ مِنْ وَجْهٍ آخَرَ وَلَكِنَّ الْمَعْنَى الْأَوَّلَ لِلْآيَةِ أَقْوَى.

وَقَدْ قَالَ وَكِيع فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: ﴿وَلَهُ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا﴾ قَالَ: هُوَ كَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ﴾ [لُقْمَانَ:٢٥] .

وَقَالَ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَلَهُ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا﴾ قَالَ: حين أخذ الميثاق.

﴿وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ﴾ أَيْ: يَوْمَ المَعَاد، فَيُجَازِي كُلًّا بِعَمَلِهِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَمَا أُنزلَ عَلَيْنَا﴾ يَعْنِي: الْقُرْآنَ ﴿وَمَا أُنزلَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ﴾ أَيْ: مِنَ الصُّحُفِ وَالْوَحْيِ: ﴿وَالأسْبَاطِ﴾ وَهُمْ بُطُونُ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ الْمُتَشَعِّبَةُ مِنْ أَوْلَادِ إِسْرَائِيلَ -هُوَ يَعْقُوبُ-الِاثْنَيْ عَشَرَ. ﴿وَمَا أُوتِيَ مُوسَى وَعِيسَى﴾ يَعْنِي: بِذَلِكَ التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ ﴿وَالنَّبِيُّونَ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ﴾ وَهَذَا يَعُم جميعَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ جُمْلَةً ﴿لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: بَلْ نُؤْمِنُ بِجَمِيعِهِمْ ﴿وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ﴾ فَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ أُرْسِلَ، وَبِكُلِّ كِتَابٍ أُنْزِلَ، لَا يَكْفُرُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ ذَلِكَ بَلْ هُمْ مُصَدِّقون بِمَا أُنْزِلَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ، وَبِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الإسْلامِ دِينًا فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ﴾ أَيْ: مَنْ سَلَكَ طَرِيقًا سِوَى مَا شَرَعَه اللَّهُ فَلَنْ يُقْبل مِنْهُ ﴿وَهُوَ فِي الآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ﴾ كَمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الصَّحِيحِ: "مَنْ عَمِلَ عَمَلا لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ أمْرُنَا فَهُوَ رَدٌّ".

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ مَوْلَى بَنِي هَاشِمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ، إِذْ ذَاكَ وَنَحْنُ بِالْمَدِينَةِ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "تَجِيءُ الأعْمَالُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، فَتَجِيءُ الصَّلاةُ فَتَقُولُ: يَا رَبِّ، أَنَا الصَّلاةُ. فَيَقُولُ: إِنَّكِ عَلَى خَيْرٍ. فَتَجِيءُ الصَّدَقَةُ فَتَقُولُ: يَا رَبِّ، أَنَا الصَّدَقَةُ. فَيَقُولُ: إِنَّكِ عَلَى خَيْرٍ. ثُمَّ يَجِيءُ الصِّيَامُ فَيَقُولُ: أَيْ يَا رَبِّ، أَنَا الصِّيَامُ. فَيَقُولُ: إِنَّكَ عَلَى خَيْرٍ. ثُمَّ تَجِيءُ الأعْمَالُ، كُل ذَلِكَ يَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: إِنَّكَ عَلَى خَيْرٍ، ثُمَّ يَجِيءُ الإسْلامُ فَيَقُولُ: يَا رَب، أَنْتَ السَّلامُ وَأَنَا الإسْلامُ. فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ [تَعَالَى] : إِنَّكَ عَلَى خَيْرٍ، بِكَ الْيَوْمَ آخُذُ وَبِكَ أُعْطِي، قَالَ اللَّهُ فِي كِتَابِهِ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الإسْلامِ دِينًا فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ وَهُوَ فِي الآخِرَةِ مِنْ الْخَاسِرِينَ﴾ .

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ. قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ: عَبَّادُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ ثِقَةٌ، وَلَكِنَّ الْحَسَنَ لَمْ يَسْمَعْ مِنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ .

---

## 3:86-89 — verses 86-89 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Ibn Jarir said: Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Bazi' al-Basri narrated to me, Yazid ibn Zurayi' narrated to us, Dawud ibn Abi Hind narrated to us, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: There was a man from the Ansar who accepted Islam, then apostatized and joined the polytheists, then he regretted it, so he sent word to his people saying, "Ask the Messenger of Allah ﷺ for me whether there is any repentance for me." He said: then this was revealed: {How would Allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief} up to His saying: {[Except those who repent after that and reform], for indeed Allah is Forgiving, Merciful}.

This is likewise related by al-Nasa'i, Ibn Hibban, and al-Hakim, through the route of Dawud ibn Abi Hind, with the same content. Al-Hakim said: its chain is sound, though the two [Bukhari and Muslim] did not include it.

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ja'far ibn Sulayman informed us, Humayd al-A'raj narrated to us, from Mujahid, who said: al-Harith ibn Suwayd came and accepted Islam along with the Prophet ﷺ, then al-Harith disbelieved and returned to his people. So Allah revealed concerning him: {How would Allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief} up to His saying: {[Except those who repent after that and reform, for indeed Allah is] Forgiving, Merciful}. He said: a man from his people brought this to him and recited it to him. Al-Harith said: "By Allah, as far as I know you are truthful, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ is more truthful than you, and Allah is the most truthful of the three." He said: so al-Harith went back and accepted Islam, and his Islam became good.

As for His saying, Exalted is He: {How would Allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief and testified that the Messenger is true, and clear proofs had come to them}, that is: the proofs and evidences had been established against them regarding the truthfulness of what the Messenger brought them, and the matter had been made clear to them, then they returned to the darkness of polytheism. So how could these people deserve guidance after having become clothed in this blindness? For this reason He said: {And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people}.

Then He said: {Those, their recompense is that upon them is the curse of Allah, the angels, and all the people}, that is: Allah curses them and His creation curses them, {abiding therein}, that is, in the curse, {the punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be reprieved}, that is: the punishment will not be relaxed for them, nor lightened for them for even a single hour.

Then He, Exalted is He, said: {Except those who repent after that and reform, for indeed Allah is Forgiving, Merciful}. This is part of His gentleness, His kindness, His compassion, His mercy, and His benevolent return toward His creation: that whoever repents to Him, He accepts his repentance.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنُ بَزِيعٍ الْبَصْرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ زُرَيع، حَدَّثَنَا دَاوُدُ بْنُ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ أَسْلَمَ ثُمَّ ارتد ولحق بالشرك، ثُمَّ نَدِمَ، فَأَرْسَلَ إِلَى قَوْمِهِ: أَنْ سَلُوا لِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: هَلْ لِي مِنْ تَوْبَةٍ؟ قَالَ: فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿كَيْفَ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ قَوْمًا كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿ [إِلا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ذَلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا] فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ﴾ .

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ، وَابْنُ حِبَّانَ، وَالْحَاكِمُ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، بِهِ. وَقَالَ الْحَاكِمُ: صَحِيحُ الْإِسْنَادِ وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ .

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَخْبَرَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيد الْأَعْرَجُ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: جَاءَ الْحَارِثُ بْنُ سُوَيد فَأَسْلَمَ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، ثُمَّ كَفَرَ الْحَارِثُ فَرَجَعَ إِلَى قَوْمِهِ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ: ﴿كَيْفَ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ قَوْمًا كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿ [إِلا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ذَلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ] غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ﴾ قَالَ: فَحَمَلَهَا إِلَيْهِ رَجُلٌ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ فَقَرَأَهَا عَلَيْهِ. فَقَالَ الْحَارِثُ: إِنَّكَ وَاللَّهِ مَا علمتُ لَصَدُوقٌ، وَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ لَأَصْدَقُ مِنْكَ، وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَأَصْدَقُ الثَّلَاثَةِ. قَالَ: فَرَجَعَ الْحَارِثُ فَأَسْلَمَ فحَسُنَ إِسْلَامُهُ .

فَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿كَيْفَ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ قَوْمًا كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ وَشَهِدُوا أَنَّ الرَّسُولَ حَقٌّ وَجَاءَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ﴾ أَيْ: قامتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الحُجَجُ وَالْبَرَاهِينُ عَلَى صِدْقِ مَا جَاءَهُمْ بِهِ الرسولُ، وَوَضَح لَهُمُ الأمرُ، ثُمَّ ارْتَدُّوا إِلَى ظُلْمة الشِّرْكِ، فَكَيْفَ يَسْتَحِقُّ هَؤُلَاءِ الْهِدَايَةَ بَعْدَ مَا تَلَبَّسُوا بِهِ مِنَ الْعَمَايَةِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿أُولَئِكَ جَزَاؤُهُمْ أَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةَ اللَّهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ﴾ أَيْ: يَلْعَنُهُمُ اللَّهُ وَيَلْعَنُهُمْ خَلْقُهُ ﴿خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا﴾ أَيْ: فِي اللَّعْنَةِ ﴿لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَلا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ﴾ أَيْ: لَا يُفتَّر عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَلَا يُخَفَّف عَنْهُمْ سَاعَةً وَاحِدَةً.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِلا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ذَلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ﴾ وَهَذَا مِنْ لُطْفِهِ وَبِرِّهِ وَرَأْفَتِهِ وَرَحْمَتِهِ وَعَائِدَتِهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ: أَنَّهُ مَنْ تَابَ إِلَيْهِ تَابَ عَلَيْهِ.

---

## 3:90-91 — verses 90-91 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted speaks in threat and warning against whoever disbelieves after his faith, then increases in disbelief, meaning he persists in it until death, informing that He will not accept repentance from them at the time of their death, just as He [the Exalted] said: {And repentance is not for those who do evil deeds until, when death comes to one of them, he says, "Indeed, I repent now," nor is it for those who die while they are disbelievers. Those We have prepared for them a painful punishment} [al-Nisa: 18].

For this reason He says here: {Never will their repentance be accepted, and those are the ones astray}, meaning those who have departed from the true path onto the path of error.
