The Hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar said: [chain] Ibn Abbas reported that a group had embraced Islam, then apostatized, then embraced Islam again, then apostatized again, and they sent word to their people asking on their behalf. This was mentioned to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and this verse was revealed: {Indeed, those who disbelieved after their faith, then increased in disbelief, never will their repentance be accepted}. Thus he narrated it, and its chain is good.

Then He says: {Indeed, those who disbelieved and died while they were disbelievers, never will an earth's fill of gold be accepted from any of them, even if he were to offer it in ransom}, meaning: whoever dies upon disbelief, no good deed will ever be accepted from him, even if he had spent an earth's fill of gold in what he regarded as an act of nearness to Allah. This is like when the Prophet ﷺ was asked about Abd Allah ibn Jud'an, who used to host guests, free captives, and feed the hungry: would that benefit him? He said: "No, for he never once said in his lifetime, 'My Lord, forgive me my sin on the Day of Judgment.'"

Likewise, even if he were to offer an earth's fill of gold in ransom, it would not be accepted from him, just as the Exalted said: {And no compensation will be accepted from it, nor will any intercession benefit it} [al-Baqarah: 123], and He said: {There is no bargaining in it, nor any friendship, nor any intercession} [al-Baqarah: 254], and He said: {There is no bargaining in it, nor any friendship} [Ibrahim: 31], and He said: {Indeed, those who disbelieved, if they had all that is on the earth and the like of it with it to ransom themselves from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection, it would not be accepted from them, and for them is a painful punishment} [al-Ma'idah: 36]. For this reason the Exalted says here: {Indeed, those who disbelieved and died while they were disbelievers, never will an earth's fill of gold be accepted from any of them, even if he were to offer it in ransom}. He joined {even if he were to offer it in ransom} to the first clause with a conjunction, which indicates that it is distinct from it. What we have mentioned is better than saying that the conjunction "wa" (and) is superfluous, and Allah knows best. This indicates that nothing will save him from Allah's punishment, even if he had spent the equivalent of the earth in gold, and even if he were to ransom himself from Allah with an earth's fill of gold, by the weight of its mountains and its hills, its dust and its sands, its plains and its rugged terrain, its land and its sea.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Anas ibn Malik reported from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "It will be said to the man among the people of the Fire on the Day of Resurrection: 'What do you think, if you had everything on the earth, would you ransom yourself with it?' He will say: 'Yes.' He will say: 'I asked of you something easier than that. I took a covenant from you, in the loins of your father Adam, that you would not associate anything with Me, but you refused and persisted in associating partners with Me.'" Thus al-Bukhari and Muslim both narrated it.

Another route: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Anas said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "A man from the people of Paradise will be brought and it will be said to him: 'O son of Adam, how did you find your dwelling?' He will say: 'O Lord, the best of dwellings.' It will be said: 'Ask and wish.' He will say: 'I do not ask nor wish for anything except that You return me to the world so that I may be killed in Your path ten times,' because of what he sees of the merit of martyrdom. And a man from the people of the Fire will be brought and it will be said to him: 'O son of Adam, how did you find your dwelling?' He will say: 'O Lord, the worst of dwellings.' It will be said to him: 'Would you ransom yourself from Me with an earth's fill of gold?' He will say: 'O Lord, yes.' He will say: 'You lie. I asked of you something less than that and easier, but you did not do it.' Then he will be returned to the Fire."

For this reason He says: {Those, for them is a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers}, meaning: they will have no one to rescue them from Allah's punishment, nor to shield them from His painful chastisement.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُتَوَعِّدًا ومتهدِّدًا لِمَنْ كَفَرَ بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِ ثُمَّ ازْدَادَ كُفْرًا، أَيِ: اسْتَمَرَّ عَلَيْهِ إِلَى الْمَمَاتِ، وَمُخْبِرًا بِأَنَّهُ لَا يَقْبَلُ لَهُمْ تَوْبَةً عِنْدَ مَمَاتِهِمْ، كَمَا قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿وَلَيْسَتِ التَّوْبَةُ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السَّيِّئَاتِ حَتَّى إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَهُمُ الْمَوْتُ [قَالَ إِنِّي تُبْتُ الآنَ وَلا الَّذِينَ يَمُوتُونَ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ أُولَئِكَ أَعْتَدْنَا لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا] ﴾ [النساء:١٨] .

وَلِهَذَا قَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿لَنْ تُقْبَلَ تَوْبَتُهُمْ وَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الضَّالُّونَ﴾ أَيِ: الْخَارِجُونَ عَنِ الْمَنْهَجِ الْحَقِّ إِلَى طَرِيقِ الغَيِّ.

قَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَزِيع، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ زُرَيع، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ؛ أَنَّ قَوْمًا أَسْلَمُوا ثُمَّ ارْتَدُّوا، ثُمَّ أَسْلَمُوا ثُمَّ ارْتَدُّوا، فَأَرْسَلُوا إِلَى قَوْمِهِمْ يَسْأَلُونَ لَهُمْ، فَذَكَرُوا ذَلِكَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ ثُمَّ ازْدَادُوا كُفْرًا لَنْ تُقْبَلَ تَوْبَتُهُمْ﴾ هَكَذَا رَوَاهُ، وَإِسْنَادُهُ جَيِّدٌ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَمَاتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِمْ مِلْءُ الأرْضِ ذَهَبًا وَلَوِ افْتَدَى بِهِ﴾ أَيْ: مَنْ مَاتَ عَلَى الْكُفْرِ فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ خَيْرٌ أَبَدًا، وَلَوْ كَانَ قَدْ أَنْفَقَ مِلْءَ الْأَرْضِ ذَهَبَا فِيمَا يَرَاهُ قُرْبة، كَمَا سُئِلَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جُدْعان -وَكَانَ يُقْرِي الضيفَ، ويَفُكُّ الْعَانِي، ويُطعم الطَّعَامَ-: هَلْ يَنْفَعُهُ ذَلِكَ؟ فَقَالَ: لَا إِنَّهُ لَمْ يَقُلْ يَوْمًا مِن الدَّهْرِ: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي خَطِيئَتِي يَوْمَ الدِّينِ .

وَكَذَلِكَ لَوِ افْتَدَى بِمِلْءِ الْأَرْضِ أَيْضًا ذَهَبًا مَا قُبِلَ مِنْهُ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا يُقْبَلُ مِنْهَا عَدْلٌ وَلا تَنْفَعُهَا شَفَاعَةٌ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ:١٢٣] ، [وَقَالَ ﴿لَا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلا خُلَّةٌ وَلا شَفَاعَةٌ﴾ ] [الْبَقَرَةِ:٢٥٤] وَقَالَ: ﴿لَا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلا خِلالٌ﴾ [إِبْرَاهِيمَ: ٣١] وَقَالَ ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ أَنَّ لَهُمْ مَا فِي الأرْضِ جَمِيعًا وَمِثْلَهُ مَعَهُ لِيَفْتَدُوا بِهِ مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ مَا تُقُبِّلَ مِنْهُمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ﴾ [الْمَائِدَةِ:٣٦] ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى هَاهُنَا: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَمَاتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِمْ مِلْءُ الأرْضِ ذَهَبًا وَلَوِ افْتَدَى بِهِ﴾ فَعَطَفَ ﴿وَلَوِ افْتَدَى بِهِ﴾ عَلَى الْأَوَّلِ، فَدَلَّ عَلَى أَنَّهُ غَيْرُهُ، وَمَا ذَكَرْنَاهُ أَحْسَنُ مِنْ أَنْ يُقَالَ: إِنَّ الْوَاوَ زَائِدَةٌ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. وَيَقْتَضِي ذَلِكَ أَلَّا يُنْقِذَهُ مِنْ عَذَابِ اللَّهِ شَيْءٌ، وَلَوْ كَانَ قَدْ أَنْفَقَ مِثْلَ الْأَرْضِ ذَهَبَا، وَلَوِ افْتَدَى نَفْسَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ بِمِلْءِ الْأَرْضِ ذَهَبَا، بوَزْن جِبالها وتِلالها وتُرابها ورِمَالها وسَهْلها ووعْرِها وبَرِّها وبَحْرِها.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا حجَّاج، حَدَّثَنِي شُعْبَة، عَنْ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ الجَوْني، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: " يُقَالُ لِلرَّجُلِ مِنْ أَهْلِ النَّارِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ: أَرَأَيْتَ لَوْ كَانَ لَكَ مَا عَلَى الأرْضِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ، أَكُنْتَ مُفْتَدِيًا بِهِ؟ قَالَ: فَيَقُولُ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: فَيَقُولُ: قَدْ أَرَدْتُ مِنْكَ أَهْوَنَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، قَدْ أَخَذْتُ عَلَيْكَ فِي ظَهْرِ أَبِيكَ آدَمَ أَلَّا تُشْرِكَ بِي شَيْئًا، فَأَبَيْتَ إِلا أَنْ تُشْرِكَ ". وَهَكَذَا أَخْرَجَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ، وَمُسْلِمٌ .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْح، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّاد، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يُؤْتَى بِالرَّجُلِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ فَيَقُولُ لَهُ: يَا ابْنَ آدَمَ، كَيْفَ وَجَدْتَ مَنزلَكَ؟ فَيَقُولُ: أَيْ رَبِّ، خَيْرُ مَنزلٍ. فَيَقُولُ: سَلْ وَتَمَنَّ. فَيَقُولُ: مَا أَسْأَلُ وَلا أَتَمَنَّى إِلا أَنْ تَرُدَّنِي إِلَى الدُّنْيَا فَأُقْتَلَ فِي سَبِيلِكَ عَشْرَ مِرَار -لِمَا يَرَى مِنْ فَضْلِ الشَّهَادَةِ. وَيُؤْتَى بِالرَّجُلِ مِنْ أَهْلِ النَّارِ فَيَقُولُ لَهُ: يَا ابْنَ آدَمَ، كَيْفَ وَجَدْتَ مَنزلَكَ؟ فَيَقُولُ: يَا رَبِّ شَرُّ مَنزلٍ. فَيَقُولُ لَهُ: تَفْتَدِي مِني بِطِلاعِ الأرْضِ ذَهَبًا؟ فَيَقُولُ: أَيْ رَبِّ، نَعَمْ. فَيَقُولُ: كَذَبْتَ، قَدْ سَأَلْتُكَ أَقَلَّ مِنْ ذَلِكَ وَأَيْسَرَ فَلَمْ تَفْعَلْ، فيُرَد إِلَى النَّارِ" .

وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿أُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنْ نَاصِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنْ أَحَدٍ يُنْقِذهم مِنْ عَذَابِ اللَّهِ، ولا يجيرهم من أليم عقابه.

---

## 3:92 — verse 92

### Translation

[Waki' narrated in his tafsir, from Sharik, from Abu Ishaq, from Amr ibn Maymun, regarding {you will never attain righteousness (al-birr)} that he said: al-birr here means Paradise.]

Imam Ahmad said: Rawh narrated to us, Malik narrated to us, from Ishaq ibn Abdullah ibn Abi Talha, who heard Anas ibn Malik say: Abu Talha was the wealthiest of the Ansar in Medina in property, and the dearest of his properties to him was Bayruha, which faced the mosque, and the Prophet ﷺ used to enter it and drink from its pure water. Anas said: When {you will never attain righteousness until you spend from what you love} was revealed, Abu Talha said: O Messenger of Allah, indeed Allah says {you will never attain righteousness until you spend from what you love}, and the dearest of my properties to me is Bayruha, and it is a charity for the sake of Allah. I hope for its righteousness and its reward stored up with Allah, the Exalted, so place it, O Messenger of Allah, wherever Allah shows you. The Prophet ﷺ said: "Wonderful! That is profitable wealth, that is profitable wealth. I have heard what you said, and I think you should give it to your close relatives." Abu Talha said: I will do so, O Messenger of Allah. So Abu Talha distributed it among his relatives and the sons of his paternal uncle. Both [Bukhari and Muslim] narrated it.

In the two Sahihs it is reported that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said: O Messenger of Allah, I have never acquired any property more precious to me than my share in Khaybar. What do you command me to do with it? He said: "Hold the principal and give the produce as charity."

The hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar said: Abu al-Khattab Ziyad ibn Yahya al-Hassani narrated to us, Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Amr narrated to us, from Abu Amr ibn Hammas, from Hamza ibn Abdullah ibn Umar, who said: Abdullah said: This verse came to my mind: {you will never attain righteousness until you spend from what you love}. So I considered what Allah had given me, and I found nothing dearer to me than a Roman slave girl. So I said: She is free for the sake of Allah. Had I been able to take back something I had given for Allah's sake, I would have married her, meaning I would have taken her as a wife.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

[رَوَى وَكِيع فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ عَنْ شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ ﴿لَنْ تَنَالُوا الْبِرَّ﴾ قَالَ: الْبِرُّ الْجَنَّةُ] وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْحٌ، حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكٌ، عَنْ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، سَمِعَ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ يَقُولُ: كَانَ أَبُو طَلْحَةَ أَكْثَرَ أَنْصَارِيٍّ بِالْمَدِينَةِ مَالًا وكانَ أحبَّ أَمْوَالِهِ إِلَيْهِ بيْرَحاءُ -وَكَانَتْ مُسْتقْبلة الْمَسْجِدِ، وَكَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَدْخُلُهَا وَيَشْرَبُ مِنْ مَاءٍ فِيهَا طَيِّبٍ-قَالَ أَنَسٌ: فَلَمَّا نَزَلَتْ: ﴿لَنْ تَنَالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ﴾ قَالَ أَبُو طَلْحَةَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿لَنْ تَنَالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ﴾ وَإِنَّ أحبَّ أَمْوَالِي إلَيَّ بيْرَحاءُ وَإِنَّهَا صَدَقَةٌ لِلَّهِ أَرْجُو بِرَّها وذُخْرَها عِنْدَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى، فَضَعْها يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ حَيْثُ أَرَاكَ اللَّهُ [تَعَالَى] فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "بَخٍ، ذَاكَ مَالٌ رَابِحٌ، ذَاكَ مَالٌ رَابِح، وَقَدْ سَمِعْتُ، وَأَنَا أرَى أنْ تجْعَلَهَا فِي الأقْرَبِينَ". فَقَالَ أَبُو طَلْحَةَ: أفْعَلُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ. فَقَسَمها أَبُو طَلْحَةَ فِي أَقَارِبِهِ وَبَنِي عَمِّهِ. أَخْرَجَاهُ .

وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ أَنَّ عُمَر [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، لَمْ أُصِبْ مَالًا قطُّ هُوَ أنْفَسُ عِنْدِي مِنْ سَهْمِي الَّذِي هُوَ بِخَيْبَرَ، فَمَا تَأْمُرُنِي بِهِ؟ قَالَ حَبِّس الأصْل وسَبِّل الثَّمَرَةَ" .

وَقَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْخَطَّابِ زِيَادُ بْنُ يَحْيَى الحَساني، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عمْرو، عَنْ أَبِي عَمْرِو بْنِ حَماس عَنْ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمر، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: حَضَرَتْنِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿لَنْ تَنَالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ﴾ فذكرتُ مَا أَعْطَانِي اللَّهُ، فَلَمْ أَجِدْ شَيْئًا أحبَّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ جَارِيَةٍ رُوميَّة، فقلتُ، هِيَ حُرَّة لِوَجْهِ اللَّهِ. فَلَوْ أنِّي أَعُودُ فِي شَيْءٍ جَعَلْتُهُ لِلَّهِ لنكَحْتُها، يَعْنِي تَزوَّجتُها .

---

## 3:93-95 — verses 93-95 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Imam Ahmad said: Hashim ibn al-Qasim narrated to us, Abd al-Hamid narrated to us, Shahr said: Ibn Abbas [may Allah be pleased with him] said: A group of Jews came to the Prophet of Allah ﷺ and said, "Tell us about matters we will ask you about, which no one knows except a prophet." He said, "Ask me whatever you wish, but grant me the covenant of Allah, and what Jacob took from his sons, that if I tell you something and you recognize it as true, you will surely follow me in Islam." They said, "That is granted to you." He said, "So ask me whatever you wish." They said, "Tell us about four matters. Tell us what food Israel forbade for himself. Tell us how is the water of the woman and the water of the man? How is it with this unlettered prophet in sleep? And who is his patron among the angels?" So he took a pledge from them that if he told them, they would surely follow him, and he said: "I put you under oath by the One who sent down the Torah to Moses: do you know that Israel fell severely ill and his illness lasted long, so he made a vow to Allah that if Allah healed him of his illness he would forbid himself the drink most beloved to him and the food most beloved to him, and the food most beloved to him was the meat of camels, and the drink most beloved to him was their milk?" They said, "By Allah, yes." He said, "O Allah, bear witness against them." Then he said, "I put you under oath by Allah, besides whom there is no god, who sent down the Torah to Moses: do you know that the water of the man is white and thick, and the water of the woman is yellow and thin, and whichever of them prevails, to it belongs the child and the resemblance, by Allah's permission. If the water of the man prevails over the water of the woman, the child will be male by Allah's permission, and if the water of the woman prevails over the water of the man, the child will be female by Allah's permission." They said, "Yes." He said, "O Allah, bear witness against them." Then he said, "I put you under oath by the One who sent down the Torah to Moses: do you know that this unlettered prophet's eyes sleep but his heart does not sleep?" They said, "By Allah, yes." He said, "O Allah, bear witness." They said, "Now tell us, who is your patron among the angels? For upon that we will either join you or part from you." He said, "My patron is Gabriel, and Allah has never sent a prophet without Gabriel being his patron." They said, "Then upon this we part from you. Had your patron been someone else, we would have followed you." At that point Allah, Exalted is He, said: {Say, whoever is an enemy to Gabriel} [al-Baqarah: 97].

Ahmad also narrated it from Husayn ibn Muhammad, from Abd al-Hamid, with the same content.

Another route: Ahmad said: Abu Ahmad al-Zubayri narrated to us, Abdullah ibn al-Walid al-Ijli narrated to us, from Bukayr ibn Shihab, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: The Jews came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said, "O Abu al-Qasim, we ask you about five things. If you tell us about them, we will know that you are a prophet and we will follow you." So he took from them the same pledge that Israel took from his sons when he said: {Allah is guarantor over what we say} [Yusuf: 66]. He said, "Bring forth your questions." They said, "Tell us about the sign of the prophet." He said, "His eyes sleep but his heart does not sleep." They said, "Tell us how a woman conceives a female and how a male." He said, "The two waters meet, and if the water of the man prevails over the water of the woman, it becomes a male, and if the water of the woman prevails over the water of the man, it becomes a female." They said, "Tell us what Israel forbade for himself." He said, "He suffered from sciatica, and found nothing that suited him except the milk of such and such," Ahmad said: some of them said this meant camels, "so he forbade their meat." They said, "You have spoken the truth." They said, "Tell us, what is this thunder?" He said, "An angel among the angels of Allah charged with the clouds, in his hand, or in his hand is a whip of fire with which he drives the clouds, directing them wherever Allah, Mighty and Majestic, commands him." They said, "Then what is this sound that is heard?" He said, "It is his voice." They said, "You have spoken truly. Only one thing remains, and it is the one on account of which we will follow you if you tell us about it, for there is no prophet but has an angel who brings him news. So tell us, who is your companion?" He said, "Gabriel, peace be upon him." They said, "That Gabriel descends with war and fighting and punishment, he is our enemy. Had you said Michael, who descends with mercy and vegetation and rain, it would have been different." Then Allah, Mighty and Majestic, sent down: {Say, whoever is an enemy to Gabriel, then indeed he brought it down upon your heart by Allah's permission, confirming what came before it, and as guidance and good tidings for the believers} [al-Baqarah: 97].

Al-Tirmidhi and al-Nasa'i narrated it from the hadith of Abdullah ibn al-Walid al-Ijli, with similar content, and al-Tirmidhi said: hasan gharib (sound, singular in transmission).

Ibn Jurayj and al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas: Israel, who is Jacob (peace be upon him), used to be afflicted by sciatica at night, which would trouble him and disturb his sleep, and the pain would leave him during the day. So he made a vow to Allah that if Allah restored him to health, he would not eat any meat with a sinew, nor eat the offspring of any animal that has sinews.

Al-Dahhak and al-Suddi said likewise. This is what Ibn Jarir related and narrated in his tafsir. He said: his sons followed him in forbidding this, taking his practice as a model and following his path. He said: as for His statement, {before the Torah was sent down}, meaning: he forbade this for himself before the Torah was sent down.

I say: this passage, following what preceded it, has two points of connection.

The first: that Israel, peace be upon him, forbade himself the things most beloved to him and gave them up for the sake of Allah, and this was permissible in their law. So there is a connection with what came before, His statement: {You will never attain righteousness until you spend from what you love}. This is what is prescribed for us as well, namely spending in obedience to Allah from what the servant loves and desires, as He said: {and gives wealth despite his love for it} [al-Baqarah: 177], and He said: {and they feed the food despite their love for it} [al-Insan: 8].

The second connection: since the preceding passage was in refutation of the Christians and their false belief concerning the Messiah, and the falsity of what they held was made clear, and the truth and certainty concerning the matter of Jesus and his mother became evident, how Allah created him by His power and will and sent him to the Children of Israel calling them to worship his Lord, He then proceeded to refute the Jews, may Allah disfigure them, and to clarify that the abrogation whose occurrence and permissibility they denied had indeed taken place. For Allah, Mighty and Majestic, had stated explicitly in their book, the Torah, that Noah (peace be upon him), when he left the ark, was permitted by Allah to eat from all the beasts of the earth. Then after this, Israel forbade himself the meat and milk of camels, and his sons followed him in that, and the Torah came confirming the prohibition of that and other things in addition. Allah, Mighty and Majestic, had permitted Adam to marry his daughters to his sons, and this was later forbidden. Taking a concubine in addition to a wife was permissible in the law of Abraham, and Abraham (the intimate friend) did this with Hagar when he took her as a concubine alongside Sarah, yet the like of this was forbidden to them in the Torah. Likewise, marrying two sisters together was widespread, and Jacob (peace be upon him) did it, marrying two sisters together, then that was forbidden to them in the Torah. All of this is stated explicitly in the Torah as they have it, and this is abrogation itself. So likewise should be what Allah prescribed for the Messiah (peace be upon him) in permitting some of what had been forbidden in the Torah. What then is the matter with them, that they did not follow him? Rather they denied him and opposed him. And likewise what Allah sent Muhammad ﷺ with, of the upright religion and the straight path, and the creed of his forefather Abraham, what is the matter with them, that they do not believe? For this reason He said, Exalted is He: {All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel had forbidden for himself before the Torah was sent down}, meaning: all foods were lawful to them before the sending down of the Torah, except what Israel had forbidden. Then He said: {Say, bring the Torah and recite it, if you are truthful}, for it speaks of what we have said. {And whoever fabricates a lie against Allah after that, then those are the wrongdoers}.

That is: whoever lies against Allah and claims that He prescribed for them the Sabbath and permanent adherence to the Torah, and that He did not send another prophet calling to Allah with proofs and arguments, after what we have made clear concerning the occurrence of abrogation and the appearance of what we have mentioned, {then those are the wrongdoers}.

Then He, Exalted is He, said: {Say, Allah has spoken the truth}, meaning: say, O Muhammad, He has spoken the truth in what He informed of and in what He prescribed in the Qur'an. {So follow the creed of Abraham, inclining to truth, and he was not among the polytheists}, meaning: follow the creed of Abraham which Allah prescribed in the Qur'an upon the tongue of Muhammad ﷺ, for it is the truth in which there is no doubt or uncertainty, and it is the way than which no prophet brought anything more complete, more clear, more evident, or more perfect, as He, Exalted is He, said: {Say, indeed my Lord has guided me to a straight path, an upright religion, the creed of Abraham, inclining to truth, and he was not among the polytheists} [al-An'am: 161], and He, Exalted is He, said: {Then We revealed to you: follow the creed of Abraham, inclining to truth, and he was not among the polytheists} [al-Nahl: 123].

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْحَمِيدِ، حَدَّثَنَا شَهْر قَالَ: قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] حَضَرَتْ عِصَابَةٌ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالُوا: حدِّثنا عَنْ خِلَالٍ نَسْأَلُكَ عَنْهُنَّ لَا يَعْلَمُهُنَّ إِلَّا نَبِيٌّ. قَالَ: "سَلُونِي عَمَّا شِئْتُمْ، وَلَكِنْ اجْعَلُوا لِي ذِمَّةَ اللَّهِ، وَمَا أَخَذَ يَعْقُوبُ عَلَى بَنِيهِ لَئِنْ أَنَا حَدَّثْتُكُمْ شَيْئًا فَعَرَفْتُمُوهُ لَتُتَابِعُنِّي عَلَى الإسْلامِ". قَالُوا: فَذَلِكَ لَكَ. قَالَ: "فَسَلُونِي عَمَّا شِئْتُمْ " قَالُوا: أَخْبرْنَا عَنْ أَرْبَعِ خِلَالٍ: أَخْبرْنَا أَيُّ الطَّعَامِ حَرَّمَ إِسْرَائِيلُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ؟ وَكَيْفَ مَاءُ الْمَرْأَةِ وَمَاءُ الرَّجُلِ؟ كَيْفَ هَذَا النَّبِيُّ الْأُمِّيُّ فِي النَّوْمِ؟ وَمَنْ وَليّه مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ؟ فَأَخَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْعَهْدَ لَئِنْ أَخْبَرَهُمْ لَيُتَابِعُنَّهُ وَقَالَ:"أَنْشُدُكُمْ بِالَّذِي أَنزلَ التَّوْرَاةَ عَلَى مُوسَى: هَلْ تَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ إِسْرَائِيلَ مَرِضَ مَرَضًا شَدِيدًا وَطَالَ سُقْمُهُ، فَنَذَرَ لِلَّهِ نَذْرًا لَئِنْ شَفَاهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ سُقْمِهِ لَيُحَرِّمَنَّ أَحَبَّ الشَّرَابِ إِلَيْهِ وَأَحَبَّ الطَّعَامِ إِلَيْهِ، وَكَانَ أَحَبَّ الطَّعَامِ إِلَيْهِ لُحْمان الإبِلِ، وَأَحَبَّ الشَّرَابِ إِلَيْهِ أَلْبَانُهَا" فَقَالُوا: اللَّهُمَّ نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "اللَّهُمَّ اشْهَدْ عَلَيْهِمْ". وَقَالَ: أَنْشُدُكُمْ بِاللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ، الَّذِي أَنزلَ التَّوْرَاةَ عَلَى مُوسَى: هَلْ تَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ مَاءَ الرَّجُلِ أَبْيَضُ غَلِيظٌ، ومَاءَ الْمَرْأَةِ أَصْفَر رَقِيقٌ، فَأَيُّهُمَا عَلا كَانَ لَهُ الْوَلَدُ وَالشَّبَهُ بإذنِ اللَّهِ، إِنْ عَلا مَاءُ الرَّجُلِ مَاءَ الْمَرْأَةِ كَانَ ذَكَرًا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَإِنْ عَلا مَاءُ الْمَرْأَةِ مَاءَ الرَّجُلِ كَانَ أُنْثَى بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ". قَالُوا: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "اللَّهُمَّ اشْهَدْ عَلَيْهِمْ". وَقَالَ: "أَنْشُدُكُمْ بِالَّذِي أَنزلَ التَّوْرَاةَ عَلَى مُوسَى: هَلْ تَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ هَذَا النَّبِيَّ الأمِّيَّ تَنَامُ عَيْنَاهُ وَلا يَنَامُ قَلْبُهُ". قَالُوا: اللَّهُمَّ نعمْ. قَالَ: "اللَّهُمَّ اشْهَدْ ". قَالُوا: وَأَنْتَ الْآنَ فَحَدِّثْنَا منْ وليُّك مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ؟ فَعِنْدَهَا نُجَامِعُكَ أَوْ نُفَارِقُكَ قَالَ: "إِنَّ وَلِيِّيَ جِبْرِيلُ، وَلَمْ يَبْعَث اللَّهُ نَبِيًّا قَطُّ إِلا وَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُ". قَالُوا: فَعِنْدَهَا نُفَارِقُكَ، وَلَوْ كَانَ وَلِيُّكَ غَيْرَهُ لتابعنَاك ، فَعِنْدَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ مَنْ كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِجِبْرِيلَ﴾ الْآيَةَ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٩٧] .

وَرَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا، عَنْ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ، بِهِ .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أَحْمَدَ الزُّبَيْرِيُّ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْوَلِيدِ العِجْليّ، عَنْ بُكَير بْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: أَقْبَلَتْ يهودُ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَقَالُوا: يَا أَبَا الْقَاسِمِ، نَسْأَلُكَ عَنْ خَمْسَةِ أَشْيَاءَ، فَإِنْ أَنْبَأْتَنَا بِهِنَّ عَرَفْنَا أَنَّكَ نَبِيٌّ وَاتَّبَعْنَاكَ، فَأَخَذَ عَلَيْهِمْ مَا أَخَذَ إِسْرَائِيلُ عَلَى بَنِيهِ إِذْ قَالَ: ﴿اللَّهُ عَلَى مَا نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌ﴾ [يُوسُفَ: ٦٦] . قَالَ: "هَاتُوا". قَالُوا: أَخْبِرْنَا عَنْ عَلَامَةِ النَّبِيِّ؟ قَالَ: "تَنَامُ عَيْنَاهُ وَلا يَنَامُ قَلْبُه". قالوا: أخبرنا كيف تُؤنِّثُ المرأةُ وَكَيْفَ تُذْكرُ؟ قَالَ: "يَلْتَقِي الماءَان، فَإِذَا عَلَا مَاءُ الرَّجُلِ مَاءَ الْمَرْأَةِ أذْكَرَتْ، وإذَا عَلا مَاءُ الْمَرْأَةِ آنثَتْ. قَالُوا: أَخْبِرْنَا مَا حَرَّم إِسْرَائِيلُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ، قَالَ: "كَانَ يَشْتَكِي عِرْقَ النَّسَا، فَلَمْ يَجِدْ شَيْئًا يُلائِمُهُ إِلَّا ألْبَانَ كَذَا وكَذَا -قَالَ أَحْمَدُ: قَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: يَعْنِي الْإِبِلَ -فَحَرَّم لُحُومَهَا". قَالُوا: صَدَقْتَ. قَالُوا: أَخْبِرْنَا مَا هَذَا الرَّعد؟ قَالَ: "مَلَكٌ مِنْ مَلائِكَةِ اللهِ مُوَكلٌ بِالسَّحَابِ بِيدِهِ -أَوْ فِي يَدِه-مِخْرَاقٌ مِنْ نَارٍ يَزْجُر بِهِ السّحابَ، يَسُوقُهُ حَيْثُ أَمَرَهُ اللهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ". قَالُوا: فَمَا هَذَا الصَّوْتُ الَّذِي يُسمع؟ قَالَ: "صَوْتُه". قَالُوا: صدقت، إنما بقيت واحدة، وهي التي نتابعك إِنْ أَخْبَرَتْنَا بِهَا، فَإِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ إِلَّا لَهُ مَلَكٌ يَأْتِيهِ بِالْخَبَرِ، فَأَخْبِرْنَا مَنْ صاحبُك؟ قَالَ: "جبْرِيلُ عَلَيْه السَّلامُ". قَالُوا: جِبْرِيلُ ذَاكَ يَنزل بالحَرْب وَالْقِتَالِ وَالْعَذَابِ عَدُوُّنا. لَوْ قلتَ: ميكائيل الذي ينزل بالرحمة والنبات والقَطْر لَكَانَ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿قُلْ مَنْ كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِجِبْرِيلَ فَإِنَّهُ نزلَهُ عَلَى قَلْبِكَ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ مُصَدِّقًا لِمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَهُدًى وَبُشْرَى لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٩٧] .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ العِجْلي، به نحوه، وقال الترمذي: حسن غريب .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْج والعَوْفَيّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: كَانَ إِسْرَائِيلُ -وَهُوَ يَعْقُوبُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ-يَعْتَريه عِرق النَّسَا بِاللَّيْلِ، وَكَانَ يُقْلِقُهُ ويُزعِجه عَنِ النَّوْمِ، ويُقْلعُ الوَجَعُ عَنْهُ بِالنَّهَارِ، فَنَذَرَ لِلَّهِ لَئِنْ عَافَاهُ اللَّهُ لَا يَأْكُلُ عِرْقًا وَلَا يأكل ولد ما له عِرْق.

وَهَكَذَا قَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ وَالسُّدِّيُّ. كَذَا حَكَاهُ وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ. قَالَ: فاتَّبعه بَنُوه فِي تَحْرِيمِ ذَلِكَ استنَانًا بِهِ وَاقْتِدَاءً بِطَرِيقِهِ. قَالَ: وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تُنزلَ التَّوْرَاةُ﴾ أَيْ: حَرَّمَ ذَلِكَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تُنَزَّلَ التَّوْرَاةُ.

قُلْتُ: وَلِهَذَا السِّيَاقِ بَعْدَ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مُنَاسَبَتَانِ .

إِحْدَاهُمَا: أَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، حَرَّمَ أَحَبَّ الْأَشْيَاءِ إِلَيْهِ وَتَرَكَهَا لِلَّهِ، وَكَانَ هَذَا سَائِغًا فِي شَرِيعَتِهِمْ فَلَهُ مُنَاسَبَةٌ بَعْدَ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿لَنْ تَنَالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ﴾ فَهَذَا هُوَ الْمَشْرُوعُ عِنْدَنَا وَهُوَ الْإِنْفَاقُ فِي طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ مِمَّا يحبُّه الْعَبْدُ وَيَشْتَهِيهِ، كَمَا قَالَ: ﴿وَآتَى الْمَالَ عَلَى حُبِّهِ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ:١٧٧] وَقَالَ ﴿وَيُطْعِمُونَ الطَّعَامَ عَلَى حُبِّهِ﴾ [الْإِنْسَانِ:٨] .

الْمُنَاسَبَةُ الثَّانِيَةُ: لمَّا تَقَدَّمَ السِّيَاقُ فِي الرَّدِّ عَلَى النَّصَارَى، وَاعْتِقَادِهِمُ الْبَاطِلِ فِي الْمَسِيحِ وَتَبَيَّنَ زَيْف مَا ذَهَبُوا إِلَيْهِ. وَظُهُورُ الْحَقِّ وَالْيَقِينِ فِي أَمْرِ عِيسَى وَأُمِّهِ، وَكَيْفَ خَلَقَهُ اللَّهُ بِقُدْرَتِهِ وَمَشِيئَتِهِ، وَبَعَثَهُ إِلَى بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ يَدْعُو إِلَى عِبَادَةِ رَبِّهِ تَعَالَى -شَرَع فِي الرَّدِّ عَلَى الْيَهُودِ، قَبَّحهم اللَّهُ، وَبَيَانِ أَنَّ النَّسْخ الَّذِي أَنْكَرُوا وُقُوعَهُ وَجَوَازَهُ قَدْ وَقَعَ، فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، قَدْ نَصَّ فِي كِتَابِهِمُ التَّوْرَاةِ أَنَّ نُوحًا، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، لَمَّا خَرَجَ مِنَ السَّفِينَةِ أَبَاحَ اللَّهُ لَهُ جَمِيعَ دَوَابِّ الْأَرْضِ يَأْكُلُ مِنْهَا، ثُمَّ بَعْدَ هَذَا حَرَّمَ إِسْرَائِيلُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ لُحْمان الْإِبِلِ وَأَلْبَانَهَا، فَاتَّبَعَهُ بَنُوهُ فِي ذَلِكَ، وَجَاءَتِ التَّوْرَاةُ بِتَحْرِيمِ ذَلِكَ، وَأَشْيَاءَ أُخَرَ زِيَادَةً عَلَى ذَلِكَ. وَكَانَ اللَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، قَدْ أَذِنَ لِآدَمَ فِي تَزْوِيجِ بَنَاتِهِ مِنْ بَنِيهِ، وَقَدْ حرَّم ذَلِكَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ. وَكَانَ التَّسَرِّي عَلَى الزَّوْجَةِ مُبَاحًا فِي شَرِيعَةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَقَدْ فَعَلَهُ [الْخَلِيلُ] إِبْرَاهِيمُ فِي هَاجَرَ لَمَّا تسرَّى بِهَا عَلَى سَارَّةَ، وَقَدْ حُرِّم مِثْلُ هَذَا فِي التَّوْرَاةِ عَلَيْهِمْ. وَكَذَلِكَ كَانَ الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ شَائِعًا وَقَدْ فَعَلَهُ يَعْقُوبُ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، جَمَعَ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ حُرِّم ذَلِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ. وَهَذَا كُلُّهُ مَنْصُوصٌ عَلَيْهِ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ عِنْدَهُمْ، فَهَذَا هُوَ النَّسْخُ بِعَيْنِهِ، فَكَذَلِكَ فَلْيَكُنْ مَا شَرَعَهُ اللَّهُ لِلْمَسِيحِ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، فِي إِحْلَالِهِ بَعْضَ مَا حَرَّمَ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ، فَمَا بَالُهُمْ لَمْ يَتْبَعُوهُ؟ بَلْ كَذَّبُوهُ وَخَالَفُوهُ؟ وَكَذَلِكَ مَا بَعَثَ اللَّهُ بِهِ مُحَمَّدًا ﷺ مِنَ الدِّينِ الْقَوِيمِ، وَالصِّرَاطِ الْمُسْتَقِيمِ، ومِلَّة أَبِيهِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ فَمَا بَالُهم لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ؟ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿كُلُّ الطَّعَامِ كَانَ حِلا لِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ إِلا مَا حَرَّمَ إِسْرَائِيلُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تُنزلَ التَّوْرَاةُ﴾ أَيْ: كَانَ حِلا لَهُمْ جميعُ الْأَطْعِمَةِ قَبْلَ نُزُولِ التَّوْرَاةِ إِلَّا مَا حرَّمه إِسْرَائِيلُ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِالتَّوْرَاةِ فَاتْلُوهَا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صَادِقِينَ﴾ ؛ فَإِنَّهَا نَاطِقَةٌ بِمَا قُلْنَاهُ ﴿فَمَنِ افْتَرَى عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ذَلِكَ فَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ﴾

أَيْ: فَمَنْ كَذَب عَلَى اللَّهِ وادَّعى أَنَّهُ شَرَع لَهُمُ السَّبْتَ وَالتَّمَسُّكَ بِالتَّوْرَاةِ دَائِمًا، وَأَنَّهُ لَمْ يَبْعَثْ نَبِيًّا آخَرَ يَدْعُو إِلَى اللَّهِ بِالْبَرَاهِينِ والحُجَج بَعْدَ هَذَا الَّذِي بَيَّنَّاه مِنْ وُقُوعِ النُّسَخِ وَظُهُورِ مَا ذَكَرْنَاهُ ﴿فَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ﴾ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ صَدَقَ اللَّهُ﴾ أَيْ: قُلْ يَا مُحَمَّدُ: صَدَقَ فِيمَا أَخْبَرَ بِهِ وَفِيمَا شَرَعَهُ فِي الْقُرْآنِ ﴿فَاتَّبِعُوا مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ﴾ أَيِ: اتَّبَعُوا مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الَّتِي شَرَعَهَا اللَّهُ فِي الْقُرْآنِ عَلَى لِسَانِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، فَإِنَّهُ الْحَقُّ الَّذِي لَا شَكَّ فِيهِ وَلَا مِرْية، وَهِيَ الطَّرِيقَةُ الَّتِي لَمْ يَأْتِ نَبِيٌّ بِأَكْمَلَ مِنْهَا وَلَا أَبْيَنَ وَلَا أَوْضَحَ وَلَا أَتَمَّ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿قُلْ إِنَّنِي هَدَانِي رَبِّي إِلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ دِينًا قِيَمًا مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ:١٦١] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿ثُمَّ أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ أَنِ اتَّبِعْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ﴾ [النَّحْلِ:١٢٣] .

---

## 3:96-97 — verses 96-97 (shared commentary)

### Translation

He informs, exalted is He, that the first house set down for the people, meaning for the people generally, for their worship and their rites, around which they circle, toward which they pray, and beside which they perform i'tikaf, is {the one at Bakkah}, meaning the Ka'bah which Ibrahim the Friend of God (peace be upon him) built, he whom each of the two groups, the Christians and the Jews, claims to follow in his religion and his way, yet they do not make pilgrimage to the house that he built at God's command to him in that matter, and to whose pilgrimage he called the people. For this reason He said: {blessed}, meaning it was set down as a blessed place, {and a guidance for all the worlds}.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Sufyan, from al-A'mash, from Ibrahim al-Taymi, from his father, from Abu Dharr, may God be pleased with him, who said: I said, "O Messenger of God, which mosque was first placed on earth?" He said, "The Sacred Mosque." I said, "Then which?" He said, "The Farthest Mosque." I said, "How much time was between them?" He said, "Forty years." I said, "Then which?" He said, "Then wherever the time for prayer overtakes you, pray, for all of it is a mosque."

Al-Bukhari and Muslim both narrated it from the hadith of al-A'mash, with the same chain.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] al-Hasan ibn Muhammad ibn al-Sabbah, Sa'id ibn Sulayman, Sharik, from Mujalid, from al-Sha'bi, from Ali, concerning His saying, exalted is He: {Indeed, the first house set down for the people is the one at Bakkah, blessed}, he said: Houses had existed as places of prayer before, but this was the first house set down for the worship of God, exalted is He.

He said: and my father narrated to us, [chain] al-Hasan ibn al-Rabi', Abu al-Ahwas, from Simak, from Khalid ibn Ar'arah, who said: A man stood before Ali and said, "Will you not tell me about the House: was it the first house set down on earth?" He said, "No, but it was the first house in which blessing was placed, the station of Ibrahim, and whoever enters it is secure." And he related the rest of the account concerning the manner of Ibrahim's building of the House, and we have already related that in full in Surat al-Baqarah, so there is no need to repeat it here.

Al-Suddi claimed that it was the first house set down on the face of the earth absolutely. But the correct view is the statement of Ali, may God be pleased with him. As for the hadith that al-Bayhaqi related concerning the building of the Ka'bah in his book Dala'il al-Nubuwwah, by the route of Ibn Lahi'ah, from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, from Abu al-Khayr, from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-'As, as a raised report (marfu'): "God sent Gabriel to Adam and Eve and commanded them to build the Ka'bah, so Adam built it, then he was commanded to circle it, and it was said to him, 'You are the first of mankind, and this is the first house set down for the people.'" This, as you see, is among the singular reports (mufradat) of Ibn Lahi'ah, and he is weak. The more likely view, and God knows best, is that this is a halted report (mawquf) attributed to Abdullah ibn Amr, and that it comes from the two saddlebags (al-zamilatayn) that he acquired on the day of al-Yarmuk, being from the speech of the People of the Book.

Concerning His saying, exalted is He: {the one at Bakkah}, Bakkah is one of the names of Makkah according to the well known view. It is said that it was named thus because it "bakka" the necks of wrongdoers and tyrants, meaning that they weep (yabkun) there and become humbled before it. It is also said that it is because people crowd together there (yatabakkun), meaning they press together.

Qatadah said: God caused all the people to press together (bakka) there, so the women pray in front of the men, and this is not done in any other land.

The same has been related from Mujahid, Ikrimah, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Amr ibn Shu'ayb, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan.

Hammad ibn Salamah mentioned, from Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Makkah extends from al-Fajj to al-Tan'im, and Bakkah is from the House to al-Batha'.

Shu'bah said, from al-Mughirah, from Ibrahim: Bakkah is the House and the Mosque. Al-Zuhri said the same.

Ikrimah said, in one report, and Maymun ibn Mihran: The House and what is around it is Bakkah, and what is beyond that is Makkah.

Abu Salih, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Atiyyah al-Awfi, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan said: Bakkah is the site of the House, and everything besides that is Makkah.

They have mentioned many names for Makkah: Makkah, Bakkah, the Ancient House (al-Bayt al-Atiq), the Sacred House, the Secure City (al-Balad al-Amin), al-Ma'mun, Umm Rahm, Umm al-Qura, Salah, al-'Arsh on the pattern of Badr, al-Qadis (because it purifies from sins), al-Muqaddasah, al-Nassah (with a nun, and also with a ba), al-Hatimah, al-Nassasah, al-Ra's, Kutha, al-Baldah, al-Baniyyah, and al-Ka'bah.

His saying: {In it are clear signs}, meaning manifest indications that it is from the building of Ibrahim, and that God, exalted is He, has magnified it and honored it.

Then He, exalted is He, said: {The station of Ibrahim (maqam Ibrahim)}, meaning the stone which, when the building rose high, he used to help him raise its foundations and walls, standing upon it while his son Isma'il handed materials up to him. It used to be attached to the wall of the House until Umar ibn al-Khattab, may God be pleased with him, during his rule, moved it back toward the east, so that those circling the House would have room, and would not disturb those praying beside it after the circumambulation. For God, exalted is He, has commanded us to pray beside it, as He said: {And take, from the station of Ibrahim, a place of prayer} [al-Baqarah: 125]. We have already presented the hadiths on this matter, so there is no need to repeat them here. To God belongs praise and grace.

Al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {In it are clear signs: the station of Ibrahim}, meaning: among them is the station of Ibrahim and al-Mash'ar.

Mujahid said: The trace of his two footprints in the station is a clear sign. The same has been related from Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, al-Hasan, Qatadah, al-Suddi, Muqatil ibn Hayyan, and others.

Abu Talib said in his poem:

"And the place where Ibrahim trod remains moist in the rock, from his two feet, walking barefoot without sandals..."

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu Sa'id and Amr al-Awdi, Waki', Sufyan, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ata', from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {The station of Ibrahim}, he said: The whole of the Sacred Precinct (al-Haram) is the station of Ibrahim. And in Amr's wording: The whole of al-Hajar is the station of Ibrahim.

It has been related from Sa'id ibn Jubayr that he said: The pilgrimage (al-Hajj) is the station of Ibrahim. This is how I saw it in the manuscript, but it is probably "the whole of al-Hajar is the station of Ibrahim," and Mujahid has stated this explicitly.

His saying: {And whoever enters it is secure}, meaning: the Sacred Precinct of Makkah, when the fearful person enters it, he is secure from every harm. This was likewise the case in the time before Islam (al-Jahiliyyah), as al-Hasan al-Basri and others said: A man might commit murder, then place a tuft of wool around his neck and enter the Sacred Precinct, and the son of the murdered man would meet him there and not molest him until he left.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu Sa'id al-Ashajj, Abu Yahya al-Taymi, from Ata', from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {And whoever enters it is secure}, he said: Whoever seeks refuge with the House, the House grants him refuge, but he is not to be sheltered, fed, or given drink, so that when he leaves, he is seized for his crime.

God, exalted is He, said: {Have they not seen that We have made a secure sanctuary, while people are being snatched away all around them?} [al-Ankabut: 67], and He, exalted is He, said: {So let them worship the Lord of this House, who has fed them against hunger and made them secure against fear} [Quraysh: 3, 4]. Indeed, among the things included in its sanctity is the prohibition of hunting its game and driving it from its nesting places, and the prohibition of cutting its trees, plucking its fruit, and cutting its grass, as the hadiths and reports establish concerning this from a group of the Companions, both as raised reports (marfu') and halted reports (mawquf).

In the two Sahihs, and the wording is that of Muslim, from Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said on the Day of the Conquest, the conquest of Makkah: "There is no emigration (hijrah) now, but rather struggle (jihad) and intention, and when you are called to go forth, then go forth." And he said on the Day of the Conquest, the conquest of Makkah: "Indeed this land, God made it sacred on the day He created the heavens and the earth, so it is sacred by the sanctity of God until the Day of Resurrection. Fighting in it was not made lawful for anyone before me, and it was not made lawful for me except for one hour of a day. So it is sacred by the sanctity of God until the Day of Resurrection: its thorny plants are not to be cut, its game is not to be driven away, its lost property is not to be picked up except by one who will announce it publicly, and its green herbage is not to be cut." Then al-Abbas said, "O Messenger of God, except al-Idhkhir, for it is used by their blacksmiths and for their houses." So he said, "Except al-Idhkhir."

Both of them (Bukhari and Muslim) narrated from Abu Hurayrah something similar or close to it. Both of them also narrated, with the wording being Muslim's, from Abu Shurayh al-Adawi that he said to Amr ibn Sa'id, while the latter was sending out expeditions to Mecca: "Permit me, O commander, to relate to you a saying which the Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered on the day after the conquest. My ears heard it, my heart retained it, and my eyes witnessed him when he spoke it. He praised Allah and glorified Him, then said: 'Mecca has been made sacred by Allah, and it was not made sacred by men. So it is not permissible for any person who believes in Allah and the Last Day to shed blood in it, nor to cut down a tree in it. If anyone permits himself to fight in it citing the Messenger of Allah's ﷺ fighting there, then tell him: Allah gave permission to His Messenger but He did not give permission to you. He only permitted it to me for a portion of a day, and its sanctity has returned today just as it was sacred yesterday. So let the one who is present convey this to the one who is absent.'" Abu Shurayh was then asked: "What did Amr say to you?" He said: "I know that better than you, O Abu Shurayh. The sanctuary does not grant protection to a sinner, nor to one fleeing after committing bloodshed, nor to one fleeing after committing a disgraceful crime."

From Jabir, he said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'It is not permissible for any of you to carry weapons in Mecca.'" Narrated by Muslim.

From Abdullah ibn Adiyy ibn al-Hamra al-Zuhri, that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, while he was standing at al-Hazwarah in the market of Mecca: "By Allah, you are indeed the best of Allah's lands, and the most beloved of Allah's lands to Allah. Had I not been driven out of you, I would not have left."

Narrated by Imam Ahmad, and this is his wording, and by al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah. Al-Tirmidhi said: "Hasan sahih (sound, authentic)." He likewise judged authentic a similar report from the hadith of Ibn Abbas, and Ahmad narrated something similar from Abu Hurayrah.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Yahya ibn Ja'dah ibn Hubayrah, regarding the saying of Allah, the Exalted, {and whoever enters it is safe}, said: "Safe from the Fire."

In the meaning of this saying is the hadith narrated by al-Bayhaqi: [chain] Abdullah ibn Abbas said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever enters the House enters into a good deed and leaves a bad deed behind, and comes out forgiven." Then he said: "Abdullah ibn al-Mu'ammal narrated it alone, and he is not strong (as a narrator)."

Regarding His saying: {And pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by mankind, for whoever is able to find a way there}, this is the verse establishing the obligation of Hajj according to the majority. It has also been said that the obligating verse is rather His saying: {And complete the Hajj and the Umrah for Allah} [al-Baqarah: 196], but the first view is more apparent.

Numerous hadiths have come stating that it is one of the pillars of Islam, its supports and its foundations, and the Muslims have agreed on this with a necessary, undeniable consensus. It is obligatory upon the one who is legally responsible only once in a lifetime, by explicit text and by consensus.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Hurayrah said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon to us and said: "O people, Hajj has been made obligatory upon you, so perform Hajj." A man said: "Every year, O Messenger of Allah?" He remained silent, until the man repeated it three times. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Had I said yes, it would have become obligatory, and you would not have been able to do it." Then he said: "Leave me as I have left you, for indeed those before you were destroyed by their excessive questioning and their disputing with their prophets. When I command you with something, do of it what you are able, and when I forbid you from something, then leave it." Narrated by Muslim from Zuhayr ibn Harb, from Yazid ibn Harun, with similar wording.

Sufyan ibn Husayn, Sulayman ibn Kathir, Abd al-Jalil ibn Humayd, and Muhammad ibn Abi Hafsah narrated, from al-Zuhri, from Abu Sinan al-Du'ali (whose name was Yazid ibn Umayyah), from Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon to us and said: "O people, Allah has decreed the Hajj obligatory upon you." Al-Aqra ibn Habis stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah, every year?" He said: "Had I said it, it would have become obligatory, and had it become obligatory you would not have carried it out, nor would you have been able to carry it out. The Hajj is once, and whoever does more than that, it is voluntary."

Narrated by Ahmad, Abu Dawud, al-Nasa'i, Ibn Majah, and al-Hakim from the hadith of al-Zuhri, with this wording. It was also narrated by Sharik, from Simak, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, similarly. It was also narrated from the hadith of Usamah ibn Yazid.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Ali said: When {And pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by mankind, for whoever is able to find a way there} was revealed, they said: "O Messenger of Allah, every year?" He remained silent. They said again: "O Messenger of Allah, every year?" He said: "No, and had I said yes, it would have become obligatory." Then Allah, the Exalted, revealed: {O you who believe, do not ask about matters which, if they were shown to you, would trouble you} [al-Ma'idah: 101].

Al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Majah, and al-Hakim likewise narrated this from the hadith of Mansur ibn Wardan, with this wording. Al-Tirmidhi then said: "Hasan gharib (singular)." But there is a problem with what he said, because al-Bukhari said that Abu al-Bakhtari did not hear from Ali.

Ibn Majah said: [chain] Anas ibn Malik said: They said: "O Messenger of Allah, is Hajj every year?" He said: "Had I said yes, it would have become obligatory, and had it become obligatory you would not have kept up with it, and had you not kept up with it you would have been punished."

In the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Ibn Jurayj, from Ata, from Jabir, from Suraqah ibn Malik, who said: "O Messenger of Allah, is this mut'ah (temporary permission) of ours for this year of ours only, or forever?" He said: "No, rather forever." In another version: "Rather forever and ever."

In the Musnad of Imam Ahmad and the Sunan of Abu Dawud, from the hadith of Waqid ibn Abi Waqid al-Laythi, from his father, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to his wives during his Hajj: "This one, then remain behind the curtains," meaning: then stay behind the curtains and do not go out of the houses.

As for capability (istita'ah), it comes in categories: sometimes a person is capable by himself, and sometimes by means of another, as is established in the books of legal rulings.

Abu Isa al-Tirmidhi said: [chain] Ibn Umar said: A man stood up to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "Who is the pilgrim (al-hajj), O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "The disheveled and unkempt one." Another stood up and said: "Which Hajj is most excellent, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Raising the voice (in supplication) and the flowing of sacrificial blood." Another stood up and said: "What is 'the way,' O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Provisions and a mount."

Ibn Majah likewise narrated this from the hadith of Ibrahim ibn Yazid, who is al-Khuzi. Al-Tirmidhi said: "We do not know it except from his hadith, and some scholars have criticized him regarding his memory." This is what he said here. But in the Book of Hajj he said: "This is a hasan hadith."

There is no doubt that all the men in this chain are trustworthy except for this al-Khuzi, and they have criticized him because of this particular hadith.

However, someone else corroborated him. Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Abbad ibn Ja'far said: I sat with Abdullah ibn Umar, who said: A man came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said to him: "What is 'the way'?" He said: "Provisions and a mount." Ibn Marduyah likewise narrated this from the report of Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Ubayd ibn Umayr, with this wording.

Then Ibn Abi Hatim said: "This has also been narrated from Ibn Abbas, Anas, al-Hasan, Mujahid, Ata, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Rabi ibn Anas, and Qatadah, in similar fashion."

This hadith has also been narrated through other chains from the hadith of Anas, Abdullah ibn Abbas, Ibn Mas'ud, and Aishah, all as raised reports (marfu'), but there is criticism regarding their chains, as is established in the Book of Legal Rulings. And Allah knows best.

The hadith master Abu Bakr ibn Marduyah devoted himself to gathering the various chains of this hadith. Al-Hakim narrated it from the hadith of Qatadah, from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Qatadah, from Anas: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about the saying of Allah, {for whoever is able to find a way there}, and it was said: "What is 'the way'?" He said: "Provisions and a mount."

Then he said: authentic according to the criterion of Muslim, but the two of them (al-Bukhari and Muslim) did not transmit it.

Ibn Jarir said: Ya'qub told me, Ibn 'Ulayyah told us, from Yunus, from al-Hasan, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited: {And Allah has upon mankind a duty owed to Him, that of pilgrimage to the House, for whoever is able to find a way there.} They said: O Messenger of Allah, what is "the way"? He said: "Provisions and a mount."

Waki' related it in his tafsir, from Sufyan, from Yunus, with the same wording.

Imam Ahmad said: 'Abd al-Razzaq told us, al-Thawri informed us, from Isma'il (he is Abu Isra'il al-Mula'i), from Fudayl, that is, Ibn 'Amr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Hasten to the Hajj," meaning the obligatory one, "for none of you knows what may befall him."

Ahmad also said: Abu Mu'awiyah told us, al-Hasan ibn 'Amr al-Fuqaymi told us, from Mihran ibn Abi Safwan, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever intends the Hajj should hasten to it."

Abu Dawud related it from Musaddad, from Abu Mu'awiyah al-Darir, with the same wording.

Ibn Jubayr related from Ibn 'Abbas concerning His saying, {whoever is able to find a way there}, that he said: whoever possesses three hundred dirhams has found a way to it.

And from 'Ikrimah, his freed client, that he said: "the way" means health.

Waki' ibn al-Jarrah related, from Abu Jannab (that is, al-Kalbi), from al-Dahhak ibn Muzahim, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said concerning {whoever is able to find a way there}: it means provisions and a camel.

Regarding His saying, {and whoever disbelieves, then indeed Allah is free of need of all the worlds}, Ibn 'Abbas, Mujahid, and others said: that is, whoever denies the obligation of the Hajj has thereby disbelieved, and Allah has no need of him.

Sa'id ibn Mansur said, from Sufyan, from Ibn Abi Najih, from 'Ikrimah, who said: when {And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it will not be accepted from him} was revealed, the Jews said: "Then we are Muslims." Allah, Mighty and Majestic, said: so dispute with them and defeat them in argument, meaning that the Prophet ﷺ said to them: "Indeed Allah has made obligatory upon the Muslims the pilgrimage to the House, for whoever is able to find a way to it." They said: "It was not written as obligatory upon us," and they refused to perform the pilgrimage. Then Allah said: {and whoever disbelieves, then indeed Allah is free of need of all the worlds}.

Ibn Abi Najih related something similar from Mujahid.

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: 'Abdullah ibn Ja'far told us, Isma'il ibn 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud informed us, Muslim ibn Ibrahim and Shadh ibn Fayyad informed us, they said: Hilal Abu Hashim al-Khurasani informed us, Abu Ishaq al-Hamdani informed us, from al-Harith, from 'Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever possesses provisions and a mount and does not perform the pilgrimage to the House of Allah, it makes no difference whether he dies a Jew or a Christian. That is because Allah has said: {And Allah has upon mankind a duty owed to Him, that of pilgrimage to the House, for whoever is able to find a way there, and whoever disbelieves, then indeed Allah is free of need of all the worlds}."

Ibn Jarir related it from the hadith of Muslim ibn Ibrahim, with the same wording.

Likewise, Ibn Abi Hatim related it from Abu Zur'ah al-Razi: Hilal ibn Fayyad told us, Hilal Abu Hashim al-Khurasani told us, and he mentioned it with the same chain of transmission. Al-Tirmidhi related it from Muhammad ibn Yahya al-Qut'i, from Muslim ibn Ibrahim, from Hilal ibn 'Abdullah, freed client of Rabi'ah ibn 'Amr ibn Muslim al-Bahili, with the same wording, and said: this is a gharib (singular) hadith, we do not know it except through this route, and there is criticism of its chain of transmission. Hilal is unknown, and al-Harith is weakened as a transmitter of hadith.

Al-Bukhari said: this Hilal transmits reports that are munkar (rejected). Ibn 'Adi said: this hadith is not preserved reliably.

Abu Bakr al-Isma'ili the Hafiz related, from the hadith of Abu 'Amr al-Awza'i: Isma'il ibn 'Ubaydullah ibn Abi al-Muhajir told me, 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ghanm told me, that he heard 'Umar ibn al-Khattab say: whoever is able to perform the Hajj and does not perform it, it makes no difference to him whether he dies a Jew or a Christian.

This is a chain of transmission that is authentic up to 'Umar, may Allah be pleased with him. Sa'id ibn Mansur related in his Sunan, from al-Hasan al-Basri, who said: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab said: I have indeed resolved to send men to these lands to look at every person who has the means but has not performed the Hajj, and to impose the jizyah upon them. They are not Muslims. They are not Muslims.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبر تَعَالَى أَنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضع لِلنَّاسِ، أَيْ: لِعُمُومِ النَّاسِ، لِعِبَادَتِهِمْ ونُسُكهم، يَطُوفون بِهِ ويُصلُّون إِلَيْهِ ويَعتكِفُون عِنْدَهُ ﴿لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْكَعْبَةَ الَّتِي بَنَاهَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ الْخَلِيلُ [عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ] الَّذِي يَزْعم كُلٌّ مِنْ طَائِفَتَيِ النَّصَارَى وَالْيَهُودِ أَنَّهُمْ عَلَى دِينِهِ ومنهجِه، وَلَا يَحجُّون إِلَى الْبَيْتِ الَّذِي بَنَاهُ عَنْ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ لَهُ فِي ذَلِكَ وَنَادَى النَّاسَ إِلَى حَجِّهِ. وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿مُبَارَكًا﴾ أَيْ وُضع مُبَارَكًا ﴿وَهُدًى لِلْعَالَمِينَ﴾

وَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ التَّيْميّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي ذَر، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ قلتُ: يَا رسولَ اللَّهِ، أيُّ مَسجِد وُضِع فِي الْأَرْضِ أوَّلُ؟ قَالَ: "الْمسْجِدُ الْحَرَامُ". قُلْتُ: ثُمَّ أَيُّ؟ قَالَ: "الْمسجِدُ الأقْصَى". قُلْتُ: كَمْ بَيْنَهُمَا؟ قَالَ: "أرْبَعُونَ سَنَةً". قلتُ: ثُمَّ أَيُّ؟ قَالَ: ثُم حَيْثُ أدْرَكْت الصَلاةَ فَصَلِّ، فَكُلُّهَا مَسْجِدٌ".

وَأَخْرَجَهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ، وَمُسْلِمٌ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ الْأَعْمَشِ، بِهِ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الصَّبَّاحِ، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيك عَنْ مُجالد، عَنِ الشَّعْبيّ عَنْ علِيّ فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِلنَّاسِ لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ مُبَارَكًا﴾ قَالَ: كَانَتِ الْبُيُوتُ قِبْلَةً، وَلَكِنَّهُ كَانَ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِعِبَادَةِ اللَّهِ [تَعَالَى] .

[قَالَ] وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ الرَّبِيعِ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الأحْوَص، عن سِماك، عن خالد ابن عَرْعَرة قَالَ: قَامَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى عَليّ فَقَالَ: أَلَا تُحَدِّثني عَنِ الْبَيْتِ: أَهْوَ أولُ بَيْتٍ وُضِع فِي الْأَرْضِ؟ قَالَ لَا وَلَكِنَّهُ أَوَّلُ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ فِيهِ الْبَرَكَةُ مَقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ كَانَ آمِنًا. وَذَكَرَ تَمَامَ الْخَبَرِ فِي كَيْفِيَّةِ بِنَاءِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْبَيْتَ، وَقَدْ ذَكَرْنَا ذَلِكَ مُستَقصًى فِي سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ فأغْنَى عَنْ إِعَادَتِهِ .

وَزَعَمَ السُّدِّي أَنَّهُ أولُ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ عَلَى وَجْهِ الْأَرْضِ مُطْلَقًا. والصحيحُ قولُ علِيّ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] فَأَمَّا الْحَدِيثُ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ فِي بِنَاءِ الْكَعْبَةِ فِي كِتَابِهِ دَلَائِلِ النُّبُوَّةِ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ ابْنِ لَهِيعة، عَنْ يَزيد بْنِ أَبِي حَبيب، عَنْ أَبِي الْخَيْرِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ مَرْفُوعًا: "بَعَثَ اللهُ جِبْرِيلَ إلَى آدَمَ وحَوَّاءَ، فَأمَرَهُمَا بِبِنَاءِ الْكَعْبَةِ، فَبَنَاهُ آدَمُ، ثُمَّ أمَرَ بِالطَّوَافِ بِهِ، وَقِيلَ لَهُ: أنْتَ أوَّلُ النَّاسِ، وهَذَا أوَّلُ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ للنَّاسِ" فإنَّهُ كَمَا تَرَى مِنْ مُفْرَدَاتِ ابْنِ لَهِيعة، وَهُوَ ضَعِيفٌ. والأشْبَهُ، وَاللَّهُ أعلمُ، أَنْ يَكُونَ هَذَا مَوْقُوفا عَلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرو. وَيَكُونُ مِنَ الزَّامِلَتَيْنِ اللَّتَيْنِ أَصَابَهُمَا يَوْمَ الْيَرْمُوك، مِنْ كَلَامِ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ﴾ بَكَّة: مِنْ أَسْمَاءِ مَكَّةَ عَلَى الْمَشْهُورِ، قِيلَ سُمِّيت بِذَلِكَ لِأَنَّهَا تَبُكّ أَعْنَاقَ الظَّلَمَةِ وَالْجَبَابِرَةِ، بِمَعْنَى: يُبَكون بِهَا وَيَخْضَعُونَ عِنْدَهَا. وَقِيلَ: لِأَنَّ النَّاسَ يَتَبَاكّون فِيهَا، أَيْ: يَزْدَحِمُونَ.

قَالَ قَتَادَةُ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَكَّ بِهِ النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا، فَيُصَلِّي النِّسَاءُ أَمَامَ الرِّجَالِ، وَلَا يَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ بِبَلَدٍ غَيْرِهَا.

وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وعَمْرو بْنِ شُعَيب، ومُقاتل بْنِ حَيَّان.

وَذَكَرَ حَمّاد بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، عن ابن عباس قال: مَكَّة مِنَ الْفَجِّ إِلَى التَّنْعِيمِ، وَبَكَّةُ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ إِلَى الْبَطْحَاءِ.

وَقَالَ شُعْبَةُ، عَنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: بَكَّة: الْبَيْتُ وَالْمَسْجِدُ. وَكَذَا قَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ.

وَقَالَ عِكْرِمَةُ فِي رِوَايَةٍ، وَمَيْمُونُ بْنُ مِهْران: الْبَيْتُ وَمَا حَوْلَهُ بَكَّةُ، وَمَا وَرَاءَ ذَلِكَ مَكَّةُ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو صَالِحٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النّخَعي، وَعَطِيَّةُ [العَوْفي] وَمُقَاتِلُ بْنُ حَيَّانَ: بَكَّةُ مَوْضِعُ الْبَيْتِ، وَمَا سِوَى ذَلِكَ مَكَّةُ.

وَقَدْ ذَكَرُوا لِمَكَّةَ أَسْمَاءً كَثِيرَةً: مَكَّةَ، وَبَكَّةَ، وَالْبَيْتَ الْعَتِيقَ، وَالْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ، وَالْبَلَدَ الْأَمِينَ، وَالْمَأْمُونَ، وأُمَّ رُحْم، وَأُمَّ القُّرَى، وَصَلَاحَ، والعرْش عَلَى وَزْنِ بَدْرٍ، وَالْقَادِسَ؛ لِأَنَّهَا تُطَهِّرُ مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ، وَالْمُقَدَّسَةَ، وَالنَّاسَّةَ: بِالنُّونِ، وَبِالْبَاءِ أَيْضًا، وَالْحَاطِمَةَ، والنسَّاسة وَالرَّأْسَ، وكُوثى، والبلدة، والبَنِيَّة، والكعبة.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فِيهِ آيَاتٌ بَيِّنَاتٌ﴾ أَيْ: دَلَالَاتٌ ظَاهِرَةٌ أَنَّهُ مِنْ بِنَاءِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى عَظَّمه وَشَرَّفَهُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿مَّقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ﴾ يَعْنِي: الَّذِي لَمَّا ارْتَفَعَ الْبِنَاءُ اسْتَعَانَ بِهِ عَلَى رَفْعِ الْقَوَاعِدِ مِنْهُ وَالْجُدْرَانِ، حَيْثُ كَانَ يَقِفُ عَلَيْهِ وَيُنَاوِلُهُ وَلَدُهُ إِسْمَاعِيلُ، وَقَدْ كَانَ مُلْتَصِقًا بِجِدَارِ الْبَيْتِ، حَتَّى أَخَّرَهُ عُمَر بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، فِي إِمَارَتِهِ إِلَى نَاحِيَةِ الشَّرْقِ بِحَيْثُ يَتَمَكَّنُ الطُّوَّاف، وَلَا يُشَوِّشون عَلَى الْمُصَلِّينَ عِنْدَهُ بَعْدَ الطَّوَافِ؛ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى قَدْ أَمَرَنَا بِالصَّلَاةِ عِنْدَهُ حَيْثُ قَالَ: ﴿وَاتَّخِذُوا مِنْ مَّقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ:١٢٥] وَقَدْ قَدَّمْنَا الْأَحَادِيثَ فِي ذَلِكَ، فأغْنَى عَنْ إِعَادَتِهِ هَاهُنَا، وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ وَالْمِنَّةُ.

وَقَالَ العَوْفي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فِيهِ آيَاتٌ بَيِّنَاتٌ مَّقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ﴾ أَيْ: فمنهُنَّ مَقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ والمَشْعَر.

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: أثرُ قَدَمَيْهِ فِي الْمَقَامِ آيَةٌ بَيِّنَةٌ. وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ عُمر بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَقَتَادَةَ، والسُّدِّي، ومُقَاتِل بْنِ حَيّان، وَغَيْرِهِمْ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو طَالِبٍ فِي قَصِيدَتِهِ:

ومَوْطئ إِبْرَاهِيمَ فِي الصَّخْرِ رَطْبةٌ ... عَلَى قَدَمَيْهِ حَافِيًا غَيْرَ ناعلِ ...

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ وعَمْرو الأوْدِي قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيج، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿مَقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ﴾ قَالَ: الحَرَم كُلُّهُ مَقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ. وَلَفْظُ عَمْرٍو: الحَجَر كُلُّهُ مَقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ.

وَرُوِيَ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْحَجُّ مَقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ. هَكَذَا رَأَيْتُ فِي النُّسْخَةِ، وَلَعَلَّهُ الحَجَر كُلُّهُ مَقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَقَدْ صَرَّحَ بِذَلِكَ مُجَاهِدٌ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ كَانَ آمِنًا﴾ يَعْنِي: حَرَمُ مَكَّةَ إِذَا دَخَلَهُ الْخَائِفُ يأمنُ مِنْ كُلِّ سُوءٍ، وَكَذَلِكَ كَانَ الْأَمْرُ فِي حَالِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، كَمَا قَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ وَغَيْرُهُ: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ يَقْتُل فيَضَع فِي عُنُقِه صوفَة وَيَدْخُلُ الْحَرَمَ فَيَلْقَاهُ ابْنُ الْمَقْتُولِ فَلَا يُهَيِّجْهُ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الأشَجّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو يَحْيَى التَّيْمِيّ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ كَانَ آمِنًا﴾ قَالَ: مَنْ عَاذَ بِالْبَيْتِ أَعَاذَهُ الْبَيْتُ، وَلَكِنْ لَا يُؤْوَى وَلَا يُطْعَم وَلَا يُسقى، فَإِذَا خَرَجَ أُخذ بِذَنْبِهِ.

وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا جَعَلْنَا حَرَمًا آمِنًا وَيُتَخَطَّفُ النَّاسُ مِنْ حَوْلِهِمْ﴾ [الْعَنْكَبُوتِ:٦٧] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَلْيَعْبُدُوا رَبَّ هَذَا الْبَيْتِ. الَّذِي أَطْعَمَهُمْ مِنْ جُوعٍ وَآمَنَهُمْ مِنْ خَوْفٍ﴾ [قُرَيْشٍ:٣، ٤] وَحَتَّى إِنَّهُ مِنْ جُمْلَةِ تَحْرِيمِهَا حُرْمة اصْطِيَادِ صَيْدِهَا وَتَنْفِيرِهِ عَنْ أَوْكَارِهِ، وحُرْمة قَطْعِ أشجارها وقَلْع ثمارها حَشيشها، كَمَا ثَبَتَتِ الْأَحَادِيثُ وَالْآثَارُ فِي ذَلِكَ عَنْ جَمَاعَةٍ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ مَرْفُوعًا وَمَوْقُوفًا.

فَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، وَاللَّفْظُ لِمُسْلِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَوْمَ الْفَتْحِ فَتْحِ مَكَّةَ: "لَا هِجْرَةَ وَلَكِنْ جِهَادٌ وَنِيَّةٌ، وإذَا استَنْفَرْتُمْ فَانْفِرُوا"، وَقَالَ يَوْمَ الْفَتْحِ فَتْحِ مَكَّةَ: "إنَّ هَذَا الْبَلَدَ حَرَّمَهُ اللهُ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمَوَاتِ والأرْضَ، فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ بِحرمَةِ اللَّهِ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، وَإِنَّهُ لَمْ يَحِلَّ الْقِتَالُ فِيهِ لِأَحَدٍ قَبْلِي، وَلَمْ يَحِلَّ لِي إِلَّا فِي سَاعَةٍ مِنْ نَهَارٍ، فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ بِحُرْمَةِ اللَّهِ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، لَا يُعْضَد شَوْكُهُ، وَلَا يُنَفَّرُ صَيْدُهُ، وَلَا يَلْتَقطْ لُقَطتَه إِلَّا مَنْ عَرَّفها، وَلَا يُخْتَلى خَلاها فقال العباس: يا رسول الله، إلا الإذْخَرَ، فَإِنَّهُ لقَيْنهم ولبُيوتهم، فَقَالَ: "إِلَّا الإذْخَر" .

وَلَهُمَا عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، مِثْلَهُ أَوْ نَحْوَهُ وَلَهُمَا وَاللَّفْظُ لِمُسْلِمٍ أَيْضًا عَن أَبِي شُرَيح العَدوي أَنَّهُ قَالَ لعَمْرو بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، وَهُوَ يَبْعَثُ الْبُعُوثَ إِلَى مكةَ: ائذَنْ لِي أَيُّهَا الْأَمِيرُ أَنْ أُحدِّثك قَولا قَامَ بِهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ الغَدَ مِنْ يَوْمِ الْفَتْحِ سَمعَتْه أُذُنَايَ وَوَعَاهُ قَلْبِي وَأَبْصَرَتْهُ عَيْنَايَ حِينَ تَكَلَّمَ بِهِ، إِنَّهُ حَمد اللَّهَ وَأَثْنَى عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ: "إنَّ مَكِّةَ حَرَّمَهَا اللهُ ولَمْ يُحَرِّمْهَا النَّاسُ، فَلا يَحِلُّ لامرئ يُؤْمِنُ باللهِ والْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ أنْ يَسْفِكَ بِهَا دَمًا، ولا يَعْضد بِهَا شَجَرةً، فَإنْ أحَد تَرخَّصَ بِقِتَالِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ فِيهَا فَقُولُوا لَهُ: إنَّ اللهَ أذِنَ لِرَسُولِهِ وَلَمْ يَأْذَنْ لَكُمْ، وإنَّمَا أذِنَ لِي فِيهَا سَاعَةً مِنْ نَهَارٍ، وَقَدْ عَادَتْ حُرْمَتُهَا الْيَوْمَ كَحُرْمَتِهَا بِالأمْسِ فَلْيُبَلِّغِ الشَّاهدُ الغائِبَ" فَقِيلَ لِأَبِي شُرَيح: ما قال لك عَمْرو؟ قال: أنا أَعْلَمُ بِذَلِكَ مِنْكَ يَا أَبَا شُرَيْحٍ، إِنَّ الحَرَم لَا يُعيذ عَاصِيًا وَلَا فَارا بِدَمٍ وَلَا فَارًّا بخَزْيَة .

وَعَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم يقول: "لَا يَحِلُّ لأحَدِكُمْ أنْ يَحْمِلَ بِمَكَّةَ السِّلاحَ" رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ.

وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَدِيّ بْنِ الْحَمْرَاءِ الزُّهْرِيِّ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ، وَهُوَ وَاقِفٌ بالحَزْوَرَة فِي سُوقِ مَكَّةَ: "واللهِ إِنَّكِ لَخَيْرُ أرْضِ اللهِ، وأحَبُّ أرْضِ اللهِ إلَى اللهِ، ولَوْلا أنِّي أُخْرِجْتُ مِنْكِ مَا خَرَجْتُ".

رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ، وَهَذَا لَفْظُهُ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ. وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ وَكَذَا صَحَّح مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ نَحْوَهُ وَرَوَى أَحْمَدُ عن أبي هريرة، نحوه .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا بِشْر بْنُ آدَمَ ابْنِ بِنْتِ أَزْهَرَ السَّمَّانُ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ زُرَيق بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ الْأَعْمَى مَوْلَى بَنِي مَخْزُومٍ، حَدَّثَنِي زِيَادُ بْنُ أَبِي عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ جَعْدَةَ بْنِ هُبَيْرَة، فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ كَانَ آمِنًا﴾ قَالَ: آمِنًا مِنَ النَّارِ.

وَفِي مَعْنَى هَذَا الْقَوْلِ الحديثُ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ: أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَبْدان، أَخْبَرَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ الْوَاسِطِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ المُؤَمَّل، عَنِ ابْنِ مُحَيْصِن، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عليه وسلم: "مَنْ دَخَلَ الْبَيْتَ دَخَلَ فِي حَسَنةٍ وَخَرَجَ مِنْ سَيِّئَةٍ، وَخَرَجَ مَغْفُورًا لَهُ": ثُمَّ قَالَ: تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْمُؤَمَّلِ، وَلَيْسَ بِقَوِيٍّ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا﴾ هَذِهِ آيَةُ وُجُوب الْحَجِّ عِنْدَ الْجُمْهُورِ. وَقِيلَ: بَلْ هِيَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأَتِمُّوا الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ لِلَّهِ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ:١٩٦] وَالْأَوَّلُ أَظْهَرُ.

وَقَدْ وَرَدَت الأحاديثُ الْمُتَعَدِّدَةُ بِأَنَّهُ أحدُ أَرْكَانِ الْإِسْلَامِ وَدَعَائِمِهِ وَقَوَاعِدِهِ، وَأَجْمَعَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِجْمَاعًا ضَرُورِيًّا، وَإِنَّمَا يَجِبُ عَلَى المكلَّف فِي العُمْر مَرّة وَاحِدَةً بِالنَّصِّ وَالْإِجْمَاعِ.

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، أَخْبَرَنَا الرَّبِيعُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ القُرَشيّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: خَطَبَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: " أيُّهَا النَّاسُ، قَدْ فُرِضَ عَلَيْكُمْ الْحَجُّ فَحُجُّوا". فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ: أَكُلَّ عَامٍ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ فَسَكَتَ، حَتَّى قَالَهَا ثَلَاثًا. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَوْ قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ، لَوَجَبَتْ، وَلَمَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ ". ثُمَّ قَالَ: "ذَرُونِي مَا تَرَكْتُكُمْ، فَإِنَّمَا هَلَكَ مَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ بِكَثْرَةِ سُؤَالِهِمْ وَاخْتِلافِهِمْ عَلَى أَنْبِيَائِهِمْ، وإذَا أَمَرْتُكُمْ بِشَيْءٍ فَأْتُوا مِنْهُ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ، وإذَا نَهَيْتُكُمْ عَنْ شَيْءٍ فَدَعُوهُ". وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ، عَنْ زُهَير بْنِ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ هَارُونَ، بِهِ نَحْوَهُ .

وَقَدْ رَوَى سُفْيان بْنُ حُسَيْنٍ، وَسُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ كَثِيرٍ، وَعَبْدُ الْجَلِيلِ بْنُ حُمَيد، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي حَفْصَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي سنَان الدُّؤَلِيِّ -وَاسْمُهُ يَزِيدُ بْنُ أُمِّيَّةَ-عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: خَطَبَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ فقال: "يَأيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إنَّ اللهَ كَتَبَ عَلَيْكُم الحَجَّ". فَقَامَ الْأَقْرَعُ بْنُ حَابِسٍ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَفِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ قَالَ: "لَوْ قُلْتُهَا، لَوَجَبَتْ، ولَوْ وَجَبَتْ لَمْ تَعْمَلُوا بِهَا، وَلَمْ تَسْتَطِيعُوا أنْ تَعْمَلُوا بِهَا؛ الحَجُّ مَرَّةً، فَمَنْ زَادَ فَهُوَ تَطَوُّعٌ".

رَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ، وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، وَالْحَاكِمُ مِنْ حَدِيثِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، بِهِ. وَرَوَاهُ شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سِمَاك، عَنْ عِكرمة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، بِنَحْوِهِ. وَرُوِيَ مِنْ حَدِيثِ أُسَامَةَ يزيد .

[وَ] قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مَنْصُورُ بْنُ وَرْدَان، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي البَخْتَرِيّ، عَنْ علِيّ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا﴾ قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، فِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ فَسَكَتَ، قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، فِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ قَالَ: "لَا ولَوْ قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ، لَوَجَبَتْ". فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ [الْمَائِدَةِ:١٠١] .

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، وَالْحَاكِمُ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ وَرْدان، بِهِ: ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ. وَفِيمَا قَالَ نَظَرٌ؛ لِأَنَّ الْبُخَارِيَّ قَالَ: لَمْ يَسْمَعْ أَبُو البَخْتَرِيّ مِنْ عَلِيٍّ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ نُمَيْر، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيدة، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ: قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، الْحَجُّ فِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ قَالَ: "لَوْ قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ، لوجَبَتْ، وَلَوْ وَجَبَتْ لَمْ تَقُومُوا بِهَا، ولَوْ لَمْ تَقُومُوا بِهَا لَعُذِّبتُمْ" .

وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ سُراقة بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مُتْعَتنا هَذِهِ لِعَامِنَا هَذَا أَمْ لِلْأَبَدِ؟ قَالَ: "لَا بَلْ لِلأبَدِ". وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ: "بَلْ لأبَد أبَدٍ" .

وَفِي مُسْنَدِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ، وَسُنَنِ أَبِي دَاوُدَ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ وَاقِدِ بْنِ أَبِي وَاقِدٍ اللَّيْثِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ لِنِسَائِهِ فِي حَجَّتِهِ: "هَذِه ثُمَّ ظُهُورَ الحُصْر" يَعْنِي: ثُمَّ الزَمْنَ ظُهور الْحُصْرِ، وَلَا تَخْرُجْنَ مِنَ الْبُيُوتِ.

وَأَمَّا الِاسْتِطَاعَةُ فَأَقْسَامٌ: تَارَةً يَكُونُ الشَّخْصُ مُسْتَطِيعًا بِنَفْسِهِ، وَتَارَةً بِغَيْرِهِ، كَمَا هُوَ مُقَرَّرٌ فِي كُتُبِ الْأَحْكَامِ.

قَالَ أَبُو عِيسَى التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ، أَخْبَرَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، أَخْبَرَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ محمَّد بْنَ عَبَّاد بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ يُحَدِّثُ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: قَامَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم فَقَالَ: مَن الْحَاجُّ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: "الشَّعثُ التَّفِل" فَقَامَ آخَرُ فَقَالَ: أَيُّ الْحَجِّ أَفْضَلُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: "العَجُّ والثَّجُّ"، فَقَامَ آخَرُ فَقَالَ: مَا السَّبِيلُ يَا رَسُولَ الله ؟ قال: "الزَّادُ والرَّاحِلَة".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ حَدِيثِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ وَهُوَ الخُوزي. قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: وَلَا نَعْرِفُهُ إِلَّا مِنْ حَدِيثِهِ، وَقَدْ تَكَلَّمَ فِيهِ بَعْضُ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ مِنْ قِبَلِ حِفْظِهِ. كَذَا قَالَ هَاهُنَا. وَقَالَ فِي كِتَابِ الحَجّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ .

[وَ] لَا يُشَكُّ أَنَّ هَذَا الْإِسْنَادَ رِجَالُهُ كُلُّهُمْ ثِقَاتٌ سِوَى الْخُوزِيِّ هَذَا، وَقَدْ تَكَلَّمُوا فِيهِ مِنْ أَجْلِ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ.

لَكِنْ قَدْ تَابَعَهُ غَيْرُهُ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْعَامِرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ اللَّيْثِيُّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبَّادِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ قَالَ: جَلَسْتُ إِلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ لَهُ: مَا السَّبِيلُ؟ قَالَ: "الزَّادُ والرِّحْلَة". وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه مِنْ رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُبَيد بْنِ عمير، بِهِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: وَقَدْ رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَأَنَسٍ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَعَطَاءٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وَالرَّبِيعِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، وَقَتَادَةَ -نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ .

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ مِنْ طُرُق أخَر مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَنَسٍ، وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، وَعَائِشَةَ كُلها مَرْفُوعَةٌ، وَلَكِنْ فِي أَسَانِيدِهَا مَقَالٌ كَمَا هُوَ مُقَرَّرٌ فِي كِتَابِ الْأَحْكَامِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَدِ اعْتَنَى الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدُويه بِجَمْعِ طُرُقِ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ. وَرَوَاهُ الْحَاكِمُ مِنْ حَدِيثِ قَتَادَة عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ سُئِلَ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ: ﴿مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا﴾ فَقِيلَ مَا السَّبِيلُ ؟ قَالَ: "الزَّاد والرَّاحِلَة". ثُمَّ قَالَ: صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ مُسْلِمٍ، وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّة، عَنْ يُونس، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: قَرَأَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا﴾ قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا السَّبِيلُ؟ قَالَ: "الزَّادُ والرَّاحِلَةُ " .

وَرَوَاهُ وَكِيع فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، بِهِ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، أَنْبَأَنَا الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ -وَهُوَ أَبُو إِسْرَائِيلَ الْمُلَائِيُّ-عَنْ فُضَيْل -يَعْنِي ابْنَ عَمْرٍو-عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "تَعَجَّلُوا إِلَى الحَجِّ -يَعْنِي الْفَرِيضَةَ-فإنَّ أحَدَكُمْ لَا يَدْرِي مَا يَعْرضُ لَهُ " .

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو الفُقَيْمي، عَنْ مِهْرَان بْنِ أَبِي صَفْوَانَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ أرَادَ الحَجَّ فَلْيَتَعَجَّلْ".

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ مُسَدَّدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مُعَاوِيَةَ الضَّرِيرِ، بِهِ .

وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ جُبَير، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا﴾ قَالَ: مَنْ مَلَك ثَلَاثَمِائَةِ دِرْهم فَقَدِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا.

وَعَنْ عِكْرمة مَوْلَاهُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: السَّبِيلُ الصِّحَّة.

وَرَوَى وَكِيعُ بْنُ الجَرّاح، عَنْ أَبِي جَنَاب -يَعْنِي الْكَلْبِيَّ-عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ بْنِ مُزاحِم، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: ﴿مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا﴾ قَالَ: الزَّادُ وَالْبَعِيرُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَمُجَاهِدٌ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ: أَيْ وَمَنْ جَحَد فَرِيضَةَ الْحَجِّ فَقَدْ كَفَرَ، وَاللَّهُ غَنِيٌّ عَنْهُ .

وَقَالَ سَعيد بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيح، عَنْ عِكْرِمة قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الإسْلامِ دِينًا فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ﴾ قَالَتِ الْيَهُودُ: فَنَحْنُ مُسْلِمُونَ. قَالَ اللَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فاخْصَمْهُمْ فَحَجَّهُمْ -يَعْنِي فَقَالَ لَهُمُ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "إنَّ اللهَ فَرَضَ عَلَى الْمسلمِينَ حَجَّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاع إِلَيْه سَبِيلا" فَقَالُوا: لَمْ يُكْتَبْ عَلَيْنَا، وأبَوْا أَنْ يَحُجُّوا. قَالَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾ .

وَرَوَى ابْنُ أَبِي نَجيح، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، نَحْوَه.

وَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، أَخْبَرَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، أَخْبَرَنَا مُسْلِمُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وشَاذ بْنُ فَيَّاضٍ قَالَا أَخْبَرَنَا هِلَالٌ أَبُو هَاشِمٍ الخُراساني، أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ الْهَمْدَانِيُّ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ مَلَكَ زَادًا وَرَاحِلَةً وَلَمْ يَحُجَّ بَيْتَ اللهِ، فَلا يَضُرُّهُ مَاتَ يَهُودِيّا أوْ نَصْرانِيّا، ذَلِكَ بِأنَّ اللهَ قَالَ: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾ .

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ حَدِيثِ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، بِهِ.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي زُرْعة الرَّازِيِّ: حَدَّثَنَا هِلَالُ بْنُ فياض، حدثنا هلال أبو هاشم الْخُرَاسَانِيُّ، فَذَكَرَهُ بِإِسْنَادِهِ مِثْلَهُ. وَرَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى القُطَعي، عَنْ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ هِلَالِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ مَوْلَى رَبيعة بْنِ عَمْرو بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ الْبَاهِلِيِّ، بِهِ، وَقَالَ: [هَذَا] حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ لَا نَعْرِفُهُ إِلَّا مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ، وَفِي إِسْنَادِهِ مَقَالٌ، وَهِلَالٌ مَجْهُولٌ، وَالْحَارِثُ يُضَعَّفُ فِي الْحَدِيثِ .

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: هِلَالٌ هَذَا مُنْكَرُ الْحَدِيثِ. وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَدِيّ: هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ لَيْسَ بِمَحْفُوظٍ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْإِسْمَاعِيلِيُّ الْحَافِظُ مِنْ حَدِيثِ [أَبِي] عَمْرٍو الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، حَدَّثَنِي إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي الْمُهَاجِرِ، حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ غَنْم أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ يَقُولُ: مَنْ أَطَاقَ الْحَجَّ فَلَمْ يَحُجَّ، فَسَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِ يَهُودِيًّا مَاتَ أَوْ نَصْرَانِيًّا.

وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ إِلَى عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، وَرَوَى سَعيد بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ فِي سُنَنِهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ: لَقَدْ هَمَمْتُ أَنَّ أَبْعَثَ رِجَالًا إِلَى هَذِهِ الْأَمْصَارِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كُلَّ مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ جَدةٌ فَلَمْ يَحُجَّ، فَيَضْرِبُوا عَلَيْهِمُ الجِزْية، مَا هُمْ بِمُسْلِمِينَ. مَا هُمْ بِمُسْلِمِينَ .

---

## 3:98-99 — verses 98-99 (shared commentary)

### Translation

This is a rebuke from Allah, may He be exalted, directed at the disbelievers among the People of the Book, for their obstinacy against the truth, their disbelief in the signs of Allah, and their turning away from His path anyone from the people of faith who sought it, doing so with all their effort and capacity, despite their knowledge that what the Messenger had brought was truth from Allah. This knowledge they possessed came from the earlier prophets and the masters among the messengers, the prayers and peace of Allah be upon them all, and from what those prophets had foretold and made known regarding the mention of the Prophet ﷺ, the unlettered one, the Hashimite, the Arab, the Makkan, the master of the children of Adam, the seal of the prophets, and the messenger of the Lord of the earth and heaven.

Allah, may He be exalted, has threatened them concerning this, declaring that He is a witness over what they did, namely their contradicting what was in their own hands from the prophets, and their fighting against the Messenger who had been foretold, through denial, rejection, and obstinacy. And He, may He be exalted, informed that He is not unaware of what they do, meaning: He will requite them for that on a day when neither wealth nor children will benefit them.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this
