هَذَا تَعْنِيفٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى لكَفَرة أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ، عَلَى عِنَادِهِمْ لِلْحَقِّ، وَكُفْرِهِمْ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ، وصَدِّهم عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ مَنْ أَرَادَهُ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْإِيمَانِ بِجُهْدِهِمْ وَطَاقَتِهِمْ مَعَ عِلْمِهِمْ بِأَنَّ مَا جَاءَ بِهِ الرَّسُولُ حَقٌّ مِنَ اللَّهِ، بِمَا عِنْدَهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ عَنِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ الْأَقْدَمِينَ، وَالسَّادَةِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ، صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ، وَمَا بَشَّروا بِهِ ونوَّهُوا، مِنْ ذِكْر النَّبِيِّ [ﷺ] الْأُمِّيِّ الْهَاشِمِيِّ الْعَرَبِيِّ الْمَكِّيِّ، سَيِّدِ وَلَدِ آدَمَ، وَخَاتَمِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ، وَرَسُولِ رَبِّ الْأَرْضِ وَالسَّمَاءِ. وَقَدْ تَوَعَّدَهُمُ [اللَّهُ] تَعَالَى عَلَى ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلَى صَنِيعهم ذَلِكَ بِمَا خَالَفُوا ما بأيديهم عن الأنبياء، ومقاتلتهم الرَّسُولَ المُبشر بِالتَّكْذِيبِ وَالْجُحُودِ وَالْعِنَادِ، وَأَخْبَرَ تَعَالَى أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ، أَيْ: وَسَيَجْزِيهِمْ عَلَى ذَلِكَ يَوْمَ لَا يَنْفَعُهُمْ مَالٌ وَلَا بنون.

---

## 3:100-101 — verses 100-101 (shared commentary)

### Translation

God, exalted is He, warns His believing servants against obeying a faction of those who were given the Scripture, those who envy the believers for what God has granted them of His favor, and for what He has bestowed upon them by sending His Messenger, just as He, exalted is He, said: {Many among the People of the Scripture wish they could turn you back into disbelievers after your belief, out of envy from themselves} [al-Baqarah: 109]. And thus He said here: {If you obey a party of those who were given the Scripture, they will turn you back after your belief into disbelievers}. Then He said: {And how can you disbelieve while the verses of God are being recited to you and among you is His Messenger?} meaning: disbelief is far removed from you and you are far above it, for the verses of God descend upon His Messenger night and day, and he recites them to you and conveys them to you. This is like His saying, exalted is He: {And what is wrong with you that you do not believe in God while the Messenger calls you to believe in your Lord, and He has already taken your covenant, if you are believers?} [al-Hadid: 8] and the verse following it.

And as it has come in the hadith: that the Messenger of God ﷺ said one day to his companions: "Which of the believers do you find most wondrous in faith?" They said: the angels. He said: "How could they not believe, when they are with their Lord?" Then they mentioned the prophets. He said: "How could they not believe, when revelation descends upon them?" They said: then us. He said: "How could you not believe, when I am among you?" They said: then which of the people are most wondrous in faith? He said: "A people who will come after you, who will find written pages and believe in what is in them."

I have already mentioned the chain of transmission of this hadith and the discussion concerning it at the beginning of my commentary on al-Bukhari, and praise be to God.

Then God, exalted is He, said: {And whoever holds fast to God has certainly been guided to a straight path}, meaning: and along with this, holding fast to God and relying upon Him is the mainstay of guidance, the provision for keeping away from misguidance, the means to rightful guidance, the path of soundness, and the attainment of what is sought.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُحَذِّرُ تَعَالَى عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَنْ أَنْ يُطِيعُوا طَائِفَةً مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ، الَّذِينَ يَحْسُدُونَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَى مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ، وَمَا مَنَحهم بِهِ مِنْ إِرْسَالِ رَسُولِهِ كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَدَّ كَثِيرٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ لَوْ يَرُدُّونَكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ إِيمَانِكُمْ كُفَّارًا حَسَدًا مِنْ عِنْدِ أَنْفُسِهِمْ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ:١٠٩] وَهَكَذَا قَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿إِنْ تُطِيعُوا فَرِيقًا مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ يَرُدُّوكُمْ بَعْدَ إِيمَانِكُمْ كَافِرِينَ﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ ﴿وَكَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ وَأَنْتُمْ تُتْلَى عَلَيْكُمْ آيَاتُ اللَّهِ وَفِيكُمْ رَسُولُهُ﴾ يَعْنِي: أَنَّ الْكُفْرَ بَعِيدٌ مِنْكُمْ وَحَاشَاكُمْ مِنْهُ؛ فَإِنَّ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ تَنْزِلُ عَلَى رَسُولِهِ لَيْلًا وَنَهَارًا، وَهُوَ يَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكُمْ وَيُبَلِّغُهَا إِلَيْكُمْ، وَهَذَا كَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَا لَكُمْ لَا تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ وَقَدْ أَخَذَ مِيثَاقَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ [الْحَدِيدِ:٨] وَالْآيَةِ بَعْدَهَا. وَكَمَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ يَوْمًا لِأَصْحَابِهِ: "أيُّ الْمُؤمِنِينَ أعْجَبُ إلَيْكُمْ إيمَانًا؟ " قَالُوا: الْمَلَائِكَةُ. قَالَ: "وَكَيْفَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ وَهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ؟! " وَذَكَرُوا الْأَنْبِيَاءَ قَالَ: "وَكَيْفَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْوَحْيُ يَنزلُ عَلَيْهِمْ؟ " قَالُوا: فَنَحْنُ. قَالَ: "وَكَيْفَ لَا تُؤْمِنُونَ وأنَا بَيْنَ أظْهُرِكُمْ؟! ". قَالُوا: فَأَيُّ النَّاسِ أَعْجَبُ إِيمَانًا؟ قَالَ: "قَوْمٌ يَجِيؤُونَ مِنْ بَعْدِكُمْ يَجِدونَ صُحُفًا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَا فِيهَا" . وَقَدْ ذَكَرْتُ سَنَد هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ وَالْكَلَامَ عَلَيْهِ فِي أَوَّلِ شَرْحِ الْبُخَارِيِّ، وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْتَصِمْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدْ هُدِيَ إِلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ﴾ أَيْ: وَمَعَ هَذَا فَالِاعْتِصَامُ بِاللَّهِ وَالتَّوَكُّلُ عَلَيْهِ هُوَ العُمْدة فِي الْهِدَايَةِ، والعُدَّة فِي مُبَاعَدَةِ الغَواية، وَالْوَسِيلَةُ إِلَى الرَّشَادِ، وَطَرِيقِ السَّدَادِ، وَحُصُولِ الْمُرَادِ.

---

## 3:102-103 — verses 102-103 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Ahmad ibn Sinan told us, Abd al-Rahman told us, from Sufyan and Shu'bah, from Zubayd al-Yami, from Murrah, from Abd Allah, meaning Ibn Mas'ud, regarding {Fear Allah as He should be feared}, he said: that He be obeyed and not disobeyed, that He be remembered and not forgotten, and that He be thanked and not shown ingratitude.

This is a sound chain, mawquf (halted report, attributed only to the Companion). [Amr ibn Maymun also related it from Ibn Mas'ud, corroborating Murrah's report.]

Ibn Mardawayh related it through the hadith of Yunus ibn Abd al-A'la, from Ibn Wahb, from Sufyan al-Thawri, from Zubayd, from Murrah, from Abd Allah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "{Fear Allah as He should be feared} means that He be obeyed and not disobeyed, that He be thanked and not shown ingratitude, and that He be remembered and not forgotten."

Al-Hakim likewise related it in his Mustadrak, through the hadith of Mis'ar, from Zubayd, from Murrah, from Ibn Mas'ud, as a raised report (marfu'), mentioning it as above. He then said: Sound according to the criterion of the two Shaykhs (Bukhari and Muslim), though they did not include it. This is what he said, but the more evident view is that it is mawquf. And Allah knows best.

Ibn Abi Hatim then said: something similar has been related from Murrah al-Hamdani, al-Rabi' ibn Khuthaym, Amr ibn Maymun, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Tawus, al-Hasan, Qatadah, Abu Sinan, and al-Suddi, along these lines.

[It is related from Anas that he said: The servant does not fear Allah as He should be feared until he restrains his tongue.]

Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Abu al-'Aliyah, al-Rabi' ibn Anas, Qatadah, Muqatil ibn Hayyan, Zayd ibn Aslam, al-Suddi, and others held that this verse is abrogated by His statement, {So fear Allah as much as you are able} [al-Taghabun 64:16].

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His statement {Fear Allah as He should be feared}: it is not abrogated, but rather {as He should be feared} means that they should strive in His way as striving should truly be done, that no reproach of any reproacher should deter them concerning Allah, and that they should establish justice even against themselves, their fathers, and their sons.

Concerning His statement {And do not die except in a state of submission (as Muslims)}: that is, maintain Islam while you are in health and soundness so that you may die upon it. For the Most Generous has made it His gracious custom that whoever lives upon something dies upon it, and whoever dies upon something is raised upon it. We seek refuge in Allah from the opposite of that.

Imam Ahmad said: Rawh told us, Shu'bah told us, he said: I heard Sulayman, from Mujahid, that the people were circumambulating the House while Ibn Abbas was sitting with a curved staff (mihjan) with him, and he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "{O you who believe, fear Allah as He should be feared, and do not die except in a state of submission} and if a single drop of zaqqum were to fall, it would ruin the livelihood of the people of the earth. How then is it for one whose only food is zaqqum?"

Al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, Ibn Majah, Ibn Hibban in his Sahih, and al-Hakim in his Mustadrak likewise related this through various chains from Shu'bah. Al-Tirmidhi said: hasan sahih (good and sound). Al-Hakim said: according to the criterion of the two Shaykhs, though they did not include it.

Imam Ahmad said: Waki' told us, al-A'mash told us, from Zayd ibn Wahb, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abd Rabb al-Ka'bah, from Abd Allah ibn Amr, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever loves to be removed from the Fire and to enter Paradise, let death overtake him while he believes in Allah and the Last Day, and while he brings to people what he would love to be brought to himself."

Imam Ahmad also said: Abu Mu'awiyah told us, al-A'mash told us, from Abu Sufyan, from Jabir, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, three days before his death: "None of you should die except while having a good opinion of Allah, Mighty and Majestic." Muslim related it through the route of al-A'mash, with the same content.

Imam Ahmad said: Hasan ibn Musa told us, Ibn Lahi'ah told us, Abu Yunus told us, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that he said: "Allah says: I am as My servant thinks of Me. If he thinks well of Me, that is for him, and if he thinks ill, that is for him."

The essence of this hadith is established in the two Sahihs through another route, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah, Mighty and Majestic, says: I am as My servant thinks of Me."

The hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar said: Muhammad ibn Abd al-Malik al-Qurashi told us, Ja'far ibn Sulayman told us, from Thabit, and I believe from Anas, who said: A man from the Ansar was ill, and the Prophet ﷺ came to visit him, meeting him in the marketplace and greeting him. He said to him: "How are you, so-and-so?" He said: Well, O Messenger of Allah. I hope in Allah, but I fear my sins. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "These two do not come together in the heart of a servant in such a circumstance without Allah granting him what he hopes for and securing him from what he fears."

He then said: We do not know of anyone who related it from Thabit other than Ja'far ibn Sulayman. Al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah likewise related it through his hadith, and al-Tirmidhi then said: gharib (singular, meaning it is narrated through only one chain). Some related it from Thabit as a mursal (a report with a missing link at the Companion level).

As for the hadith which Imam Ahmad related: Muhammad ibn Ja'far told us, Shu'bah told us, from Abu Bishr, from Yusuf ibn Mahak, from Hakim ibn Hizam, who said: I pledged allegiance to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that I would not fall except while standing. Al-Nasa'i related it in his Sunan from Isma'il ibn Mas'ud, from Khalid ibn al-Harith, from Shu'bah, with the same content, and he titled it: "Chapter on how one falls for prostration." He then related it likewise, and it was said: its meaning is, that I would not die except as a Muslim, and it was said: its meaning is, that I would not be killed except while advancing, not retreating, which returns to the first meaning.

Concerning His statement {And hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together, and do not become divided}: it has been said that {the rope of Allah} means the covenant of Allah, as He says in the verse following it: {Humiliation has been cast upon them wherever they are found, except through a rope from Allah and a rope from the people} [Al Imran 3:112], meaning through a covenant and a guarantee of protection. It has also been said that {a rope from Allah} means the Qur'an, as in the hadith of al-Harith al-A'war, from Ali, as a raised report, describing the Qur'an: "It is the firm rope of Allah, and His straight path."

A specific hadith on this meaning has been reported. The hafiz imam Abu Ja'far al-Tabari said: Sa'id ibn Yahya al-Umawi told us, Asbat ibn Muhammad told us, from Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Sulayman al-Arzami, from Atiyyah, from Abu Sa'id, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The Book of Allah, it is the rope of Allah stretched from the heaven to the earth."

Ibn Mardawayh related, through the route of Ibrahim ibn Muslim al-Hajari, from Abu al-Ahwas, from Abd Allah, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Indeed this Qur'an is the firm rope of Allah, it is the manifest light, it is the beneficial healing, a protection for whoever holds fast to it, and a deliverance for whoever follows it."

Something similar has been related from the hadith of Hudhayfah and Zayd ibn Arqam. [Waki' said: al-A'mash told us, from Abu Wa'il, who said: Abd Allah said: This path is frequented, the devils frequent it, saying: O servant of Allah, by this way, come this way. So hold fast to the rope of Allah, for the rope of Allah is the Qur'an.]

Concerning His statement {and do not become divided}: He commanded them to unity and forbade them from division. Numerous hadiths have come forbidding division and commanding unity and harmony, as in the Sahih of Muslim, through the hadith of Suhayl ibn Abi Salih, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Indeed Allah is pleased for you with three things and displeased for you with three things. He is pleased for you that you worship Him and associate nothing with Him, that you hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and not become divided, and that you give sincere counsel to whomever Allah has placed in authority over your affairs. And He is displeased for you with idle talk and rumor-mongering, excessive questioning, and wasting wealth."

They were guaranteed protection, upon their agreement, from error, as numerous hadiths have also reported. Yet division and disagreement were feared for them, and this indeed occurred within this ummah, for they split into seventy-three sects, among them one saved sect bound for Paradise and delivered from the punishment of the Fire, namely those who follow what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his Companions followed.

Concerning His statement {And remember the favor of Allah upon you when you were enemies and He united your hearts, so that by His favor you became brothers, and you were on the brink of a pit of the Fire and He saved you from it} to the end of the verse: this passage concerns the affair of the Aws and the Khazraj, for there had been many wars between them in the pre-Islamic period (Jahiliyyah), fierce enmity, grudges, rancor, and vendettas because of which their fighting and battles had lasted long. Then when Allah brought Islam, and those among them who entered it did so, they became brothers loving one another for the majesty of Allah, joined together in Allah's cause, and cooperating in righteousness and piety. Allah, exalted is He, says: {He it is who supported you with His help and with the believers, and united their hearts. Had you spent all that is on the earth, you could not have united their hearts, but Allah united them. Indeed He is Mighty, Wise} [al-Anfal 8:62]. They had been on the brink of a pit of the Fire because of their disbelief, and Allah removed them from it by guiding them to faith. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ reminded them of this favor on the day he distributed the spoils of Hunayn, when some of them found fault because he had preferentially given to others in the distribution according to what Allah showed him. He addressed them, saying: "O assembly of the Ansar, did I not find you astray, and Allah guided you through me? And you were divided, and Allah united you through me? And you were poor, and Allah enriched you through me?" Every time he said something, they would say: Allah and His Messenger are more generous.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Yasar and others mentioned that this verse was revealed concerning the affair of the Aws and the Khazraj. It happened that a man from the Jews passed by an assembly of the Aws and the Khazraj, and it grieved him to see the agreement and harmony they were upon. So he sent a man along with him and ordered him to sit among them and remind them of their wars on the day of Bu'ath and those other wars. He did so, and this remained his constant practice until the people's tempers flared and some grew angry with others, and they rose up against one another, calling out their old battle cries and seeking their weapons, and they arranged to meet at the Harrah (lava field). This news reached the Prophet ﷺ, and he came to them and began to calm them, saying: "Is it a call of the Jahiliyyah while I am among you?" And he recited to them this verse. So they regretted what they had done, reconciled, embraced one another, and set down their weapons. May Allah be pleased with them. Ikrimah mentioned that this was revealed concerning them when they rose up against one another over the affair of the slander (al-ifk). And Allah knows best.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ سِنان، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ وشُعْبَة، عَنْ زُبَيْد الْيَامِيِّ، عَنْ مُرَّة، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ -هُوَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ- ﴿اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِ﴾ قَالَ: أَنْ يُطاع فَلَا يُعْصَى، وَأَنْ يُذْكَر فَلَا يُنْسَى، وَأَنْ يُشْكَر فَلَا يُكْفَر .

وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ مَوْقُوفٌ، [وَقَدْ تَابَعَ مُرَّةَ عَلَيْهِ عَمْرُو بْنُ مَيْمُونٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ] .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه مِنْ حَدِيثِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنِ ابْنِ وَهْب، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ زُبَيْد، عَنْ مُرَّة، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: " ﴿اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِ﴾ أَنْ يُطَاعَ فَلا يُعْصَى، وَيُشْكَرَ فَلا يُكْفَرَ، ويُذْكَر فَلا يُنْسَى".

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ مِسْعَر، عَنْ زُبَيْد، عَنْ مُرَّة، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، مَرْفُوعًا فَذَكَرَهُ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ الشَّيْخَيْنِ وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ. كَذَا قَالَ. وَالْأَظْهَرُ أَنَّهُ مَوْقُوفٌ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: ورُوي نحوهُ عَنْ مُرة الهَمْداني، وَالرَّبِيعِ بْنِ خُثَيم، وعمرو بن ميمون، وإبراهيم النَّخَعي، وطاووس، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَقَتَادَةَ، وَأَبِي سِنان، والسُّدِّي، نحوُ ذَلِكَ.

[وَرُوِيَ عَنْ أَنَسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: لَا يَتَّقِي الْعَبْدُ اللَّهَ حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِ حَتَّى يُخْزَنَ مِنْ لِسَانِهِ] .

وَقَدْ ذَهَبَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَير، وَأَبُو الْعَالِيَةِ، وَالرَّبِيعُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، وَقَتَادَةُ، وَمُقَاتِلُ بْنُ حَيّان، وَزَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ، والسُّدِّي وَغَيْرُهُمْ إِلَى أَنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ مَنْسُوخَةٌ بِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ﴾ [التَّغَابُنِ:١٦] .

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِ﴾ قَالَ: لَمْ تُنْسخ، وَلَكِنْ ﴿حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِ﴾ أَنْ يُجَاهِدُوا فِي سَبِيلِهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ، وَلَا تَأْخُذْهُمْ فِي اللَّهِ لَوْمَة لَائِمٍ، وَيَقُومُوا بالقِسْط وَلَوْ عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَآبَائِهِمْ وَأَبْنَائِهِمْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلا وَأَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ﴾ أَيْ: حَافَظُوا عَلَى الْإِسْلَامِ فِي حَالِ صِحَّتِكُمْ وَسَلَامَتِكُمْ لِتَمُوتُوا عَلَيْهِ، فَإِنَّ الْكَرِيمَ قَدْ أَجْرَى عَادَتَهُ بِكَرَمِهِ أَنَّهُ مَنْ عَاشَ عَلَى شَيْءٍ مَاتَ عَلَيْهِ، وَمَنْ مَاتَ عَلَى شَيْءٍ بُعث عَلَيْهِ، فَعِيَاذًا بِاللَّهِ مِنْ خِلَافِ ذَلِكَ.

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْح، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبة قَالَ: سمعتُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، أَنَّ النَّاسَ كَانُوا يَطُوفُونَ بِالْبَيْتِ، وابنُ عَبَّاسٍ جَالِسٌ مَعَهُ مِحْجَن، فَقَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: " ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِ وَلا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلا وَأَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ﴾ وَلَوْ أنَّ قَطْرَةً مِنَ الزَّقُّومِ قُطِرَتْ لأمَرّتْ عَلَى أهْلِ الأرْضِ عِيشَتَهُمْ فَكَيْفَ بِمَنْ لَيْسَ لَهُ طَعَامٌ إِلَّا الزَّقُّومُ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، وَابْنُ حِبَّان فِي صَحِيحِهِ، وَالْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ، من طُرُقٍ عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، بِهِ. وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ. وَقَالَ الْحَاكِمُ: عَلَى شَرْطِ الشَّيْخَيْنِ، وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ وَهْب، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَبْدِ رَبِّ الْكَعْبَةِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرو قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ أَحَبَّ أنْ يُزَحْزَحَ عَنِ النَّار وَيَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ، فَلْتُدْرِكْهُ مَنِيَّتُهُ، وَهُوَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ، ويَأْتِي إلَى النَّاسِ مَا يُحِبُّ أنْ يُؤتَى إلَيْهِ " .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم يَقُولُ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ بِثَلَاثٍ: "لَا يَمُوتَنَّ أحَدُكُمْ إِلَّا وَهُوَ يُحْسِنُ الظَّنَّ بِاللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ". وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ مِنْ طَرِيقِ الْأَعْمَشِ، بِهِ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ لَهِيعة، حَدَّثَنَا [أَبُو] يُونُسَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "إنَّ اللهَ قَالَ: أنَا عِنْدَ ظَنِّ عَبْدِي بِي، فإنْ ظَنَّ بِي خَيْرًا فَلَهُ، وَإنْ ظَنَّ شَرا فَلَهُ " .

وَأَصْلُ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ ثَابِتٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ وَجْهٍ آخَرَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يَقُولُ اللهُ [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] أنَا عِنْدَ ظَنِّ عَبْدِي بِي" .

وَقَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ القُرَشي، حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ -وَأَحْسَبُهُ-عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ مَرِيضًا، فَجَاءَهُ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَعودُه، فَوَافَقَهُ فِي السُّوقِ فسلَّم عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ لَهُ: "كَيْفَ أنْتَ يَا فُلانُ؟ " قَالَ بِخَيْرٍ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أرجو الله أخاف ذُنُوبِي. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَا يَجْتَمِعَانِ فِي قَلْبِ عَبْدٍ فِي هَذَا الْمَوْطِنِ إِلَّا أعْطَاهُ اللهُ مَا يَرْجُو وآمَنَهُ ممَّا يَخَافُ".

ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَا نَعْلَمُ رَوَاهُ عَنْ ثَابِتٍ غَيْرَ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ. وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ حَدِيثِهِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: غَرِيبٌ. وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ بَعْضُهُمْ عَنْ ثَابِتٍ مُرْسَلًا .

فَأَمَّا الْحَدِيثُ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعبة، عَنْ أَبِي بِشْر، عن يُوسُفَ بْنِ مَاهُك، عَنْ حَكِيمِ بْنِ حِزَام قَالَ: بايعتُ رسولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَلَى أَلَّا أخِرَّ إِلَّا قَائِمًا. وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ فِي سُنَنِهِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، بِهِ، وَتَرْجَمَ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ: (بَابُ كَيْفَ يَخِرُّ لِلسُّجُودِ) ثُمَّ سَاقَهُ مِثْلَهُ فَقِيلَ: مَعْنَاهُ: عَلَى أَلَّا أَمُوتَ إِلَّا مُسْلِمًا، وَقِيلَ: مَعْنَاهُ: [عَلَى] أَلَّا أُقتل إِلَّا مُقبِلا غَيْرَ مُدبِر، وَهُوَ يَرْجِعُ إِلَى الْأَوَّلِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَلا تَفَرَّقُوا﴾ قِيلَ ﴿بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ، كَمَا قَالَ فِي الْآيَةِ بَعْدَهَا: ﴿ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الذِّلَّةُ أَيْنَ مَا ثُقِفُوا إِلا بِحَبْلٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَحَبْلٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ:١١٢] أَيْ بِعَهْدٍ وَذِمَّةٍ وَقِيلَ: ﴿بِحَبْلٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْقُرْآنَ، كَمَا فِي حَدِيثِ الْحَارِثِ الْأَعْوَرِ، عَنْ علِيّ مَرْفُوعًا فِي صِفَةِ الْقُرْآنِ: "هُوَ حَبْلُ اللهِ الْمتِينُ، وَصِرَاطُهُ الْمُسْتَقِيمُ".

وَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي ذَلِكَ حَدِيثٌ خَاصٌّ بِهَذَا الْمَعْنَى، فَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الطَّبَرِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى الْأُمَوِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ العَرْزَمي، عَنْ عَطِيَّةَ عَنْ [أَبِي] سَعِيدٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "كِتَابُ اللهِ، هُوَ حَبْلُ اللهِ الْمَمْدُودُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ إلَى الأرْضِ" .

وَرَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه مِنْ طَرِيقِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ الهَجَريّ، عَنْ أَبِي الأحْوَص، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إنَّ هَذَا الْقُرْآنَ هُوَ حَبْلُ اللهِ الْمتِينُ، وَهُوَ النُّورُ الْمُبِينُ وهُوَ الشِّفَاءُ النَّافِعُ، عِصْمةٌ لِمَنْ تَمَسَّكَ بِهِ، ونَجَاةٌ لِمَنِ اتَّبَعَهُ" .

وُروي مِنْ حَدِيثِ حُذَيْفَةَ وَزَيْدِ بْنِ أَرْقَمَ نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ. [وَقَالَ وَكِيع: حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: إِنَّ هَذَا الصِّرَاطَ مُحْتَضَرٌ تَحْضُرُهُ الشَّيَاطِينُ، يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ، بِهَذَا الطَّرِيقِ هَلُمَّ إِلَى الطَّرِيقِ، فَاعْتَصَمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ حَبْلَ اللَّهِ الْقُرْآنُ] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا تَفَرَّقُوا﴾ أمَرَهُم بِالْجَمَاعَةِ وَنَهَاهُمْ عَنِ التَّفْرِقَةِ وَقَدْ وَرَدَتِ الأحاديثُ الْمُتَعَدِّدَةُ بِالنَّهْيِ عَنِ التَّفَرُّقِ وَالْأَمْرِ بِالِاجْتِمَاعِ وَالِائْتِلَافِ كَمَا فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُهَيل بْنِ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عليه وسلم قال: "إنَّ اللهَ يَرْضَى لَكُمْ ثَلاثًا، وَيَسْخَطُ لَكُمْ ثَلاثًا، يَرْضى لَكُمْ: أنْ تَعْبدُوهُ وَلا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئًا، وأنْ تَعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللهِ جَمِيعًا وَلا تَفَرَّقُوا، وأنْ تُنَاصِحُوا مَنْ وَلاهُ اللهُ أمْرَكُمْ؛ وَيَسْخَطُ لَكُمْ ثَلاثًا: قيلَ وَقَالَ، وَكَثْرَةَ السُّؤَالِ، وإِضَاعَةَ الْمَالِ" .

وَقَدْ ضُمِنتْ لَهُمُ العِصْمةُ، عِنْدَ اتِّفَاقِهِمْ، مِنَ الْخَطَأِ، كَمَا وَرَدَتْ بِذَلِكَ الْأَحَادِيثُ الْمُتَعَدِّدَةُ أَيْضًا، وخِيفَ عَلَيْهِمُ الِافْتِرَاقُ، وَالِاخْتِلَافُ، وَقَدْ وَقَعَ ذَلِكَ فِي هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ فَافْتَرَقُوا عَلَى ثَلَاثٍ وَسَبْعِينَ فِرْقَةً، مِنْهَا فِرْقَةٌ نَاجِيَةٌ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ ومُسَلمة مِنْ عَذَابِ النَّارِ، وَهُمُ الَّذِينَ عَلَى مَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَأَصْحَابُهُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ كُنْتُمْ أَعْدَاءً فَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُمْ بِنِعْمَتِهِ إِخْوَانًا [وَكُنْتُمْ عَلَى شَفَا حُفْرَةٍ مِنَ النَّارِ فَأَنْقَذَكُمْ مِنْهَا] ﴾ إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ، وَهَذَا السِّيَاقُ فِي شَأْنِ الأوْس والخَزْرَج، فَإِنَّهُ كَانَتْ بَيْنَهُمْ حُروبٌ كَثِيرَةٌ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، وَعَدَاوَةٌ شَدِيدَةٌ وَضَغَائِنُ، وإحَنٌ وذُحُول طَالَ بِسَبَبِهَا قِتَالُهُمْ وَالْوَقَائِعُ بَيْنَهُمْ، فَلَمَّا جَاءَ اللَّهُ بِالْإِسْلَامِ فَدَخَلَ فِيهِ مَنْ دَخَلَ مِنْهُمْ، صَارُوا إِخْوَانًا مُتَحَابِّينَ بِجَلَالِ اللَّهِ، مُتَوَاصِلِينَ فِي ذَاتِ اللَّهِ، مُتَعَاوِنِينَ عَلَى الْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوَى، قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿هُوَ الَّذِي أَيَّدَكَ بِنَصْرِهِ وَبِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ لَوْ أَنْفَقْتَ مَا فِي الأرْضِ جَمِيعًا مَا أَلَّفْتَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ أَلَّفَ بَيْنَهُمْ [إِنَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ] ﴾ [الْأَنْفَالِ:٦٢] وَكَانُوا عَلَى شَفَا حُفْرة مِنَ النَّارِ بِسَبَبِ كُفْرِهِمْ، فَأَبْعَدَهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهَا: أنْ هَدَاهُم لِلْإِيمَانِ. وَقَدِ امْتَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ بِذَلِكَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يوم قَسَم غَنَائِمَ حُنَيْنٍ، فَعتَبَ مَنْ عَتَبَ مِنْهُمْ لَمَّا فَضَّل عَلَيْهِمْ فِي القِسْمَة بِمَا أَرَاهُ اللَّهُ، فَخَطَبَهُمْ فَقَالَ: "يَا مَعْشَرَ الأنْصَارِ، ألَمْ أجدْكُمْ ضُلالا فَهَدَاكُمُ اللهُ بِي، وَكُنْتُمْ مُتَفَرِّقِينَ فَألَّفَكُمُ اللهُ بِي، وَعَالَةً فأغْنَاكُمْ اللهُ بِي؟ " كُلَّمَا قَالَ شَيْئًا قَالُوا: اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَمَنُّ.

وَقَدْ ذَكَرَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ يَسار وَغَيْرُهُ: أَنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ نَزَلَتْ فِي شَأْنِ الْأَوْسِ وَالْخَزْرَجِ، وَذَلِكَ أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْيَهُودِ مَرَّ بِمَلَأٍ مِنَ الْأَوْسِ وَالْخَزْرَجِ، فَسَاءَهُ مَا هُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الِاتِّفَاقِ والألْفَة، فَبَعَثَ رَجُلًا مَعَهُ وَأَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَجْلِسَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَيُذَكِّرَهُمْ مَا كَانَ مِنْ حُرُوبِهِمْ يَوْمَ بُعَاث وَتِلْكَ الْحُرُوبِ، فَفَعَلَ، فَلَمْ يَزَلْ ذَلِكَ دأبُه حَتَّى حَمِيَتْ نُفُوسُ الْقَوْمِ وَغَضِبَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ، وَتَثَاوَرُوا، وَنَادَوْا بِشِعَارِهِمْ وَطَلَبُوا أَسْلِحَتَهُمْ، وَتَوَاعَدُوا إِلَى الْحَرَّةِ، فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَأَتَاهُمْ فَجَعَلَ يُسكِّنهم وَيَقُولُ: "أبِدَعْوَى الجَاهِلِيَّةِ وأَنَا بَيْنَ أظْهُرِكُمْ؟ " وَتَلَا عَلَيْهِمْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ، فَنَدِمُوا عَلَى مَا كَانَ مِنْهُمْ، وَاصْطَلَحُوا وَتَعَانَقُوا، وَأَلْقَوُا السِّلَاحَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَذَكَرَ عِكْرِمة أَنَّ ذَلِكَ نَزَلَ فِيهِمْ حِينَ تَثَاوَرُوا فِي قَضِيَّةِ الإفْك. والله أعلم.

---

## 3:104-109 — verses 104-109 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty says: {And let there be among you a community} meaning, a group standing up to carry out the command of God, in calling to good and enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong. {And those are the successful ones}. Al-Dahhak said: they are the elite of the Companions and the elite of the transmitters, meaning the strivers (mujahidin) and the scholars.

Abu Ja'far al-Baqir said: The Messenger of God ﷺ recited {And let there be among you a community calling to good}, then said: "The good is following the Qur'an and my sunnah." Reported by Ibn Marduyah.

The purpose of this verse is that a group from the community should take up this task, even though it is obligatory upon every individual of the community according to his capacity, as is established in Sahih Muslim on the authority of Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever among you sees a wrong, let him change it with his hand; if he cannot, then with his tongue; if he cannot, then with his heart, and that is the weakest degree of faith." In another version: "and there is nothing beyond that of faith, not even the weight of a mustard seed."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] ... from Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, that the Prophet ﷺ said: "By the One in whose hand is my soul, you must enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong, or God will soon send upon you a punishment from Himself, and then you will call upon Him and He will not answer you."

Al-Tirmidhi and Ibn Majah also reported it through the hadith of Amr ibn Abi Amr, with the same chain, and al-Tirmidhi said: it is hasan (sound). There are many hadiths on this subject, along with the noble verses, as will be explained in their proper places.

Then the Almighty says: {And do not be like those who split up and differed after clear proofs had come to them [and those, for them is a great punishment]}. He forbids this community from being like the past nations in their splitting and disagreement, and their abandonment of enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong, despite the proof having been established against them.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] ... from Abu Amir Abdullah ibn Luhayy, who said: We performed the pilgrimage with Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, and when we arrived at Makkah, he stood up after performing the noon prayer and said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Indeed the people of the two Scriptures split in their religion into seventy-two sects, and this community will split into seventy-three sects, meaning factions of desire (ahwa'), all of them in the Fire except one, and that is the Jama'ah (the main body). And there will emerge among my community peoples with whom these desires will run their course, just as rabies runs through a dog's body until it enters every vein and every joint, leaving none of it untouched. By God, O assembly of the Arabs, if you do not uphold what your Prophet ﷺ brought, others among mankind are even less likely to uphold it."

This is also how Abu Dawud reported it, from Ahmad ibn Hanbal and Muhammad ibn Yahya, both from Abu al-Mughirah, whose name is Abd al-Qudus ibn al-Hajjaj al-Shami, with the same chain. This hadith has been transmitted through several routes.

Regarding the Almighty's saying: {On the Day when some faces will be whitened and some faces will be blackened}, meaning: the Day of Resurrection, when the faces of the people of the Sunnah and the Jama'ah will be whitened, and the faces of the people of innovation and division will be blackened. This was said by Ibn Abbas, may God be pleased with him and his father.

{As for those whose faces are blackened, "Did you disbelieve after your belief?"} Al-Hasan al-Basri said: they are the hypocrites: {"Then taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve."} This description applies to every disbeliever in general.

{And as for those whose faces are whitened, they will be in the mercy of God, abiding therein forever} meaning: Paradise, remaining in it forever, never desiring to leave it. Abu Isa al-Tirmidhi said, when commenting on this verse: [chain] ... from Abu Ghalib, who said: Abu Umamah saw heads set up on the steps of Damascus, and Abu Umamah said: "Dogs of the Fire, the worst of the slain under the canopy of the sky, and the best of the slain are those who killed them." Then he recited {On the Day when some faces will be whitened and some faces will be blackened} to the end of the verse. I said to Abu Umamah: Did you hear this from the Messenger of God ﷺ? He said: "If I had not heard it once, or twice, or three, or four times, up to seven times, I would not have narrated it to you."

Then he said: this is a hasan hadith. Ibn Majah also reported it through the hadith of Sufyan ibn Uyaynah from Abu Ghalib, and Ahmad included it in his Musnad, from Abd al-Razzaq, from Ma'mar, from Abu Ghalib, in similar wording. Ibn Marduyah also reported, when commenting on this verse, from Abu Dharr, a lengthy, gharib (singular), and truly remarkable hadith.

Then the Almighty says: {These are the signs of God} meaning: these are the signs of God, His proofs and His clear evidences, {We recite them to you}, O Muhammad, {in truth} meaning: We disclose what the matter truly is, in this world and in the Hereafter.

{And God does not intend injustice for the worlds} meaning: He is not unjust to them; rather, He is the Judge, the Just, who does no wrong, because He is capable of all things and knowing of all things, and therefore has no need to wrong any of His creatures. It is for this reason that He says: {And to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth} meaning: all of it is His possession and His servants. {And to God do all matters return} meaning: He is the One who disposes of affairs in this world and the Hereafter, the Judge in this world and the Hereafter.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أُمَّةٌ﴾ أَيْ: مُنْتَصِبَةٌ لِلْقِيَامِ بِأَمْرِ اللَّهِ، فِي الدَّعْوَةِ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ، وَالْأَمْرِ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّهْيِ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ ﴿وَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ﴾ قَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ: هُمْ خَاصَّةُ الصَّحَابَةِ وَخَاصَّةُ الرُّواة، يَعْنِي: الْمُجَاهِدِينَ وَالْعُلَمَاءَ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الْبَاقِرُ: قَرَأَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: ﴿وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أُمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ: "الْخَيْرُ اتِّبَاعِ القُرآنِ وَسُنَّتِي" رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ.

وَالْمَقْصُودُ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ أَنْ تَكُونَ فرْقَة مِنَ الأمَّة مُتَصَدِّيَةٌ لِهَذَا الشَّأْنِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ ذَلِكَ وَاجِبًا عَلَى كُلِّ فَرْدٍ مِنَ الْأُمَّةِ بِحَسْبِهِ، كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ رَأَى مِنْكُمْ مُنْكَرًا فَلْيُغَيِّرْهُ بِيَده، فَإنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَبِلِسَانِهِ، فَإنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَبِقَلْبِهِ، وَذَلِكَ أضْعَفُ الإيمَانِ". وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ: "وَلَيْسَ وَرَاءَ ذَلِكَ مِنَ الإيمَانِ حَبَّةُ خَرْدَلٍ" .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ الْهَاشِمِيُّ، أَخْبَرَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي عَمْرو بْنُ أَبِي عَمْرٍو، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْأَشْهَلِيِّ، عَنْ حُذَيْفَةَ بْنِ الْيَمَانِ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ قَالَ: "وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِه لَتَأْمُرُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ولَتَنْهَوُنَّ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، أَوْ لَيُوشِكَنَّ اللهُ أنْ يَبْعَثَ عَلَيْكُمْ عِقَابًا مِنْ عِنْدِهِ، ثُمَّ لَتَدْعُنَّهُ فَلا يَسْتَجِيبُ لَكُمْ".

وَرَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَمْرو بْنِ أَبِي عَمْرٍو، بِهِ وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ وَالْأَحَادِيثُ فِي هَذَا الْبَابِ كَثِيرَةٌ مَعَ الْآيَاتِ الْكَرِيمَةِ كَمَا سَيَأْتِي تَفْسِيرُهَا فِي أَمَاكِنِهَا.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ تَفَرَّقُوا وَاخْتَلَفُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ [وَأُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ] ﴾ يَنْهَى هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةَ أَنْ تَكُونَ كَالْأُمَمِ الْمَاضِيَةِ فِي تَفَرُّقِهِمْ وَاخْتِلَافِهِمْ، وَتَرْكِهِمُ الْأَمْرَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّهْيَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ مَعَ قِيَامِ الْحُجَّةِ عَلَيْهِمْ.

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْمُغِيرَةِ، حَدَّثَنَا صَفْوان، حَدَّثَنِي أزْهَر بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْهَوْزَنِي عن أَبِي عَامِرٍ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ لُحَيٍّ قَالَ: حَجَجْنَا مَعَ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ، فَلَمَّا قَدِمْنَا مَكَّةَ قَامَ حِينَ صَلَّى [صَلَاةَ] الظُّهْرِ فَقَالَ: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إنَّ أهْلَ الْكَتَابَيْنِ افْتَرَقُوا فِي دِينِهِمْ عَلَى ثنتيْنِ وَسَبْعِينَ مِلَّةً، وإنَّ هذِهِ الأمَّةَ سَتَفْتَرِقُ عَلَى ثَلاثٍ وَسَبْعِينَ مِلَّةً -يَعْنِي الْأَهْوَاءَ-كُلُّهَا فِي النَّار إِلَّا وَاحِدَةٌ، وَهِيَ الْجَمَاعَةُ، وَإِنَّهُ سَيَخْرُجُ فِي أُمَّتِي أَقْوَامٌ تُجَارى بِهِمْ تِلْكَ الأهْواء، كَمَا يَتَجَارى الكَلبُ بصَاحِبِهِ، لَا يَبْقَى مِنْهُ عِرْقٌ وَلا مَفْصِلٌ إِلَّا دَخَلَهُ. واللهِ -يَا مَعْشَر العَربِ-لَئِنْ لَمْ تَقُومُوا بِمَا جَاءَ بِهِ نَبِيُّكُمْ ﷺ لَغَيْرُكم مِن النَّاسِ أحْرَى أَلَّا يَقُومَ بِهِ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى، كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ أَبِي الْمُغِيرَةِ -وَاسْمُهُ عَبْدُ الْقُدُّوسِ بْنُ الْحَجَّاجِ الشَّامِيُّ-بِهِ، وَقَدْ رُوي هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ مِنْ طُرُقٍ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَوْمَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهٌ وَتَسْوَدُّ وُجُوهٌ﴾ يَعْنِي: يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، حِينَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهُ أَهْلِ السُّنَّةِ وَالْجَمَاعَةِ، وَتَسْوَدُّ وُجُوهُ أَهْلِ البِدْعَة وَالْفُرْقَةِ، قَالَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا .

﴿فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ اسْوَدَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ أَكَفَرْتُمْ بَعْدَ إِيمَانِكُمْ﴾ قَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ: وَهُمُ الْمُنَافِقُونَ: ﴿فَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ﴾ وَهَذَا الْوَصْفُ يَعُمّ كُلَّ كَافِرٍ.

﴿وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ ابْيَضَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فَفِي رَحْمَةِ اللَّهِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْجَنَّةَ، مَاكِثُونَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا لَا يَبْغُونَ عَنْهَا حوَلا. وَقَدْ قَالَ أَبُو عِيسَى التِّرْمِذِيُّ عِنْدَ تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْب، حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، عَنْ رَبِيع -وَهُوَ ابْنُ صَبِيح -وحَمَّاد بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي غَالِبٍ قَالَ: رَأَى أَبُو أُمَامَةَ رُءُوسًا مَنْصُوبَةً عَلَى دَرَج دِمَشْقَ، فَقَالَ أَبُو أُمَامَةَ: كِلَابُ النَّارِ، شَرُّ قَتْلَى تَحْتَ أَدِيمِ السَّمَاءِ، خَيْرُ قَتْلَى مَنْ قَتَلُوهُ، ثُمَّ قَرَأَ: ﴿يَوْمَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهٌ وَتَسْوَدُّ وُجُوهٌ﴾ إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ. قُلْتُ لِأَبِي أُمَامَةَ: أَنْتَ سَمِعْتَهُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ؟ قَالَ: لَوْ لَمْ أَسْمَعْهُ إِلَّا مَرَّةً أَوْ مَرَّتَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثًا أَوْ أَرْبَعًا -حَتَّى عَدّ سَبْعًا-مَا حَدّثتكموه.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ: وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ عَنْ أَبِي غَالِبٍ، وَأَخْرَجَهُ أَحْمَدُ فِي مُسْنَدِهِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَر، عَنْ أَبِي غَالِبٍ، بِنَحْوِهِ . وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه عِنْدَ تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ، حَدِيثًا مُطَوَّلًا غَرِيبًا عَجِيبًا جِدًّا.

ثُمَّ قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿تِلْكَ آيَاتُ اللَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: هَذِهِ آيَاتُ اللَّهِ وحُجَجُه وَبَيِّنَاتُهُ ﴿نَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكَ﴾ يَا مُحَمَّدُ ﴿بِالْحَقِّ﴾ أَيْ: نَكْشِفُ مَا الْأَمْرُ عَلَيْهِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ.

﴿وَمَا اللَّهُ يُرِيدُ ظُلْمًا لِلْعَالَمِينَ﴾ أَيْ: لَيْسَ بِظَالِمٍ لَهُمْ بَلْ هُوَ الحَكَم الْعَدْلُ الَّذِي لَا يَجُورُ؛ لِأَنَّهُ القادر عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ، الْعَالِمُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ، فَلَا يَحْتَاجُ مَعَ ذَلِكَ إِلَى أَنْ يَظْلِمَ أَحَدًا مِنْ خَلْقِهِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الأرْضِ﴾ أَيِ: الْجَمِيعُ ملْك لَهُ وَعَبِيدٌ لَهُ. ﴿وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الأمُورُ﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ الْمُتَصَرِّفُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، الْحَاكِمُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ.

---

## 3:110-112 — verses 110-112 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty informs us about this Muhammadan nation that it is the best of nations, saying: {You were the best nation brought forth for mankind}.

Al-Bukhari said: [chain] from Abu Hurayrah, regarding {You were the best nation brought forth for mankind}, he said: the best of people for people, you bring them in chains around their necks until they enter into Islam.

And likewise Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, Ikrimah, Ata, al-Rabi ibn Anas, and Atiyyah al-Awfi said regarding {You were the best nation brought forth for mankind}: meaning, the best of people for people.

The meaning is that they are the best of nations and the most beneficial of people to people. This is why He said: {You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and believe in Allah}.

The Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from Durrah bint Abi Lahab, she said: a man stood up before the Prophet ﷺ while he was on the pulpit and said: O Messenger of Allah, who are the best of people? He said: "The best of people are those who recite the Qur'an most, are most God-fearing, most given to enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong, and most attentive to maintaining ties of kinship."

Ahmad related it in his Musnad, al-Nasa'i in his Sunan, and al-Hakim in his Mustadrak, from the hadith of Simak, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {You were the best nation brought forth for mankind}. He said: they are those who emigrated with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ from Mecca to Medina.

The correct view is that this verse is general, applying to the whole nation, each generation according to its own standing. The best of their generations are those among whom the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was sent, then those who follow them, then those who follow them, as He said in another verse: {And thus We have made you a middle nation}, meaning: chosen, {so that you may be witnesses over mankind and the Messenger a witness over you}, to the end of the verse.

In the Musnad of Imam Ahmad, the Jami of al-Tirmidhi, the Sunan of Ibn Majah, and the Mustadrak of al-Hakim, from the report of Hakim ibn Mu'awiyah ibn Haydah, from his father, he said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "You complete seventy nations, you are the best of them, and you are the most honored before Allah, Mighty and Majestic."

This is a well known hadith, and al-Tirmidhi rated it hasan (sound). It is also transmitted from the hadith of Mu'adh ibn Jabal and Abu Sa'id al-Khudri in similar wording.

This nation attained the foremost rank in goodness only through its Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, for he is the noblest of Allah's creation, the most honored of the messengers before Allah, and Allah sent him with a complete and mighty law, the like of which He had not given to any prophet or messenger before him. Thus acting according to his path and way, a small amount of it accomplishes what a much greater amount of the deeds of others cannot accomplish, as stated by Imam Ahmad:

[chain] from Muhammad ibn Ali, that is, Ibn al-Hanafiyyah, that he heard Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, say: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I have been given what no prophet before me was given." We said: O Messenger of Allah, what is it? He said: "I have been given victory through terror instilled in the enemy, I have been given the keys of the earth, I have been named Ahmad, the earth has been made a means of purification for me, and my nation has been made the best of nations." Ahmad alone transmitted it by this route, and its chain is hasan (sound).

Imam Ahmad also said: [chain] Yazid ibn Maysarah said: I heard Umm al-Darda, may Allah be pleased with her, say: I heard Abu al-Darda say: I heard Abu al-Qasim ﷺ, and I never heard him called by this name before or after, say that Allah, the Almighty, says: "O Jesus, I am going to raise up after you a nation which, if what they love befalls them, praises and gives thanks, and if what they dislike befalls them, reckons it as reward and endures patiently, and yet has neither forbearance nor knowledge." He said: "O Lord, how can this be for them, having neither forbearance nor knowledge?" He said: "I will grant them from My forbearance and My knowledge."

There are hadiths whose mention is fitting here:

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, may Allah be pleased with him, he said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I have been given seventy thousand who will enter Paradise without reckoning, their faces like the moon on the night of the full moon, their hearts as one heart. So I asked my Lord, Mighty and Majestic, for more, and He granted me, with each one, seventy thousand more." Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, said: I see that this will reach the people of the towns and will also fall upon the outlying edges of the desert dwellers.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "My Lord has given me seventy thousand who will enter Paradise without reckoning." Umar said: O Messenger of Allah, why did you not ask for more? He said: "I did ask for more, and He granted me, with every man, seventy thousand more." Umar said: Why did you not ask for even more? He said: "I did ask for more, and He gave me thus." And Abdullah ibn Bakr opened his hands before him, and Abdullah said: he spread out his two arms and scooped with them. Hisham said: this is from Allah, and its number cannot be known.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Shurayh ibn Ubayd said: Thawban fell ill in Hims, while Abdullah ibn Qurt al-Azdi was its governor, and he did not visit him. Then a man of the Kala'i tribe entered upon Thawban to visit him, and Thawban said to him: "Can you write?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Write." So he wrote to the governor Abdullah ibn Qurt: "From Thawban, freedman of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. To proceed: had Moses and Jesus, peace be upon them both, had a servant present with you, you would have visited him." Then he folded the letter and said to him: "Will you deliver it to him?" He said: "Yes." So the man set off with his letter and delivered it to Ibn Qurt. When he saw it, he rose in alarm, and the people said: "What is the matter with him? Has something happened?" So he went to Thawban and entered upon him, visited him, and sat with him a while, then rose to leave. Thawban took hold of his cloak and said: "Sit, that I may tell you a hadith I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. I heard him say: 'Seventy thousand of my nation will surely enter Paradise, with no reckoning and no punishment upon them, and with each thousand, seventy thousand more.'"

Ahmad alone transmitted it by this route, and the chain of its narrators are all trustworthy Levantine narrators of Hims, so it is a sound hadith, and praise be to Allah.

Another route: al-Tabarani said: [chain] from Thawban, he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "My Lord, Mighty and Majestic, has promised me seventy thousand of my nation who will not be held to account, with each thousand, seventy thousand more." This is perhaps the version that is preserved, with the addition of Abu Asma al-Rahabi between Shurayh and Thawban, and Allah knows best.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from Ibn Mas'ud, he said: we spoke a great deal one night in the presence of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, then we came to him in the morning and he said: "The prophets were shown to me tonight with their nations. A prophet would pass by with three people with him, a prophet with a small band with him, a prophet with a small group with him, and a prophet with no one with him at all, until Moses, peace be upon him, passed by me with a huge multitude of the Children of Israel with him, and I was struck with admiration. I said: 'Who are these?' It was said to me: 'This is your brother Moses, with him are the Children of Israel.'" He said: "I said: 'Then where is my nation?' It was said: 'Look to your right.' I looked, and behold, the hills were filled with the faces of men. Then it was said to me: 'Look to your left.' I looked, and behold, the horizon was filled with the faces of men. It was said to me: 'Are you pleased?' I said: 'I am pleased, O Lord, I am pleased, O Lord.'" He said: "Then it was said to me: 'With these are seventy thousand who will enter Paradise without reckoning.'" The Prophet ﷺ said: "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, if you are able to be among the seventy thousand, then do so, and if you fall short of that, then be among the people of the hills, and if you fall short of that, then be among the people of the horizon, for I have seen there people jostling one another." Then Ukashah ibn Mihsan stood up and said: "Pray to Allah, O Messenger of Allah, that He make me one of them," meaning one of the seventy thousand, so he prayed for him.

Then another man stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah, pray to Allah to make me one of them." He said: "Ukashah has beaten you to it." He said: Then we discussed it and said, "Who do you think these seventy thousand are?" A people who were born into Islam and never associated anything with Allah until they died. That reached the Prophet ﷺ, so he said: "They are the ones who do not cauterize themselves, do not seek incantations, do not take omens, and rely upon their Lord."

Thus Ahmad narrated it with this chain and this wording. He also narrated it from Abd al-Samad, from Hisham, from Qatadah, with his chain, similarly, and added after the saying "I am pleased, O Lord, I am pleased, O Lord": He said, "Are you pleased?" I said, "Yes." He said, "Look to your left." He said, "So I looked, and the horizon was filled with the faces of men." Then he said, "Are you pleased?" I said, "I am pleased." This chain is sound (sahih) from this angle; Ahmad alone transmitted it, and the two Shaykhs (Bukhari and Muslim) did not include it.

Another hadith: Ahmad ibn Mani' said: [chain] Abd al-Malik ibn Abd al-Aziz told us, Hammad told us, from Asim, from Zirr, from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Prophet ﷺ said: "The nations were presented to me at the pilgrimage season, and my nation was slow to appear to me. Then I saw them, and their great numbers and their appearance pleased me greatly. They had filled the plain and the mountain." He said: "Are you pleased, O Muhammad?" I said, "Yes." He said: "Then along with these there are seventy thousand who will enter Paradise without reckoning, and they are the ones who do not seek incantations, do not cauterize themselves, and rely upon their Lord." Ukashah stood up and said, "O Messenger of Allah, pray to Allah to make me one of them." He said: "You are one of them." Then another man stood up and said: [Pray to Allah to make me one of them, so he said:] "Ukashah has beaten you to it." Al-Hafiz al-Diya' al-Maqdisi narrated this and said: "This, in my view, meets the standard of Muslim."

Another hadith: Al-Tabarani said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Judh'ui the judge told us, Uqbah ibn Mukram told us, Muhammad ibn Abi Adi told us, from Hisham ibn Hassan, from Muhammad ibn Sirin, from Imran ibn Husayn, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Seventy thousand from my nation will enter Paradise without reckoning and without punishment." It was said: "Who are they?" He said: "They are the ones who do not cauterize themselves, do not seek incantations, do not take omens, and rely upon their Lord." Muslim narrated it through the route of Hisham ibn Hassan, and in his version the mention of Ukashah is included.

Another hadith: It is established in the two Sahihs, through the narration of al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, that Abu Hurayrah told him, saying: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "A group of my nation will enter Paradise, and they are seventy thousand, their faces shining like the shining of the moon on the night of the full moon." Abu Hurayrah said: Ukashah ibn Mihsan al-Asadi then stood up, lifting a striped cloak he was wearing, and said, "O Messenger of Allah, pray to Allah to make me one of them." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Allah, make him one of them." Then a man from the Ansar stood up and said, "O Messenger of Allah, pray to Allah to make me one of them." He said: "Ukashah has beaten you to it."

Another hadith: Abu al-Qasim al-Tabarani said: [chain] Yahya ibn Uthman told us, Sa'id ibn Abi Maryam told us, Abu Ghassan told us, from Abu Hazim, from Sahl ibn Sa'd, that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Seventy thousand of my nation, or seven hundred thousand, will surely enter, holding on to one another, until the first of them and the last of them enter Paradise, and their faces will be like the moon on the night of the full moon."

Bukhari and Muslim both recorded it, from Qutaybah, from Abd al-Aziz ibn Abi Hazim, from his father, from Sahl, through the same chain.

Another hadith: Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj said in his Sahih: [chain] Sa'id ibn Mansur told us, Hushaym told us, Husayn ibn Abd al-Rahman informed us, saying: I was with Sa'id ibn Jubayr, and he said: "Which of you saw the star that fell last night?" I said, "I did." Then I said, "I was not in prayer, but I had been stung." He said, "What did you do?" I said, "I sought an incantation." He said, "What led you to do that?" I said, "A hadith that al-Sha'bi told us." He said, "What did al-Sha'bi tell you?" I said, "He told us, from Buraydah ibn al-Husayb al-Aslami, that he said: 'There is no incantation except for the evil eye or for scorpion sting.'" He said: "Whoever stops at what he has heard has done well, but Ibn Abbas told us, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: 'The nations were presented to me, and I saw a prophet with a small group, and a prophet with one or two men, and a prophet with no one at all, when suddenly a vast multitude was raised up before me, and I thought they were my nation, but it was said to me, "This is Moses (peace be upon him) and his people, but look at the horizon." So I looked, and there was a vast multitude. It was said to me, "Look at the other horizon." There was a vast multitude. It was said to me, "This is your nation, and with them are seventy thousand who will enter Paradise without reckoning and without punishment."' Then he got up and entered his house, and the people fell to discussing those who would enter Paradise without reckoning and without punishment. Some of them said, 'Perhaps they are those who accompanied the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.' Others said, 'Perhaps they are those who were born into Islam and never associated anything with Allah,' and they mentioned other things. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out to them and said, 'What are you discussing?' They told him, and he said: 'They are the ones who do not seek incantations for themselves, do not have incantations sought for them, do not take omens, and rely upon their Lord.'" Then Ukashah ibn Mihsan stood up and said, "Pray to Allah to make me one of them." He said, "You are one of them." Then another man stood up and said, "Pray to Allah to make me one of them." He said, "Ukashah has beaten you to it."

Bukhari also recorded it from Usayd ibn Zayd, from Hushaym, but in his version the phrase "do not seek incantations for themselves" is not present.

Another hadith: Ahmad said: [chain] Rawh ibn Ubadah told us, Ibn Jurayj told us, Abu al-Zubayr informed me that he heard Jabir ibn Abdullah say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he mentioned a hadith, in which it said: "The first group to be saved, whose faces are like the moon on the night of the full moon, are seventy thousand, who will not be brought to account. Then those who follow them will be like the brightest star in the sky. Then it continues likewise." He mentioned the rest of it. Muslim narrated it through the hadith of Rawh, except that he did not mention the Prophet ﷺ by name.

Another hadith: Al-Hafiz Abu Bakr ibn Abi Asim said in his book al-Sunan: [chain] Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah told us, Isma'il ibn Ayyash told us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, who said: I heard Abu Umamah al-Bahili say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "My Lord promised me that He would admit into Paradise, from my nation, seventy thousand, with every thousand another seventy thousand, upon whom there would be no reckoning and no punishment, along with three handfuls from the handfuls of my Lord, Mighty and Majestic."

Al-Tabarani likewise narrated it through the route of Hisham ibn Ammar, from Isma'il ibn Ayyash, with the same content, and this is a good chain (jayyid).

Another route from Abu Umamah: Ibn Abi Asim said: [chain] Duhaym told us, al-Walid ibn Muslim told us, Safwan ibn Amr told us, from Sulaym ibn Amir, from Abu al-Yaman al-Hawzani, whose name was Amir ibn Abdullah ibn Luhayy, from Abu Umamah, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: "Allah has promised me that He will admit into Paradise, from my nation, seventy thousand without reckoning." Yazid ibn al-Akhnas said: "By Allah, those are, among your nation, O Messenger of Allah, like the reddish fly among the flies." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah has promised me seventy thousand, with every thousand another seventy thousand, and He has added for me three handfuls." This is also a hasan (good) chain.

Another hadith: Abu al-Qasim al-Tabarani said: [chain] Ahmad ibn Khulayd told us, Abu Tawbah told us, Mu'awiyah ibn Sallam told us, from Zayd ibn Sallam, that he heard Abu Sallam say: Amir ibn Zayd al-Bukali told me that he heard Utbah ibn Abd al-Sulami, may Allah be pleased with him, say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "My Lord, Mighty and Majestic, has promised me that He will admit into Paradise, from my nation, seventy thousand without reckoning. Then every thousand of them will intercede for seventy thousand more. Then my Lord, Mighty and Majestic, will scoop out with His two hands three handfuls." Umar then exclaimed "Allahu Akbar" and said: "As for the first seventy thousand, Allah will let them intercede for their fathers, their sons, and their kinsfolk, and I hope Allah will place me among one of the last handfuls."

Al-Hafiz al-Diya' al-Maqdisi said in his book Sifat al-Jannah: "I know of no defect in this chain." And Allah knows best.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yahya ibn Sa'id narrated to us, Hisham (meaning al-Dastawa'i) narrated to us, Yahya ibn Abi Kathir narrated to us, from Hilal ibn Abi Maymunah, Ata' ibn Yasar narrated to us that Rifa'ah al-Juhani told him, saying: We set out with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ until, when we were at al-Kudayd, or he said at Qudayd, and he mentioned a hadith, in which he said: Then he said, "My Lord, Mighty and Majestic, has promised me that He will admit seventy thousand of my nation into Paradise without reckoning, and I hope that they will not enter it until you and those of your spouses and offspring who are righteous have settled into dwellings in Paradise."

Al-Diya' [al-Maqdisi] said: This, in my view, meets the conditions of Muslim.

Another hadith: Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us, from Qatadah, from al-Nadr ibn Anas, from Anas, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah has promised me that He will admit four hundred thousand of my nation into Paradise." Abu Bakr said, "Increase us, O Messenger of Allah." He said, "By Allah, thus it is." Umar said, "That is enough for you, O Abu Bakr." Abu Bakr said, "Leave me be, what harm is it to you if Allah admits all of us into Paradise?" Umar said, "If Allah wills, He will admit His creation into Paradise with a single handful." The Prophet ﷺ said, "Umar has spoken the truth."

This hadith with this chain was narrated uniquely by Abd al-Razzaq, as al-Diya' stated. It was also related by the Hafiz Abu Nu'aym al-Asbahani:

Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Makhlad narrated to us, Ibrahim ibn al-Haytham al-Baladi narrated to us, Sulayman ibn Harb narrated to us, Abu Hilal narrated to us, from Qatadah, from Anas, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "My Lord has promised me that He will admit one hundred thousand of my nation into Paradise." Abu Bakr said, "O Messenger of Allah, increase us." He said, "And thus," and Sulayman ibn Harb gestured with his hand accordingly. I said, "O Messenger of Allah, increase us." Umar said, "Allah is able to admit people into Paradise with a single scoop." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Umar has spoken the truth." This is a gharib (singular) hadith from this route, and Abu Hilal's name is Muhammad ibn Sulaym al-Rasibi, a Basran.

Another route from Anas: The Hafiz Abu Ya'la said: Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr narrated to us, Abd al-Qahir ibn al-Sarri al-Sulami narrated to us, Humayd narrated to us, from Anas, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Seventy thousand of my nation will enter Paradise." They said, "Increase us, O Messenger of Allah." He said, "For each man, seventy thousand." They said, "Increase us," and he was upon a sand dune, so he said, "Thus," and he scooped with his hand. They said, "O Messenger of Allah, may Allah keep far away whoever enters the Fire after this."

This is a good chain, its men are trustworthy, except for Abd al-Qahir ibn al-Sarri, about whom Ibn Ma'in was asked and said: "Sound (saalih)."

Another hadith: Al-Tabarani related, from the hadith of Qatadah, from Abu Bakr ibn Anas, from Abu Bakr ibn Umayr, from his father, that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Allah has promised me that He will admit three hundred thousand of my nation into Paradise." Umayr said, "O Messenger of Allah, increase us." He gestured thus with his hand. Umayr said, "O Messenger of Allah, increase us." Umar said, "That is enough for you. Allah, if He wills, will admit people into Paradise with a single scoop or a single handful." The Prophet of Allah ﷺ said, "Umar has spoken the truth."

Another hadith: Al-Tabarani said: Ahmad ibn Khulayd narrated to us, Abu Tawbah narrated to us, Mu'awiyah ibn Sallam narrated to us, from Zayd ibn Sallam, that he heard Abu Sallam say: Abdullah ibn Amir narrated to me, that Qays al-Kindi related that Abu Sa'id al-Anmari told him that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "My Lord, Mighty and Majestic, has promised me that He will admit seventy thousand of my nation into Paradise without reckoning, and each thousand will intercede for seventy thousand, then my Lord will scoop three scoops with His two hands." Thus Qays said. I said to Abu Sa'id, "Did you hear this from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ yourself?" He said, "Yes, with my ear, and my heart retained it." Abu Sa'id said: Then he, meaning the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, said, "And that, if Allah, Mighty and Majestic, wills, will encompass the emigrants of my nation, and Allah will complete the rest of it from our Bedouins."

This hadith was also related by Muhammad ibn Sahl ibn Askar, from Abu Tawbah al-Rabi' ibn Nafi', with his chain, similarly.

And he added: Abu Sa'id said, "This was calculated in the presence of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and it reached four hundred million and ninety million."

Another hadith: Abu al-Qasim al-Tabarani said: Hashim ibn Marthad al-Tabarani narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Ayyash narrated to us, my father narrated to me, Damdam ibn Zur'ah narrated to me, from Shurayh ibn Ubayd, from Abu Malik, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Indeed, by the One in Whose hand is the soul of Muhammad, there will surely be raised from among you on the Day of Resurrection, into Paradise, a group like the dark night, all of them together, they shall stride across the earth. The angels will say, 'Why has more come with Muhammad than came with the prophets?'" This is a hasan (good) chain.

Another type of hadith indicating the virtue of this nation and its honor in its nobility before Allah, and that it is the best of nations in this world and the next.

Imam Ahmad said: Yahya ibn Sa'id narrated to us, Ibn Jurayj narrated to us, Abu al-Zubayr informed me, from Jabir, that he heard the Prophet ﷺ say: "I hope that those who follow me from my nation will be a quarter of Paradise on the Day of Resurrection." He said: So we said "Allahu Akbar." Then he said, "I hope you will be a third of the people." He said: So we said "Allahu Akbar." Then he said, "I hope you will be half." This was likewise related from Rawh, from Ibn Jurayj, with it. And it meets the conditions of Muslim.

It is established in the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i, from Amr ibn Maymun, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to us, "Are you not pleased to be a quarter of the people of Paradise?" So we said "Allahu Akbar." Then he said, "Are you not pleased to be a third of the people of Paradise?" So we said "Allahu Akbar." Then he said, "I hope that you will be half of the people of Paradise."

Another route from Ibn Mas'ud: Al-Tabarani said: Ahmad ibn al-Qasim ibn Musawir narrated to us, Affan ibn Muslim narrated to us, Abd al-Wahid ibn Ziyad narrated to us, al-Harith ibn Hasirah narrated to me, al-Qasim ibn Abd al-Rahman narrated to me, from his father, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "How would you be if a quarter of Paradise were yours, and the rest of the people had three quarters of it?" They said, "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said, "How would you be if a third were yours?" They said, "That is more." He said, "How would you be if half were yours?" They said, "That is more." So the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The people of Paradise are one hundred and twenty ranks, eighty of them are yours."

Al-Tabarani said: al-Harith ibn Hasirah narrated this uniquely.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Abd al-Samad narrated to us, Abd al-Aziz ibn Muslim narrated to us, Dirar ibn Murrah Abu Sinan al-Shaybani narrated to us, from Muharib ibn Dithar, from Ibn Buraydah, from his father, that the Prophet ﷺ said: "The people of Paradise are one hundred and twenty ranks, this nation constitutes eighty ranks of that."

It was likewise related from Affan, from Abd al-Aziz, with it. Al-Tirmidhi extracted it from the hadith of Abu Sinan, with it, and said: This is a hasan hadith. Ibn Majah related it from the hadith of Sufyan al-Thawri, from Alqamah ibn Marthad, from Sulayman ibn Buraydah, from his father, with it.

Another hadith: Al-Tabarani related, from the hadith of Sulayman ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Dimashqi, Khalid ibn Yazid al-Bajali narrated to us, Sulayman ibn Ali ibn Abdullah ibn Abbas narrated to us, from his father, from his grandfather, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: "The people of Paradise are one hundred and twenty ranks, eighty of them are from my nation."

Khalid ibn Yazid al-Bajali narrated this uniquely, and Ibn Adi has spoken critically of him.

Another hadith: Al-Tabarani said: Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal narrated to us, Musa ibn Ghaylan narrated to us, Hashim ibn Makhlad narrated to us, Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Abu Amr, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: When {A group from the earlier peoples, and a group from the later peoples} [al-Waqi'ah: 38-39] was revealed, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "You are a quarter of the people of Paradise, you are a third of the people of Paradise, you are half the people of Paradise, you are two thirds of the people of Paradise."

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us, from Ibn Tawus, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "We are the last, the first, on the Day of Resurrection, we are the first of the people to enter Paradise, except that they were given the Book before us, and we were given it after them, and Allah guided us to the truth concerning that in which they differed. So this is the day about which they differed. The people follow us in it. Tomorrow it belongs to the Jews, and the day after to the Christians."

Bukhari and Muslim narrated it from the hadith of Abdullah ibn Tawus, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, from the Prophet ﷺ as a marfu' (elevated) report with similar wording. Muslim also narrated it via al-A'mash, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "We are the last, [yet] the foremost on the Day of Resurrection, and we are the first to enter Paradise." He mentioned the rest of the hadith.

Another hadith: al-Daraqutni narrated in al-Afrad from the hadith of Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Aqil, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Umar ibn al-Khattab, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: "Paradise has been forbidden to all the prophets until I enter it, and it has been forbidden to the nations until my nation enters it."

He then said: Ibn Aqil transmitted it alone from al-Zuhri, and no one else narrated it from him. Zuhayr ibn Muhammad transmitted it alone from Ibn Aqil, and Amr ibn Abi Salamah transmitted it alone from Zuhayr.

Abu Ahmad ibn Adi al-Hafiz narrated it, saying: [chain] Ahmad ibn al-Husayn ibn Ishaq told us, Abu Bakr al-A'yan Muhammad ibn Abi Attab told us, Abu Hafs al-Tinnisi, that is Amr ibn Abi Salamah, told us, Sadaqah al-Dimashqi told us, from Zuhayr ibn Muhammad, from Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Aqil, from al-Zuhri.

Al-Tha'labi narrated it: [chain] Abu Abbas al-Makhladi told us, Abu Nu'aym Abd al-Malik ibn Muhammad informed us, Ahmad ibn Isa al-Tinnisi informed us, Amr ibn Abi Salamah told us, Sadaqah ibn Abdullah told us, from Zuhayr ibn Muhammad, from Ibn Aqil, with the same content.

These hadiths pertain to the meaning of His saying, Exalted is He: {You are the best nation brought forth for mankind, enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong, and believing in Allah}. Whoever among this nation is characterized by these qualities enters, along with them, into this praise and commendation bestowed upon them. As Qatadah said: It has reached us that Umar ibn al-Khattab [may Allah be pleased with him], during a pilgrimage he performed, saw the people hurrying, so he recited this verse: {You are the best nation brought forth for mankind}, then said: Whoever is pleased to be among that nation, let him fulfill Allah's condition within it. Ibn Jarir narrated this.

Whoever does not possess this quality resembles the People of the Book whom Allah censured with His saying: {They did not forbid one another from wrongdoing that they committed. [Evil indeed was what they used to do] [al-Ma'idah: 79]}. For this reason, after Allah, Exalted is He, praised this nation for these qualities, He proceeded to censure the People of the Book and rebuke them, saying: {And if the People of the Book had believed}, meaning: in what was revealed to Muhammad ﷺ, {it would have been better for them. Among them are believers, but most of them are defiantly disobedient}, meaning: few among them believe in Allah and in what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them, while most of them persist in misguidance, disbelief, defiance, and disobedience.

Then Allah, Exalted is He, said, informing His believing servants and giving them glad tidings that victory and triumph would be theirs over the disbelieving, heretical People of the Book: {They will not harm you except with some annoyance, and if they fight you, they will turn their backs to you in flight, then they will not be helped}. And so it came to pass, for on the day of Khaybar Allah humiliated them and ground down their noses. Likewise those before them among the Jews of Medina, Banu Qaynuqa, Banu al-Nadir, and Banu Qurayzah, Allah humiliated all of them. Likewise the Christians of Syria (al-Sham), the Companions broke them in more than one location and stripped them of dominion over Syria forever and for all time. And a group upholding Islam will continue to stand firm in Syria until Isa ibn Maryam (peace be upon him) descends while they remain in that state, and he will judge, peace be upon him, by the law of Muhammad, upon whom be the best of prayers and peace, and he will break the cross, kill the swine, abolish the jizyah, and accept nothing but Islam.

Then Allah, Exalted is He, said: {Humiliation has been struck upon them wherever they are found, except through a rope from Allah and a rope from the people}, meaning: Allah has bound them to humiliation and abasement wherever they may be, so they have no security {except through a rope from Allah}, meaning: through a covenant of protection from Allah, which is the contract of dhimmah (protected covenant status) granted to them, the imposition of jizyah upon them, and their being bound to the rulings of the religion. {And a rope from the people}, meaning: a security given by them and to them, as in the case of a truce, a treaty partner, or a captive when one of the Muslims grants him safety, even if that Muslim is a woman, and likewise a slave, according to one of the two opinions of the scholars.

Ibn Abbas said regarding {except through a rope from Allah and a rope from the people}: meaning, through a covenant from Allah and a covenant from the people. Mujahid, Ikrimah, Ata, al-Dahhak, al-Hasan, Qatadah, al-Suddi, and al-Rabi' ibn Anas said likewise.

As for His saying: {And they incurred wrath from Allah}, meaning: they were made to bear, and they did bear, wrath from Allah, and they deserved it. {And humiliation was struck upon them}, meaning: they were made to bear it, both by decree and by law. For this reason He said: {That is because they used to disbelieve in the signs of Allah and killed the prophets without right}, meaning: what drove them to that was arrogance, transgression, and envy, so that brought upon them humiliation, abasement, and destitution forever, joined to the humiliation of the Hereafter. Then Allah, Exalted is He, said: {That is because they disobeyed and used to transgress}, meaning: what drove them to disbelief in the signs of Allah and to killing the messengers of Allah, and what was decreed for them on account of that, was that they were excessive in disobeying the commands of Allah, Mighty and Majestic, in indulging in acts of disobedience to Allah, and in transgressing against the law of Allah. We seek refuge in Allah from that, and Allah is the one whose help is sought.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Yunus ibn Habib told us, Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi told us, Shu'bah told us, from Sulayman al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, from Abu Ma'mar al-Azdi, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Children of Israel used to kill three hundred prophets in a single day, then the market for their vegetables would open at the end of that same day.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى عَنْ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ الْمُحَمَّدِيَّةِ بِأَنَّهُمْ خَيْرُ الْأُمَمِ فَقَالَ: ﴿كُنْتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ﴾ .

قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَيْسَرة، عَنْ أَبِي حَازِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ: ﴿كُنْتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ﴾ قَالَ: خَيْرَ النَّاسِ لِلنَّاسِ، تَأْتُونَ بِهِمْ فِي السَّلَاسِلِ فِي أَعْنَاقِهِمْ حَتَّى يَدْخُلُوا فِي الْإِسْلَامِ .

وَهَكَذَا قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، ومُجاهد، وعِكْرِمة، وعَطاء، وَالرَّبِيعُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، وَعَطِيَّةُ العَوْفيّ: ﴿كُنْتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ﴾ يَعْنِي: خَيْرَ النَّاسِ لِلنَّاسِ.

وَالْمَعْنَى: أَنَّهُمْ خَيْرُ الْأُمَمِ وَأَنْفَعُ النَّاسِ لِلنَّاسِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿تَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ﴾

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سِماك، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَيرة عن زوج [ذُرّةَ] بِنْتِ أَبِي لَهَب، [عَنْ دُرَّةَ بِنْتِ أَبِي لَهَبٍ] قَالَتْ: قَامَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ وَهُوَ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ، فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَيُّ النَّاسِ خَيْرٌ؟ فَقَالَ: "خَيْرُ النَّاسِ أقْرَؤهُمْ وَأَتْقَاهُمْ للهِ، وآمَرُهُمْ بِالمعروفِ، وأنْهَاهُمْ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، وَأَوْصَلُهُمْ لِلرَّحِمِ" .

وَرَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ فِي مُسْنَدِهِ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ فِي سُنَنِهِ، وَالْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ سِمَاكٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿كُنْتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ﴾ قَالَ: هُمُ الَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ من مكة إلى المدينة .

وَالصَّحِيحُ أَنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ عامةٌ فِي جَمِيعِ الْأُمَّةِ، كُلُّ قَرْن بِحَسْبِهِ، وَخَيْرُ قُرُونِهِمُ الَّذِينَ بُعثَ فِيهِمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، ثُمَّ الَّذِينَ يَلونهم، ثُمَّ الَّذِينَ يَلُونَهُمْ، كَمَا قَالَ فِي الْآيَةِ الْأُخْرَى: ﴿وَكَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَاكُمْ أُمَّةً وَسَطًا﴾ أَيْ: خِيَارًا ﴿لِتَكُونُوا شُهَدَاءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ [وَيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيدًا] ﴾ الْآيَةَ.

وَفِي مُسْنَدِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ، وَجَامِعِ التِّرْمِذِيِّ، وَسُنَنِ ابْنِ مَاجَهْ، وَمُسْتَدْرَكِ الْحَاكِمِ، مِنْ رِوَايَةِ حَكِيمِ بْنِ مُعَاوية بْنِ حَيْدَة، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أَنْتُمْ تُوفُونَ سَبْعِينَ أُمَّةً، أنْتُمْ خَيْرُهَا، وأنْتُمْ أكْرَمُ عَلَى اللهِ عزَّ وجَلَّ" .

وَهُوَ حَدِيثٌ مَشْهُورٌ، وَقَدْ حَسَّنه التِّرْمِذِيُّ. وَيُرْوَى مِنْ حَدِيثِ مُعَاذِ بْنِ جَبَلٍ، وَأَبِي سَعِيدٍ [الْخُدْرِيِّ] نَحْوَهُ.

وَإِنَّمَا حَازَتْ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةُ قَصَبَ السَّبْق إِلَى الْخَيْرَاتِ بِنَبِيِّهَا مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ فَإِنَّهُ أشرفُ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ أَكْرَمُ الرُّسُلِ عَلَى اللَّهِ، وَبَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ بِشَرْعٍ كَامِلٍ عَظِيمٍ لَمْ يُعْطه نَبِيًّا قَبْلَهُ وَلَا رَسُولًا مِنَ الرُّسُلِ. فَالْعَمَلُ [عَلَى] مِنْهَاجِهِ وَسَبِيلِهِ، يَقُومُ القليلُ مِنْهُ مَا لَا يَقُومُ العملُ الكثيرُ مِنْ أَعْمَالِ غَيْرِهِمْ مَقَامَهُ، كَمَا قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ:

حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ زُهَير، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَقِيلٍ-عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، وَهُوَ ابْنُ الْحَنَفِيَّةِ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أُعْطِيتُ مَا لَمْ يُعْطَ أَحَدٌ مِنْ الأنْبِيَاءِ". فَقُلْنَا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا هُوَ؟ قَالَ: " نُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ وَأُعْطِيتُ مَفَاتِيحَ الأرْضِ، وَسُمِّيتُ أَحْمَدَ، وَجُعِلَ التُّرَابُ لِي طَهُورًا، وَجُعِلَتْ أُمَّتِي خَيْرَ الأمَمِ". تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ، وَإِسْنَادُهُ حَسَنٌ .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْعَلَاءِ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ سَوَّار، حَدَّثَنَا لَيْث، عن معاوية عن بن أَبِي حُلَيْس يَزِيدَ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أُمَّ الدَّرْدَاءِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا، تَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْقَاسِمِ ﷺ، وَمَا سَمِعْتُهُ يُكَنِّيهِ قَبْلَهَا وَلَا بَعْدَهَا، يَقُولُ إنَّ اللهَ تَعَالَى يَقُولُ: يَا عِيسَى، إنِّي بَاعِثٌ بَعْدَكَ أُمَّةً، إنْ أَصَابَهُمْ مَا يُحِبُّونَ حَمِدُوا وشَكَرُوا، وإنْ أصَابَهُمْ مَا يَكْرَهُونَ احْتَسَبُوا وَصَبَرُوا، وَلا حِلْمَ وَلا عِلْمَ". قَالَ: يَا رَبِّ، كَيْفَ هَذَا لهُمْ، وَلا حِلْمَ وَلا عِلْمَ؟. قَالَ: "أُعْطِيهِمْ مِن حِلْمِي وعلمي" .

وَقَدْ وَرَدَتْ أحاديثُ يُنَاسِبُ ذكرُها هَاهُنَا:

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ، حَدَّثَنَا الْمَسْعُودِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا بُكَيْر بْنُ الأخْنَس، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الصِّدِّيقِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أُعْطِيتُ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفًا يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ، وُجُوهُهُمْ كَالْقَمَرِ لَيْلَةَ الْبَدْر، قُلُوبُهُمْ عَلَى قَلْبِ رَجُلٍ وَاحِدٍ، فَاسْتَزَدْتُ رَبِّي، عَزَّ وجَلَّ، فَزَادَنِي مَعَ كُل وَاحِدٍ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفًا". فَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ: فَرَأَيْتُ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ آتٍ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْقُرَى، ومصيبٌ مِنْ حَافَاتِ الْبَوَادِي .
