The theologians (mutakallimun) express this concept by the term takhyil (imaginative representation), which is the perception of something not sensory as though it were sensory, just as the sheep imagines the friendship of the ram and the enmity of the wolf.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُخَاطِبًا عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الَّذِينَ أُصِيبوا يومَ أُحُد، وقُتِل مِنْهُمْ سَبْعُونَ: ﴿قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ سُنَنٌ﴾ أَيْ: قَدْ جَرَى نَحْوَ هَذَا عَلَى الْأُمَمِ الَّذِينَ كَانُوا مَنْ قَبْلِكُمْ مِنْ أَتْبَاعِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ، ثُمَّ كَانَتِ الْعَاقِبَةُ لَهُمْ وَالدَّائِرَةُ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿فَسِيرُوا فِي الأرْضِ فَانْظُروا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ﴾ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿هَذَا بَيَانٌ لِلنَّاسِ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْقُرْآنَ فِيهِ بَيَانٌ لِلْأُمُورِ عَلَى جَلِيَّتِهَا، وَكَيْفَ كَانَ الأممُ الْأَقْدَمُونَ مَعَ أَعْدَائِهِمْ ﴿وَهُدًى وَمَوْعِظَةٌ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْقُرْآنَ فِيهِ خَبَرُ مَا قَبْلَكُمْ وَ ﴿هُدًى﴾ لِقُلُوبِكُمْ وَ ﴿مَوْعِظَةٌ﴾ أَيْ: زَاجِرٌ [عَنِ الْمَحَارِمِ وَالْمَآثِمِ] .

ثُمَّ قَالَ مُسَلِّيًا لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ: ﴿وَلا تَهِنُوا﴾ أَيْ: لَا تَضعفوا بِسَبَبِ مَا جَرَى ﴿وَلا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَنْتُمُ الأعْلَوْنَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ﴾

أَيِ: الْعَاقِبَةُ وَالنُّصْرَةُ لَكُمْ أَيُّهَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ.

﴿إِنْ يَمْسَسْكُمْ قَرْحٌ فَقَدْ مَسَّ الْقَوْمَ قَرْحٌ مِثْلُهُ﴾ أَيْ: إِنْ كُنْتُمْ قَدْ أَصَابَتْكُمْ جراحٌ وقُتل مِنْكُمْ طائفةٌ، فَقَدْ أَصَابَ أَعْدَاءَكُمْ قَرِيبٌ مِنْ ذَلِكَ مِنْ قَتْلٍ وَجِرَاحٍ ﴿وَتِلْكَ الأيَّامُ نُدَاوِلُهَا بَيْنَ النَّاسِ﴾ أَيْ: نُديل عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَعْدَاءَ تَارَةً، وَإِنْ كَانَتِ الْعَاقِبَةُ لَكُمْ لِمَا لَنَا فِي ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْحِكَمِ ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلِيَعْلَمَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: فِي مِثْلِ هَذَا لنَرَى، أَيْ: مَنْ يَصبر عَلَى مُنَاجَزَةِ الْأَعْدَاءِ ﴿وَيَتَّخِذَ مِنْكُمْ شُهَدَاءَ﴾ يَعْنِي: يُقْتَلُون فِي سَبِيلِهِ، ويَبْذُلون مُهَجهم فِي مَرْضَاتِهِ. ﴿وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الظَّالِمِينَ. وَلِيُمَحِّصَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا﴾ أَيْ: يُكَفِّرَ عَنْهُمْ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِمْ، إِنْ كَانَ لَهُمْ ذُنُوبٌ وَإِلَّا رُفعَ لَهُمْ فِي دَرَجَاتِهِمْ بِحَسَبِ مَا أُصِيبُوا بِهِ، وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَيَمْحَقَ الْكَافِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: فَإِنَّهُمْ إِذَا ظَفَرُوا بَغَوا وبَطروا فَيَكُونُ ذَلِكَ سَبَبَ دَمَارِهِمْ وَهَلَاكِهِمْ ومَحْقهم وَفَنَائِهِمْ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَنْ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ جَاهَدُوا مِنْكُمْ وَيَعْلَمَ الصَّابِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: أَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنْ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمْ تُبْتَلوا بِالْقِتَالِ وَالشَّدَائِدِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى فِي سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ: ﴿أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَنْ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِكُمْ مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ مَسَّتْهُمُ الْبَأْسَاءُ وَالضَّرَّاءُ وَزُلْزِلُوا [حَتَّى يَقُولَ الرَّسُولُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ مَتَى نَصْرُ اللَّهِ أَلا إِنَّ نَصْرَ اللَّهِ قَرِيبٌ] ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ:٢١٤] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿الم أَحَسِبَ النَّاسُ أَنْ يُتْرَكُوا أَنْ يَقُولُوا آمَنَّا وَهُمْ لَا يُفْتَنُونَ [وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ فَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ الْكَاذِبِينَ] ﴾ [الْعَنْكَبُوتِ:١-٣] ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَنْ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ جَاهَدُوا مِنْكُمْ وَيَعْلَمَ الصَّابِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: لَا يَحْصُلُ لَكُمْ دُخُولُ الْجَنَّةِ حَتَّى تُبْتَلَوا وَيَرَى اللَّهُ مِنْكُمُ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ وَالصَّابِرِينَ عَلَى مُقَارَنَةِ الْأَعْدَاءِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلَقَدْ كُنْتُمْ تَمَنَّوْنَ الْمَوْتَ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَلْقَوْهُ فَقَدْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ وَأَنْتُمْ تَنْظُرُونَ﴾ أَيْ: قَدْ كُنْتُمْ -أَيُّهَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ-قَبْلَ هَذَا الْيَوْمِ تَتَمَنَّوْنَ لِقَاءَ الْعَدُوِّ وَتَتَحَرَّقُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ، وَتَوَدُّونَ مُنَاجَزَتَهُمْ وَمُصَابَرَتَهُمْ، فَهَا قَدْ حَصَلَ لَكُمُ الَّذِي تَمَنَّيْتُمُوهُ وَطَلَبْتُمُوهُ، فدونَكم فَقَاتِلُوا وَصَابِرُوا.

وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "لَا تَمَنَّوْا لِقَاءَ الْعَدُوِّ، وَسَلُوا اللَّهَ الْعَافِيَةَ، فَإذَا لَقِيتُمُوهُمْ فَاصْبِرُوا، وَاعْلَمُوا أنَّ الْجَنَّةَ تَحْتَ ظِلالِ السُّيُوفِ" .

وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿فَقَدْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْمَوْتَ شَاهَدْتُمُوهُ فِي لَمَعان السُّيُوفِ وَحَدِّ الأسِنّة وَاشْتِبَاكِ الرِّماح، وَصُفُوفِ الرِّجَالِ لِلْقِتَالِ.

وَالْمُتَكَلِّمُونَ يُعَبِّرُونَ عَنْ هَذَا بالتخْييل، وَهُوَ مُشَاهَدَةُ مَا لَيْسَ بِمَحْسُوسٍ كَالْمَحْسُوسِ كَمَا تَتَخَيل الشَّاةَ صداقة الكبش وعداوة الذئب.

---

## 3:144-148 — verses 144-148 (shared commentary)

### Translation

When those among the Muslims who fled on the Day of Uhud fled, and those who were killed among them were killed, Satan cried out: "Truly Muhammad has been killed!" Ibn Qami'ah returned to the polytheists and said to them: "I have killed Muhammad." He had in fact struck the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and wounded his head, and this took hold in the hearts of many people, so that they believed the Messenger of Allah had indeed been killed, and they accepted this as possible for him, just as Allah has related concerning many of the prophets (peace be upon them). This produced weakness, faintheartedness, and withdrawal from the fighting. It was concerning this that Allah, Mighty and Majestic, sent down to His Messenger ﷺ: {Muhammad is not but a messenger; messengers have passed on before him}, meaning: he has a precedent in them regarding the message and regarding the possibility of being killed just as they were.

Ibn Abi Najih said, on the authority of his father, that a man from the Muhajirun passed by a man from the Ansar who was writhing in his own blood, and said to him: "O so-and-so, have you heard that Muhammad ﷺ has been killed?" The Ansari man said: "If Muhammad [ﷺ] has indeed been killed, then he has conveyed the message. So fight for your religion." Then it was revealed: {Muhammad is not but a messenger; messengers have passed on before him}. This was related by [the hafiz Abu Bakr] al-Bayhaqi in Dala'il al-Nubuwwah.

Then the Exalted said, rebuking those who had fallen into weakness: {If then he dies or is killed, will you turn back on your heels?}, meaning: will you retreat backward? {And whoever turns back on his heels will not harm Allah at all, and Allah will reward the grateful}, meaning: those who stood firm in obedience to Him, fought for His religion, and followed His Messenger both in his life and after his death.

Likewise, it is established in the authentic collections, the musnads, the sunan works, and other books of Islam, through multiple chains that establish certainty, and I have mentioned this in my Musnad of the two shaykhs, Abu Bakr and Umar, may Allah be pleased with both of them, that al-Siddiq, may Allah be pleased with him, recited this verse when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ died.

Al-Bukhari said: [chain] Yahya ibn Bukayr told us, al-Layth told us, from Uqayl, from Ibn Shihab, Abu Salamah informed me, that Aishah, may Allah be pleased with her, informed him that Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, came on a horse from his dwelling at al-Sunh until he dismounted and entered the mosque. He did not speak to the people until he went in to Aishah, and made his way toward the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who was covered with a striped cloth. He uncovered his face [ﷺ], then bent over him, kissed him, and wept. Then he said: "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you. By Allah, Allah will never combine two deaths upon you. As for the death that was decreed for you, you have already tasted it."

Al-Zuhri said: Abu Salamah narrated to me from Ibn Abbas, that Abu Bakr came out while Umar was speaking to the people, and he said: "Sit down, O Umar." But Umar refused to sit. The people turned toward Abu Bakr and left Umar. Abu Bakr said: "To proceed: whoever used to worship Muhammad, then Muhammad has indeed died. And whoever used to worship Allah, then Allah is living and does not die." Allah the Exalted has said: {Muhammad is not but a messenger; messengers have passed on before him} up to His saying: {and Allah will reward the grateful}. He said: "By Allah, it was as if the people had not known that Allah had sent down this verse until Abu Bakr recited it, so the people received it from him, all of them, and no one heard it thereafter without reciting it himself."

Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab informed me that Umar said: "By Allah, no sooner had I heard Abu Bakr recite it than I was so stunned that my legs could not support me, and I fell down to the ground."

Abu al-Qasim al-Tabarani said: [chain] Ali ibn Abd al-Aziz told us, Amr ibn Hammad ibn Talhah al-Qannad told us, Asbat ibn Nasr told us, from Simak ibn Harb, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, that Ali used to say during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah, regarding {If then he dies or is killed, will you turn back on your heels?}: "By Allah, we will not turn back on our heels after Allah has guided us. By Allah, if he dies or is killed, I will fight for that for which he fought until I die. By Allah, I am truly his brother, his ally, his cousin, and his heir. So who has more right to him than I?"

Concerning His saying: {And it is not for any soul to die except by the permission of Allah, at a decreed term}, meaning: no one dies except by the decree of Allah, and only after fulfilling the term that Allah has set for him. This is why He said: {at a decreed term}, similar to His saying: {And no aged person is granted a long life, nor is anything diminished of his life, except that it is in a record} [Fatir: 11], and similar to His saying: {He it is who created you from clay, then decreed a term, and a specified term is with Him} [al-An'am: 2].

This verse contains encouragement for the fearful and inducement for them toward fighting, for advancing and retreating neither diminish nor increase one's lifespan, as Ibn Abi Hatim said:

[chain] Al-Abbas ibn Yazid al-Abdi told us, he said: I heard Abu Mu'awiyah, from al-A'mash, from Habib ibn Suhban, who said: A man among the Muslims, namely Hujr ibn Adi, said: "What prevents you from crossing over to this enemy, over this little trickle of water?" meaning the Tigris. {And it is not for any soul to die except by the permission of Allah, at a decreed term}. Then he drove his horse into the Tigris, and when he plunged in, the people plunged in as well. When the enemy saw them, they said: "Demons!" and fled.

Concerning His saying: {And whoever desires the reward of this world, We will give him of it, and whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter, We will give him of it}, meaning: whoever's deeds are for this world will attain from it whatever Allah has decreed for him, but he will have no share in the Hereafter. And whoever intends by his deeds the Abode of the Hereafter, Allah will give him from it along with what has been allotted to him in this world, as He said: {Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, We increase for him his harvest, and whoever desires the harvest of this world, We give him thereof, but he will have no share in the Hereafter} [al-Shura: 20]. And the Exalted said: {Whoever desires the immediate life, We hasten for him therein what We will, for whomever We intend, then We have made for him Hell, which he will burn in, disgraced and rejected. And whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it with the striving it deserves, while he is a believer, then those, their striving will be appreciated} [al-Isra: 18-19]. Likewise here He said: {and We will reward the grateful}, meaning: We will give them of Our bounty and mercy in this world and the Hereafter according to their gratitude and their deeds.

Then the Exalted said, consoling the Muslims for what had occurred in their hearts on the Day of Uhud: {And how many a prophet fought alongside whom were many devoted men of Allah}. It has been said that its meaning is: how many a prophet was killed, and many devoted men from among his companions were killed alongside him. This is the view chosen by Ibn Jarir, for he said: as for those who read it as {Ribbiyun kathir were killed alongside him}, they said that the killing is meant to refer to the prophet and to some, not all, of the devoted men who were with him, and that the negation of weakness and faintheartedness applies to those devoted men who remained and were not killed.

He said: and whoever reads it as {qatala} (fought) chose that reading because he said: if they had been killed, then His saying {they did not weaken} would have no clear sense, for it would be impossible to describe them as not having weakened or become faint after having been killed.

Then he preferred the reading of those who read {Ribbiyun kathir were killed alongside him}, because Allah, the Exalted, rebuked, in these verses and those preceding them, those who fled on the Day of Uhud and abandoned the fighting, or who heard the crier crying out: "Muhammad has been killed." So Allah reproached them for their flight and their abandonment of the fighting, and said to them: {If then he dies or is killed}, O believers, will you apostatize from your religion and turn back on your heels?

It has also been said: how many a prophet was there before whom many devoted men from among his companions were killed.

The words of Ibn Ishaq in the Sirah suggest another view, for he said: that is, how many a prophet was struck by death, and with him were Ribbiyun, meaning groups of people, and they did not weaken after their prophet, nor did they become faint before their enemy, nor did they submit meekly to what befell them in striving for Allah and for their religion, and that is patience. {And Allah loves the patient}.

Thus he made His saying {with whom were many devoted men} a circumstantial clause. Al-Suhayli championed this view and argued for it strongly, and it has a valid basis in His saying {they did not weaken because of what befell them}, and so forth. Likewise al-Umawi related it in his Maghazi from the book of Muhammad ibn Ibrahim, and no one else has stated otherwise.

Some read it as {qatala ma'ahu ribbiyyun kathir} (many devoted men fought alongside him). Sufyan al-Thawri said, from Asim, from Zirr, from Ibn Mas'ud: {ribbiyyun kathir} means thousands.

Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Ikrimah, al-Hasan, Qatadah, al-Suddi, al-Rabi', and Ata al-Khurasani said: al-Ribbiyyun means large multitudes.

Abd al-Razzaq said, from Ma'mar, from al-Hasan: {ribbiyyun kathir} means many scholars. And from him also: patient, righteous, God-fearing scholars.

Ibn Jarir related, from some of the grammarians of Basrah, that al-Ribbiyyun are those who worship the Lord, Mighty and Majestic. He said: some responded to this, saying: if that were so, it would be said "Rabbiyyun," with a fatha on the ra.

Ibn Zayd said: "al-Ribbiyyun are the followers and the common folk, while al-Rabbabiyyun are the rulers."

{So they did not weaken because of what befell them in the way of Allah, nor did they become faint, nor did they submit meekly}. Qatadah and al-Rabi' ibn Anas said: {nor did they become faint} at the killing of their prophet, {nor did they submit meekly} means: they did not turn away from supporting them nor from their religion, but rather they fought for what the Prophet of Allah had fought for until they met Allah.

Ibn Abbas said: {nor did they submit meekly} means they did not become humbled. Al-Suddi and Ibn Zayd said: nor did they submit to their enemy.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq, Qatadah, and al-Suddi said: meaning that did not befall them when their prophet was killed.

{And Allah loves the patient. And their words were nothing but that they said: Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our excess in our affair, make firm our feet, and grant us victory over the disbelieving people}, meaning: they had no habitual utterance except this.

{So Allah gave them the reward of this world}, meaning: victory, triumph, and good outcome, {and the good reward of the Hereafter}, meaning: He combined this for them along with that. {And Allah loves those who do good}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

لَمَّا انْهَزَمَ مَنِ انْهَزَمَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ يَوْمَ أُحُد، وقُتِل مَنْ قُتِلَ مِنْهُمْ، نَادَى الشَّيْطَانُ: أَلَا إِنَّ مُحَمَّدًا قَدْ قُتل. وَرَجَعَ ابْنُ قَمِيئَةَ إِلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ: قتلتُ مُحَمَّدًا. وَإِنَّمَا كَانَ قَدْ ضَرَبَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَشَجَّه فِي رَأْسِهِ، فَوَقَعَ ذَلِكَ فِي قُلُوبِ كَثِيرٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَاعْتَقَدُوا أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَدْ قُتل، وَجَوَّزُوا عَلَيْهِ ذَلِكَ، كَمَا قَدْ قَصَّ اللَّهُ عَنْ كَثِيرٍ مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ، عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ، فَحَصَلَ وهَن وَضَعْفٌ وتَأخر عَنِ الْقِتَالِ فَفِي ذَلِكَ أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] عَلَى رَسُولِهِ ﷺ: ﴿وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ﴾ أَيْ: لَهُ أسْوة بِهِمْ فِي الرِّسَالَةِ وَفِي جَوَازِ الْقَتْلِ عَلَيْهِ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي نَجيح، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ مَر عَلَى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ وَهُوَ يَتَشَحَّطُ فِي دَمِهِ، فَقَالَ لَهُ: يَا فُلَانُ أشعرتَ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا ﷺ قَدْ قُتِل؟ فَقَالَ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ: إِنْ كَانَ مُحَمَّدٌ [ﷺ] قَدْ قُتِل فَقَدْ بَلَّغَ، فَقَاتِلُوا عَنْ دِينِكُمْ، فَنَزَلَ: ﴿وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ﴾ رَوَاهُ [الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ] الْبَيْهَقِيُّ فِي دَلَائِلِ النُّبُوَّةِ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى مُنْكِرًا عَلَى مَنْ حَصَلَ لَهُ ضَعْفٌ: ﴿أَفَإِنْ مَاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انْقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَى أَعْقَابِكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: رَجَعْتُمُ القَهْقرى ﴿وَمَنْ يَنْقَلِبْ عَلَى عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَنْ يَضُرَّ اللَّهَ شَيْئًا وَسَيَجْزِي اللَّهُ الشَّاكِرِينَ﴾ أَيِ: الَّذِينَ قَامُوا بِطَاعَتِهِ وَقَاتَلُوا عَنْ دِينِهِ، وَاتَّبَعُوا رَسُولَهُ حَيًّا وَمَيِّتًا.

وَكَذَلِكَ ثَبَتَ فِي الصِّحَاحِ وَالْمَسَانِدِ وَالسُّنَنِ وَغَيْرِهَا مِنْ كُتْبِ الْإِسْلَامِ مَنْ طُرق مُتَعَدِّدَةٍ تُفِيدُ القطْع، وَقَدْ ذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ فِي مُسْندي الشَّيْخَيْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ وعُمَرَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا؛ أَنَّ الصِّدِّيقَ -رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ-تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ لَمَّا مَاتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ .

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ بُكَير، حَدَّثَنَا اللَّيْثُ، عَنْ عُقيل عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو سَلَمة؛ أَنَّ عَائِشَةَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا، أَخْبَرَتْهُ أَنَّ أَبَا بَكْرٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَقْبَلُ عَلَى فَرَس مِنْ مَسْكنه بالسَّنْح حَتَّى نَزَلَ فَدَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ، فَلَمْ يُكلم النَّاسَ حَتَّى دَخَلَ عَلَى عَائِشَةَ فتيمَّم رَسُول اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم وَهُوَ مُغَشى بِثَوْبٍ حِبَرَةٍ، فَكَشَفَ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ [ﷺ] ثُمَّ أَكُبَّ عَلَيْهِ وقَبَّله وَبَكَى، ثُمَّ قَالَ: بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَأُمِّي. وَاللَّهِ لَا يَجْمَعُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ موْتَتَين؛ أَمَّا الْمَوْتَةُ الَّتِي كُتبت عَلَيْكَ فَقَدْ مُتَّها.

وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ: وَحَدَّثَنِي أَبُو سَلمة عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ أَبَا بَكْرٍ خَرَجَ وَعُمَرُ يُحَدِّث النَّاسَ فَقَالَ: اجْلِسْ يَا عُمَرُ فَأَبَى عمرُ أَنْ يَجْلِسَ، فَأَقْبَلَ النَّاسُ إِلَيْهِ وَتَرَكُوا عُمَرَ، فَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: أَمَّا بَعْدُ، مَنْ كانَ يَعْبُدُ مُحَمَّدًا فَإِنَّ مُحَمَّدًا قَدْ مَاتَ، وَمَنْ كَانَ يَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ حَيّ لَا يَمُوتُ، قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَسَيَجْزِي اللَّهُ الشَّاكِرِينَ﴾ قَالَ: فَوَاللَّهِ لكَأنّ النَّاسَ لَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ أَنْزَلَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ حَتَّى تَلَاهَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ، فَتَلَقَّاهَا النَّاسُ مِنْهُ كُلُّهُمْ، فَمَا سَمِعَهَا بَشَرٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ إِلَّا تَلَاهَا .

وَأَخْبَرَنِي سَعِيدُ بْنُ المُسَيَّب أَنَّ عُمر قَالَ: وَاللَّهِ مَا هُوَ إِلَّا أَنْ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ تَلَاهَا فَعقرتُ حَتَّى مَا تُقِلُّنِي رِجْلَايَ وَحَتَّى هَوَيتُ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ .

وَقَالَ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ الطَّبَرَانِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ حَمَّادِ بْنِ طَلْحَةَ القنَّاد، حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ نَصْرٍ، عَنْ سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْب، عَنْ عكْرمة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّ عَلِيًّا كَانَ يَقُولُ فِي حَيَاةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ: ﴿أَفَإِنْ مَاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انْقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَى أَعْقَابِكُمْ﴾ وَاللَّهِ لَا نَنْقَلِبُ عَلَى أَعْقَابِنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَانَا اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ لَئِنْ مَاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ لَأُقَاتِلَنَّ عَلَى مَا قَاتَلَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى أَمُوتَ، وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأَخُوهُ، ووليُّه، وَابْنُ عَمِّهِ، وَوَارِثُهُ فَمَنْ أَحَقُّ بِهِ مِنِّي؟ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَا كَانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَنْ تَمُوتَ إِلا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ كِتَابًا مُؤَجَّلا﴾ أَيْ: لَا يَمُوتُ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا بِقَدَرِ اللَّهِ، وَحَتَّى يَسْتَوْفِيَ المدةَ الَّتِي ضَرَبَهَا اللَّهُ لَهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿كِتَابًا مُؤَجَّلا﴾ كَقَوْلِهِ ﴿وَمَا يُعَمَّرُ مِنْ مُعَمَّرٍ وَلا يُنْقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِ إِلا فِي كِتَابٍ﴾ [فَاطِرِ:١١] وَكَقَوْلِهِ ﴿هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ ثُمَّ قَضَى أَجَلا وَأَجَلٌ مُسَمًّى عِنْدَهُ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ:٢] .

وَهَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِيهَا تَشْجِيعٌ للجُبَناء وَتَرْغِيبٌ لَهُمْ فِي الْقِتَالِ، فَإِنَّ الْإِقْدَامَ وَالْإِحْجَامَ لَا يَنْقُص مِنَ الْعُمُرِ وَلَا يَزِيدُ فِيهِ كَمَا قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ:

حَدَّثَنَا الْعَبَّاسُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ الْعَبْدِيُّ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ صُهبان، قَالَ: قَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ -وَهُوَ حُجْرُ بْنُ عَدِيّ-: مَا يَمْنَعُكُمْ أَنْ تعبُروا إِلَى هَؤُلَاءِ الْعَدُوِّ، هَذِهِ النُّطْفَةُ؟ -يَعْنِي دِجْلَة- ﴿وَمَا كَانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَنْ تَمُوتَ إِلا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ كِتَابًا مُؤَجَّلا﴾ ثُمَّ أَقْحَمَ فَرَسَهُ دِجْلَةَ فَلَمَّا أَقْحَمَ أَقْحَمَ النَّاسُ فَلَمَّا رَآهُمُ الْعَدُوُّ قَالُوا: ديوان، فهربوا .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الآخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا﴾ أَيْ: مَنْ كَانَ عَمَلُهُ لِلدُّنْيَا فَقَدْ نَالَ مِنْهَا مَا قدّرَه اللَّهُ لَهُ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ [مِنْ] نَصِيبٍ، وَمَنْ قَصَدَ بِعَمَلِهِ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ أَعْطَاهُ اللَّهُ مِنْهَا مَعَ مَا قَسَّمَ لَهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا كَمَا قَالَ: ﴿مَنْ كَانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الآخِرَةِ نزدْ لَهُ فِي حَرْثِهِ وَمَنْ كَانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا وَمَا لَهُ فِي الآخِرَةِ مِنْ نَصِيبٍ﴾ [الشُّورَى:٢٠] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿مَنْ كَانَ يُرِيدُ الْعَاجِلَةَ عَجَّلْنَا لَهُ فِيهَا مَا نَشَاءُ لِمَنْ نُرِيدُ ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا لَهُ جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلاهَا مَذْمُومًا مَدْحُورًا وَمَنْ أَرَادَ الآخِرَةَ وَسَعَى لَهَا سَعْيَهَا وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولَئِكَ كَانَ سَعْيُهُمْ مَشْكُورًا﴾ [الْإِسْرَاءِ: ١٨، ١٩] وَهَكَذَا قَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿وَسَنَجْزِي الشَّاكِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: سَنُعْطِيهِمْ مِنْ فَضْلِنَا وَرَحْمَتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ بِحَسْبِ شُكْرهم وَعَمَلِهِمْ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى -مُسَلِّيًا لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ عَمَّا كَانَ وَقَعَ فِي نُفُوسِهِمْ يَوْمَ أُحُد-: ﴿وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ قَاتَلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ﴾ قِيلَ: مَعْنَاهُ: كَمْ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ قُتِل وَقُتِلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ كَثِيرٌ. وَهَذَا الْقَوْلُ هُوَ اخْتِيَارُ ابْنِ جَرِيرٍ، فَإِنَّهُ قَالَ: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ قرؤوا: ﴿قُتِلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ﴾ فَإِنَّهُمْ قَالُوا: إِنَّمَا عَنَى بِالْقَتْلِ النَّبِيَّ وَبَعْضَ مِنْ مَعَهُ مِنَ الرِّبِّيِّينَ دُونَ جَمِيعِهِمْ، وَإِنَّمَا نَفَى الْوَهَنَ وَالضَّعْفَ عَمَّنْ بَقِيَ مِنَ الرِّبِّيِّينَ مِمَّنْ لَمْ يُقْتَلْ.

قَالَ: وَمَنْ قَرَأَ ﴿قَاتَلَ﴾ فَإِنَّهُ اخْتَارَ ذَلِكَ لِأَنَّهُ قَالَ: لَوْ قُتِلُوا لَمْ يَكُنْ لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَمَا وَهَنُوا﴾ وَجْهٌ مَعْرُوفٌ؛ لِأَنَّهُمْ يَسْتَحِيلُ أَنْ يُوصَفوا بِأَنَّهُمْ لَمْ يَهِنُوا وَلَمْ يَضْعُفُوا بَعْدَ مَا قُتِلُوا.

ثُمَّ اخْتَارَ قِرَاءَةَ مَنْ قَرَأَ ﴿قُتِلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ﴾ ؛ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ [تَعَالَى] عَاتَبَ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَاتِ وَالَّتِي قَبْلَهَا مَنِ انْهَزَمَ يَوْمَ أُحُدٍ، وَتَرَكُوا الْقِتَالَ أَوْ سَمِعُوا الصَّائِحَ يَصِيحُ: "إِنَّ مُحَمَّدًا قَدْ قُتِلَ". فَعَذَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ عَلَى فِرَارِهِمْ وترْكِهم الْقِتَالَ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ: ﴿أَفَإِنْ مَاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ﴾ أَيُّهَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ارْتَدَدْتُمْ عَنْ دِينِكُمْ وَانْقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَى أَعْقَابِكُمْ؟.

وَقِيلَ: وَكَمْ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ قُتِلَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ .

وَكَلَامُ ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ فِي السِّيرَةِ يَقْتَضِي قَوْلًا آخَرَ، [فَإِنَّهُ] قَالَ: أَيْ وَكَأَيْنٍ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ أَصَابَهُ الْقَتْلُ، وَمَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ، أَيْ: جَمَاعَاتٌ فَمَا وَهَنُوا بَعْدَ نَبِيِّهِمْ، وَمَا ضَعُفُوا عَنْ عَدُوِّهِمْ، وَمَا اسْتَكَانُوا لِمَا أَصَابَهُمْ فِي الْجِهَادِ عَنِ اللَّهِ وَعَنْ دِينِهِمْ، وَذَلِكَ الصَّبْرُ، ﴿وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الصَّابِرِينَ﴾ .

فَجَعَلَ قَوْلَهُ: ﴿مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ﴾ حَالًا وَقَدْ نَصَرَ هَذَا الْقَوْلَ السُّهَيْلِيُّ وَبَالَغَ فِيهِ، وَلَهُ اتِّجَاهٌ لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَمَا وَهَنُوا لِمَا أَصَابَهُمْ﴾ الْآيَةَ، وَكَذَلِكَ حَكَاهُ الْأُمَوِيُّ فِي مَغَازِيهِ، عَنْ كِتَابِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَلَمْ يَقُلْ غَيْرُهُ.

وَقَرَأَ بَعْضُهُمْ: ﴿قَاتَلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ﴾ قَالَ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ زِرٍّ، عن ابن مَسْعُودٍ ﴿رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ﴾ أَيْ: أُلُوفٌ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٌ وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَير، وعِكْرِمة، وَالْحَسَنُ، وَقَتَادَةُ، والسُّدِّي، والرَّبِيع، وَعَطَاءٌ الْخُرَاسَانِيِّ: الرِّبِّيُّونَ: الْجُمُوعُ الْكَثِيرَةُ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَر عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: ﴿رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ﴾ أَيْ: عُلَمَاءُ كَثِيرٌ، وَعَنْهُ أَيْضًا: عُلَمَاءُ صُبُرٌ أَبْرَارُ أَتْقِيَاءُ.

وَحَكَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، عَنْ بَعْضِ نُحَاةِ الْبَصْرَةِ: أَنَّ الرِّبِّيِّينَ هُمُ الَّذِينَ يَعْبُدُونَ الرَّبَّ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، قَالَ: وَرَدَّ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ: لَوْ كَانَ كَذَلِكَ لَقِيلَ رَبيون، بِفَتْحِ الرَّاءِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ زَيْدٍ: "الرِّبِّيُّونَ: الْأَتْبَاعُ، وَالرَّعِيَّةُ، وَالرَّبَّابِيُّونَ: الْوُلَاةُ.

﴿فَمَا وَهَنُوا لِمَا أَصَابَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَمَا ضَعُفُوا وَمَا اسْتَكَانُوا﴾ قَالَ قَتَادَةُ وَالرَّبِيعُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ: ﴿وَمَا ضَعُفُوا﴾ بِقَتْلِ نَبِيِّهِمْ ﴿وَمَا اسْتَكَانُوا﴾ يَقُولُ: فَمَا ارْتَدُّوا عَنْ نُصْرَتِهِمْ وَلَا عَنْ دِينِهِمْ، أنْ قَاتَلُوا عَلَى مَا قَاتَلَ عَلَيْهِ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ حَتَّى لَحِقُوا بِاللَّهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ﴿وَمَا اسْتَكَانُوا﴾ تَخَشَّعوا. وَقَالَ السُّدِّي وَابْنُ زَيْدٍ: وَمَا ذَلُّوا لِعَدُوِّهِمْ.

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، وَقَتَادَةُ وَالسُّدِّيُّ: أَيْ مَا أَصَابَهُمْ ذَلِكَ حِينَ قُتِل نَبِيُّهُمْ.

﴿وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الصَّابِرِينَ. وَمَا كَانَ قَوْلَهُمْ إِلا أَنْ قَالُوا رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِي أَمْرِنَا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ هِجيرى إِلَّا ذَلِكَ.

﴿فَآتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ ثَوَابَ الدُّنْيَا﴾ أَيِ: النَّصْرَ وَالظَّفَرَ وَالْعَاقِبَةَ ﴿وَحُسْنَ ثَوَابِ الآخِرَةِ﴾ أَيْ: جمَع لَهُمْ ذَلِكَ مَعَ هَذَا، ﴿وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ﴾ .

---

## 3:149-153 — verses 149-153 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty warns His believing servants against obeying the disbelievers and the hypocrites, for obeying them brings about ruin in this world and the hereafter. Hence He said: {If you obey those who have disbelieved, they will turn you back on your heels, and you will end up losers}.

Then He commanded them to obey Him, to take Him as their ally, to seek help from Him, and to rely upon Him, saying: {Rather, Allah is your protector, and He is the best of helpers}.

Then He gave them the glad tidings that He would cast into the hearts of their enemies fear of them and humiliation before them, because of their disbelief and their shirk (associating partners with Allah), along with what He had stored up for them in the hereafter of punishment and chastisement. He said: {We will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieved because they associated with Allah that for which He had not sent down any authority, and their abode is the Fire, and wretched is the dwelling place of the wrongdoers}.

It is established in the Sahihayn (the two Sahihs) on the authority of Jabir ibn Abd Allah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I have been given five things which no prophet before me was given: I was granted victory through terror cast a month's journey ahead of me, the earth was made for me a place of prayer and a means of purification, the spoils of war were made lawful for me, I was given intercession, and every prophet used to be sent to his own people specifically, while I was sent to all of mankind generally."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from Abu Umamah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "My Lord favored me over the prophets, or he said, over the nations, in four things." He said: "I was sent to all of mankind, the entire earth was made for me and for my nation a place of prayer and a means of purification, so wherever the time of prayer overtakes a man of my nation, there is his place of prayer and his means of purification. I was granted victory through terror cast a month's journey ahead of me into the hearts of my enemies, and the spoils of war were made lawful for me."

Al-Tirmidhi narrated it through the hadith of Sulayman al-Taymi, from Sayyar al-Qurashi al-Umawi, their client, of Damascus, who settled in Basra, from Abu Umamah Sudayy ibn Ajlan, may Allah be pleased with him, with this wording. He said: hasan sahih (a sound and authentic hadith).

Sa'id ibn Mansur said: [chain] that Abu Yunus narrated to him, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I was granted victory over the enemy through terror."

Muslim narrated it through the hadith of Ibn Wahb.

Imam Ahmad narrated: [chain] from Abu Musa, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I have been given five things: I was sent to the red and the black, the earth was made for me a means of purification and a place of prayer, the spoils of war were made lawful for me though they were not lawful for anyone before me, I was granted victory through terror for a month, I was given intercession, and there is no prophet but that he has asked for his intercession, but I have saved mine, then I have made it for whoever dies not associating anything with Allah."

Ahmad alone narrated this.

Al-Awfi narrated from Ibn Abbas concerning His saying: {We will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieved}, that he said: Allah cast terror into the heart of Abu Sufyan, so he returned to Mecca, and the Prophet ﷺ said: "Abu Sufyan has struck a portion of you and has returned, and Allah has cast terror into his heart." Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it.

As for His saying: {And Allah had certainly fulfilled His promise to you when you were killing them by His permission}, Ibn Abbas said: Allah promised them victory.

This verse may be used as evidence for one of the two earlier views concerning His saying: {When you said to the believers, "Is it not sufficient for you that your Lord should reinforce you with three thousand angels sent down? Yes, if you remain patient and fear Allah and they come upon you suddenly, your Lord will reinforce you with five thousand angels having marks"}, namely that this occurred on the day of Uhud, because their enemy numbered three thousand fighters. When they confronted them, victory and triumph belonged to Islam at the beginning of the day. But when what happened occurred, of the archers' disobedience and the failure of some of the fighters, the promise that had been conditioned upon steadfastness and obedience was delayed. Hence He said: {And Allah had certainly fulfilled His promise to you}, that is, at the beginning of the day, {when you were killing them}, that is, slaying them, {by His permission}, that is, by His giving you power over them, {until when you lost courage}. Ibn Jurayj said: Ibn Abbas said: al-fashal (losing courage) means cowardice, {and disputed among yourselves concerning the order and disobeyed}, as happened with the archers, {after He had shown you that which you love}, which was the victory over them, {among you were some who desired this world}, and they were those who inclined toward the spoils when they saw the defeat, {and among you were some who desired the hereafter. Then He turned you back from them so that He might test you}, then He gave them the upper hand over you in order to try you and test you, {and He has certainly forgiven you}, that is, He forgave you for that deed. This, and Allah knows best, was because of the great number and equipment of the enemy, and the small number and equipment of the Muslims.

Ibn Jurayj said concerning His saying: {and He has certainly forgiven you}, that He did not annihilate you completely. Muhammad ibn Ishaq said the same. Ibn Jarir narrated both of these. {And Allah is possessor of bounty for the believers}.

Imam Ahmad narrated: [chain] from Ubayd Allah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Allah never granted victory in any battle as He granted it on the day of Uhud. He said: We found that strange, so Ibn Abbas said: Between me and whoever finds that strange is the Book of Allah. Allah says concerning the day of Uhud: {And Allah had certainly fulfilled His promise to you when you were killing them by His permission}. Ibn Abbas says: al-hass means killing, {until when you lost courage and disputed among yourselves concerning the order and disobeyed after He had shown you that which you love, among you were some who desired this world and among you were some who desired the hereafter} to the end of the verse. He meant by this the archers, because the Prophet ﷺ had stationed them in a position, then said: "Protect our backs. If you see us being killed, do not come to our aid, and if you see us gaining spoils, do not join us in them." So when the Prophet ﷺ gained the spoils and the Muslims took possession of the polytheists' camp, the archers all rushed down together and entered the camp to plunder, while the ranks of the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had come together and were thus, interlocking their hands together, and had become entangled. When the archers abandoned that gap which they had been guarding, the cavalry entered upon the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ from that spot, and they struck one another and became confused, and many of the Muslims were killed. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his companions had had the advantage at the beginning of the day, until seven or nine of the bearers of the polytheists' banner were killed. Then the Muslims scattered in a rush toward the mountain, but they did not reach, as people say, the cave; rather it was beneath al-Mihras. Satan cried out: Muhammad has been killed! And there was no doubt that it was true. We remained thus, not doubting that it was true, until the Messenger of Allah ﷺ appeared between the two Sa'ds, and we recognized him by the way he turned his head as he walked. He said: We rejoiced as though nothing that had befallen us had happened. He said: He climbed up toward us saying: "Allah's anger has grown intense against a people who bloodied the face of the Messenger of Allah." And another time he said: "O Allah, they have no right to prevail over us." Until he reached us, and he stayed there a while. Then Abu Sufyan cried out from the bottom of the mountain: "Show your superiority, Hubal!" twice, meaning his gods, "Where is the son of Abu Kabshah? Where is the son of Abu Quhafah? Where is the son of al-Khattab?" Umar said: "O Messenger of Allah, shall I answer him?" He said: "Yes." He said: When he said, "Show your superiority, Hubal," Umar said: "Allah is more exalted and more glorious." Abu Sufyan said: "Good for it then, so return to it," or "so proceed." Then he said: "Where is the son of Abu Kabshah? Where is the son of Abu Quhafah? Where is the son of al-Khattab?" Umar said: "This is the Messenger of Allah, and this is Abu Bakr, and here I am, Umar." Abu Sufyan said: "A day for a day like the day of Badr; days alternate between people, and war is a matter of turns." Umar said: "Not equal, our dead are in Paradise and your dead are in the Fire." He said: "You claim that, so then we have failed and lost." Then Abu Sufyan said: "You will find mutilation among your dead, but that was not by the opinion of our leaders." He said: Then pre-Islamic pride overcame him, and he said: "As for that, if it happened, we did not dislike it."

This is a gharib hadith (singular report), and a strange chain of transmission, and it is among the mursal (chain missing a link) reports of Ibn Abbas, for he did not witness Uhud, nor did his father.

Al-Hakim recorded it in his Mustadrak, [chain] from Sulayman ibn Dawud ibn Ali ibn Abd Allah ibn Abbas, with this wording.

Ibn Abi Hatim and al-Bayhaqi likewise related it in Dala'il al-Nubuwwah, from the hadith of Sulayman ibn Dawud al-Hashimi, with the same wording, and part of it has corroborating reports in the authentic collections and elsewhere. Imam Ahmad said:

Affan told us, Hammad told us, Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib told us, from al-Sha'bi, from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: The women were, on the day of Uhud, behind the Muslims, finishing off the wounded among the polytheists. Had I sworn an oath that day, I would have hoped to be truthful in saying that none of us desired this worldly life, until God, mighty and exalted, revealed: {Among you were some who desired this world and among you were some who desired the Hereafter. Then He turned you back from them that He might test you.} When the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ disobeyed and defied what they had been commanded, the Messenger of God ﷺ was left isolated among nine men: seven from the Ansar and two from Quraysh, he being the tenth of them. When they pressed hard upon him, [he said: "May God have mercy on a man who drives them back from us." A man from the Ansar rose and fought for a while until he was killed. When they pressed upon him] again, he said: "May God have mercy on a man who drives them back from us." He kept saying this until all seven were killed. Then the Messenger of God ﷺ said to his companion: "We have not been fair to our companions."

Then Abu Sufyan came forward and called out: "Exalted be Hubal!" The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Say: God is more exalted and more majestic." So they said: "God is more exalted and more majestic." Abu Sufyan said: "We have al-Uzza, and you have no Uzza." The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Say: God is our protector, and the disbelievers have no protector." Then Abu Sufyan said: "A day for the day of Badr. A day against us and a day for us, a day we suffer loss and a day we gain victory. Hanzalah for Hanzalah, so-and-so for so-and-so, so-and-so for so-and-so." The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Not equal. As for our slain, they are alive, provided for, but your slain are in the Fire, being punished." Abu Sufyan said: "There has indeed been mutilation among the people, though it was not by the counsel of any assembly among us. I neither commanded it nor forbade it, and I neither loved it nor disliked it, and it neither grieved me nor pleased me." Then, they looked, and there was Hamzah, his belly having been cut open, and Hind had taken his liver and chewed it but could not swallow it. The Messenger of God ﷺ asked: "Did she eat any of it?" They said: "No." He said: "God would never let any part of Hamzah enter the Fire."

Then the Messenger of God ﷺ placed Hamzah down and prayed over him. A man from the Ansar was brought and placed beside him, and he prayed over him too. The Ansari man was then lifted away and Hamzah left in place. Then another was brought and placed beside Hamzah, and he prayed [over him], then he too was lifted away and Hamzah left in place, until he had prayed over him that day seventy times.

Ahmad alone related this.

Al-Bukhari said: Ubaydullah ibn Musa told us, from Isra'il, from Abu Ishaq, from al-Bara', who said: We met the polytheists that day, and the Prophet ﷺ had stationed a company of archers, placing Abdullah, meaning Ibn Jubayr, in command of them, and said: "Do not leave your position. If you see us prevailing over them, still do not leave it, and if you see them prevailing over us, do not come to our aid." When we met them, they fled, until we saw the women running swiftly up the mountain, lifting their legs, their anklets exposed. Then our men began saying: "The spoils, the spoils!" Abdullah said: "The Prophet ﷺ made a covenant with me that you should not leave this place." But they refused, and when they refused, God turned their faces away, and seventy of them were killed. Then Abu Sufyan appeared and called out: "Is Muhammad among the people?" The Prophet ﷺ said: "Do not answer him." He called: "Is the son of Abu Quhafah among the people?" He said: "Do not answer him." He called: "Is the son of al-Khattab among the people?" Then he said: "These men have been killed, for had they been alive they would have answered." Umar could not restrain himself and said: "You lie, enemy of God. God has kept alive for you what will grieve you." Then Abu Sufyan said: "Exalted be Hubal!" The Prophet ﷺ said: "Answer him." They said: "What shall we say?" He said: "Say: God is more exalted and more majestic." Abu Sufyan said: "We have al-Uzza, and you have no Uzza." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Answer him." They said: "What shall we say?" He said: "Say: God is our protector, and you have no protector." Abu Sufyan said: "A day for the day of Badr. War is turns of fortune. You will find mutilation, though I did not order it, and it did not displease me."

Al-Bukhari related this alone by this route, then he related it from Amr ibn Khalid, from Zuhayr ibn Mu'awiyah, from Abu Ishaq, from al-Bara', similarly, and it will come again in fuller form.

Al-Bukhari also said: Ubaydullah ibn Sa'id told us, Abu Usamah told us, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Aishah, may God be pleased with her, who said: On the day of Uhud, when the polytheists were routed, Satan cried out: "O servants of God, beware those behind you!" So their front ranks turned back, and they clashed with their own rear ranks. Hudhayfah caught sight of someone and it turned out to be his own father, al-Yaman. He cried: "O servants of God, my father, my father!" But by God, they did not restrain themselves until they had killed him. Hudhayfah said: "May God forgive you." Urwah said: By God, some remnant of goodness never left Hudhayfah until he met God, mighty and exalted.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said: Yahya ibn Abbad ibn Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr told me, from his father, from his grandfather, that al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam said: By God, I saw myself looking at the servants of [Hind] and her companions, skirts hitched up, fleeing, with nothing at all, little or much, to hinder their flight. The archers had inclined toward the camp when we had driven the enemy away from it, wanting the spoils, and left our backs exposed to the cavalry, which then came upon us from behind. And a voice cried out: "Muhammad has been killed!" So we turned back, and the enemy turned upon us after we had struck down the standard bearers, until none of them could come near it.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said: The standard of the polytheists lay fallen continuously, until Amrah bint Alqamah al-Harithiyyah took it up and gave it to Quraysh, who then rallied around it.

Al-Suddi related from Abd Khayr, who said: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: I never thought that any of the Companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, desired this worldly life, until what was revealed concerning us on the day of Uhud came down: {Among you were some who desired this world and among you were some who desired the Hereafter.}

This has been related through more than one route from Ibn Mas'ud, and likewise related from Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf and Abu Talhah, both of which Ibn Mardawayh related in his Tafsir.

As for His saying: {Then He turned you back from them that He might test you}, Ibn Ishaq said: al-Qasim ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Rafi', one of the Banu Adi ibn al-Najjar, told me: Anas ibn al-Nadr, uncle of Anas ibn Malik, came upon Umar ibn al-Khattab and Talhah ibn Ubaydullah, among a group of men from the Muhajirun and the Ansar, who had thrown down their hands (given up). He said: "What has stopped you?" They said: "The Messenger of God ﷺ has been killed." He said: "Then what use is life to you after him? Rise and die as he died." Then he faced the enemy and fought until he was killed.

Al-Bukhari said: Hassan ibn Hassan told us, Muhammad ibn Talhah told us, Humayd told us, from Anas ibn Malik: that his uncle, meaning Anas ibn al-Nadr, had been absent from Badr, and said: "I was absent from the first battle of the Messenger of God ﷺ. If God grants me to be present with the Messenger of God ﷺ again, God will surely see what I will do." Then he encountered the day of Uhud, and the people were routed, and he said: "O God, I disown before You what these people have done," meaning the Muslims, "and I declare myself innocent before You of what the polytheists have brought." Then he advanced with his sword and met Sa'd ibn Mu'adh and said: "Where are you going, Sa'd? I smell the fragrance of Paradise beyond Uhud." Then he went on and was killed, and he could not be recognized until his sister recognized him by a mole on his fingers, and he had over eighty wounds from stabbing, striking, and being shot with arrows.

This is the wording of al-Bukhari, and Muslim also related it, from the hadith of Thabit, from Anas, in similar wording.

Al-Bukhari also said: Abdan told us, Abu Hamzah informed us, from Uthman ibn Mawhab, who said: A man came to perform the pilgrimage to the House, and he saw a group of people sitting. He asked: "Who are these people sitting here?" They said: "These are Quraysh." He asked: "Who is the elder?" They said: "Ibn Umar." So he went to him and said: "I wish to ask you about something, so tell me." He said: "I ask you by the sanctity of this House, do you know that Uthman ibn Affan fled on the day of Uhud?" He said: "Yes." He asked: "Do you know that he was absent from Badr and did not take part in it?" He said: "Yes." He asked: "Do you know that he stayed behind from the Pledge of al-Ridwan and did not attend it?" He said: "Yes." At this the man exclaimed in triumph. Ibn Umar said: "Come here and let me inform you and clarify for you what you have asked me. As for his fleeing on the day of Uhud, I bear witness that God pardoned him for it. As for his absence from Badr, his wife, the daughter of the Prophet ﷺ, was beneath him in marriage, and she was ill, so the Messenger of God ﷺ told him: 'You shall have the reward of a man who was present at Badr, and his share.' As for his absence from the Pledge of al-Ridwan, had there been anyone more honored in the heart of Mecca than Uthman, the Prophet ﷺ would have sent him in his place. He sent Uthman, and the Pledge of al-Ridwan took place after Uthman had gone to Mecca." Then the Prophet ﷺ said, gesturing with his right hand: "This is the hand of Uthman."

He struck the palm against his hand and said, "This is Uthman's hand, go with it now with you."

Al-Bukhari then narrated it through another route from Abu Awana, from Uthman ibn Abd Allah ibn Mawhab.

Concerning His saying, {when you were fleeing up the mountain without paying any attention to anyone}, that is, He turned you away from them {when you were fleeing up the mountain} meaning, into the mountain, fleeing from your enemies.

Al-Hasan and Qatada read {idh tus'iduna} meaning, into the mountain, {and were not paying attention to anyone}, that is, while you were not turning to look at anyone out of bewilderment, fear and terror, {while the Messenger was calling you from behind}, meaning, while he, whom you had left behind your backs, was calling you to give up fleeing from the enemies and to turn back, return and rally again.

Al-Suddi said: When the polytheists pressed hard upon the Muslims at Uhud and routed them, some of them entered Madina, while others went up the mountain to the rock and stood upon it. The Messenger ﷺ began calling out to the people: "To me, servants of Allah! To me, servants of Allah!" So Allah mentioned their going up the mountain, then mentioned the Prophet's ﷺ calling out to them, saying: {when you were fleeing up the mountain without paying any attention to anyone, while the Messenger was calling you from behind}.

Ibn Abbas, Qatada, al-Rabi, and Ibn Zayd said likewise.

Abd Allah ibn al-Zib'ara, mentioning the Muslims' defeat on the day of Uhud in his poem, while he was still a polytheist who had not yet accepted Islam, said, beginning it with:

O raven of separation, if you have heard, then speak, for you only utter something that has already happened...

Good and evil each have their extent, and each of these has a face and a precedent...

Until he said:

Would that my forefathers at Badr had witnessed the terror of the Khazraj from the strike of spears

When she rubbed her chest at Quba and the killing grew fierce among Banu Abd al-Ashhal

Then they lightened at that, dancing, the dance of young ones climbing up the mountain

We killed twice as many of their nobles, and we balanced the tilt of Badr, so it was set right

Al-haffan means the young of livestock.

The Prophet ﷺ had at one point been left alone with twelve men of his companions, as Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Zuhayr told us, Abu Ishaq told us that al-Bara ibn Azib said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ appointed Abd Allah ibn Jubayr over the archers on the day of Uhud, and they were fifty men. He said: He positioned them in a certain place and said, "If you see us being snatched up by the birds, do not leave your position until I send for you, and if you see us prevailing over the enemy and treading them underfoot, do not leave your position until I send for you." He said: Then they [the Muslims] routed them [the enemy]. He said: By Allah, I saw the women running swiftly up the mountain, their shins and anklets showing, lifting up their garments. Then the companions of Abd Allah said: The spoils, O people, the spoils! Your companions have prevailed, so what are you waiting for? Abd Allah ibn Jubayr said: Have you forgotten what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to you? They said: By Allah, we will indeed go to the people and take our share of the spoils. But when they came to them, their faces were turned away and they came back defeated. That is what is meant by the Messenger calling them from behind. So none remained with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ except twelve men, and they inflicted seventy casualties upon us. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his companions had inflicted one hundred and forty upon the polytheists on the day of Badr: seventy captives and seventy killed. Abu Sufyan said: "Is Muhammad among the people? Is Muhammad among the people? Is Muhammad among the people?" three times. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade them to answer him. Then he said: "Is Ibn Abi Quhafa among the people? Is Ibn Abi Quhafa among the people? Is Ibn al-Khattab among the people? Is Ibn al-Khattab among the people?" Then he turned to his companions and said: "As for these, they have been killed, you have been spared them." Umar could not restrain himself and said: "You lie, by Allah, O enemy of Allah! Those you named are all alive, and there remains for you what will grieve you." Abu Sufyan said: "A day for a day like Badr, and war is turns of fortune. You will indeed find among the slain some mutilation which I did not order but which did not displease me." Then he began chanting rajaz verse, saying: "Exalted be Hubal! Exalted be Hubal!" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Will you not answer him?" They said: "O Messenger of Allah, what shall we say?" He said: "Say: Allah is more exalted and more majestic." He [Abu Sufyan] said: "We have al-Uzza and you have no Uzza." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Will you not answer him?" They said: "O Messenger of Allah, what shall we say?" He said: "Say: Allah is our protector and you have no protector."

Al-Bukhari narrated it from the hadith of Zuhayr ibn Muawiya in abridged form, and he narrated it from the hadith of Israil, from Abu Ishaq, in fuller form than this, as has preceded. And Allah knows best.

Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Dala'il al-Nubuwwa from the hadith of Umara ibn Ghaziyya, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, who said: The people were routed away from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on the day of Uhud, and there remained with him eleven men of the Ansar, and Talha ibn Ubayd Allah, while he [the Prophet] was climbing the mountain. The polytheists met them, and he said, "Is there no one for these?" Talha said, "I am, O Messenger of Allah." He said, "Stay as you are, O Talha." Then a man of the Ansar said, "I am, O Messenger of Allah," and he fought for him, while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and those who remained with him went up. Then the Ansari man was killed and they caught up with him [the Prophet]. He said, "Is there no man for these?" Talha said the same as before, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said the same as before. Then a man of the Ansar said, "I am, O Messenger of Allah," and he fought for him while his companions climbed, then he was killed and they caught up with him. He kept saying the same as the first time, and Talha would say, "I am, O Messenger of Allah," but he would hold him back, and a man of the Ansar would ask his permission to fight, and he would grant it, and he would fight like those before him, until none remained with him except Talha, and they were both overwhelmed. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Who is for these?" Talha said, "I am." He fought like the combined fighting of all who had gone before him, and his fingers were struck. He said, "Hiss (an exclamation of pain)." The Messenger of Allah said, "If you had said, 'In the name of Allah,' and mentioned the name of Allah, the angels would have lifted you up while the people watched you, until you entered into the expanse of the sky." Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ climbed up to his companions while they were gathered together.

Al-Bukhari narrated from Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shayba, from Waki, from Ismail, from Qays ibn Abi Hazim, who said: I saw Talha's hand, paralyzed, with which he had shielded the Prophet ﷺ, that is, on the day of Uhud.

In the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman, from his father, from Abu Uthman al-Nahdi, who said: None remained with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on some of those days on which the Messenger of Allah ﷺ fought except Talha ibn Ubayd Allah and Sa'd, from their account. Hammad ibn Salama said, from Ali ibn Zayd and Thabit, from Anas ibn Malik: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was left alone on the day of Uhud with seven of the Ansar and two men of Quraysh. When they [the enemy] pressed hard upon him, he said, "Who will drive them from us, and he shall have Paradise, or he shall be my companion in Paradise?" A man of the Ansar came forward and fought until he was killed. Then they pressed hard upon him again, and he said, "Who will drive them from us and he shall have Paradise?" A man of the Ansar came forward and fought until he was killed. This continued until all seven were killed. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to his two companions, "We have not been fair to our companions."

Muslim narrated it from Hudba ibn Khalid, from Hammad ibn Salama, with similar wording.

Al-Hasan ibn Arafa said: [chain] Ibn Marwan ibn Muawiya told us, from Hashim ibn Hashim al-Zuhri, who said: I heard Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab say: I heard Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas [may Allah be pleased with him] say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ scattered out his quiver for me on the day of Uhud and said, "Shoot, may my father and mother be your ransom."

Al-Bukhari extracted it from Abd Allah ibn Muhammad, from Marwan ibn Muawiya.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said: [chain] Salih ibn Kaysan told me, from someone among the family of Sa'd, from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, that he shot arrows on the day of Uhud in defense of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Sa'd said: I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ handing me arrows and saying, "Shoot, may my father and mother be your ransom," until he was even handing me an arrow without a head, and I would shoot it.

It is established in the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Ibrahim ibn Sa'd, from his father, from his grandfather, from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, who said: I saw on the day of Uhud, on the right of the Prophet ﷺ and on his left, two men wearing white garments, fighting for him most fiercely, whom I had never seen before that day nor after it, meaning Gabriel and Michael (peace be upon them).

Abu al-Aswad said, from Urwa ibn al-Zubayr, who said: Ubayy ibn Khalaf, brother of Banu Jumah, had sworn while he was in Makka that he would kill the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. When his oath reached the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he said, "Rather, I shall kill him, if Allah wills." Then on the day of Uhud, Ubayy came forward clad in armor, covered up, saying: "May I not be saved if Muhammad is saved."

He charged at the Messenger of Allah ﷺ intending to kill him, but Mus'ab ibn 'Umayr, the brother of Banu 'Abd al-Dar, confronted him, shielding the Messenger of Allah ﷺ with himself, and Mus'ab ibn 'Umayr was killed. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ then caught sight of the collarbone of Ubayy ibn Khalaf through a gap between the coat of mail and the helmet, and he struck him there with his spear. He fell to the ground from his horse, though no blood came out from his wound. His companions came to him and carried him off while he was bellowing like a bull's bellow. They said to him, "What is making you so distressed? It is only a scratch." He then told them of the saying of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "I will kill Ubayy." Then he said, "By the One in whose hand is my soul, if what has befallen me had befallen the people of Dhu al-Majaz, they would all have died." So he died and went to the Fire. Away with the companions of the blazing Fire.

Musa ibn 'Uqbah related this in his Maghazi, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, in similar wording.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq mentioned: When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was helped up into the ravine, Ubayy ibn Khalaf caught up with him saying, "May I not be saved if you are saved." The people said, "O Messenger of Allah, shall a man of us turn upon him?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Leave him." When he drew near, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took the spear from al-Harith ibn al-Simmah. Some of the people mentioned to me that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took it from him, he shook it with such a shake that we scattered from around him the way hair scatters off a camel's back when it shakes itself. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ turned to face him and struck him in the neck with a thrust that made him tumble repeatedly off his horse.

Al-Waqidi mentioned, from Yunus ibn Bukayr, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from 'Asim ibn 'Amr ibn Qatadah, from 'Abd Allah ibn Ka'b ibn Malik, from his father, something similar to that.

Al-Waqidi said: Ibn 'Umar used to say: Ubayy ibn Khalaf died at Batn Rabigh. He said: "I was traveling through Batn Rabigh after part of the night had passed when suddenly I saw a fire blazing, and I was struck with awe of it. There was a man coming out of it, dragging a chain that pulled at him, tormented by thirst, while another man was saying, 'Do not give him to drink, for this is the one killed by the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, this is Ubayy ibn Khalaf.'"

It is established in the two Sahihs, through the narration of 'Abd al-Razzaq, from Ma'mar, from Hammam ibn Munabbih, from Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The wrath of Allah grew fierce against a people who did this to the Messenger of Allah," and he was at that time pointing to his front tooth, "the wrath of Allah grew fierce against a man whom the Messenger of Allah ﷺ kills in the path of Allah."

Al-Bukhari also related it from the hadith of Ibn Jurayj, from 'Amr ibn Dinar, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "The wrath of Allah grew fierce against one whom the Messenger of Allah ﷺ killed with his own hand in the path of Allah. The wrath of Allah grew fierce against a people who bloodied the face of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ."

Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Yasar, may Allah have mercy on him, said: The front tooth of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was struck, his cheek was gashed, and his lip was wounded. The one who inflicted this on him was 'Utbah ibn Abi Waqqas.

Salih ibn Kaysan related to me, from someone who related to him, from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, who said: "I never desired to kill anyone as much as I desired to kill 'Utbah ibn Abi Waqqas, even though, as far as I knew, he was of bad character and hated among his people. What sufficed me regarding him was the saying of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: 'The wrath of Allah grew fierce against one who bloodied the face of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'"

'Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us, from al-Zuhri, from 'Uthman al-Jazari, from Miqsam, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ invoked against 'Utbah ibn Abi Waqqas on the day of Uhud, when he broke his front tooth and bloodied his face, saying: "O Allah, do not let a year pass over him before he dies as a disbeliever." No year passed over him before he died as a disbeliever, bound for the Fire.

Al-Waqidi mentioned, from Ibn Abi Sabrah, from Ishaq ibn 'Abd Allah ibn Abi Farwah, from Abu al-Huwayrith, from Nafi' ibn Jubayr, who said: "I heard a man among the Emigrants say: 'I witnessed Uhud, and I looked at the arrows coming from every direction while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was in the midst of them, all of it being turned away from him. I saw 'Abd Allah ibn Shihab al-Zuhri saying that day: Show me Muhammad, may I not be saved if he is saved, while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was right beside him with no one accompanying him.'" He then drew near him, and Safwan later rebuked him for that, so he said, "By Allah, I did not see him. I swear by Allah he was protected from us. We went out, four of us, and pledged and swore together to kill him, but we were never able to get to him."

Al-Waqidi said: What is established with us is that the one who struck the cheek of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was Ibn Qami'ah, and the one who bloodied his lip and struck his front tooth was 'Utbah ibn Abi Waqqas.

Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi said: Ibn al-Mubarak related to us, from Ishaq ibn Yahya ibn Talhah ibn 'Ubayd Allah, 'Isa ibn Talhah informed me, from the Mother of the Believers 'A'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, who said: Whenever Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, mentioned the day of Uhud, he would say, "That day belonged entirely to Talhah." Then he began to narrate, saying: "I was the first to return on the day of Uhud, and I saw a man fighting alongside the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, defending him," and I think he said, "out of zealous devotion." He said: "I said, 'Be Talhah,' since what I had missed, I had missed. I said, 'Let it be a man from my own people, that would be dearer to me.' Between me and the polytheists was a man I did not recognize, and I was closer to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ than he was, and he was moving with such swift steps that I could not keep pace with him in my memory of it. It turned out to be Abu 'Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah. We reached the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and found that his front tooth had been broken, his face had been gashed, and two rings of chain mail from his helmet had become embedded in his cheek. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'Attend to your companion,' meaning Talhah, who had lost much blood, but we paid no attention to what he said." He said: "I went to pull that out from his face, but Abu 'Ubaydah said, 'I adjure you by my right over you, do not do it, leave it to me.' So I left it to him. He was reluctant to pull it out with his hand for fear of hurting the Prophet ﷺ, so he clamped it with his mouth and pulled out one of the rings, and his own front tooth came out along with the ring. I went to do what he had done, but he said, 'I adjure you by my right over you, do not do it, leave it to me.'" He said: "He did the same as he had done the first time, and his other front tooth came out along with the ring. Abu 'Ubaydah, may Allah be pleased with him, became, of all people, the most attractive despite his missing front teeth. We then attended to the condition of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, then we came to Talhah in one of those pits, and found on him more than seventy, or fewer, or more, wounds from stabbing, shooting, and striking, and one of his fingers had been cut off. We attended to his condition."

Al-Haytham ibn Kulayb and al-Tabarani related it from the hadith of Ishaq ibn Yahya with the same chain. In al-Haytham's version: Abu 'Ubaydah said, "I adjure you, O Abu Bakr, will you not leave it to me?" Then Abu 'Ubaydah took the arrowhead with his mouth and began working it loose gently, reluctant to hurt the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, then he pulled the arrowhead out with his mouth, and Abu 'Ubaydah's front tooth came out with it.

He mentioned the rest of it, and al-Hafiz al-Dhiya' al-Maqdisi selected it in his book. However, 'Ali ibn al-Madini graded this hadith as weak because of this Ishaq ibn Yahya, for Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan, Ahmad, Yahya ibn Ma'in, al-Bukhari, Abu Zur'ah, Abu Hatim, Muhammad ibn Sa'd, al-Nasa'i, and others spoke critically of him.

Ibn Wahb said: 'Amr ibn al-Harith informed me that 'Umar ibn al-Sa'ib related to him that it reached him that Malik, the father of Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, when the Prophet ﷺ was wounded on the day of Uhud, sucked the wound until he had cleansed it and it showed white. It was said to him, "Spit it out." He said, "No, by Allah, I will never spit it out." Then he turned back to fight, and the Prophet ﷺ said, "Whoever wishes to look at a man among the people of Paradise, let him look at this one." He was then martyred.

It is established in the two Sahihs, through the route of 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Abi Hazim, from his father, from Sahl ibn Sa'd, that he was asked about the wound of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he said: "The face of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was wounded, his front tooth was broken, and the helmet on his head was smashed. Fatimah, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, was washing away the blood while 'Ali poured water on it with a shield. When Fatimah, may Allah be pleased with her, saw that the water was only increasing the flow of blood, she took a piece of a mat, burned it, and when it turned to ash, she pressed it onto the wound, and the bleeding stopped."

Regarding His saying, {So He repaid you with distress upon distress}: that is, He recompensed you with distress upon distress, just as the Arabs say "I settled with (nazaltu bi) the tribe of so-and-so" and "I settled upon (nazaltu 'ala) the tribe of so-and-so."

Ibn Jarir said: Likewise His saying, {and I will surely crucify you on the trunks of palm trees} [Ta Ha: 71], meaning upon the trunks of palm trees.

Ibn 'Abbas said: The first distress was because of the defeat, and when it was said, "Muhammad ﷺ has been killed," and the second was when the polytheists rose above them on top of the mountain, and the Prophet ﷺ said, "O Allah, they have no right to rise above us."

From Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf: the first grief was because of the defeat, and the second was when it was said, "Muhammad ﷺ has been killed," which was, in their estimation, greater than the defeat itself.

Both of these were narrated by Ibn Mardawayh, and something similar has been related from Umar ibn al-Khattab. Ibn Abi Hatim also mentioned something similar from Qatadah.

Al-Suddi said: the first grief was because of the booty and the victory that had eluded them, and the second was because of the enemy's gaining the upper hand over them.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said, regarding {He rewarded you with grief upon grief}: that is, distress after distress, the killing of those of your brothers who were killed, the ascendancy of your enemy over you, and what settled into your hearts from the words of those who said, "Your Prophet has been killed." So all of that came upon you in succession, grief upon grief.

Mujahid and Qatadah said: the first grief was their hearing of Muhammad's killing, and the second was what befell them of killing and wounds. From Qatadah and al-Rabi' ibn Anas the reverse of this is reported.

From al-Suddi: the first was what eluded them of victory and booty, and the second was the enemy's gaining the upper hand over them, and this has already been mentioned from al-Suddi above.

Ibn Jarir said: the most correct of these views is the one which holds that {He rewarded you with grief upon grief} means: He rewarded you, O believers, with your grief at Allah's depriving you of the booty of the polytheists, of victory over them, and of triumph against them, and with what befell you that day of killing and wounds, after He had shown you in all of that what you loved, because of your disobedience to your Lord and your opposition to the command of the Prophet ﷺ. This was grief at your supposing that your Prophet had been killed, and at the enemy's turning against you after your having routed them.

As for His saying, {so that you would not grieve over what escaped you} that is, over the booty which your enemy prevented you from obtaining, {nor over what befell you} of killing and wounds. This was said by Ibn Abbas, Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, al-Hasan, Qatadah, and al-Suddi. {And Allah is well aware of what you do.}

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُحَذِّرُ تَعَالَى عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَنْ طَاعَةِ الْكَافِرِينَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ فَإِنَّ طَاعَتَهُمْ تُورِثُ الرَّدَى فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِنْ تُطِيعُوا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَرُدُّوكُمْ عَلَى أَعْقَابِكُمْ فَتَنْقَلِبُوا خَاسِرِينَ﴾ .

ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُمْ بِطَاعَتِهِ وَمُوَالَاتِهِ، وَالِاسْتِعَانَةِ بِهِ، وَالتَّوَكُّلِ عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ: ﴿بَلِ اللَّهُ مَوْلاكُمْ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ النَّاصِرِينَ﴾ .

ثُمَّ بَشَّرَهُمْ بِأَنَّهُ سَيُلقي فِي قُلُوبِ أَعْدَائِهِمُ الْخَوْفَ مِنْهُمْ وَالذِّلَّةَ لَهُمْ، بِسَبَبِ كُفْرِهِمْ وَشِرْكِهِمْ، مَعَ مَا ادَّخَرَهُ لَهُمْ فِي الدَّارِ الْآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ والنَّكال، فَقَالَ: ﴿سَنُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ بِمَا أَشْرَكُوا بِاللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنزلْ بِهِ سُلْطَانًا وَمَأْوَاهُمُ النَّارُ وَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الظَّالِمِينَ﴾ .

وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أُعْطِيتُ خَمْسًا لَمْ يُعْطَهُنَّ أَحَدٌ مِنْ الأنْبِيَاءِ قَبْلِي: نُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ مَسِيرَةَ شَهْرٍ، وَجُعِلَتْ لِي الأرْضُ مَسْجِدًا وَطَهُورًا، وَأُحِلَّتْ لِي الْغَنَائِمُ، وَأُعْطِيتُ الشَّفَاعَة وَكَانَ النَّبِيُّ يُبْعَثُ إِلَى قَوْمِهِ خَاصَّةً وَبُعِثْتُ إِلَى النَّاسِ عَامَّةً .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيّ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ -يَعْنِي التَّيْمِيَّ-عَنْ سَيّار، عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عليه وسلم قال: "فَضَّلَني [رَبِّي] عَلَى الأنْبِيَاء -أَوْ قَالَ: عَلَى الأمَمِ-بأَرْبَعٍ" قَالَ "أُرْسِلْتُ إلَى النَّاسِ كَافَّةً وَجُعِلَتْ لِيَ الأرْضُ كُلُّهَا وَلأمَّتِي مَسْجِدًا وَطَهُورًا فَأيْنَمَا أدْرَكَتْ رَجُلا مِنْ أُمَّتِي الصَّلاةُ فَعِنْدَهُ مَسْجِدُهُ و طَهُوُرهُ، ونُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ مَسِيرَة شَهْرٍ يَقْذِفُهُ فِي قُلُوبِ أَعْدَائِي وأحَل لِيَ الغنائِم".

وَرَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُلَيْمَانَ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ سَيَّار القُرَشي الْأُمَوِيِّ مَوْلَاهُمُ الدِّمَشْقِيِّ -سَكَنَ الْبَصْرَةَ-عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ صُدَيّ بْنِ عَجْلان، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، بِهِ. وَقَالَ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ .

وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ: أَخْبَرَنَا ابْنُ وَهْب، أَخْبَرَنِي عَمْرُو بْنُ الْحَارِثِ: أَنَّ أَبَا يُونُسَ حَدَّثَهُ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم قَالَ: "نُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ عَلَى الْعَدُوِّ".

وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ وَهْبٍ .

وَرَوَى الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا حُسَيْنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ أَبِي بُرْدَة، عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَبِي مُوسَى قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أُعْطِيتُ خَمْسًا: بُعِثْتُ إلَى الأحْمَرِ وَالأسْوَدِ، وَجعلَتْ لِيَ الأرْض طَهُورًا ومَسْجِدًا، وأُحِلَّتْ لِيَ الْغَنَائِم وَلَمْ تَحِل لِمَنْ كَانَ قَبْلِي، ونُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ شَهْرًا، وأُعْطِيتُ الشَّفَاعَةَ، وَلَيْسَ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ إِلَّا وَقَدْ سَأَل شَفَاعَتَهُ، وإنِّي اخْتَبَأتُ شَفَاعَتِي، ثُمَّ جَعَلْتُهَا لِمَنْ مَاتَ لَا يُشْرِكُ بِاللهِ شَيْئًا".

تفرد به أحمد .

وَرَوَى العَوْفيّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿سَنُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ﴾ قَالَ: قَذَفَ اللَّهُ فِي قَلْبِ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ الرُّعْبَ، فَرَجَعَ إِلَى مَكَّةَ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "إنَّ أبَا سُفْيَانَ قَدْ أَصَابَ مِنْكُمْ طَرَفا، وَقَدْ رَجَعَ، وقَذَفَ اللَّهُ فِي قَلْبِهِ الرُّعْبِ". رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلَقَدْ صَدَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ إِذْ تَحُسُّونَهُمْ بِإِذْنِهِ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: وَعَدَهُمُ اللَّهُ النَّصْرَ.

وَقَدْ يُسْتَدَلُّ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ عَلَى أَحَدِ الْقَوْلَيْنِ الْمُتَقَدِّمَيْنِ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿إِذْ تَقُولُ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَلَنْ يَكْفِيَكُمْ أَنْ يُمِدَّكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِثَلاثَةِ آلافٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ مُنزلِينَ. بَلَى إِنْ تَصْبِرُوا وَتَتَّقُوا وَيَأْتُوكُمْ مِنْ فَوْرِهِمْ هَذَا يُمْدِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِخَمْسَةِ آلافٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ مُسَوِّمِينَ﴾ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ كَانَ يَوْمَ أُحُدٍ لِأَنَّ عَدُوَّهُمْ كَانَ ثَلَاثَةَ آلَافِ مُقَاتِلٍ، فَلَمَّا وَاجَهُوهُمْ كَانَ الظَّفَرُ وَالنَّصْرُ أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ لِلْإِسْلَامِ، فَلَمَّا حَصَلَ مَا حَصَلَ مِنْ عِصْيَانِ الرُّماة وَفَشَلِ بَعْضِ الْمُقَاتِلَةِ، تَأَخَّرَ الْوَعْدُ الَّذِي كَانَ مَشْرُوطًا بِالثَّبَاتِ وَالطَّاعَةِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَلَقَدْ صَدَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ﴾ أَيْ: أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ ﴿إِذْ تَحُسُّونَهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: تَقْتُلُونَهُمْ ﴿بِإِذْنِهِ﴾ أَيْ: بِتَسْلِيطِهِ إِيَّاكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ ﴿حَتَّى إِذَا فَشِلْتُمْ﴾ وَقَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ: قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: الْفَشَلُ الْجُبْنُ، ﴿وَتَنَازَعْتُمْ فِي الأمْرِ وَعَصَيْتُمْ﴾ كَمَا وَقَعَ لِلرُّمَاةِ ﴿مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا أَرَاكُمْ مَا تُحِبُّونَ﴾ وَهُوَ الظَّفَرُ مِنْهُمْ ﴿مِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الدُّنْيَا﴾ وَهْمُ الَّذِينَ رَغِبُوا فِي الْمَغْنَمِ حِينَ رَأَوُا الْهَزِيمَةَ ﴿وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الآخِرَةَ ثُمَّ صَرَفَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ لِيَبْتَلِيَكُمْ﴾ ثُمَّ أَدَالَهُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ لِيَخْتَبِرَكُمْ وَيَمْتَحِنَكُمْ ﴿وَلَقَدْ عَفَا عَنْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: غَفَرَ لَكُمْ ذَلِكَ الصَّنِيع، وَذَلِكَ -وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ-لِكَثْرَةِ عَدد الْعَدُوِّ وعُدَدهم، وَقِلَّةِ عَدد الْمُسْلِمِينَ وعُدَدهم.

قَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ: قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلَقَدْ عَفَا عَنْكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: لَمْ يَسْتَأْصِلْكُمْ. وَكَذَا قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، رَوَاهُمَا ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ ﴿وَاللَّهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ دَاوُدَ أخبرنا عبد الرحمن ابن أَبِي الزِّنَادِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عُبَيد اللَّهِ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: مَا نَصَرَ اللَّهُ فِي مَوْطِن كَمَا نَصَرَهُ يَوْمَ أُحُدٍ. قَالَ: فَأَنْكَرْنَا ذَلِكَ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ مَنْ أَنْكَرَ ذَلِكَ كتابُ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ فِي يَوْمِ أُحُدٍ: ﴿وَلَقَدْ صَدَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ إِذْ تَحُسُّونَهُمْ بِإِذْنِهِ﴾ يَقُولُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: والحَسُّ: الْقَتْلُ ﴿حَتَّى إِذَا فَشِلْتُمْ وَتَنَازَعْتُمْ فِي الأمْرِ وَعَصَيْتُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا أَرَاكُمْ مَا تُحِبُّونَ مِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الدُّنْيَا وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الآخِرَةَ﴾ الْآيَةَ وَإِنَّمَا عَنَى بِهَذَا الرُّمَاةَ، وَذَلِكَ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ أَقَامَهُمْ فِي مَوْضِعٍ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: "احْمُوا ظُهُورَنَا، فَإنْ رَأيْتُمُونَا نُقْتَلُ فَلا تَنْصُرُونَا وَإنْ رَأَيْتُمُونَا قَدْ غَنِمْنَا فَلا تُشْرِكُونَا. فَلَمَّا غَنِمَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ وأباحُوا عَسْكَرَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ أَكَبَّتِ الرُّماة جَمِيعًا [وَدَخَلُوا] فِي الْعَسْكَرِ يَنْهَبُونَ، وَلَقَدِ الْتَقَتْ صُفُوفُ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَهُم هَكَذَا -وَشَبَّكَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ-وَانْتَشَبُوا، فَلَمَّا أَخَلَّ الرُّمَاةُ تِلْكَ الْخَلَّةِ الَّتِي كَانُوا فِيهَا، دَخَلَتِ الْخَيْلُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ الْمَوْضِعِ عَلَى أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَضَرَبَ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا وَالْتَبُسُوا، وقُتل من المسلمين ناس كَثِيرٌ، وَقَدْ كَانَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَأَصْحَابِهِ أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ، حَتَّى قُتِل مِنْ أَصْحَابِ لِوَاءِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ سَبْعَةٌ أَوْ تسعةٌ، وَجَالَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ جَوْلَةً نَحْوَ الْجَبَلِ وَلَمْ يَبْلُغُوا -حَيْثُ يَقُولُ النَّاسُ-الْغَارَ، إِنَّمَا كَانَ تَحْتَ المِهْراس، وَصَاحَ الشَّيْطَانُ: قُتل مُحَمَّدٌ، فَلَمْ يُشَك فِيهِ أَنَّهُ حَقٌّ، فَمَا زِلْنَا كَذَلِكَ مَا نَشُك أَنَّهُ حَقٌّ، حَتَّى طَلَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بَيْنَ السَّعْدَيْنِ، نَعْرِفُهُ بِتَلَفُّتِهِ إِذَا مَشَى -قَالَ: فَفَرِحْنَا حَتَّى كَأَنَّهُ لَمْ يُصِبْنَا مَا أَصَابَنَا-قَالَ: فَرَقِيَ نَحْوَنَا وَهُوَ يَقُولُ: "اشْتَدَّ غَضَبَ اللهِ عَلَى قَوْمٍ دَمَّوْا وَجْهَ رَسُولِ اللهِ". وَيَقُولُ مَرَّةً أُخْرَى: "اللَّهم إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ لَهم أنْ يَعْلُونَا". حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَيْنَا، فَمَكَثَ سَاعَةً، فَإِذَا أَبُو سُفْيَانَ يَصِيحُ فِي أَسْفَلِ الْجَبَلِ: اعْلُ هُبَلُ، مَرَّتَيْنِ -يَعْنِي آلِهَتَهُ-أَيْنَ ابْنُ أَبِي كَبْشة؟ أَيْنَ ابْنُ أَبِي قحَافة؟ أَيْنَ ابْنُ الْخَطَّابِ؟ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَلَا أُجِيبَهُ؟ قَالَ: "بَلَى" قَالَ: فَلَمَّا قَالَ: اعْلُ هُبَلُ. قَالَ عُمَرُ: اللَّهُ أَعْلَى وَأَجَلُّ. فَقَالَ أَبُو سُفْيَانَ: قَدْ أَنْعَمَتْ عَيْنُهَا فعَادِ عَنْهَا أَوْ: فَعَالِ! فَقَالَ: أَيْنَ ابْنُ أَبِي كَبْشَةَ؟ أَيْنَ ابْنُ أَبِي قُحَافة؟ أَيْنَ ابْنُ الْخَطَّابِ؟ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: هَذَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، وَهَذَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ، وَهَا أَنَا ذَا عُمَرُ. قَالَ: فَقَالَ أَبُو سُفْيَانَ: يَوْمٌ بِيَوْمِ بَدْرٍ، الْأَيَّامُ دُوَل، وَإِنَّ الْحَرْبَ سِجَال. قَالَ: فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: لَا سَوَاءً، قَتْلَانَا فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَقَتْلَاكُمْ فِي النَّارِ. قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ ذَلِكَ، لَقَدْ خِبْنا إِذًا وخَسِرْنا ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو سُفْيَانَ: إِنَّكُمْ سَتَجِدُونَ فِي قَتْلَاكُمْ مُثْلَةً وَلَمْ يَكُنْ ذَلِكَ عَلَى رَأْيِ سُرَاتِنَا. قَالَ: ثُمَّ أدركَتْه حَمِيَّة الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَقَالَ: أَمَا إِنَّهُ إِنْ كَانَ ذَلِكَ لَمْ نَكْرهْه.

هَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ، وَسِيَاقٌ عَجِيبٌ، وَهُوَ مِنْ مُرْسَلَاتِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، فَإِنَّهُ لَمْ يَشْهَدْ أحُدًا وَلَا أَبُوهُ.

وَقَدْ أَخْرَجَهُ الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ عَنْ أَبِي النَّضْر الْفَقِيهِ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ دَاوُدَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، بِهِ. وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ وَالْبَيْهَقِيُّ فِي دَلَائِلِ النُّبُوَّةِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ دَاوُدَ الْهَاشِمِيِّ، بِهِ وَلِبَعْضِهِ شَوَاهِدٌ فِي الصِّحَاحِ وَغَيْرِهَا، فَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ:

حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادٌ، حَدَّثَنَا عَطَاءُ بْنُ السَّائِبِ عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: إِنَّ النِّسَاءَ كُنَّ يَوْمَ أُحُدٍ، خلْف الْمُسْلِمِينَ، يُجْهزْن عَلَى جَرْحى الْمُشْرِكِينَ، فَلَوْ حَلَفت يَوْمَئِذٍ رَجَوْتُ أَنْ أبَر: أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ أَحَدٌ مِنَّا يُرِيدُ الدُّنْيَا، حَتَّى أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿مِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الدُّنْيَا وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الآخِرَةَ ثُمَّ صَرَفَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ لِيَبْتَلِيَكُمْ﴾ فَلَمَّا خَالَفَ أَصْحَابُ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ وعَصَوا مَا أُمِرُوا بِهِ، أُفْرِدَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي تِسْعَةٍ: سَبْعَةٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ، وَرَجُلَيْنِ مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ، وَهُوَ عَاشِرُهُمْ، فَلَمَّا رهقُوه [قَالَ: "رَحِمَ اللهُ رَجُلًا رَدَّهُمْ عَنَّا". قَالَ: فَقَامَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ فَقَاتَلَ سَاعَةً حَتَّى قُتِلَ، فَلَمَّا رَهقُوه] أَيْضًا قَالَ: "رَحِمَ اللهُ رَجُلا رَدَّهُمْ عَنَّا". فَلَمْ يَزَلْ يَقُولُ ذَا حَتَّى قُتِل السَّبْعَةُ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِصَاحِبِهِ: "مَا أَنْصَفْنَا أَصْحَابَنَا".

فَجَاءَ أَبُو سُفْيَانَ فَقَالَ: اعْلُ هُبَلُ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "قُولُوا: اللهُ أعْلَى وأجَلُّ". فَقَالُوا: اللَّهُ أَعْلَى وَأَجَلُّ. فَقَالَ أَبُو سُفْيَانَ: لَنَا العُزَّى وَلَا عُزَّى لَكُمْ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: قُولُوا: "اللهُ مَوْلانَا، وَالْكَافِرُونَ لَا مَوْلَى لَهُم". ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو سُفْيَانَ: يومٌ بيوْم بَدْر، يومٌ عَلَيْنَا وَيَوْمٌ لَنَا وَيَوْمٌ نُسَاءُ وَيَوْمٌ نُسَر. حَنْظَلَةَ بِحَنْظَلَةَ، وَفُلَانٌ بِفُلَانٍ، وَفُلَانٌ بِفُلَانٍ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَا سَوَاء. أمَّا قَتْلانَا فَأْحَيْاءٌ يُرْزَقُونَ، وَقْتَلاكُمْ فِي النَّارِ يُعَذَّبُونَ". قَالَ أَبُو سُفْيَانَ: قَدْ كَانَ فِي الْقَوْمِ مَثُلَةٌ، وإنْ كانَتْ لَعَنْ غَيْرِ مَلأ منَّا، مَا أمرتُ وَلَا نَهَيْتُ، وَلَا أحْبَبْتُ وَلَا كَرِهتُ، وَلَا سَاءَنِي وَلَا سرَّني. قَالَ: فَنَظَرُوا فَإِذَا حمزةُ قَدْ بُقِرَ بَطْنُه، وأخذتْ هنْد كَبده فلاكَتْها فَلَمْ تَسْتَطِعْ أَنْ تَأْكُلَهَا، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أكَلَتْ شَيْئًا؟ " قَالُوا: لَا. قَالَ: "مَا كَانَ اللهُ ليُدْخِلَ شَيْئًا مِنْ حَمْزَةَ فِي النَّارِ".

قَالَ: فَوَضَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ حَمْزَةَ فَصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ، وَجِيء بِرَجُلٍ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ فَوُضِع إِلَى جَنْبِهِ فصلَّى عَلَيْهِ، فَرُفِعَ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ وتُرِكَ حَمْزَةُ، ثُمَّ جِيءَ بِآخَرٍ فوضعَه إِلَى جَنْبِ حَمْزَةَ فَصَلَّى [عَلَيْهِ] ثُمَّ رُفِعَ وتُرِكَ حَمْزَةُ، حَتَّى صلَّى عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ سَبْعِينَ صَلَاةً.
