But they concealed that, and exchanged what they had been promised of good in this world and the next for something inferior and trifling, a paltry worldly gain. What a wretched bargain was their bargain, and what a wretched pledge was their pledge.

In this is a warning to the scholars not to follow their path, lest what befell them befall the scholars, and they be driven along the same path. It is incumbent upon the scholars to disburse the beneficial knowledge in their possession, that which points to righteous action, and not to conceal any of it. It has come in a hadith reported through numerous chains from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "Whoever is asked about knowledge and conceals it will be bridled on the Day of Resurrection with a bridle of fire."

As for His saying, Most High: {Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have brought about and love to be praised for what they have not done [do not think them to be safe from the punishment]} to the end of the verse, He means by this those who show off and boast of things they were not given, as has come in the Two Sahihs from the Messenger of God ﷺ: "Whoever claims a false claim in order to increase himself thereby, God will only increase him in diminishment." And in the Sahih: "One who claims to have what he was not given is like one who wears two garments of falsehood."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Hajjaj, from Ibn Jurayj, Ibn Abi Mulaykah informed me that Humayd ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf told him that Marwan said, "Go, O Rafi'," to his doorkeeper, "to Ibn Abbas, may God be pleased with him, and say: If every one of us who rejoices in what he has brought about and loves to be praised for what he has not done is to be punished, then we shall all be punished!" Ibn Abbas said, "What have you to do with this? This was only revealed concerning the People of the Book." Then Ibn Abbas recited: {And when God took a covenant from those who were given the Book, that you would surely make it clear to the people and not conceal it, but they cast it behind their backs and purchased with it a small price. How wretched is what they purchased.} And Ibn Abbas recited: {Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have brought about and love to be praised for what they have not done} to the end of the verse. Ibn Abbas said: The Prophet ﷺ asked them about something, and they concealed it and told him something else. They went out having shown him that they had informed him of what he had asked them about, and they sought to be praised by him for that, and they rejoiced in what they had achieved by concealing what he had asked them about.

Thus it was reported by al-Bukhari in the Tafsir, and Muslim, and al-Tirmidhi and al-Nasa'i in their Tafsirs, and Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Jarir, Ibn Mardawayh, and al-Hakim in his Mustadrak, all of them from the hadith of Abd al-Malik ibn Jurayj, in similar wording. Al-Bukhari also reported it from the hadith of Ibn Jurayj, from Ibn Abi Mulaykah, from Alqamah ibn Waqqas: that Marwan said to his doorkeeper, "Go, O Rafi', to Ibn Abbas," and he mentioned it.

Al-Bukhari said: [chain] Sa'id ibn Abi Maryam, Muhammad ibn Ja'far informed us, Zayd ibn Aslam told me, from Ata' ibn Yasar, from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, may God be pleased with him: that some men among the hypocrites, in the time of the Messenger of God ﷺ, when the Messenger of God ﷺ went out on a military expedition, would stay behind, and rejoice in their staying behind, contrary to the Messenger of God ﷺ. Then when the Messenger of God ﷺ returned from the expedition, they would make excuses to him and swear oaths, and they loved to be praised for what they had not done. So it was revealed: {Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have brought about and love to be praised for what they have not done} to the end of the verse.

Muslim likewise reported it from the hadith of Ibn Abi Maryam, in similar wording. Ibn Mardawayh reported it in his Tafsir from the hadith of al-Layth ibn Sa'd, from Hisham ibn Sa'd, from Zayd ibn Aslam, who said: Abu Sa'id, Rafi' ibn Khadij, and Zayd ibn Thabit were with Marwan, and he said, "O Abu Sa'id, have you seen the saying of God, Most High: {Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have brought about and love to be praised for what they have not done}? We rejoice in what we have brought about and we love to be praised for what we have not done!" Abu Sa'id said, "This is not that. Rather, that refers to some people among the hypocrites who used to stay behind whenever the Messenger of God ﷺ sent out an expedition. If some setback befell it, they rejoiced in their having stayed behind, and if God granted them victory and conquest, they would swear oaths to the returning fighters in order to please them, so as to be praised for their joy at the victory and conquest." Marwan said, "What has this to do with that?" Abu Sa'id said, "This man knows that too," pointing to Zayd. Marwan said, "Is that so, O Zayd?" He said, "Yes, Abu Sa'id has spoken the truth." Then Abu Sa'id said, "And this one knows it too," meaning Rafi' ibn Khadij, "but he fears that if he tells you, you will take away his young she-camels in the collection of the sadaqah (charity tax)." When they went out, Zayd said to Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, "Will you not praise me for bearing witness on your behalf?" Abu Sa'id said, "You bore witness to the truth." Zayd said, "So will you not praise me for having borne witness to the truth?"

Then he reported it from the hadith of Malik, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Rafi' ibn Khadij: that he and Zayd ibn Thabit were with Marwan ibn al-Hakam, who was the governor of Medina, and Marwan said, "O Rafi', concerning what was this verse revealed?" And he mentioned it as reported above from Abu Sa'id, may God be pleased with them. Marwan continued afterward to send and ask Ibn Abbas, as mentioned before, and he told him what we have mentioned. There is no contradiction between what Ibn Abbas mentioned and what these others said, because the verse is general, encompassing all that has been mentioned. And God knows best.

Ibn Mardawayh also reported, from the hadith of Muhammad ibn Abi Atiq and Musa ibn Uqbah, from al-Zuhri, from Muhammad ibn Thabit al-Ansari: that Thabit ibn Qays al-Ansari said, "O Messenger of God, by God, I fear that I may have perished." He said, "Why?" He said, "God has forbidden a man to love being praised for what he has not done, and I find that I love praise. And God has forbidden pride, and I find that I love beauty. And God has forbidden us to raise our voices above your voice, and I am a man of a loud voice." The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "Would you not be pleased to live praised, be killed a martyr, and enter Paradise?" He said, "Yes indeed, O Messenger of God." So he lived praised, and was killed a martyr on the day of Musaylimah the Liar.

As for His saying: {So do not think them to be safe from the punishment}, it is read with the "ta" as an address to a single person, and with the "ya" as a report about them, meaning: do not think that they are saved from the punishment. Rather, it must inevitably come to them. Hence He said: {and for them is a painful punishment}.

Then He said: {And to God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and God has power over all things}, meaning: He is the Owner of everything, and the One capable over everything, so nothing can render Him incapable. So fear Him and do not oppose Him, and beware of His vengeance and His wrath, for He is the Magnificent, than whom none is more magnificent, the All-Capable, than whom none is more capable.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

هَذَا تَوْبِيخٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَتَهْدِيدٌ لِأَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ، الَّذِينَ أخَذ عَلَيْهِمُ الْعَهْدَ عَلَى أَلْسِنَةِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ أَنْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِمُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، وَأَنْ يُنَوِّهُوا بِذِكْرِهِ فِي النَّاسِ لِيَكُونُوا عَلَى أهْبَة مِنْ أَمْرِهِ، فَإِذَا أَرْسَلَهُ اللَّهُ تابعوه، فَكَتَمُوا ذَلِكَ وَتَعَوَّضُوا عَمَّا وَعَدُوا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْخَيْرِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ بِالدُّونِ الطَّفِيفِ، وَالْحَظِّ الدُّنْيَوِيِّ السَّخِيفِ، فَبِئْسَتِ الصَّفْقَةُ صَفْقَتُهُمْ، وَبِئْسَتِ الْبَيْعَةُ بَيْعَتُهُمْ.

وَفِي هَذَا تَحْذير لِلْعُلَمَاءِ أَنْ يَسْلُكُوا مَسْلَكَهُمْ فَيُصِيبَهُمْ مَا أَصَابَهُمْ، ويُسْلكَ بِهِمْ مَسْلكهم، فَعَلَى الْعُلَمَاءِ أَنْ يَبْذُلُوا مَا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ النَّافِعِ، الدَّالِّ عَلَى الْعَمَلِ الصَّالِحِ، وَلَا يَكْتُمُوا مِنْهُ شَيْئًا، فَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الْمَرْوِيِّ مِنْ طُرُقٍ مُتَعَدِّدَةٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "مَنْ سُئِل عَنْ عِلْم فكَتَمه ألْجِم يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ بِلجَامٍ مِنْ نَارٍ".

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَا أَتَوْا وَيُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يُحْمَدُوا بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا [فَلا تَحْسَبَنَّهُمْ بِمَفَازَةٍ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ الْمُرَائِينَ الْمُتَكَثِّرِينَ بِمَا لَمْ يُعْطَوا، كَمَا جَاءَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنِ ادَّعَى دَعْوى كَاذِبَةً لِيتَكَثَّر بِهَا لَمْ يَزِدْه اللَّهُ إِلَّا قِلَّة" وَفِي الصَّحِيحِ: "الْمُتَشَبِّعُ بِمَا لَمْ يُعْطَ كَلَابِسِ ثَوْبَي زُور" .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاج، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، أَخْبَرَنِي ابْنُ أَبِي مُلَيكة أَنَّ حُمَيد بْنَ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَوْف أَخْبَرَهُ: أَنَّ مَرْوَانَ قَالَ: اذْهَبْ يَا رَافِعُ -لبَوَّابه-إِلَى ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، فَقُلْ لَئِنْ كَانَ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ منَّا فَرح بِمَا أتَى وَأَحَبَّ أَنْ يُحْمَدَ بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلْ -معَذَّبًا، لنُعَذبن أَجْمَعُونَ؟ فَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: وَمَا لَكُمْ وَهَذِهِ؟ إِنَّمَا نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ فِي أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ، ثُمَّ تَلَا ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَإِذْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ لَتُبَيِّنُنَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ وَلا تَكْتُمُونَهُ فَنَبَذُوهُ وَرَاءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَاشْتَرَوْا بِهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلا فَبِئْسَ مَا يَشْتَرُونَ﴾ وَتَلَا ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَا أَتَوْا وَيُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يُحْمَدُوا بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا﴾ الْآيَةَ. وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: سَأَلَهُمُ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ عَنْ شَيْءٍ، فَكَتَمُوهُ وَأَخْبَرُوهُ بِغَيْرِهِ، فَخَرَجُوا قَدْ أرَوْه أَنْ قَدْ أَخْبَرُوهُ بِمَا سَأَلَهُمْ عَنْهُ، وَاسْتَحْمَدُوا بِذَلِكَ إِلَيْهِ، وَفَرِحُوا بِمَا أُوتُوا مِنْ كِتْمَانِهِمْ مَا سَأَلَهُمْ عَنْهُ.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي التَّفْسِيرِ، وَمُسْلِمٌ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ فِي تَفْسِيرَيْهِمَا، وَابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ وَابْنُ جَرِيرٍ وَابْنُ مَرْدُويه، وَالْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ، كُلُّهُمْ مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ جُرَيج، بِنَحْوِهِ وَرَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ أَيْضًا مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ عَنِ عَلقمة بن وقاص: أن مَرْوان قَالَ لِبَوَّابِهِ: اذهبْ يَا رَافِعُ إِلَى ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، فَذَكَرَهُ .

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، أَنْبَأَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنِي زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ؛ أَنَّ رِجَالًا مِنَ الْمُنَافِقِينَ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم كان إِذَا خرَج رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِلَى الْغَزْوِ تَخَلَّفوا عَنْهُ، وفَرِحوا بِمَقْعَدِهِمْ خِلَافَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَإِذَا قَدم رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ مِنَ الْغَزْوِ اعْتَذَرُوا إِلَيْهِ وَحَلَفُوا، وَأَحَبُّوا أَنْ يُحْمَدُوا بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا، فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَا أَتَوْا وَيُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يُحْمَدُوا بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا﴾ الْآيَةَ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، بِنَحْوِهِ وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ اللَّيْثِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ هشَام بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ قَالَ: كَانَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ وَرَافِعُ بْنُ خَديج وَزَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ عِنْدَ مَرْوان فَقَالَ: يَا أَبَا سَعِيدٍ، رَأيت قَوْلَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَا أَتَوْا وَيُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يُحْمَدُوا بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا﴾ وَنَحْنُ نَفْرَحُ بِمَا أتَيْنا ونُحِب أَنْ نُحْمَد بِمَا لَمْ نَفْعَلْ؟ فَقَالَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ: إِنَّ هَذَا لَيْسَ مِنْ ذَاكَ، إِنَّمَا ذَاكَ أَنَّ نَاسًا مِنَ الْمُنَافِقِينَ كَانُوا يَتخلَّفون إِذَا بَعَثَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بَعْثًا، فَإِنْ كَانَ فِيهِ نَكْبة فَرِحُوا بِتَخَلُّفِهِمْ، وَإِنْ كَانَ لَهُمْ نَصْر مِنَ اللَّهِ وَفَتْحٌ حَلَفُوا لَهُمْ لِيُرْضُوهُمْ وَيَحْمَدُوهُمْ عَلَى سُرُورِهِمْ بِالنَّصْرِ وَالْفَتْحِ. فَقَالَ مَرْوَانُ: أَيْنَ هَذَا مِنْ هَذَا؟ فَقَالَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ: وَهَذَا يَعْلَمُ هَذَا، فَقَالَ مَرْوَانُ: أَكَذَلِكَ يَا زَيْدُ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ، صَدَقَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ. ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ: وَهَذَا يَعْلَمُ ذَاكَ -يَعْنِي رَافِعَ بْنَ خَدِيجٍ-وَلَكِنَّهُ يَخْشَى إِنْ أَخْبَرَكَ أَنْ تَنْزِعَ قَلائصه فِي الصَّدَقَةِ. فَلَمَّا خَرَجُوا قَالَ زَيْدٌ لِأَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ: أَلَا تَحْمَدُنِي عَلَى شَهَادَةٍ لَكَ ؟ فَقَالَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ: شهدتَ الْحَقَّ. فَقَالَ زَيْدٌ: أَوْ لَا تَحْمَدُنِي عَلَى مَا شَهِدْتُ الْحَقَّ؟

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ مِنْ حَدِيثِ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ رَافِعِ بْنِ خَدِيجٍ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ هُوَ وَزَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ عِنْدَ مَروان بْنِ الْحَكَمِ، وَهُوَ أَمِيرُ الْمَدِينَةِ، فَقَالَ مَرْوَانُ: يَا رَافِعُ، فِي أَيِّ شَيْءٍ نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ؟ فَذَكَرَهُ كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ، وَكَانَ مَرْوان يَبْعَثُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ يَسْأَلُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ، فَقَالَ لَهُ مَا ذَكَرْنَاهُ، وَلَا منَافاة بَيْنَ مَا ذَكَرَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَمَا قَالَهُ هَؤُلَاءِ؛ لِأَنَّ الْآيَةَ عَامَّةٌ فِي جَمِيعِ مَا ذَكَرَ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويه أَيْضًا مِنْ حَدِيثِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي عَتِيق وَمُوسَى بْنِ عُقْبة، عَنِ الزهْري، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ؛ أَنَّ ثَابِتَ بْنَ قَيْسٍ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، وَاللَّهِ لَقَدْ خَشِيتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ هَلَكْتُ. قَالَ: "لِمَ؟ " قَالَ: نَهَى اللَّهُ الْمَرْءَ أَنْ يُحِب أَنْ يُحْمَدَ بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلْ، وَأَجِدُنِي أُحِبُّ الحمدَ. وَنَهَى اللَّهُ عَنِ الخُيلاء، وَأَجِدُنِي أُحِبُّ الْجَمَالَ، وَنَهَى اللَّهُ أَنْ نَرْفَعَ أَصْوَاتَنَا فَوْقَ صَوْتِكَ، وَأَنَا امْرُؤٌ جَهْوَرِيُّ الصَّوْتِ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أَلَّا تَرْضى أَنْ تَعِيش حَمِيدا، وتُقْتَل شَهِيدا، وَتَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ؟ " قَالَ: بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ. فَعَاشَ حَمِيدًا، وقُتل شَهِيدَا يَوْمَ مُسَيْلَمة الْكَذَّابِ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَلا تَحْسَبَنَّهُمْ بِمَفَازَةٍ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ﴾ يُقْرَأُ بِالتَّاءِ عَلَى مُخَاطَبَةِ الْمُفْرَدِ، وَبِالْيَاءِ عَلَى الْإِخْبَارِ عَنْهُمْ، أَيْ: لَا تَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ نَاجُونَ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ، بَلْ لَا بُدَّ لَهُمْ مِنْهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ وَاللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ مَالِكُ كُل شَيْءٍ، وَالْقَادِرُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ فَلَا يُعْجِزُهُ شَيْءٌ، فَهَابُوهُ وَلَا تُخَالِفُوهُ، وَاحْذَرُوا نِقْمَتَهُ وَغَضَبَهُ، فَإِنَّهُ الْعَظِيمُ الَّذِي لَا أَعْظَمَ مِنْهُ، الْقَدِيرُ الَّذِي لَا أَقْدَرَ مِنْهُ.

---

## 3:190-194 — verses 190-194 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Al-Tabarani said: Al-Hasan ibn Ishaq al-Tustari reported to us, Yahya al-Himmani reported to us, Ya'qub al-Qummi reported to us, from Ja'far ibn Abi al-Mughira, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas who said: Quraysh came to the Jews and said, "With what did Moses come to you?" They said, "His staff and his hand shining white to those who looked upon it." Then they came to the Christians and said, "How was Jesus?" They said, "He used to heal the blind and the leper and give life to the dead." So they came to the Prophet ﷺ and said, "Call upon your Lord for us to turn al-Safa into gold for us." He called upon his Lord, and this verse came down: {Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the alternation of night and day, are signs for people of understanding.} So let them reflect upon it. This is problematic, for this verse is Medinan, while their request for al-Safa to be turned into gold occurred in Mecca. And Allah knows best.

The meaning of the verse is that He, Exalted, says: {Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth}, that is, this one in its loftiness and vastness, and that one in its lowness, density and humbleness, and what is in them of the great observable signs: moving stars and fixed ones, seas, mountains, deserts, trees, plants, crops and fruits, animals, minerals, and benefits, differing in colors, tastes, smells, and properties. {and the alternation of night and day}, that is, their succession and their trading off in length and shortness. At times one lengthens while the other shortens, then they become equal, then one takes from the other so that the one which was short becomes long, and the one which was long becomes short. All of that is the determination of the Almighty, the Wise. Therefore He said: {for people of understanding}, that is, those of complete, sharp intellects who perceive things according to their true realities as they clearly are, and who are not like the deaf and mute who do not understand, those about whom Allah, Exalted, said: {And how many a sign in the heavens and the earth do they pass by, while they are turning away from it. And most of them do not believe in Allah except while they associate partners with Him.} [Yusuf: 105, 106]

Then Allah, Exalted, described the people of understanding, saying: {Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on their sides}, as is established in Sahih al-Bukhari from Imran ibn Husayn, may Allah be pleased with him, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Pray standing; if you cannot, then sitting; if you cannot, then on your side." That is, they do not cease remembering Him in all their states, with their inner selves, their consciences, and their tongues. {and they reflect upon the creation of the heavens and the earth}, that is, they comprehend what is in them of the wisdoms that point to the greatness of the Creator, His power, His knowledge, His wisdom, His choice, and His mercy.

Shaykh Abu Sulayman al-Darani said: "I go out from my house, and my eye falls upon nothing except that I see in it either a blessing from Allah upon me, or a lesson for me." Ibn Abi al-Dunya related this in his book "Reflection and Contemplation."

It is reported from al-Hasan al-Basri that he said: "An hour's reflection is better than a night spent in prayer." Al-Fudayl said: al-Hasan said: "Reflection is a mirror that shows you your good deeds and your bad deeds." Sufyan ibn Uyayna said: "Reflection is a light that enters your heart." He would sometimes recite this line of poetry:

"If a man possesses reflection, then in everything there is for him a lesson."

It is reported from Jesus (peace be upon him) that he said: "Blessed is he whose speech is remembrance, whose silence is reflection, and whose gaze is upon lessons."

Luqman the Wise said: "Prolonged solitude inspires reflection more, and prolonged reflection is evidence of knocking upon the gate of Paradise."

Wahb ibn Munabbih said: "Never has a person's reflection been prolonged except that he understood, and never has a person understood except that he came to know, and never has a person come to know except that he acted."

Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz said: "Speech in remembrance of Allah, Mighty and Majestic, is good, but reflection upon the blessings of Allah is the best form of worship."

Mughith al-Aswad said: "Visit the graves every day so that they may make you reflect, witness the standing place [of judgment] with your hearts, and look at how the two groups will depart, to Paradise or to the Fire, and make your hearts and bodies feel the remembrance of the Fire, its clubs of iron, and its layers." He used to weep at that until he was carried away, fallen among his companions, having lost his senses.

Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak said: "A man passed by a monk who was near a graveyard and a rubbish heap, and called out to him, saying: 'O monk, you have with you two of the treasures of this world from which you may take a lesson: the treasure of men and the treasure of wealth.'"

It is reported from Ibn Umar that when he wished to tend to his heart, he would come to a ruin and stand at its door, calling out in a sorrowful voice, saying: "Where are your inhabitants?" Then he would return to himself and say: {Everything is perishing except His Face} [al-Qasas: 88].

It is reported from Ibn Abbas that he said: "Two moderate prostrations performed with reflection are better than a night of standing in prayer with a heedless heart."

Al-Hasan said: "O son of Adam, eat in a third of your stomach, drink in a third of it, and leave the other third for breathing, for reflection."

One of the wise men said: "Whoever looks upon this world without taking a lesson from it, the sight of his heart becomes obscured to the extent of that heedlessness."

Bishr ibn al-Harith al-Hafi said: "If people were to reflect upon the greatness of Allah, Exalted, they would not disobey Him."

Al-Hasan reported, from Amir ibn Abd Qays, who said: "I heard not one, nor two, nor three, but many of the companions of the Prophet ﷺ saying: 'The light of faith, or the radiance of faith, is reflection.'"

It is reported from Jesus (peace be upon him) that he said: "O weak son of Adam, fear Allah wherever you are, be a guest in this world, take the mosques as your home, teach your eyes weeping, your body patience, and your heart reflection, and do not concern yourself with tomorrow's provision."

It is reported from the Commander of the Faithful, Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, may Allah be pleased with him, that he wept one day among his companions, and was asked about it, and he said: "I reflected upon this world, its pleasures and its desires, and I took a lesson from it through it: its desires scarcely come to an end before their bitterness clouds them. And even if there were no lesson in it for one who seeks a lesson, there are indeed admonitions in it for one who remembers."

Ibn Abi al-Dunya said: al-Husayn ibn Abd al-Rahman recited to me:

"The delight of the believer is reflection, the pleasure of the believer is the lessons.
We praise Allah alone, we are all in danger.
Many a heedless one, his life has already passed and he did not perceive it.
Many a life that was above one's desires, adorned with flowers,
amid the murmur of springs and the shade of trees,
and joy from the plants and sweetness from the fruits,
the swiftness of time with its changes altered it and its people.
We praise Allah alone, indeed in this is a lesson.
Indeed in this is a lesson for the intelligent one, if he takes heed."

Allah, Exalted, has censured those who do not take a lesson from His creations that point to His essence, His attributes, His legislation, His decree, and His signs, saying: {And how many a sign in the heavens and the earth do they pass by, while they are turning away from it. And most of them do not believe in Allah except while they associate partners with Him.} [Yusuf: 105, 106] And He praised His believing servants: {Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and reflect upon the creation of the heavens and the earth}, saying, {Our Lord, You have not created this in vain}, that is, You have not created this creation without purpose, but rather in truth, so that You may recompense those who do evil for what they did, and recompense those who do good with what is best. Then they exalted Him above vanity and the creation of falsehood, saying {Exalted are You}, that is, above creating anything in vain, {so protect us from the punishment of the Fire}, that is, O You who created creation in truth and justice, O You who are exalted above deficiencies, faults, and vanity, protect us from the punishment of the Fire by Your power and Your strength, and direct us to deeds with which You will be pleased with us, and grant us success in righteous deeds by which You guide us to the gardens of bliss, and by which You shelter us from Your painful punishment.

Then they said: {Our Lord, indeed whomever You admit to the Fire, You have disgraced him}, that is, You have humiliated him and made his disgrace manifest to the assembled people, {and the wrongdoers have no helpers}, that is, on the Day of Resurrection there is no protector for them from You, and no escape for them from what You have willed for them.

{Our Lord, indeed we have heard a caller calling to faith}, that is, one summoning, calling to faith, and he is the Messenger ﷺ, {saying, "Believe in your Lord," and we believed}, that is, he says: "Believe in your Lord," and we believed, that is, we responded to him and followed him. {Our Lord, forgive us our sins}, that is, through our faith and our following of Your Prophet, forgive us our sins, that is, conceal them, {and remove from us our misdeeds}, that is, in what is between us and You, {and take us in death with the righteous}, that is, join us with the righteous. {Our Lord, and grant us what You have promised us through Your messengers}, it was said its meaning is: through faith in Your messengers. And it was said its meaning is: upon the tongues of Your messengers, and this is more apparent.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] narrated to us, from Anas ibn Malik, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Ascalon is one of the two brides. Allah will raise up from it on the Day of Resurrection seventy thousand who will not be called to account, and He will raise up from it fifty thousand martyrs coming as delegations to Allah. And in it are rows of the martyrs, their heads severed and carried in their hands, their neck veins gushing with blood, saying: {Our Lord, grant us what You promised us through Your messengers, and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed You do not break the promise}. So He will say: My servant has spoken the truth. Wash them in the river of the egg. So they will emerge from it pure and white, and they will roam freely in Paradise wherever they wish."

This hadith is counted among the gharib (singular) reports of the Musnad, and some consider it fabricated. And Allah knows best.

{And do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection}, that is, before all of creation. {Indeed You do not break the promise}, that is, the promise which You informed Your messengers about must inevitably come to pass, namely the standing on the Day of Resurrection before You.

Al-Hafiz Abu Ya'la said: [chain] narrated to us, that Jabir ibn Abdullah reported to him: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The disgrace and humiliation that will befall the son of Adam on the Day of Resurrection, in the standing before Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will be such that the servant will wish to be ordered to the Fire instead." This is a gharib (singular) hadith.

It is established that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite these ten verses from the end of Surat Al Imran when he rose at night for his tahajjud (night prayer). Al-Bukhari, may Allah have mercy on him, said:

[chain] narrated to us, from Ibn Abbas who said: I spent the night at the home of my maternal aunt Maymunah. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ conversed with his family for a while, then went to sleep. When the last third of the night came, he sat up and looked at the sky and said: {Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of night and day are signs for those of understanding}. Then he got up, performed ablution, and used the tooth-stick. Then he prayed eleven rak'ahs. Then Bilal gave the call to prayer, so he prayed two rak'ahs, then went out and led the people in the dawn prayer.

Muslim likewise related it from Abu Bakr ibn Ishaq al-San'ani, from Ibn Abi Maryam, with the same chain. Then al-Bukhari related it through other chains from Malik, from Makhramah ibn Sulayman, from Kurayb, from Ibn Abbas: that he spent the night at the home of Maymunah, wife of the Prophet ﷺ, who was his maternal aunt. He said: I lay across the width of the pillow, while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his wife lay along its length. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ slept until, when it was midnight, or a little before it, or a little after it, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ woke from his sleep and began wiping the sleep from his face with his hand. Then he recited the ten closing verses of Surat Al Imran. Then he got up to a water-skin hanging up and performed ablution from it, doing so thoroughly, then stood to pray. Ibn Abbas said: I got up and did as he had done, then went and stood beside him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ placed his right hand on my head and took hold of my right ear, twisting it. Then he prayed two rak'ahs, then two, then two, then two, then two, then two, then prayed witr. Then he lay down until the muezzin came to him, whereupon he got up and prayed two light rak'ahs, then went out and prayed the dawn prayer.

The rest of the group of hadith compilers likewise reported it through various chains from Malik, with the same chain. Muslim also related it, as did Abu Dawud, through other routes, from Makhramah ibn Sulayman, with the same chain.

Another route for this hadith from Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with both of them.

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] narrated to us, from Ali ibn Abdullah ibn Abbas, from Abdullah ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Abbas ordered me to spend the night with the family of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and observe his prayer. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ led the people in the final night prayer, and when no one remained in the mosque besides him, he got up and passed by me and said: "Who is this? Abdullah?" I said: Yes. He said: "What is it?" I said: Al-Abbas ordered me to spend tonight with you. He said: "Then come along, come along." When he entered, he said: "Spread a bed for Abdullah?" So a cushion made of coarse cloth was brought. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ slept on it until I heard his breathing, then he sat up straight on his bed. He said: he raised his head to the sky and said: "Glory be to the Sovereign, the Most Holy," three times, then recited these verses from the end of Surat Al Imran until he finished it.

Muslim, Abu Dawud, and al-Nasa'i also related a hadith on this matter from Ali ibn Abdullah ibn Abbas.

Another route was related by Ibn Mardawayh, from the hadith of Asim ibn Bahdalah, from one of his companions, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas: that the Prophet ﷺ went out one night after part of the night had passed, and looked at the sky, and recited this verse: {Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of night and day are signs for those of understanding} to the end of the surah. Then he said: "O Allah, place light in my heart, light in my hearing, light in my sight, light on my right, light on my left, light before me, light behind me, light above me, light below me, and magnify for me light on the Day of Resurrection." This supplication is established in some of the chains of the Sahih, through the narration of Kurayb, from Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with him.

Then Ibn Mardawayh and Ibn Abi Hatim related from the hadith of Ja'far ibn Abi al-Mughirah, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: The Quraysh went to the Jews and said: What signs did Moses (peace be upon him) bring you? They said: His staff and his hand that shone white for those who looked at it. And they went to the Christians and said: How was Jesus (peace be upon him) among you? They said: He used to heal the blind and the leper and bring the dead to life. So they went to the Prophet ﷺ and said: Call upon your Lord for us, that He make al-Safa into gold for us. So he called upon his Lord, Mighty and Majestic, and then {Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of night and day are signs for those of understanding} was revealed. He said: "Let them reflect upon it." This is the wording of Ibn Mardawayh.

The account of al-Tabarani for this hadith was given previously at the beginning of the verse, and it implies that these verses were Meccan, whereas it is well known that they are Medinan. The evidence for this is the following hadith. Ibn Mardawayh said:

[chain] narrated to us, from al-Kalbi, that is Abu Janab al-Kalbi, from Ata who said: I went along with Ibn Umar and Ubayd ibn Umayr to Aisha, may Allah be pleased with her. We entered upon her while a curtain separated us from her. She said: O Ubayd, what keeps you from visiting us? He said: The words of the poet:

Visit occasionally, and love will increase.

Ibn Umar then said: Leave that, and tell us about the most amazing thing you saw from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. She wept and said: All his affairs were amazing. He came to me one night until his skin touched my skin, then he said: Let me worship my Lord, Mighty and Majestic. She said: I said: By Allah, I love being near you, and I also love that you worship your Lord. So he got up to the water-skin and performed ablution without pouring much water, then stood to pray, and he wept until his beard became wet. Then he prostrated and wept until he wet the ground. Then he lay on his side and wept, until Bilal came to call him to the dawn prayer. She said: He said: O Messenger of Allah, what makes you weep, when Allah has already forgiven you your sin, what came before and what came after? He said: "Woe to you, O Bilal, and what should prevent me from weeping, when this night was revealed to me: {Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of night and day are signs for those of understanding}?" Then he said: "Woe to him who recites it and does not reflect upon it."

Abd ibn Humayd related it, from Ja'far ibn Awn, from Abu Janab al-Kalbi, from Ata, at greater length and with a fuller account than this.

Likewise Abu Hatim Ibn Hibban related it in his Sahih, from Imran ibn Musa, from Uthman ibn Abi Shaybah, from Yahya ibn Zakariyya, from Ibrahim ibn Suwayd al-Nakha'i, from Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Sulayman, from Ata, who said: I entered, along with Abdullah ibn Umar and Ubayd ibn Umayr, upon Aisha, and he mentioned something similar.

Likewise Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Abi al-Dunya related it in his book "al-Tafakkur wa al-I'tibar" (Reflection and Consideration), from Shuja ibn Ashras, with the same chain.

Then he said: Al-Hasan ibn Abd al-Aziz related to me: I heard Sunayd mentioning from Sufyan, that is al-Thawri, raising it up (as a marfu' report), that he said: Whoever recites the end of Al Imran and does not reflect upon it, woe to him, counting on his fingers ten times. Al-Hasan ibn Abd al-Aziz said: Ubayd ibn al-Sa'ib informed me, saying: It was said to al-Awza'i: What is the utmost degree of reflection upon them? He said: He recites them while understanding their meaning.

Ibn Abi al-Dunya said: Qasim ibn Hashim related to me, Ali ibn Ayyash related to us, Abd al-Rahman ibn Sulayman related to us, who said: I asked al-Awza'i about the minimum that one who seeks to attach himself to reflection upon them can hold onto, and what would save him from this woe. He lowered his head for a little while, then said: He recites them while understanding their meaning.

[Another report in which there is gharabah (a singular, unusual transmission): Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: Abd al-Rahman ibn Bashir ibn Numayr informed us, Ishaq ibn Ibrahim al-Busti informed us, [chain] and he said: Ishaq ibn Ibrahim ibn Zayd informed us, Ahmad ibn Amr related to us, both of them said: Hisham ibn Ammar informed us, Sulayman ibn Musa al-Zuhri informed us, Mazahir ibn Aslam al-Makhzumi informed us, Sa'id ibn Abi Sa'id al-Maqburi informed us, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite the ten verses from the end of Surat Al Imran every night. Mazahir ibn Aslam is weak (da'if).]

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ الطَّبَرَانِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ التُسْتَرِي، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى الحِمَّاني، حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ القُمِّي، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي الْمُغِيرَةِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْر، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قال: أَتَتْ قُرَيْشٌ الْيَهُودَ فَقَالُوا: بِمَ جَاءَكُمْ مُوسَى؟ قَالُوا: عَصَاهُ وَيَدُهُ بَيْضَاءُ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ. وَأَتَوُا النَّصَارَى فَقَالُوا: كَيْفَ كَانَ عِيسَى؟ قَالُوا: كَانَ يُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ وَالْأَبْرَصَ ويُحيي الْمَوْتَى: فَأَتَوُا النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَقَالُوا: ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يَجْعَلُ لَنَا الصَّفا ذَهَبًا. فَدَعَا رَبَّهُ، فَنَزَلَتْ هذه الآية: ﴿إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لآيَاتٍ لأولِي الألْبَابِ﴾ فَلْيَتَفَكَّرُوا فيها وَهَذَا مُشْكل، فَإِنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ مَدَنِيَّةٌ. وَسُؤَالُهُمْ أَنْ يَكُونَ الصَّفَا ذَهَبًا كَانَ بِمَكَّةَ. وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَمَعْنَى الْآيَةِ أَنَّهُ يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ﴾ أَيْ: هَذِهِ فِي ارْتِفَاعِهَا وَاتِّسَاعِهَا، وَهَذِهِ فِي انْخِفَاضِهَا وَكَثَافَتِهَا وَاتِّضَاعِهَا وَمَا فِيهِمَا مِنَ الْآيَاتِ الْمُشَاهَدَةِ الْعَظِيمَةِ مِنْ كَوَاكِبَ سَيَّارَاتٍ، وثوابتَ وَبِحَارٍ، وَجِبَالٍ وَقِفَارٍ وَأَشْجَارٍ وَنَبَاتٍ وَزُرُوعٍ وَثِمَارٍ، وَحَيَوَانٍ وَمَعَادِنَ وَمَنَافِعَ، مُخْتَلِفَةِ الْأَلْوَانِ وَالطَّعُومِ وَالرَّوَائِحِ وَالْخَوَاصِّ ﴿وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ﴾ أَيْ: تَعَاقُبُهُمَا وتَقَارضهما الطُّولَ وَالْقِصَرَ، فَتَارَةً يطُول هَذَا وَيَقْصُرُ هَذَا، ثُمَّ يَعْتَدِلَانِ، ثُمَّ يَأْخُذُ هَذَا مِنْ هَذَا فَيَطُولُ الَّذِي كَانَ قَصِيرًا، وَيَقْصُرُ الَّذِي كَانَ طَوِيلًا وَكُلُّ ذَلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿لأولِي الألْبَابِ﴾ أَيِ: الْعُقُولِ التَّامَّةِ الذَّكِيَّةِ الَّتِي تُدْرِكُ الْأَشْيَاءَ بِحَقَائِقِهَا عَلَى جَلِيَّاتِهَا، وَلَيْسُوا كَالصُّمِّ البُكْم الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ الَّذِينَ قَالَ اللَّهُ [تَعَالَى] فِيهِمْ: ﴿وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ آيَةٍ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهَا وَهُمْ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضُونَ. وَمَا يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِاللَّهِ إِلا وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ﴾ [يُوسُفَ:١٠٥، ١٠٦] .

ثُمَّ وَصَفَ تَعَالَى أُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ فَقَالَ: ﴿الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَى جُنُوبِهِمْ﴾ كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي صَحِيحِ الْبُخَارِيِّ عَنْ عِمْران بْنِ حُصَين، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "صَلِّ قَائِمًا، فَإِنْ لَمْ تَسْتَطِعْ فَقَاعِدًا، فَإِنْ لَم تَسْتَطِعْ فَعَلَى جَنْبِكَ أَيْ: لَا يَقْطَعُونَ ذِكْره فِي جَمِيعِ أَحْوَالِهِمْ بِسَرَائِرِهِمْ وَضَمَائِرِهِمْ وَأَلْسِنَتِهِمْ ﴿وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ﴾ أَيْ: يَفْهَمُونَ مَا فِيهِمَا مِنَ الحكَم الدَّالَّةِ عَلَى عَظَمَةِ الْخَالِقِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ، وَعِلْمِهِ وَحِكْمَتِهِ، وَاخْتِيَارِهِ وَرَحْمَتِهِ.

وَقَالَ الشَّيْخُ أَبُو سُلَيْمَانَ الدَّارَانِيُّ: إِنِّي لأخرجُ مِنْ مَنْزِلِي، فَمَا يَقَعُ بَصَرِي عَلَى شَيْءٍ إِلَّا رَأَيْتُ لِلَّهِ عَلَي فِيهِ نِعْمَة، أوْ لِي فِيهِ عِبْرَة. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي الدُّنْيَا فِي كِتَابِ "التَّفَكُّرِ وَالِاعْتِبَارِ".

وَعَنِ الْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: تَفَكُّر سَاعَة خَيْرٌ مِنْ قِيَامِ لَيْلَةٍ. وَقَالَ الفُضَيل: قَالَ الْحَسَنُ: الْفِكْرَةُ مِرْآة تُرِيكَ حَسنَاتك وَسَيِّئَاتِكَ. وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ: الْفِكْرَةُ نُورٌ يَدْخُلُ قَلْبَكَ. وَرُبَّمَا تَمَثَّلَ بِهَذَا الْبَيْتِ:

إِذَا الْمَرْءُ كَانَتْ لَهُ فكْرَةٌ ... فِفِي كُلِّ شَيْءٍ لَهُ عبرَة ...

وَعَنْ عِيسَى، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: طُوبَى لِمَنْ كَانَ قِيلُه تَذَكُّرًا، وصَمْته تَفكُّرًا، ونَظَره عِبَرًا.

وَقَالَ لُقْمَانُ الْحَكِيمُ: إِنَّ طُولَ الْوَحْدَةِ ألْهَمُ لِلْفِكْرَةِ، وطولَ الفكْرة دَلِيلٌ عَلَى طَرْق بَابِ الْجَنَّةِ.

وَقَالَ وَهَبُ بْنُ مُنَبِّه: مَا طَالَتْ فِكْرَةُ امرِئ قَطُّ إِلَّا فَهِمَ، وَمَا فَهِمَ امْرُؤٌ قَطُّ إِلَّا عَلِمَ، وَمَا عَلِمَ امْرُؤٌ قَطُّ إلا عمل.

وَقَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ: الْكَلَامُ بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، حَسَن، وَالْفِكْرَةُ فِي نِعَمِ اللَّهِ أَفْضَلُ الْعِبَادَةِ.

وَقَالَ مُغِيثٌ الْأَسْوَدُ: زُورُوا الْقُبُورَ كُلَّ يَوْمِ تُفَكِّرُكُمْ، وَشَاهِدُوا الْمَوْقِفَ بِقُلُوبِكُمْ، وَانْظُرُوا إِلَى الْمُنْصَرَفِ بِالْفَرِيقَيْنِ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ أَوِ النَّارِ، وَأَشْعِرُوا قُلُوبَكُمْ وَأَبْدَانَكُمْ ذِكْرَ النَّارِ وَمَقَامِعَهَا وَأَطْبَاقَهَا، وَكَانَ يَبْكِي عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى يُرْفع صَريعا مِنْ بَيْنِ أَصْحَابِهِ، قَدْ ذَهَبَ عَقْلُهُ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ: مَرَّ رَجُلٌ برَاهبٍ عِنْدَ مَقْبَرة ومَزْبَلَة، فَنَادَاهُ فَقَالَ: يَا رَاهِبُ، إِنَّ عِنْدَكَ كَنزين مِنْ كُنُوزِ الدُّنْيَا لَكَ فِيهِمَا مُعْتَبَر، كَنْزُ الرِّجَالِ وَكَنْزُ الْأَمْوَالِ.

وَعَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَتَعَاهَدَ قَلْبَهُ، يَأْتِي الخَرِبة فَيَقِفُ عَلَى بَابِهَا، فَيُنَادِي بِصَوْتٍ حَزِينٍ فَيَقُولُ: أَيْنَ أهْلُك؟ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ إِلَى نَفْسِهِ فَيَقُولُ: ﴿كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلا وَجْهَهُ﴾ [الْقَصَصِ:٨٨] .

وَعَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: رَكْعَتَانِ مُقْتَصِدَتَانِ فِي تَفكُّر، خَيْرٌ مِنْ قِيَامِ لَيْلَةٍ وَالْقَلْبُ سَاهٍ .

وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ: يَا ابْنَ آدَمَ، كُلْ فِي ثُلُثِ بَطْنِكَ، وَاشْرَبْ فِي ثُلْثِهِ، وَدَعْ ثُلُثَهُ الْآخَرَ تتنفَّس لِلْفِكْرَةِ.

وَقَالَ بَعْضُ الْحُكَمَاءِ: مَنْ نَظَرَ إِلَى الدُّنْيَا بِغَيْرِ الْعِبْرَةِ انطَمَسَ مِنْ بَصَرِ قَلْبِهِ بِقَدْرِ تِلْكَ الغَفْلَة.

وَقَالَ بِشْر بْنُ الْحَارِثِ الْحَافِي: لَوْ تَفَكَّرَ النَّاسُ فِي عَظَمَةِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى لَمَا عَصَوْهُ.

وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ، عَنْ عَامِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ قَيْسٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ غَيْرَ وَاحِدٍ وَلَا اثْنَيْنِ وَلَا ثَلَاثَةً مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ يَقُولُونَ: إِنَّ ضِيَاءَ الْإِيمَانِ، أَوْ نُورَ الْإِيمَانِ، التَّفَكُّرُ.

وَعَنْ عِيسَى، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: يَا ابْنَ آدَمَ الضَّعِيفَ، اتَّقِ اللَّهَ حَيْثُمَا كُنْتَ، وكُنْ فِي الدُّنْيَا ضَيْفًا، واتَّخِذِ المساجدَ بَيْتًا، وعَلِّم عَيْنَيْكَ الْبُكَاءَ، وجَسَدك الصَّبْر، وَقَلْبَكَ الفِكْر، وَلَا تَهْتَمَّ بِرِزْقِ غَدٍ.

وَعَنْ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَنَّهُ بَكَى يَوْمًا بَيْنَ أَصْحَابِهِ، فسُئل عَنْ ذَلِكَ، فَقَالَ: فَكَّرت فِي الدُّنْيَا وَلِذَّاتِهَا وَشَهَوَاتِهَا، فَاعْتَبَرْتُ مِنْهَا بِهَا، مَا تَكَادُ شَهَوَاتُهَا تَنْقَضي حَتَّى تُكَدِّرَهَا مرارتُها، وَلَئِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ فِيهَا عِبْرَةٌ لِمَنِ اعْتَبَرَ إِنَّ فِيهَا مَوَاعِظَ لِمَنِ ادَّكَرَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي الدُّنْيَا: أَنْشَدَنِي الحُسَين بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ: نزهَة الْمُؤْمِنِ الفكَرْ ... لَذَّةُ الْمُؤْمِنِ العِبرْ ...

نحمدُ اللهَ وَحْدَه ... نحْنُ كُلٌّ عَلَى خَطَرْ ...

رُبّ لاهٍ وعُمْرُه ... قَدْ تَقَضّى وَمَا شَعَرْ ...

رُبّ عَيْشٍ قَدْ كَانَ فَوْ ... قَ المُنَى مُونقَ الزَهَرْ ...

فِي خَرير مِنَ العيُو ... نِ وَظل مِنَ الشَّجَرْ ...

وسُرُور مِنَ النَّبا ... تِ وَطيب منَ الثَمَرْ ...

غَيَّرَتْه وَأهْلَهُ سرعةُ الدّهْر بالغَيرْ ..

نَحْمَد اللَّهَ وَحْدَهُ ... إِنَّ فِي ذَا لَمُعْتَبَرْ ...

إِنَّ فِي ذَا لَعبرةً ... لِلَبِيبٍ إِنِ اعْتَبَرْ ...

وَقَدْ ذَمَّ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى مَنْ لَا يَعْتَبِرُ بِمَخْلُوقَاتِهِ الدَّالَّةِ عَلَى ذَاتِهِ وَصِفَاتِهِ وَشَرْعِهِ وَقَدْرِهِ وَآيَاتِهِ، فَقَالَ: ﴿وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ آيَةٍ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهَا وَهُمْ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضُونَ. وَمَا يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِاللَّهِ إِلا وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ﴾ [يُوسُفَ:١٠٥، ١٠٦] وَمَدَحَ عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ: ﴿الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَى جُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ﴾ قَائِلِينَ ﴿رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَذَا بَاطِلا﴾ أَيْ: مَا خَلَقْتَ هَذَا الْخَلْقَ عَبَثًا، بَلْ بالحق لتجزي الذين أساؤوا بِمَا عَمِلُوا، وَتَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا بِالْحُسْنَى. ثُمَّ نَزَّهُوهُ عَنِ الْعَبَثِ وَخَلْقِ الْبَاطِلِ فَقَالُوا: ﴿سُبْحَانَكَ﴾ أَيْ: عَنْ أَنْ تَخْلُقَ شَيْئًا بَاطِلًا ﴿فَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ﴾ أَيْ: يَا مَنْ خَلَق الْخَلْقَ بِالْحَقِّ وَالْعَدْلِ يَا مَنْ هُوَ مُنزه عَنِ النَّقَائِصِ وَالْعَيْبِ وَالْعَبَثِ، قِنَا مِنْ عَذَابِ النَّارِ بِحَوْلِكَ وَقُوَّتِكَ وَقيضْنَا لِأَعْمَالٍ تَرْضَى بِهَا عَنَّا، وَوَفِّقْنَا لِعَمَلٍ صَالِحٍ تَهْدِينَا بِهِ إِلَى جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ، وَتُجِيرُنَا بِهِ مِنْ عَذَابِكَ الْأَلِيمِ.

ثُمَّ قَالُوا: ﴿رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ مَنْ تُدْخِلِ النَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُ﴾ أَيْ: أَهَنْتَهُ وَأَظْهَرْتَ خِزْيَهُ لِأَهْلِ الْجَمْعِ ﴿وَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنْصَارٍ﴾ أَيْ: يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا مُجِير لَهُمْ مِنْكَ، وَلَا مُحِيد لَهُمْ عَمَّا أَرَدْتَ بِهِمْ.

﴿رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا سَمِعْنَا مُنَادِيًا يُنَادِي لِلإيمَانِ﴾ أَيْ: دَاعِيًا يَدْعُو إِلَى الْإِيمَانِ، وَهُوَ الرَّسُولُ ﷺ ﴿أَنْ آمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ فَآمَنَّا﴾ أَيْ يَقُولُ: ﴿آمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ فَآمَنَّا﴾ أَيْ: فَاسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُ وَاتَّبَعْنَاهُ ﴿رَبَّنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا﴾ أَيْ: بِإِيمَانِنَا وَاتِّبَاعِنَا نَبِيَّكَ فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا، أَيِ: اسْتُرْهَا ﴿وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئَاتِنَا﴾ أَيْ: فِيمَا بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكَ ﴿وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ الأبْرَارِ﴾ أَيْ: أَلْحِقْنَا بِالصَّالِحِينَ ﴿رَبَّنَا وَآتِنَا مَا وَعَدتَّنَا عَلَى رُسُلِكَ﴾ قِيلَ: مَعْنَاهُ: عَلَى الْإِيمَانِ بِرُسُلِكَ. وَقِيلَ: مَعْنَاهُ: عَلَى أَلْسِنَةِ رُسُلِكَ. وَهَذَا أَظْهَرُ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْيَمَانِ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عِقَال، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "عَسْقَلان أَحَدُ الْعَرُوسَيْنِ، يَبْعَثُ اللَّهُ مِنْهَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفًا لَا حِسَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ، وَيَبْعَثُ مِنْهَا خَمْسِينَ أَلْفًا شُهَدَاءَ وُفُودًا إِلَى اللَّهِ، وَبِهَا صُفُوف الشهداء، رؤوسهم مُقَّطعة فِي أَيْدِيهِمْ، تَثِجّ أَوْدَاجُهُمْ دَمًا، يَقُولُونَ: ﴿رَبَّنَا وَآتِنَا مَا وَعَدتَّنَا عَلَى رُسُلِكَ وَلا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ﴾ فَيَقُولُ: صَدَق عَبْدِي، اغْسِلُوهُمْ بِنَهْرِ الْبَيْضَةِ. فَيَخْرُجُونَ مِنْهُ نَقَاءً بِيضًا، فَيَسْرَحُونَ فِي الْجَنَّةِ حَيْثُ شاؤوا".

وَهَذَا الْحَدِيثُ يُعد مِنْ غَرَائِبِ الْمُسْنَدِ، وَمِنْهُمْ من يجعله موضوعا، والله أعلم .

﴿وَلا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ﴾ أي: على رؤوس الْخَلَائِقِ ﴿إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ﴾ أَيْ: لَا بُدَّ مِنَ الْمِيعَادِ الَّذِي أخبرتَ عَنْهُ رسُلَك، وَهُوَ الْقِيَامُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ بَيْنَ يَدَيْكَ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو يَعْلَى: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَارِثُ بْنُ سُرَيْج حَدَّثَنَا الْمُعْتَمِرُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ عِيسَى، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُنْكَدِرِ؛ أَنَّ جَابِرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ حَدَّثَهُ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "الْعَارُ وَالتَّخْزِيَةُ تَبْلُغُ مِنَ ابْنِ آدَمَ فِي الْقِيَامَةِ فِي الْمَقَامِ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، مَا يَتَمَنَّى الْعَبْدُ أَنْ يُؤْمَرَ بِهِ إِلَى النَّارِ" حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ .

وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ كَانَ يَقْرَأُ هَذِهِ الْآيَاتِ الْعَشْرَ مِنْ آخِرِ آلِ عِمْرَانَ إِذَا قَامَ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ لِتَهَجُّدِهِ، فَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ:

حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي شَرِيكُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي نَمْر، عَنْ كُرَيب عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: بِتُّ عِنْدَ خَالَتِي مَيْمُونَةَ، فَتَحَدَّثَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ مَعَ أَهْلِهِ سَاعَةً ثُمَّ رَقَدَ، فَلَمَّا كَانَ ثُلث اللَّيْلِ الْآخِرِ قَعد فَنَظَرَ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَقَالَ: ﴿إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لآيَاتٍ لأولِي الألْبَابِ﴾ ثُمَّ قَامَ فَتَوَضَّأَ وَاسْتَنَّ. فَصَلَّى إِحْدَى عَشْرَة رَكْعَةً. ثُمَّ أَذَّنَ بلالٌ فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَصَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ الصُّبْحَ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، بِهِ ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ مِنْ طُرقٍ عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ مَخْرَمَة بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ كُرَيْبٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ بَاتَ عِنْدَ مَيْمُونَةَ زَوْجِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، وَهِيَ خَالَتُهُ، قَالَ: فَاضْطَجَعْتُ فِي عَرْض الْوِسَادَةِ، وَاضْطَجَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَأَهْلُهُ فِي طُولها، فَنَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ حَتَّى إِذَا انْتَصَفَ اللَّيْلُ -أَوْ قَبْلَهُ بِقَلِيلٍ، أَوْ بَعْدَهُ بِقَلِيلٍ -استيقظَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ مِنْ مَنَامِهِ، فَجَعَلَ يمسحُ النومَ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ بِيَدِهِ، ثُمَّ قَرَأَ الْعَشْرَ الْآيَاتِ الخواتيمَ مِنْ سُورة آلِ عِمْرَانَ، ثُم قَامَ إِلَى شَنّ مُعَلَّقَةٍ فَتَوَضَّأَ مِنْهَا فَأَحْسَنَ وُضُوءه ثُمَّ قَامَ يُصَلِّي -قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: فَقُمْتُ فَصَنَعْتُ مِثْلَ مَا صَنَعَ، ثُمَّ ذَهَبتُ فَقُمْتُ إِلَى جَنْبه -فَوَضَعَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَدَه الْيُمْنَى عَلَى رَأْسِي، وَأَخَذَ بِأُذُنِي الْيُمْنَى يَفْتلُها فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ أَوْتَرَ، ثُمَّ اضْطَجَعَ حَتَّى جَاءَهُ الْمُؤَذِّنُ، فَقَامَ فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَصَلَّى الصُّبْحَ.

وَهَكَذَا أَخْرَجَهُ بَقِيَّةُ الْجَمَاعَةِ مِنْ طُرُق عَنْ مَالِكٍ، بِهِ وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ أَيْضًا وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ مِنْ وُجُوهٍ أخرَ، عن مخرمة بن سليمان، به .

" طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى " لِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا] .

قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدُويه: حدثنا محمد بن أحمد بن محمد بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي مسرَّة أَنْبَأَنَا خَلاد بْنُ يَحْيَى، أَنْبَأَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْمِنْهَالِ بْنِ عَمْرو، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: أَمَرَنِي الْعَبَّاسُ أَنْ أَبِيتَ بِآلِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَأَحْفَظُ صَلَاتَهُ. قَالَ: فَصَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِالنَّاسِ صَلَاةَ الْعِشَاءِ الْآخِرَةِ، حَتَّى إِذَا لَمْ يَبْقَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ أَحَدٌ غَيْرُهُ قَامَ فَمَرَّ بِي، فَقَالَ: "مَنْ هَذَا؟ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ؟ " فَقُلْتُ نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "فَمَه؟ " قُلْتُ: أَمَرَنِي العباسُ أَنْ أَبِيتَ بِكُمُ اللَّيْلَةَ. قَالَ: "فَالْحَقِ الْحَقْ" فَلَمَّا أَنْ دَخَلَ قَالَ: "افرشَنْ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ؟ " فَأَتَى بِوِسَادَةٍ مِنْ مُسُوحٍ، قَالَ فَنَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَلَيْهَا حَتَّى سَمعتُ غَطِيطه، ثُمَّ اسْتَوَى عَلَى فِرَاشِهِ قَاعِدًا، قَالَ: فَرَفَع رأسَه إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَقَالَ: "سُبحان الْمَلِكِ الْقُدُّوسِ" ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ، ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَاتِ مِنْ آخِرِ سُورَةِ آلِ عِمْرَانَ حَتَّى خَتَمَهَا.

وَقَدْ رَوَى مُسْلِمٌ وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ حَدِيثًا فِي ذَلِكَ أَيْضًا .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى رَوَاهَا ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه، مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَاصِمِ بْنِ بَهْدَلَة، عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ؛ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ خَرَجَ ذَاتَ لَيْلَةٍ بَعْدَ مَا مَضى لَيْلٌ، فَنَظَرَ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ، وَتَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لآيَاتٍ لأولِي الألْبَابِ﴾ إِلَى آخِرِ السُّورَةِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: "اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْ فِي قَلْبِي نُورا، وَفِي سَمْعي نُورًا، وَفِي بَصَري نُورًا، وَعَنْ يَمِينِي نُورًا، وَعَنْ شِمَالِي نُورًا، وَمِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيّ نُورًا، وَمِنْ خَلْفي نُورًا، وَمِنْ فَوْقي نُورًا، وَمِنْ تَحْتِي نُورًا، وأعْظِم لِي نُورًا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ " وَهَذَا الدُّعَاءُ ثَابِتٌ فِي بَعْضِ طُرُقِ الصَّحِيحِ، مِنْ رِوَايَةِ كُريب، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ. .

ثُمَّ رَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه وَابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ مِنْ حَدِيثِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي الْمُغِيرَةِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: أَتَتْ قُرَيْشٌ الْيَهُودَ فَقَالُوا: بِمَا جَاءَكُمْ مُوسَى مِنَ الْآيَاتِ؟ قَالُوا: عَصَاهُ وَيَدُهُ الْبَيْضَاءُ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ. وَأَتَوُا النَّصَارَى فَقَالُوا: كَيْفَ كَانَ عِيسَى فِيكُمْ؟ قَالُوا: كَانَ يُبرئ الْأَكْمَهَ وَالْأَبْرَصَ وَيُحْيِي الْمَوْتَى. فَأَتَوُا النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَقَالُوا: ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يَجْعَلُ لَنَا الصَّفَا ذَهَبًا. فَدَعَا رَبَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لآيَاتٍ لأولِي الألْبَابِ﴾ قَالَ: "فليتفكروا فيها" لفظ ابن مَرْدُويَه.

وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ سِيَاقُ الطَّبَرَانِيِّ لِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ فِي أَوَّلِ الْآيَةِ، وَهَذَا يَقْتَضِي أَنْ تَكُونَ هَذِهِ الْآيَاتُ مَكِّيَّةٌ، وَالْمَشْهُورُ أَنَّهَا مَدَنِيَّةُ، وَدَلِيلُهُ الْحَدِيثُ الْآخَرُ، قَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدويه:

حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، أَخْبَرَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الْحَرَّانِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا شُجَاعُ بْنُ أَشْرَسَ، حَدَّثَنَا حَشْرج بْنُ نَبَاتَةَ الْوَاسِطِيُّ أَبُو مُكْرَمٍ، عَنِ الْكَلْبِيِّ -هُوَ أَبُو جَنَاب [الْكَلْبِيُّ] -عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: انْطَلَقْتُ أَنَا وَابْنُ عُمَرَ وعُبَيد بْنُ عُمَير إِلَى عَائِشَةَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا، فَدَخَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا وَبَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهَا حِجَابٌ، فَقَالَتْ: يَا عُبَيْدُ، مَا يَمْنَعُكَ مِنْ زِيَارَتِنَا؟ قَالَ: قَوْلُ الشَّاعِرِ:

زُر غِبًّا تَزْدَدْ حُبّا ...

فَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ: ذَرِينَا أَخْبِرِينَا بِأَعْجَبِ شَيْءٍ رَأَيْتِهِ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ. فَبَكَتْ وَقَالَتْ: كُلُّ أَمْرِهِ كَانَ عَجَبًا، أَتَانِي فِي لَيْلَتِي حَتَّى مَسَّ جِلْدُهُ جِلْدِي، ثُمَّ قَالَ: ذَرِينِي أَتَعَبَّدُ لِرَبِّي [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] قَالَتْ: فَقُلْتُ: وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّ قُرْبَكَ، وَإِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ تَعبد لِرَبِّكَ. فَقَامَ إِلَى الْقِرْبَةِ فَتَوَضَّأَ وَلَمْ يُكْثِرْ صَبَّ الْمَاءِ، ثُمَّ قَامَ يُصَلِّي، فَبَكَى حَتَّى بَلَّ لِحْيَتَهُ، ثُمَّ سَجَدَ فَبَكَى حَتَّى بَل الْأَرْضَ، ثُمَّ اضْطَجَعَ عَلَى جَنْبِهِ فَبَكَى، حَتَّى إِذَا أَتَى بِلَالٌ يُؤذنه بِصَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ قَالَتْ: فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا يُبكيك؟ وَقَدْ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَكَ ذَنْبَكَ مَا تَقَدَّمَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ، فَقَالَ: "وَيْحَكَ يَا بِلَالُ، وَمَا يَمْنَعُنِي أَنْ أَبْكِيَ وَقَدْ أُنْزِلَ عَلَيَّ فِي هَذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ: ﴿إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لآيَاتٍ لأولِي الألْبَابِ﴾ " ثُمَّ قَالَ: "وَيْلٌ لِمَنْ قَرَأَهَا وَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرْ فِيهَا".

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ عَبْد بْنُ حُمَيد، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ عَوْن، عَنْ أَبِي جَنَاب الْكَلْبِيِّ عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، بِأَطْوَلَ مِنْ هَذَا وَأَتَمَّ سِيَاقًا .

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو حاتم ابن حِبَّانَ فِي صَحِيحِهِ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ زَكَرِيَّا، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ سُوَيد النَّخعي، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ أَنَا [وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ] وعُبَيد بْنُ عُمَير عَلَى عَائِشَةَ فَذَكَرَ نَحْوَهُ.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي الدُّنْيَا فِي كِتَابِ "التَّفَكُّرِ وَالِاعْتِبَارِ" عَنْ شُجَاعِ بْنِ أَشْرَصَ، بِهِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ: سَمِعْتُ سُنَيْدًا يَذْكُرُ عَنْ سُفْيَانَ -هُوَ الثَّوْرِيُّ-رَفَعَهُ قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ آخِرَ آلِ عِمْرَانَ فَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرْ فِيهِ ويْلَه. يَعُدُّ بِأَصَابِعِهِ عَشْرًا. قَالَ الْحَسَنُ بن عبد العزيز: فأخبرني عُبَيد بْنُ السَّائِبِ قَالَ: قِيلَ لِلْأَوْزَاعِيِّ: مَا غاية التفكر فيهن؟ قال: يقرأهن وَهُوَ يَعْقِلُهُنَّ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي الدُّنْيَا: وَحَدَّثَنِي قَاسِمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَيَّاش، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْأَوْزَاعِيَّ عَنْ أَدْنَى مَا يَتَعلق بِهِ الْمُتَعَلِّقُ مِنَ الْفِكْرِ فِيهِنَّ وَمَا يُنْجِيهِ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَيْلِ؟ فَأَطْرَقَ هُنَيّة ثُمَّ قَالَ: يَقْرَؤُهُنَّ وَهُوَ يَعْقلُهُن.

[حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ فِيهِ غَرَابَةٌ: قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: أَنْبَأَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ بَشِيرِ بْنِ نُمَيْرٍ، أَنْبَأَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْبُسْتِيُّ ح وَقَالَ: أَنْبَأَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو قَالَا أَنْبَأَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ، أَنْبَأَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى الزُّهْرِيُّ، أَنْبَأَنَا مَظَاهِرُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ الْمَخْزُومِيُّ، أَنْبَأَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْمَقْبُرِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ كَانَ يَقْرَأُ عَشْرَ آيَاتٍ مِنْ آخِرِ سُورَةِ آلِ عِمْرَانَ كُلَّ لَيْلَةٍ. مُظَاهِرُ بن أسلم ضعيف] .

---

## 3:195 — verse 195

### Translation

The Almighty says: {So their Lord answered them}, meaning: their Lord responded to them, as the poet said: "A caller called out: O one who answers the summons... yet no one answering answered him at that time..."

Sa'id ibn Mansur said: Sufyan related to us, from 'Amr ibn Dinar, from Salamah, a man from the household of Umm Salamah, who said: Umm Salamah said: "O Messenger of Allah, we do not hear Allah mention women in connection with the migration (hijrah) at all." So Allah [Mighty and Majestic] revealed: {So their Lord answered them: I do not let the work of any worker among you be lost, whether male or female} to the end of the verse. And the Ansar said: "She was the first female traveler to arrive among us."

Al-Hakim related this in his Mustadrak from the hadith of Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah, then said: "Sound (sahih) according to the criterion of al-Bukhari, though the two of them (al-Bukhari and Muslim) did not include it."

Ibn Abi Najih related, from Mujahid, from Umm Salamah, that she said: "The last verse to be revealed was this verse: {So their Lord answered them: I do not let the work of any worker among you be lost, whether male or female; you are all from one another} to its end." Ibn Mardawayh related it.

The meaning of the verse is that when the believers possessed of understanding asked what was mentioned before, their Lord answered them, and this is followed by the connective "fa" (so), indicating direct sequence, as the Almighty said: {And when My servants ask you about Me, indeed I am near. I answer the call of the caller when he calls upon Me. So let them respond to Me and believe in Me, that they may be rightly guided} [al-Baqarah: 186].

His saying: {I do not let the work of any worker among you be lost, whether male or female} is an explanation of the answering, meaning He said to them, in answer to them, that He does not let the work of any worker before Him be lost. Rather, He gives every worker the full measure due for his work, whether male or female.

His saying: {you are all from one another} means: you are all equal in My reward. {And those who migrated}, meaning: they left the abode of shirk (polytheism) and came to the abode of faith, and parted from loved ones, companions, brothers, and neighbors. {And were driven out of their homes}, meaning: the polytheists pressed them with harm until they forced them to leave from among them. This is why He said: {and were harmed in My cause}, meaning: their only offense in the eyes of the people was that they believed in Allah alone, as the Almighty said: {who expel the Messenger and yourselves only because you believe in Allah, your Lord} [al-Mumtahinah: 1]. And the Almighty said: {And they resented them for nothing except that they believed in Allah, the Almighty, the Praiseworthy} [al-Buruj: 8].

His saying: {and fought and were killed}, this is the highest of stations: that one fights in the cause of Allah, so his steed is hamstrung and his face is smeared with his own blood and dust. It is established in the sound hadith (sahih) that a man said: "O Messenger of Allah, what do you think, if I am killed in the cause of Allah, enduring patiently, seeking reward, advancing and not retreating, will Allah forgive my sins for me?" He said: "Yes." Then he said: "How did you put it?" So the man repeated what he had said, and the Prophet ﷺ said: "Yes, except for debt. Gabriel just told me that."

This is why the Almighty said: {I will surely remove from them their misdeeds and admit them into gardens beneath which rivers flow}, meaning: rivers of various kinds of drink flow through their midst, of milk, honey, wine, and water that does not stagnate, and other than that, of what no eye has seen, no ear has heard, and no human heart has ever conceived.

His saying: {a reward from Allah's own presence}, He attributed it to Himself and ascribed it to Himself to indicate that it is immense, for the Great and Generous One gives only abundantly and generously, as the poet said: "If He punishes, it is deserved; and if He gives abundantly, then He does not hold back..."

His saying: {and with Allah is the best reward}, meaning: with Him is the best recompense for whoever does righteous deeds.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: It was mentioned from Duhaym ibn Ibrahim: al-Walid ibn Muslim related to us: Hariz ibn 'Uthman informed me: that Shaddad ibn Aws used to say: "O people, do not accuse Allah regarding His decree, for He does not wrong any believer. So when something you love befalls one of you, let him praise Allah, and when something you dislike befalls him, let him be patient and seek reward, for indeed with Allah is the best reward."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَاسْتَجَابَ لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: فَأَجَابَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ، كَمَا قَالَ الشَّاعِرُ: وداعٍ دَعَا: يَا مَن يُجِيبُ إِلَى النِّدَى ... فَلم يَسْتجبْه عنْد ذَاكَ مُجِيبُ ...

قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ، رَجُلٌ مِنْ آلِ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَتْ أُمُّ سَلَمَةَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، لَا نَسْمَع اللهَ ذَكَر النِّسَاءَ فِي الْهِجْرَةِ بِشَيْءٍ؟ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] ﴿فَاسْتَجَابَ لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ أَنِّي لَا أُضِيعُ عَمَلَ عَامِلٍ مِنْكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى﴾ إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ. وَقَالَتِ الْأَنْصَارُ: هِيَ أَوَّلُ ظَعِينَةٍ قَدمت عَلَيْنَا.

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنة، ثُمَّ قَالَ: صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ الْبُخَارِيِّ، وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ .

وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ أَبِي نَجيح، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ أَمِّ سَلَمة قَالَتْ: آخِرُ آيَةٍ أُنْزِلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿فَاسْتَجَابَ لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ أَنِّي لَا أُضِيعُ عَمَلَ عَامِلٍ مِنْكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى بَعْضُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ﴾ إِلَى آخِرِهَا. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه.

وَمَعْنَى الْآيَةِ: أَنَّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ذَوِي الْأَلْبَابِ لَمَّا سَأَلُوا -مِمَّا تَقَدَّمَ ذِكْرُهُ-فَاسْتَجَابَ لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ -عَقَّبَ ذَلِكَ بِفَاءِ التَّعْقِيبِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِي عَنِّي فَإِنِّي قَرِيبٌ أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الدَّاعِ إِذَا دَعَانِ فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا لِي وَلْيُؤْمِنُوا بِي لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْشُدُونَ﴾ [البقرة:١٨٦] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَنِّي لَا أُضِيعُ عَمَلَ عَامِلٍ مِنْكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى﴾ هَذَا تَفْسِيرٌ لِلْإِجَابَةِ، أَيْ قَالَ لَهُمْ مُجِيبًا لَهُمْ: أَنَّهُ لَا يَضِيعُ عَمَلُ عَامِلٍ لَدَيْهِ، بَلْ يُوَفّي كُلَّ عَامِلٍ بِقِسْطِ عَمَلِهِ، مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿بَعْضُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ﴾ أَيْ: جَمِيعُكُمْ فِي ثَوَابِي سَواء ﴿فَالَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا﴾ أَيْ: تَرَكُوا دَارَ الشِّرك وأتَوا إِلَى دَارِ الْإِيمَانِ وَفَارَقُوا الْأَحْبَابَ وَالْخُلَّانَ وَالْإِخْوَانَ وَالْجِيرَانَ، ﴿وَأُخْرِجُوا مِنْ دِيَارِهِمْ﴾ أَيْ: ضايقهم المشركون بالأذى حتى ألجؤوهم إِلَى الْخُرُوجِ مِنْ بَيْنِ أَظْهُرِهِمْ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَأُوذُوا فِي سَبِيلِي﴾ أَيْ: إِنَّمَا كَانَ ذنْبُهم إِلَى النَّاسِ أَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَحْدَهُ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿يُخْرِجُونَ الرَّسُولَ وَإِيَّاكُمْ أَنْ تُؤْمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ رَبِّكُمْ﴾ [الْمُمْتَحِنَةِ:١] . وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَا نَقَمُوا مِنْهُمْ إِلا أَنْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَمِيدِ﴾ [الْبُرُوجِ:٨] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَقَاتَلُوا وَقُتِلُوا﴾ وَهَذَا أَعْلَى الْمَقَامَاتِ أَنْ يُقَاتِلَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ، فيُعْقَر جَواده، ويعفَّر وَجْهُهُ بِدَمِهِ وَتُرَابِهِ، وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحِ أَنَّ رَجُلًا قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ قُتلت فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ صَابِرًا مُحْتَسبا مُقْبلا غَيْرَ مُدبِر، أيُكَفِّر اللَّهُ عَنِّي خَطَايَايَ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ" ثُمَّ قَالَ: "كَيْفَ قُلْتَ؟ ": فَأَعَادَ عَلَيْهِ مَا قَالَ، فَقَالَ: "نَعَمْ، إِلَّا الدَّين، قَالَهُ لِي جِبْرِيلُ آنِفًا".

وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿لأكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ وَلأدْخِلَنَّهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الأنْهَارُ﴾ أَيْ: تَجْرِي فِي خِلَالِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ مِنْ أَنْوَاعِ الْمَشَارِبِ، مِنْ لَبَنٍ وَعَسَلٍ وَخَمْرٍ وَمَاءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ وَغَيْرِ ذَلِكَ، مِمَّا لَا عَيْنَ رَأتْ، وَلَا أُذُنٌ سَمِعت، وَلَا خَطَر عَلَى قَلْبِ بَشَر.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ثَوَابًا مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَضَافَهُ إِلَيْهِ وَنَسَبَهُ إِلَيْهِ لِيدل عَلَى أَنَّهُ عَظِيمٌ؛ لِأَنَّ الْعَظِيمَ الْكَرِيمَ لَا يُعْطِي إِلَّا جَزيلا كَثِيرًا، كَمَا قَالَ الشَّاعِرُ: إِنْ يُعَذب يَكُن غَرامًا وَإِنْ يُعْ ... طِ جَزيلا فإنَّه لَا يُبَالي ...

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ عِنْدَهُ حُسْنُ الثَّوَابِ﴾ أَيْ: عِنْدَهُ حُسْن الْجَزَاءِ لِمَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: ذُكِرَ عَنْ دُحَيم بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي حَرِيز بْنُ عُثْمَانَ: أَنَّ شَدَّادَ بْنَ أَوْسٍ كَانَ يَقُولُ: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، لَا تَتهِموا اللَّهَ فِي قَضَائِهِ، فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَبْغِي عَلَى مُؤْمِنٍ، فَإِذَا نَزَلَ بِأَحَدِكُمْ شَيْءٌ مما يُحِب فليحْمَد الله، وإذا أنزل بِهِ شَيْءٌ مِمَّا يَكْرَهُ فَليَصْبر وَلِيَحْتَسِبْ، فَإِنَّ الله عنده حسن الثواب.

---

## 3:196-198 — verses 196-198 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty says: Do not look at the ease and delight and joy in which these disbelievers live in luxury, for soon all of this will vanish from them, and they will become bound by their evil deeds. We only extend their enjoyment to them as a gradual leading on (istidraj), and all that they possess is {a brief enjoyment, then their abode is Hell, and evil is the resting place}.

This verse is like His saying: {None dispute concerning the signs of Allah except those who disbelieve, so let not their movement about through the land deceive you} [Ghafir: 4], and He said: {Indeed, those who fabricate lies against Allah will not succeed. A brief enjoyment in this world, then to Us is their return, then We will make them taste the severe punishment for what they used to disbelieve} [Yunus: 69, 70], and He said: {We give them enjoyment for a little, then We will drive them to a harsh punishment} [Luqman: 24], and He said: {So grant respite to the disbelievers, give them respite a little} [al-Tariq: 17], meaning a short while, and He said: {Is he whom We have promised a good promise, which he will meet, like he whom We have given the enjoyment of the life of this world, then on the Day of Resurrection he will be among those brought forth?} [al-Qasas: 61].

Thus, when He mentioned the condition of the disbelievers in this world and mentioned that their end is the Fire, He said after that: "{But those who fear their Lord will have gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever, as an accommodation} meaning, hospitality from Allah, {and what is with Allah is better for the righteous (al-abrar)}."

Ibn Marduyah said: [chain] from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "They were only named al-abrar (the righteous) because they were dutiful to their fathers and their sons. Just as your parents have a right over you, likewise your child has a right over you."

This is how Ibn Marduyah narrated it from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As as a raised report (marfu'). Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] from Ibn Umar, who said: Allah only named them al-abrar because they were dutiful to their fathers and their sons. Just as your parents have a right over you, likewise your child has a right over you. This is more fitting, and Allah knows best.

Then Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] from al-Hasan, who said: al-abrar are those who do not harm even an ant (dharr).

Ibn Abi Hatim also said: [chain] from al-Aswad, who said: Abdullah, meaning Ibn Mas'ud, said: There is no soul, righteous or wicked, for whom death is not better for it. If it was righteous, then Allah has said: {and what is with Allah is better for the righteous}.

This was likewise narrated by Abd al-Razzaq, from al-A'mash, from al-Thawri, with the same chain, and he recited: {And let not those who disbelieve think that Our prolonging their days is good for themselves. We only extend it for them so that they may increase in sin, and for them is a humiliating punishment} [Al Imran: 178].

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] from Abu al-Darda, who used to say: There is no believer for whom death is not better for him, and there is no disbeliever for whom death is not better for him. Whoever does not believe me, then indeed Allah says: {and what is with Allah is better for the righteous}, and He says: {And let not those who disbelieve think that Our prolonging their days is good for themselves. We only extend it for them so that they may increase in sin, and for them is a humiliating punishment}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: لَا تَنْظُرُوا إِلَى مَا هَؤُلَاءِ الْكُفَّارِ مُتْرفون فِيهِ، مِنَ النِّعْمَة والغِبْطَة وَالسُّرُورِ، فعَمّا قَلِيلٍ يَزُولُ هَذَا كُلُّهُ عَنْهُمْ، وَيُصْبِحُونَ مُرتَهنين بِأَعْمَالِهِمُ السَّيِّئَةِ، فَإِنَّمَا نَمُدّ لَهُمْ فِيمَا هُمْ فِيهِ اسْتِدْرَاجًا، وَجَمِيعُ مَا هُمْ فِيهِ ﴿مَتَاعٌ قَلِيلٌ ثُمَّ مَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهَادُ﴾

وَهَذِهِ الْآيَةُ كَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿مَا يُجَادِلُ فِي آيَاتِ اللَّهِ إِلا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَلا يَغْرُرْكَ تَقَلُّبُهُمْ فِي الْبِلادِ﴾ [غَافِرٍ:٤] ، وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ لَا يُفْلِحُونَ. مَتَاعٌ فِي الدُّنْيَا ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ثُمَّ نُذِيقُهُمُ الْعَذَابَ الشَّدِيدَ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ﴾ [يُونُسَ:٦٩، ٧٠] ، وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿نُمَتِّعُهُمْ قَلِيلا ثُمَّ نَضْطَرُّهُمْ إِلَى عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ﴾ [لُقْمَانَ:٢٤] ، وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَمَهِّلِ الْكَافِرِينَ أَمْهِلْهُمْ رُوَيْدًا﴾ [الطَّارِقِ:١٧] ، أَيْ: قَلِيلًا وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿أَفَمَنْ وَعَدْنَاهُ وَعْدًا حَسَنًا فَهُوَ لاقِيهِ كَمَنْ مَتَّعْنَاهُ مَتَاعَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ثُمَّ هُوَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ مِنَ الْمُحْضَرِينَ﴾ [الْقَصَصِ:٦١] .

وَهَكَذَا لَمَّا ذَكَرَ حَالَ الْكُفَّارِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَذَكَرَ مَآلَهُمْ إِلَى النَّارِ قَالَ بَعْدَهُ: " ﴿لَكِنِ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا رَبَّهُمْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الأنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا نُزُلًا﴾ أَيْ: ضِيَافَةً مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ ﴿وَمَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لِلأبْرَارِ﴾

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ نَصْرٍ أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو طَاهِرٍ سَهْلُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، أَنْبَأَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ عَمَّار، أَنْبَأَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، أَنْبَأَنَا عُبَيد الله بن الوليد الوصافي عَنْ مُحَارب بْنِ دِثَار، عَنْ عَبْد اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِنَّمَا سُمّوا الْأَبْرَارَ لِأَنَّهُمْ بَرّوا الْآبَاءَ وَالْأَبْنَاءَ، كَمَا أَنَّ لِوَالِدَيْكَ عَلَيْكَ حَقًّا، كَذَلِكَ لِوَلَدِكَ عَلَيْكَ حَقٌّ".

كَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه عَنْ عَبْد اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ مَرْفُوعًا وَقَدْ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ جَنَاب، حَدَّثَنَا عيسى بن يونس، عَنْ عُبَيد اللَّهِ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ الْوَصَّافِيِّ عَنْ مُحَارِبِ بْنِ دِثَارٍ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا سَمَّاهُمُ اللَّهُ أَبْرَارًا لِأَنَّهُمْ بَرّوا الْآبَاءَ وَالْأَبْنَاءَ، كَمَا أَنَّ لِوَالِدَيْكَ عَلَيْكَ حَقًّا، كَذَلِكَ لِوَلَدِكَ عَلَيْكَ حَقٌّ، وَهَذَا أَشْبَهُ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مُسْلِمُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ الدَّسْتَوائي، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: الْأَبْرَارُ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْذُونَ الذَّرّ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ خيْثَمَة، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ-: مَا مِنْ نَفْس بَرّة وَلَا فَاجِرَةٍ إِلَّا الْمَوْتُ خيرٌ لَهَا، لَئِنْ كَانَ بَرًّا لَقَدْ قَالَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَمَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لِلأبْرَارِ﴾

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنِ الثَّوْرِيِّ، بِهِ، وَقَرَأَ: ﴿وَلا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ لأنْفُسِهِمْ إِنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ لِيَزْدَادُوا إِثْمًا وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُهِينٌ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ:١٧٨] .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي الْمُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ فَرَجِ بْنِ فَضَالَةَ، عَنْ لُقْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ إِلَّا وَالْمَوْتُ خَيْرٌ لَهُ، وَمَا مِنْ كَافِرٍ إِلَّا وَالْمَوْتُ خَيْرٌ لَهُ، وَمَنْ لَمْ يُصَدِّقْنِي فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿وَمَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لِلأبْرَارِ﴾ وَيَقُولُ: ﴿وَلا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ لأنْفُسِهِمْ إِنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ لِيَزْدَادُوا إِثْمًا وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُهِينٌ﴾

---

## 3:199-200 — verses 199-200 (shared commentary)

### Translation

He, exalted, informs about a group of the People of the Book, that they believe in Allah with true belief, and in what was sent down upon Muhammad, along with what they already believe of the earlier scriptures, and that they are humbled before Allah, meaning: obedient to Him, submissive and self-abasing in His presence. {They do not sell the verses of Allah for a small price}, meaning: they do not conceal with their own hands the glad tidings of Muhammad ﷺ, and his description, his attributes, his prophetic mission, and the description of his nation, which they mentioned. These are the choicest of the People of the Book and their elite, whether they were Jews or Christians. Allah, exalted, said in Surat al-Qasas: {Those to whom We gave the Scripture before it, they believe in it. And when it is recited to them, they say, "We believe in it; indeed, it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed we were, before it, Muslims." Those will be given their reward twice for what they patiently endured} [al-Qasas: 52-54]. And He, exalted, said: {Those to whom We have given the Book recite it with its true recitation; those are the ones who believe in it} [al-Baqarah: 121]. And He said: {And among the people of Moses is a community that guides by truth and thereby establishes justice} [al-A'raf: 159]. And He, exalted, said: {They are not all the same; among the People of the Book is a community standing firm, reciting the verses of Allah during the hours of the night, while they prostrate} [Al 'Imran: 113]. And He, exalted, said: {Say, "Believe in it or do not believe." Indeed, those who were given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, fall upon their faces in prostration, and they say, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, the promise of our Lord has been fulfilled." And they fall upon their faces weeping, and it increases them in humility} [al-Isra: 107-109].

These qualities are found among the Jews, but only in a small number, as was found in Abdullah ibn Salam and those like him among the Jewish rabbis who believed, and they did not reach ten souls. As for the Christians, many of them are rightly guided and submit to the truth, as Allah, exalted, said: {You will surely find the most intense of people in hostility toward the believers to be the Jews and those who associate partners with Allah. And you will surely find the nearest of them in affection to the believers those who say, "We are Christians." That is because among them are priests and monks and because they are not arrogant. And when they hear what was revealed to the Messenger, you see their eyes overflowing with tears because of what they have recognized of the truth. They say, "Our Lord, we have believed, so record us among the witnesses. And why should we not believe in Allah and what has come to us of the truth, and we long for our Lord to admit us with the righteous people?" So Allah rewarded them for what they said with gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally} [al-Ma'idah: 82-85].

And thus He said here: {Those will have their reward with their Lord. Indeed, Allah is swift in account.}

It is established in the hadith that Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, when he recited Surat {Kaf Ha Ya 'Ayn Sad} in the presence of the Negus, king of Abyssinia, with the patriarchs and priests present, he wept and they wept with him, until they had soaked their beards.

And it is established in the two Sahihs that when the Negus died, the Prophet ﷺ announced his death to his companions, and said: "Indeed a brother of yours in Abyssinia has died, so pray over him." So he went out with them to the open ground, arranged them in rows, and prayed over him.

Ibn Abi Hatim and the hafiz Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh narrated from the hadith of Hammad ibn Salamah, from Thabit, from Anas ibn Malik, who said: When the Negus died, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Seek forgiveness for your brother." Some of the people said: He commands us to seek forgiveness for a foreign non-Arab who died in the land of Abyssinia. Then this was revealed: {And indeed, among the People of the Book are those who believe in Allah and what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them, humbled before Allah} the verse.

Abd ibn Humayd and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it by another route from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Thabit, from al-Hasan, from the Prophet ﷺ. Then Ibn Mardawayh also narrated it through routes from Humayd, from Anas ibn Malik, similar to what preceded.

Ibn Jarir also narrated it from the hadith of Abu Bakr al-Hudhali, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Jabir, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to us when the Negus died: "Indeed your brother Ashamah has died." So the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out and prayed as one prays over the dead, saying the takbir over him four times. The hypocrites said: He prays over a foreign non-Arab who died in the land of Abyssinia. So Allah, mighty and majestic, revealed: {And indeed, among the People of the Book are those who believe in Allah and what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them, humbled before Allah, they do not sell the verses of Allah for a small price. Those will have their reward with their Lord. Indeed, Allah is swift in account.}

The hafiz Abu Abdullah al-Hakim narrated in his Mustadrak: [chain] Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr, from his father, who said: An enemy from their own land descended upon the Negus, so the Emigrants came to him and said: We would like to go out to them so that we may fight alongside you, and you may see our courage, and we may repay you for what you have done for us. He said: No remedy through the support of Allah, mighty and majestic, is better than a remedy through the support of people. He said: And concerning this was revealed: {And indeed, among the People of the Book are those who believe in Allah and what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them, humbled before Allah} the verse. Then he said: This is a hadith of sound chain, though the two of them (Bukhari and Muslim) did not narrate it.

Abu Dawud said: [chain] A'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, said: When the Negus died, we used to be told that a light was continually seen over his grave.

Ibn Abi Najih said, from Mujahid: {And indeed, among the People of the Book} means the Muslims among the People of the Book.

Abbad ibn Mansur said: I asked al-Hasan al-Basri about the saying of Allah, exalted: {And indeed, among the People of the Book are those who believe in Allah and what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them, humbled before Allah} the verse. He said: They are the People of the Book who were before Muhammad ﷺ, and they followed him and recognized Islam, so Allah, exalted, gave them a double reward: for the faith they held before Muhammad ﷺ, and for their following Muhammad ﷺ. Both of these were narrated by Ibn Abi Hatim.

It is established in the two Sahihs, from Abu Musa, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Three will be given their reward twice." And he mentioned among them: "and a man of the People of the Book who believed in his prophet and believed in me."

His saying: {They do not sell the verses of Allah for a small price} means: they do not conceal the knowledge that is in their hands, as the disreputable group among them did, but rather they give it freely without charge. And so He said: {Those will have their reward with their Lord. Indeed, Allah is swift in account.}

Mujahid said: {Swift in account} means: swift in reckoning. Narrated by Ibn Abi Hatim and others.

His saying: {O you who have believed, be patient, and compete in patience, and remain stationed} al-Hasan al-Basri, may Allah have mercy on him, said: They were commanded to be patient regarding their religion which Allah had approved for them, which is Islam, so that they would not abandon it for ease nor for hardship, nor for adversity nor for prosperity, until they died as Muslims, and to compete in patience against the enemies who conceal their religion. Likewise said more than one of the scholars of the early generations.

As for al-murabatah (stationing oneself, standing guard), it is the constant devotion to a place of worship and steadfastness therein. And it was said: it is waiting for the next prayer after a prayer, as said by [Mujahid and] Ibn Abbas, Sahl ibn Hunayf, Muhammad ibn Ka'b al-Qurazi, and others.

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated here the hadith which Muslim and al-Nasa'i narrated, from the hadith of Malik ibn Anas, from al-Ala' ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Ya'qub, freedman of al-Huraqah, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Shall I not inform you of that by which Allah erases sins and raises ranks?
